gmw_00008230.name: GL-z12
gmw_00008230.overview: GL-z12 is a luminous high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 12.3 to 12.4 [arXiv:2207.09434, arXiv:2208.01612]. It has built up an estimated stellar mass of 10^9 solar masses [arXiv:2207.09434] and exhibits a compact structure that may imply active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2208.01612].

sources.summary: The paper presents GL-z12 as a secure luminous z>10 GLASS candidate with a strong Lyman break, photometric redshift near 12.4, about 10^9 solar masses, and negligible lensing.

quotes.quote: Our most secure candidates are two M_ UV 21 systems: GLASS-z12 and GLASS-z10. These galaxies display abrupt 1.8 mag breaks in their spectral energy distributions, consistent with complete absorption of flux bluewards of Lyman- that is redshifted to z=12.4^ +0.1 _ 0.3 and z=10.4^ +0.4 _ 0.5 . Lower redshift interlopers such as quiescent galaxies with strong Balmer breaks would be comfortably detected at >5 in multiple bands where instead we find no flux. From SED modeling we infer that these galaxies have already built up 10^9 solar masses in stars over the 300-400 Myrs after the Big Bang.

We derive p(z>10) 100 for GL-z12 and p(z>9.4) 100 for GL-z10, with their dramatic 1.8 mag breaks explained by total absorption of photons bluewards of Lyman- by neutral Hydrogen in the intergalactic medium.
For both GL-z10 and GL-z12 we find negligible lensing ( <1.1 ) from all foreground sources within 10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GL-z12-1 as a bright z about 12 GLASS candidate, extended relative to the PSF but compact compared with other candidates, implying possible AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We find that some bright candidates, i.e., GL-z9-1, GL-z12-1, CR2-z12-1, CR-z16-1, and S5-z16-1, are clearly more extended than the PSF, although GL-z12-1 is compact compared to other candidates, implying potential AGN activity.

Using the ERS CEERS and GLASS datasets, 2022arXiv220709434N found two bright galaxy candidates at z 10 and 12 , GLASS-z10 and GLASS-z12, which correspond to GL-z9-1 and GL-z12-1 in our sample, respectively. Their estimates of the photometric redshifts ( z=10.35^ +0.38 _ 0.51 and z=12.38^ +0.13 _ 0.27 for GL-z10-1 and GL-z12-1, respectively, with prospector , from the ApJL published version ) are consistent with our estimates ( z=10.49^ +0.53 _ 0.72 and z=12.28^ +0.08 _ 0.07 ).

gmw_00008231.name: ID 14
gmw_00008231.overview: ID 14 is a massive, isolated galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10.51 solar masses [arXiv:1909.04672]. Although it is the closest galaxy to the quasar line of sight in its field, its elevated equivalent width and narrow absorption components suggest it may actually reside in an incompletely covered group environment [arXiv:1909.04672].

sources.summary: ID 14 is the massive isolated galaxy nearest one quasar sightline; its elevated equivalent width and narrow absorption components may indicate an incompletely covered group environment.

quotes.quote: Finally, we comment on the properties of the only absorber associated with an isolated galaxy. The complete isolation of this source (ID 14) is unlikely, given its stellar mass ( (M_*/M_ ) = 10.51 ). It is possibly living in a massive halo which in turn is expected to host satellite galaxies (see below). And while this source is the closest to the quasar line of sight among individual galaxies in the MUDF, its equivalent width is still elevated for its impact parameter. Moreover, the several narrow kinematic components found in the absorption spectrum seem to suggest, in analogy with the other \ arising from groups, that this absorber could be associated with a group that we do not fully cover in the MUSE footprint.

gmw_00008232.name: IRAS F20036-1547
gmw_00008232.overview: IRAS F20036-1547 is an active galactic nucleus classified based on its WISE colors [arXiv:1912.05548]. The galaxy hosts a fast molecular gas outflow driven by a combination of active galactic nucleus and starburst activity [arXiv:1912.05548].

sources.summary: IRAS F20036-1547 shows a blueshifted OH profile indicating a 200-500 km/s molecular outflow; the paper estimates a 290 solar-mass-per-year outflow rate, WISE AGN colors, and mass-loading lower limit 0.7.

quotes.quote: In two cases, IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402, the blueshifted absorption profiles indicate the presence of powerful and fast ( 200-500 km s ^ 1 ) molecular gas outflows. Consistent with an inside-out quenching scenario, these outflows are depleting the central reservoir of star-forming molecular gas at a similar rate than the intense star formation activity.

If IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402 follow the trend observed in nearby ULIRGs shown in Figure fig:EW_dMdt , then following Equation eq:EW we expect the mass outflow rates to be M _ out,mol 290 M_ yr ^ 1 and 570 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively. Both IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402 are classified as AGN according to their WISE colors, which implies that the outflows could be driven by a combination of AGN and starburst activity.

For IRAS F20036-1547 there are no constraints on the AGN contribution to the luminosity, so by assuming that all the far-infrared emission is associated to the starburst we estimate a mass loading factor lower limit of 0.7 .

gmw_00008233.name: IRAS F13352+6402
gmw_00008233.overview: IRAS F13352+6402 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy classified as an active galactic nucleus based on its WISE colors [arXiv:1912.05548]. The galaxy hosts a fast molecular gas outflow driven by a combination of active galactic nucleus and starburst activity [arXiv:1912.05548].

sources.summary: IRAS F13352+6402 has a powerful 200-500 km/s molecular outflow, very high OH equivalent width, an estimated 570 solar-mass-per-year outflow rate, WISE AGN colors, and mass loading near 1.5.

quotes.quote: In two cases, IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402, the blueshifted absorption profiles indicate the presence of powerful and fast ( 200-500 km s ^ 1 ) molecular gas outflows. Consistent with an inside-out quenching scenario, these outflows are depleting the central reservoir of star-forming molecular gas at a similar rate than the intense star formation activity.

The three ULIRGs in our sample stand out as having large \ equivalent widths relative to other systems of similar luminosity. In particular, IRAS F13352+6402 has one of the highest equivalent widths measured to date, only comparable to that measured in Arp 220 rhc_gonzalez-alfonso12 or IRAS 20087-0308 rhc_spoon13 . Calculating the mass outflow rate solely based on the often optically thick \ doublet profile is extremely challenging.

If IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402 follow the trend observed in nearby ULIRGs shown in Figure fig:EW_dMdt , then following Equation eq:EW we expect the mass outflow rates to be M _ out,mol 290 M_ yr ^ 1 and 570 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively. Both IRAS F20036-1547 and IRAS F13352+6402 are classified as AGN according to their WISE colors, which implies that the outflows could be driven by a combination of AGN and starburst activity. For IRAS F13352+6402 the AGN contribution to the bolometric luminosity may be as low as 10 rhc_nardini10 , so if we convert the far-infrared luminosity associated to the starburst into a SFR we find a mass loading factor 1.5 .

gmw_00008234.name: IRAS F10091+4704
gmw_00008234.overview: IRAS F10091+4704 is a double-nuclei galaxy merger [arXiv:0908.4271, arXiv:1912.05548] classified as a Seyfert 2 ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0908.4271]. The starburst-dominated system hosts a molecular gas inflow with a high median velocity [arXiv:1912.05548] alongside a high-velocity outflow [arXiv:0908.4271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2 ULIRG and finds blended Mg absorption plus an outflow extending to about -800 in higher-SNR data.

quotes.quote: The more recent Kewley (2006) division of these optical-emission-line ratios into excitation types classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN, FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2, FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary, FSC 1630+15 at the LINER/Composite boundary, and FSC 2349+24 as a Seyfert 2.
The position angle of the longslit was selected to cover both nuclei of FSC 1009+47 and FSC 2349+24.

FSC1009+47: In the FSC1009+47 spectrum, the absorption trough extends from roughly the systemic velocity to about -450 . At the coarse level of comparison allowed by the poor spectral SNR, the shapes of 5892 and profiles match without any scaling. Better SNR at shows the outflow reaches -800 .

sources.summary: IRAS F10091+4704 is a starburst-dominated merger with a unique inverted P-Cygni OH profile, the highest measured OH inflow velocity, and an inferred molecular inflow rate near 100 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: In the case of IRAS F10091+4704 --a merger system where the nuclei have apparently coalesced rhc_veilleux02 -, we detect a clear inverted P-Cygni profile which provides strong evidence for the presence of a molecular gas inflow. This profile is unique among ULIRGs, and only comparable in significance to the inverted \ P-Cygni profile observed in Circinus rhc_stone16 . As Figure fig:comp shows, the inflowing molecular gas in this galaxy has the highest OH median velocity ( v_ 50 =+430 km s ^ 1 ) measured to date. If we use a scaled version the OH-based mass inflow rate measured in NGC 4418 of 12 M_ yr ^ 1 rhc_gonzalez-alfonso12 , we estimate a molecular inflow rate for IRAS F10091+4704 of M _ mol,in 12 (211/113) (3.7 10^ 12 /2 10^ 11 )^ 1/2 100 M_ yr ^ 1 . This inflow rate is about 30 of the total SFR of the system.

gmw_00008235.name: A
gmw_00008235.overview: Galaxy A is the brightest of three objects considered near a damped Lyman-alpha absorber sightline [arXiv:1609.00384]. It is visible in archival SDSS imaging and has a photometric redshift of 0.08, which is inconsistent with the absorber's redshift [arXiv:1609.00384].

sources.summary: Candidate A is the brightest object near the DLA sightline and matches an earlier SDSS galaxy, but its z=0.08 photometric redshift is inconsistent with the absorber.

quotes.quote: The brightest of the three objects considered in this study, A (at an impact parameter of 30 kpc), was faintly visible before the raw images had been processed and is also visible in archival SDSS imaging, which we show for comparison in Figure sdss . This is the same galaxy identified by Ellison et al. (2012) and Zwaan et al. (2015). However, as mentioned above, the photometric redshift of galaxy A ( z=0.08 ) is inconsistent with that of the DLA ( z_ abs =0.602 ).

gmw_00008236.name: B
gmw_00008236.overview: Galaxy B is a newly detected damped Lyman-alpha system host candidate [arXiv:1609.00384]. It has a stellar mass of approximately log(M/Msun) = 9.9 and is located at an impact parameter of 15 kpc [arXiv:1609.00384].

sources.summary: Candidate B is a new lower-impact-parameter DLA-host candidate, with stellar mass log(M/Msun)=9.9 at 15 kpc, and remains plausible under the paper's scaling-relation tests.

quotes.quote: Based on the K-band photometry of our NIRI observations, we determine stellar masses of log (M _ /M _ ) = 9.9, 9.7 and 11.1 for the three candidates, which are located at impact parameters of 15, 17 and 30 kpc respectively. The two galaxies at the lowest impact parameters are new detections in our NIRI data. Based on a photometric redshift of z=0.08 (Ellison et al. 2012), the unresolved nature of the object, and inconsistency with the N(HI) -- impact parameter relation (e.g. Krogager et al. 2012; Christensen et al. 2014), we conclude that the DLA is not associated with the highest mass, largest separation object of the three candidates. The remaining two galaxies are consistent with these scaling relations and therefore remain plausible candidates for the DLA host.

gmw_00008237.name: C
gmw_00008237.overview: Candidate C is a galaxy identified as a plausible host for a damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:1609.00384]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 9.7 solar masses and is located at an impact parameter of 17 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1609.00384].

sources.summary: Candidate C is a new lower-impact-parameter DLA-host candidate, with stellar mass log(M/Msun)=9.7 at 17 kpc, and remains plausible under the paper's scaling-relation tests.

quotes.quote: Based on the K-band photometry of our NIRI observations, we determine stellar masses of log (M _ /M _ ) = 9.9, 9.7 and 11.1 for the three candidates, which are located at impact parameters of 15, 17 and 30 kpc respectively. The two galaxies at the lowest impact parameters are new detections in our NIRI data. Based on a photometric redshift of z=0.08 (Ellison et al. 2012), the unresolved nature of the object, and inconsistency with the N(HI) -- impact parameter relation (e.g. Krogager et al. 2012; Christensen et al. 2014), we conclude that the DLA is not associated with the highest mass, largest separation object of the three candidates. The remaining two galaxies are consistent with these scaling relations and therefore remain plausible candidates for the DLA host.

gmw_00008238.name: MRC0355-037
gmw_00008238.overview: MRC0355-037 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.10291]. Its surrounding field exhibits a factor-of-two overdensity of submillimeter galaxies with flux densities above 4 mJy [arXiv:1806.10291].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the MRC0355-037 field as one of three radio-galaxy environments with a factor-of-two SMG overdensity above 4 mJy, significant at about 3 sigma.

quotes.quote: We find overdensities of a factor of 2 in the source counts of 3 individual fields (4C+23.56, PKS1138-262 and MRC0355-037) over areas of 200 sq deg.

In the fields towards 4C+23.56, PKS1138-262 and MRC0355-037, however, the numbers of sources with S _ > 4 mJy exceeds the number of expected sources in a blank field by a factor of 2 . The significance of these overdensities is > 3 , which means that the probability of finding overdensities like these ones by chance is < 0.3 .
Only in the surroundings of 4C+23.56, PKS1138-262 and MRC0355-037 did we detect individual overdensity signals of 2 with a significance of 3 .

gmw_00008239.name: Mrk 1450
gmw_00008239.overview: Mrk 1450 is a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2006.02447]. It has a low far-infrared metallicity that is reconciled by direct line measurements [arXiv:2006.02447].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 1450 as a DGS outlier whose fiducial far-infrared metallicity is low because of SFR proxy assumptions; direct line measurements reconcile its abundance.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1450 : Metallicity from the fiducial \ method is lower by ( O/H )=-0.48 than the based value reported in . This discrepancy appears to arise largely from the SFR, which is used only as a proxy for \ line luminosity. The extinction-corrected \ Balmer line flux Izotov1994 corresponds to SFR =0.09 (via Equation eq:L_Ha ), comparable to the SFR =0.11 reported by Sargsyan2009 based on 1.4 GHz measurements, and lower than the 0.43 from Madden2013 . Adopting this direct measurement of \ line luminosity along with , , and ICF Izotov2014,Izotov1994 , we find good agreement with ( O/H )=0.02 .

gmw_00008240.name: NGC 1350
gmw_00008240.overview: NGC 1350 is a barred spiral galaxy located in the outskirts of the Fornax Cluster [arXiv:1811.11386]. It features an inner ring and an outer pseudo-ring [arXiv:2304.00515], with very little atomic gas in its central region [arXiv:1811.11386] and no evidence of recent tidal interactions [arXiv:1811.11386].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1350 as a barred spiral on the Fornax outskirts, with no nearby interaction evidence and very little central atomic gas, resembling an H I hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 1350 is classified as (R')SB()ab and lies in the outskirts of the Fornax Cluster, at a distance of 7 Mpc ( 5R _ 200 ) from the cluster centre. This rules out the possibility of ram pressure removing the 1 gas from the galaxy. As per the nearest neighbour estimates, we do not find any close neighbours to this galaxy that may have interacted with it in the recent few Gyrs. We also do not find any evidence of tidal interactions, which if any, should have shown in its 1 disk profile. This galaxy has very little 1 in its central parts, almost as if there is an 1 ``hole" (see Fig. fig:galaxy-maps ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1350 as a barred ring galaxy where HI traces the outer ring, resonance radii match rings, and SFR/HI surface densities peak near resonances.

quotes.quote: NGC 1350: This galaxy is classified as (R')SB()ab. The composite optical image in the centre left panel of Fig. fig:mosaic3 shows a bar, an inner ring and a outer pseudo-ring. The 1 gas is observed to trace the outer ring. The velocity map shows no slight warping. NGC 1350 is observed to posses an inner ring and an outer ring, as seen in the optical image. We are able to derive the iUHR, oUHR and OLR radius for this galaxy and find that the oUHR location (pink ellipse) is somewhat in agreement with location of the inner ring, while the OLR radius (orange ellipse) traces the outer ring quite well.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1350 as a double-ringed example whose phase-shift crossings place one pattern inside the bar and another between the inner and outer pseudorings.

quotes.quote: The phase-shift distribution of double-ringed galaxy NGC 1350 (Figure olrrings , upper frames) shows two P/N crossings corresponding to the two circles superposed on the image. The smaller circle seems associated with a pattern inside the bar, while the larger circle lies between the inner and outer pseudorings. Thus, in this case there is some support for the findings from the numerical models.

gmw_00008241.name: UGCA 168
gmw_00008241.overview: UGCA 168, also known as ESO 373-008, is an Scd spiral galaxy located in the low-density outskirts of the LGG 180 group [arXiv:1811.11386]. Photometric observations reveal vertical-profile bumps that indicate the presence of an extraplanar dust component extending high above the mid-plane [arXiv:2207.11589].

sources.summary: The paper says UGCA 168 may not be truly atomic-gas deficient, noting possible R-band uncertainty and describing it as an Scd galaxy in LGG 180's low-density outskirts.

quotes.quote: It is worth noting that UGCA 168 does not appear to be 1 deficient as can be seen by its location in the f_ atm q relation. There may be a number of reasons for this. Firstly, this discrepancy may be associated with any uncertainty in the measured R band magnitude for this galaxy.

UGCA 168 is an Scd type galaxy residing in the outskirts ( 470 kpc or 2R _ 200 ) of the group LGG 180. This is a loose group with 12 members (Pisano et al. pisano11 ). Pisano et al. note that they do not observe any intra-group X-ray emission in the LGG 180 group, thus indicating that the IGM is not dense enough for ram pressure to take effect.

sources.summary: The paper flags ESO 373-008 as a Main-sample galaxy with vertical-profile bumps suggesting an additional extended dust component, arguing the extraplanar FIR excess is real rather than PSF or inclination artefact.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies from the Main sample (NGC 4244, ESO 373-008, NGC 5529, and NGC 5746) show bumps in their cumulative vertical profiles above the model profile which can point to the existence of another vertically extended component.

We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

gmw_00008242.name: ESO 139-G12
gmw_00008242.overview: ESO 139-G12 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of approximately 0.017 [arXiv:1207.3186]. The galaxy is correlated with two ultra-high-energy cosmic rays [arXiv:1109.0267, arXiv:1207.3186] and exhibits a low level of X-ray absorption [arXiv:1109.0267, arXiv:1207.3186].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 139-G12 as a UHECR-correlated AGN and uses X-ray spectral fitting to estimate its flux, absorption, luminosity, and bolometric output.

quotes.quote: The AGN ESO 139-G12 is correlated with two UHECRs, making it a particularly interesting source candidate. Previously only an upper limit on its bolometric luminosity existed, derived by zfg09 . This source is bright enough for us to determine its flux by fitting the spectrum using xspec . Assuming an absorbed power law, this yields a hard photon index of 0.72 ^ +0.09 _ 0.09 (error denotes 90 confidence interval) and very low absorption: N _ H < 8.2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 at 90 confidence level (Figure fit ).

When combined with its redshift, z = 0.017 ( 74 Mpc assuming a standard cosmology with H_o = 70 ), we find the 2-10 keV luminosity of ESO 139-G12: L_ 2-10 3.1^ +0.4 _ 0.7 10^ 42 erg \ s^ 2 . The conversion factor relating L_ 2-10 to the bolometric luminosity depends on the relative activity of the AGN LxConv . In the absence of a black hole mass measurement for this galaxy, we follow zfg09 and take L_ bol = 20 L_ 2-10 , as is typical for an AGN with Eddington ratio less than 0.1. We therefore estimate the bolometric luminosity of ESO 139-G12 to be L_ bol 6.2 ^ +0.8 _ 1.4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper measures ESO 139-G12 with Chandra, derives its X-ray and bolometric luminosity, and finds it is a weak AGN candidate correlated with two UHECRs.

quotes.quote: The AGN ESO 139-G12 is correlated with two UHECRs, making it a particularly interesting source candidate. This source is bright enough to determine its flux by fitting the spectrum using xspec . The fit, assuming an absorbed power law, returned a hard photon index of 0.72 ^ +0.09 _ 0.09 (error denotes 90 confidence interval) and very low absorption, N _ H < 8.2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 at 90 confidence level (Figure 1). From this fit we extracted a 2-10 keV flux of 4.7 ^ +0.6 _ 1.1 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (error denotes 90 confidence interval).

This gives L_ bol 6.2 ^ +0.8 _ 1.4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 - the first measurement of the bolometric luminosity of ESO 139-G12 . Although ESO 139-G12 is the only galaxy we observed which appears to have nuclear activity, it is always possible that the host galaxy simply dominates the AGN energetically, making the AGN impossible to detect.

gmw_00008243.name: gal1
gmw_00008243.overview: The galaxy system gal1 is an interacting merger of gas-rich galaxies with strong ongoing star formation [arXiv:2101.07293]. It exhibits green-valley colors and displays a complex, mixed pattern of ionized gas kinematics and chemical abundance [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper treats gal1 as a special merging system with green-valley colors, mixed ionization/chemical behavior, gas-rich galaxies, and strong ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: In the latter subset, all but one galaxy ( gal6 ) have NUV->4 , with four falling in the green valley regime with 4<NUV-<5 ( gal1 , gal3 , gal11 , gal12 ), and two in the red sequence ( gal5 and gal8 , but we note that gal5 could also be counted as a green valley galaxy if the dividing lines are made tilted).

One interesting case is the galaxy system 1. Since the log( [ O iii ] /H ) in this system shows relatively small variation, it is hard to tell whether this galaxy belongs to the first group or the second. According to its optical images, it is certainly in a merging system.

In addition, the kinematics of the ionized gas resemble those in the second group. However, as we will see in the next section, the chemical abundance of this system shows a similar pattern to that of the first group. It appears that this system is in a different evolutionary stage compared with other systems in our sample, where the galaxies are still gas-rich and strongly forming stars.

gmw_00008244.name: gal2
gmw_00008244.overview: The galaxy gal2 is a very red, quiescent system located on the red sequence [arXiv:2101.07293]. It features an H-alpha blob associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions or mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper identifies gal2 as a very red, quiescent red-sequence system whose H-alpha blob resembles those in quenched interaction or merger systems.

quotes.quote: The galaxy discovered by gal2 in our sample Lin-17 is not shown in the figure, due to its unusually large value of NUV magnitude ( NUV=-11.0 11.3 ) according to NSA. Although the exact value of NUV- is uncertain, it should be true that the galaxy is very red, based on the quiescent nature of the galaxy as discussed in depth in Lin-17 . As a sanity check, we also look into the SFR vs. M _ * diagram, which leads us to the same classification and confirms that gal2 lies in the red sequence.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

gmw_00008245.name: gal3
gmw_00008245.overview: The galaxy system gal3 is a high-mass system associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions or mergers [arXiv:2101.07293]. Its host galaxies are already quenched, and the system features ionized gas blobs characterized by high metallicities [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper places gal3 among high-mass systems whose H-alpha blobs have high metallicities and merger-associated ionization patterns, treating the system as part of a quenched interaction group.

quotes.quote: The other group includes galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12, which present a continuous distribution from the SF region to the AGN region, with the \ blobs sitting in the lower left position of the data distribution. On the other hand, these \ blob spaxels locate to the lower right position relative to the SF sequence, indicating overall high metallicities, which is consistent with the fact that their host galaxies are all high-mass galaxies (see fig:mass_color ). Comparing this with the result we obtain in the previous section, one can see that these two groups are also show certain differences in morphology and environments, which serves as another piece of evidence that the visually found \ blobs in our sample might have different origins.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

gmw_00008246.name: gal6
gmw_00008246.overview: The host galaxy gal6 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1902.03754] that is inactive and devoid of an active nucleus [arXiv:1902.03754]. It is a high-mass, quenched system [arXiv:2101.07293] that shows evidence of past galaxy-galaxy interactions or mergers [arXiv:1902.03754, arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper describes HX3 as an elliptical, inactive host whose associated star-forming region has a large velocity offset, raising tidal-tail, gas-accretion, or neighbouring-galaxy photoionization interpretations.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy has an elliptical morphology (G18) with no clear indication of recent merger/integration in its light profile. The host galaxy of X3 is however, devoid of any emission lines and thus does not have an active nucleus. Interestingly, X3 is a large star forming region, however it has a velocity which is very different (about 400 km/s) from the disc as seen in the stellar velocity profile (due to poor SNR of the host galaxy the velocity map is not visible). It is possible that this galaxy may have undergone a recent merger with a galaxy which may have left a tidal tail or there has been a recent event of gas accretion as in the case of Cheung16b . Given that there is such a high velocity difference, it is also possible that the emission is not associated with HX3, and is rather associated with a neighbouring galaxy (SDSS ObjID: 1237651252560461948), which is about 570 km/s offset from it, which also has an active nucleus.
Thus, a past interaction with HX3 may have left a tidal tail, which is being photoionised by the neighbouring galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper places gal6 among high-mass systems whose H-alpha blobs have high metallicities and merger-associated ionization patterns, treating the system as part of a quenched interaction group.

quotes.quote: The other group includes galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12, which present a continuous distribution from the SF region to the AGN region, with the \ blobs sitting in the lower left position of the data distribution. On the other hand, these \ blob spaxels locate to the lower right position relative to the SF sequence, indicating overall high metallicities, which is consistent with the fact that their host galaxies are all high-mass galaxies (see fig:mass_color ). Comparing this with the result we obtain in the previous section, one can see that these two groups are also show certain differences in morphology and environments, which serves as another piece of evidence that the visually found \ blobs in our sample might have different origins.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

gmw_00008247.name: gal11
gmw_00008247.overview: The galaxy gal11 is a high-mass system that belongs to a quenched group of galaxies [arXiv:2101.07293]. It features ionized hydrogen blobs associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions and mergers [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper places gal11 among high-mass systems whose H-alpha blobs have high metallicities and merger-associated ionization patterns, treating the system as part of a quenched interaction group.

quotes.quote: The other group includes galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12, which present a continuous distribution from the SF region to the AGN region, with the \ blobs sitting in the lower left position of the data distribution. On the other hand, these \ blob spaxels locate to the lower right position relative to the SF sequence, indicating overall high metallicities, which is consistent with the fact that their host galaxies are all high-mass galaxies (see fig:mass_color ). Comparing this with the result we obtain in the previous section, one can see that these two groups are also show certain differences in morphology and environments, which serves as another piece of evidence that the visually found \ blobs in our sample might have different origins.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

gmw_00008248.name: gal4
gmw_00008248.overview: The galaxy gal4 is classified as a low-mass blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2101.07293]. It features an off-galaxy star-forming region characterized by low metallicity and low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper places gal4 in the low-mass blue subset whose H-alpha blob appears as a low-metallicity off-galaxy star-forming region with low velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Our galaxies can be broadly divided into two subsets: low-mass blue galaxies ( gal4 , gal7 , gal9 , gal10 and gal13 ) with M_ <10^ 10 M_ and NUV-<4 , and high-mass galaxies with M_ >10^ 10 M_ and relatively red colors.

The \ blobs in the former group (consisting of galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13) appear as low-metallicity and off-galaxy starburst or SF regions, while the \ blobs in the latter group (consisting of galaxy system 2, 3, 6, 11, and 12) seem to be associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions/mergers in which the main galaxies are already quenched.

On the other hand, galaxy system 4, 7, 9, 10, and 13 exhibit low velocity dispersions across the entire system, which is consistent with what we see in fig:mass_color . Therefore, kinematically speaking, these low-mass blue galaxies (or group 1, which we introduced back in sec:photo ) seem to form a distinct group as well.

gmw_00008253.name: gal5
gmw_00008253.overview: The galaxy gal5 is classified as a merging system that has retired from star formation [arXiv:2101.07293]. It lacks strong emission lines other than H-alpha, and its optical residual structure does not match the morphology of its H-alpha blobs [arXiv:2101.07293].

sources.summary: The paper groups gal5 with merger systems retired from star formation, noting weak non-H-alpha lines and no residual optical structure matching the H-alpha blob.

quotes.quote: The rest of the galaxy systems in our sample (5 and 8) are short of strong emission lines other than \ line, thus showing only a few \ blob spaxels in BPT diagrams. Nevertheless, according to the evidence we find in previous sections, both of them should belong to the second group, i.e. merging galaxies retired from star formation. We note that there is no clear sign of AGN in our sample.
In gal2 , gal5 , and gal8 , we could not find any residual structure at the locations of the \ blobs that resembles the morphology we see in the \ intensity maps.

gmw_00008255.name: PJ120207.6
gmw_00008255.overview: PJ120207.6 is a strongly lensed, hyperluminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of 2.442 [arXiv:2405.09140]. It contains a diffuse, low-excitation gas component [arXiv:1811.11215], and there is no evidence that its gas heating is driven by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1811.11215].

sources.summary: PLCK G138.6+62.0 is unresolved in submillimeter interferometry, has magnification estimated from FIR temperature, a diffuse low-excitation gas component, and no evidence for X-ray-dominated heating.

quotes.quote: PLCK G200.6+46.1 and PLCK G138.6+62.0 are the only unresolved sources in our follow-up submm interferometry of the whole sample. The main lens galaxies in these systems also lack spectroscopic redshift measurements, which precludes the calculation of detailed lensing models. Since most results rely on line ratios, we nonetheless included these GEMS in the overall analysis and used their position in the L_ FIR - T_ dust parameter space relative to the sequence of unlensed SMGs to derive their gravitational magnification see figure 5 and justification in canameras15 .

The properties of the cooler and more extended low-excitation component in PLCK G092.5+42.9 and PLCK G244.8+54.9 are similar to average conditions in local ULIRGs but, for PLCK G113.7+61.0 and PLCK G138.6+62.0 this component is diffuse, with n_ H_2 = 10^ 2.4 - 10^ 2.8 cm ^ 3 . This suggests that some high-redshift dusty starbursts contain a cool, low-density gas phase, comparable to that over the Galactic disk.

Although atomic carbon diagnostics suggest that PLCK G138.6+62.0 falls in the regime covered both by PDRs and XDRs nesvadba18 , we find L_ CO(7-6) /L_ CO(1-0) and L_ CO(7-6) /L_ CO(3-2) to be lower than those in the grid of XDR models by a factor of about 3--4. Consequently, there is no evidence that gas heating in the GEMS can be attributed to incident X-ray radiation fields from a central AGN above the lower value of 1.6 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 considered in the models.

sources.summary: The paper observes strongly lensed hyperluminous infrared galaxy PJ120207.6 at z=2.442 and detects its faint CO emission with the eQ receiver.

quotes.quote: We chose a strongly-lensed Hyper Luminous Infrared Galaxy, PJ120207.6 at a redshift of 2.442. The parameters of this target are listed in Table tab:high-z . For the observations, we set the center frequency to 33.497 GHz, following harrington18 . During the observations, the typical system noise temperature was about 60--80 K. We successfully detected the subtle CO emission originating from PJ 120207.6, which is shown in Figure fig:high-z .
The total on-source integration time was 2 hours.

gmw_00008256.name: NGC 3457
gmw_00008256.overview: NGC 3457 is an early-type elliptical galaxy with a stellar mass of a few 10^9 solar masses [arXiv:0912.4266]. It is a member of the LGG 225 group that shows no morphological signatures of distortion [arXiv:0912.4266], hosting an old stellar population with an average stellar age of 4 Gyr [arXiv:0912.4266, arXiv:1401.4003].

sources.summary: NGC 3457 is an undistorted early-type LGG 225 member, UV-faint and red, with SED modeling implying an old stellar population and a mass of a few 10^9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: The early-type members of LGG 225, NGC 3457 and NGC 3522, have masses of the order of a few 10 ^9 M _ , comparable to the Local Group ellipticals. The two early-type galaxies members of the group, NGC 3457 and NGC 3522 do not show signatures of distortion. Also for LGG 225, the (FUV-NUV) colors show a large dispersion, the two ellipticals (NGC 3457 and NGC 3522) being the UV-faintest and reddest galaxies in the sample, however their magnitudes have a large uncertainty due to the short exposure times.

In this context, although no morphological peculiarities are shown by both NGC 3457 and NGC 3522, we investigated the galaxies SEDs (see Figure SED ) since FUV and NUV bands are a sensitive probe of young stellar populations e.g. In the first case, NGC 3457 has a derived age close to the oldest of the range found for spirals and it is about three times more massive than NGC 3522. The derived masses, (between 2 and 4 10 ^9 M _ for NGC 3457 and 1-2 10 ^9 M _ for NGC 3522) are comparable to the least massive spirals in LGG 225 and to the ellipticals in our LG NGC 205, M32, Mateo98 .

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3457 with a galaxy-encounter simulation, matching its B-band luminosity, multiwavelength SED, stellar age, stellar mass, and gas content.

quotes.quote: NGC 3457       This E is well matched by a simulation of an encounter between two triaxial collapsing halos of equal mass and perpendicular spins, like UGC 06324, but with a total mass 10 times larger than that of UGC 06324 (Table table2 ). The predicted B band absolute magnitude, M_B=-18.54 mag (Table table3 ), agrees well with the measured value in Table table1 . All the total photometric data, from FUV to 100 m, are well matched by the SED provided by our simulation at the selected snapshot (Figure figure1 ). Warm and cold dust components in the FIR SED, where only upper limits are available, have the same properties as on average for Es in paola4 and paola6 . The current age of the simulated galaxy is 11.4 Gyr and its average stellar age within R _ 25 , weighted by B band luminosity, is 4 Gyr (Table table3 ).

gmw_00008257.name: NGC 3522
gmw_00008257.overview: NGC 3522 is a dwarf early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0912.4266, arXiv:1401.4003] that belongs to the LGG 225 group [arXiv:0912.4266]. It is a red, ultraviolet-faint system [arXiv:0912.4266] that shows no signs of morphological distortion [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: NGC 3522 is described as an undistorted early-type LGG 225 member, UV-faint and red, less massive than NGC 3457, and compatible with the paper's SED modeling.

quotes.quote: The early-type members of LGG 225, NGC 3457 and NGC 3522, have masses of the order of a few 10 ^9 M _ , comparable to the Local Group ellipticals. The two early-type galaxies members of the group, NGC 3457 and NGC 3522 do not show signatures of distortion. Also for LGG 225, the (FUV-NUV) colors show a large dispersion, the two ellipticals (NGC 3457 and NGC 3522) being the UV-faintest and reddest galaxies in the sample, however their magnitudes have a large uncertainty due to the short exposure times.

In this context, although no morphological peculiarities are shown by both NGC 3457 and NGC 3522, we investigated the galaxies SEDs (see Figure SED ) since FUV and NUV bands are a sensitive probe of young stellar populations e.g. In the first case, NGC 3457 has a derived age close to the oldest of the range found for spirals and it is about three times more massive than NGC 3522. The derived masses, (between 2 and 4 10 ^9 M _ for NGC 3457 and 1-2 10 ^9 M _ for NGC 3522) are comparable to the least massive spirals in LGG 225 and to the ellipticals in our LG NGC 205, M32, Mateo98 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3522 as a dwarf early-type galaxy formed by a 1:1 merger, matching its luminosity, SED, stellar ages, stellar mass, and gas properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3522       A merging of two halos with a mass ratio of 1:1 and a total mass five times less than that of NGC 3457 (Table table2 ) accounts for the evolution of this dwarf ETG. The spins of the systems are equal ( =0.06; MC03), parallel, and both are aligned with the shorter of their principal axes. They merge into a unique configuration about 9.4 Gyr ago, the current age of the simulated galaxy being 12.6 Gyr. The SFR proceeds about 3.1 Gyr before the merger and contributes to about 16 of the total SFR. The B band total magnitude of the simulated galaxy is -17.81 mag, in good agreement with the values in Table table1 .

gmw_00008258.name: NGC 1311
gmw_00008258.overview: NGC 1311 is an edge-on spiral galaxy with an undistorted disk structure [arXiv:0912.4266]. The galaxy contains numerous regions of active star formation, and the presence of dust gives its body a broken appearance in ultraviolet images [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: NGC 1311 is viewed edge-on with an undistorted disk, active star-forming structure, no UV bar despite RC3 classification, and a dust-driven broken appearance in NUV/FUV images.

quotes.quote: IC 1959 and NGC 1311 are viewed edge-on. In both galaxies the disk structure appears undistorted. Meurer06 describe IC 1959 and NGC 1311 as galaxies with an elongated shape mixed with gas and a large number of regions of active star formation, also prominent in H _ . The presence of dust in NGC 1311 gives a `broken' appearance to its body, in both the NUV and FUV images. The UV images of IC 1959 and NGC 1311 do not show clear evidence of bars, while they are classified as barred in the RC3 catalog.

Extinction certainly affects the color: a signature is clearly visible in the `broken' appearance of NGC 1311's disk (see Figure 93 ). Bars are easily recognizable in the UV bands, although both NGC 1311 and IC 1959, indicated as barred galaxies in the RC3 classification, do not show a bar in our UV images.

gmw_00008259.name: ESO305-009
gmw_00008259.overview: ESO305-009 is a barred spiral galaxy with open spiral arms [arXiv:0912.4266]. These structural features, along with its open arms, may indicate past interaction events [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: ESO305-009 is described as a spiral with a bar, among the bluest LGG 127 galaxies in FUV-NUV color, and showing open arms linked to possible past interactions.

quotes.quote: A bar structure is present also in the spiral ESO305-009. The (FUV-NUV) colors of LGG 127 (Table 3) show a large dispersion, NGC 1827 being the reddest galaxy with (FUV-NUV)=0.80 0.10 and ESO305-009 and ESO305-017 the bluest with (FUV-NUV)=0.31 0.11. A possible signature of past interaction events is the presence of a bar structure, seen in LGG 127's, NGC 1744 and ESO305-009 which show in addition open arms, and multiple arms, as NGC 1808 in the same group.

gmw_00008260.name: NGC 3454
gmw_00008260.overview: NGC 3454 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that forms a close pair with its companion, NGC 3455 [arXiv:0912.4266]. This pair exhibits tidal distortions and blue color profiles, suggesting that their ongoing interaction has triggered recent star formation [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: NGC 3454 is discussed as the close projected and redshift-space companion of NGC 3455, with the pair showing tidal distortion and blue color profiles consistent with interaction-triggered recent star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3454 and NGC 3455 are close both in projection and in redshift space (Table 1). The first galaxy displays a clean edge-on spiral morphology while the outer eastern arms of NGC 3455 appear tidally distorted. Summarizing, although cases of morphological distortion/asymmetries and multiple arms are present in all groups, only in LGG 225 there is a direct evidence of tidal distortion, as in the cases of pairs NGC 3447/NGC 3447A and NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

Blue color profiles of the interacting pair NGC 3454/NGC 3455, and NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, suggest that recent star formation events could be triggered by tidal interaction. Some LGG 225 members display unambiguously interacting (NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, NGC 3454/NGC 3455) and distorted morphologies (UGC 6035) particularly evident in the UV imaging. There is evidence that such episodes are triggered by on-going interaction as in the case of NGC 3447A or NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

gmw_00008261.name: UGC 6035
gmw_00008261.overview: UGC 6035 is an irregular galaxy belonging to the LGG 225 group [arXiv:0912.4266]. It exhibits a distorted morphology likely caused by a galaxy interaction, with ultraviolet emission extending far beyond its optical boundary and indicating a very young stellar population [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: UGC 6035 is described as an irregular, probably interaction-distorted LGG 225 member whose UV emission reaches beyond its optical image and whose FUV-NUV color suggests very young stars.

quotes.quote: UGC 6035 has a foreground star superimposed on its main body, it appears also very irregular and probably distorted by an interaction event. Furthermore the UV emission in NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, in NGC 3455 and UGC 6035 extends much farther out than the respective optical images (Figure 225 ). This could be also the case of the strongly distorted galaxy UGC 6035, whose (FUV-NUV) color suggests the presence of very young stellar population. Some LGG 225 members display unambiguously interacting (NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, NGC 3454/NGC 3455) and distorted morphologies (UGC 6035) particularly evident in the UV imaging.

gmw_00008262.name: JO113
gmw_00008262.overview: JO113 is a galaxy featuring a ram pressure stripped tail where star formation serves as the predominant ionization mechanism [arXiv:1811.00823]. Pure star formation dominates the emission in this tail, though its star formation rate accounts for only 1 to 2 percent of the galaxy's total [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: The paper finds JO113's tail is star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics, pure-SF emission dominates its tail, tail SFR is only 1-2% of total, and diffuse emission is unusually prominent.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160). The only two galaxies with significantly lower fractions ( 55 ) are JO113 and JW100, in which the diffuse emission is more prominent.

gmw_00008263.name: JO141
gmw_00008263.overview: JO141 is a galaxy featuring a stripped tail where star formation serves as the predominant ionization mechanism [arXiv:1811.00823]. This pure star-forming component dominates the tail's emission, though only about one to two percent of the galaxy's total star formation rate occurs within the tail itself [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO141's stripped tail is classified as star-formation dominated in the diagnostic diagrams; the pure-SF component dominates the tail, while only about 1-2% of total SFR occurs in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

This pure star-forming component dominates the tail's emission, though the tail accounts for only one to two percent of the galaxy's total star formation rate [arXiv:1811.00823].

gmw_00008265.name: JO175
gmw_00008265.overview: The galaxy JO175 features a tail where star formation serves as the predominant ionization mechanism [arXiv:1811.00823]. This star formation accounts for more than half of the tail's total emission and contributes approximately 4% to 5% of the galaxy's overall star formation rate [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO175's tail is star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics; SF accounts for more than half of the tail emission, and about 4-5% of the total SFR occurs in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

SF still accounts for more than the half of the tail emission in JO175 and JO60 (66 and 58 , respectively), and only for about 20-30 in JO135 (29 ) , JO204 (36 ) and JO194 (21 ). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

gmw_00008266.name: JO60
gmw_00008266.overview: JO60 is a jellyfish galaxy characterized by a long, ram-pressure stripped tail [arXiv:2208.11955]. Star formation is the predominant ionization mechanism in this tail [arXiv:1811.00823], which also exhibits unilateral extraplanar radio emission [arXiv:2208.11955].

sources.summary: JO60's tail is star-formation dominated in the main diagnostics; SF accounts for more than half of the tail emission, and about 4-5% of total SFR occurs in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

SF still accounts for more than the half of the tail emission in JO175 and JO60 (66 and 58 , respectively), and only for about 20-30 in JO135 (29 ) , JO204 (36 ) and JO194 (21 ). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

sources.summary: The paper describes JO60 as a jellyfish galaxy whose radio emission is truncated while H follows the stellar disk, with unilateral extraplanar radio emission and a long ram-pressure tail.

quotes.quote: Figure radio-halpha shows galaxies in which radio and H emission are similarly truncated with respect to the stellar disk (JW39 and JO49), or where radio emission appears to be more truncated than H (JW100 and JO206), or where only radio is truncated whilst H closely follows the stellar disk (JO60).
JW39, JO60 and JW100 show unilateral, extraplanar radio emission extending for up to 40 arcsec in the direction of the stripped H tail.
The gap between the total and the disk-only slopes is more striking for those galaxies with the most evident radio tails, that are JW39, JO60, JW100, and JO206.

gmw_00008267.name: JO95
gmw_00008267.overview: JO95 is a jellyfish galaxy with a tail dominated by star formation [arXiv:1811.00823]. The galaxy is a low-total-star-formation-rate outlier that forms stars at a rate of 0.4 solar masses per year, with approximately 4 to 5 percent of this activity occurring within its tail [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO95's tail is star-formation dominated in the diagnostics and pure-SF component, but the galaxy is a low-total-SFR outlier; about 4-5% of total SFR occurs in the tail.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

Considering instead only the pure SF component (no Composite) in the NII DD, this dominates ( 80 ) the emission in the tails of 9 of our galaxies (JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO201, JO206, JO49, JO95, JW39). Our jellyfishes form in total between 1.5 and 6 M_ yr^ 1 , with two outliers being JO194 with 8.3 M_ yr^ 1 and JO95 with 0.4 M_ yr^ 1 (Table 3). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

gmw_00008268.name: SMACS z12a
gmw_00008268.overview: SMACS z12a is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately z=12 [arXiv:2207.12338]. It is characterized by a young stellar age, a moderate stellar mass, and blue colors [arXiv:2207.12338].

sources.summary: SMACS z12a is one of the paper's robust z~12 candidates, detected in four filters with a clear continuum break, blue colors, high photometric redshift, young age, and moderate stellar mass.

quotes.quote: In the next lower redshift range, one of the two most robust candidates, SMACS z12a, is presented in Fig. fig:candidate-stamp-id-93 . Again, the source is well detected in all four filters and shows a clear continuum break. As before, there is a good agreement between the two photometric redshifts z=12.20 ^ +0.21 _ 0.12 ( BEAGLE ) and z=12.11 0.19 ( EAZY ).

The PDF from EAZY is broader than the BEAGLE one, because of the challenge in matching the blue colors ( 2.60 0.32 ) of this object (Fig. fig:SEF-fit-id-93 ). This source is also among the highest-redshift candidates identified photometrically in the literature e.g. harikane22,naidu22,castellano22 , around 360 Myr after the Big Bang. The derived parameters are very similar to SMACS z16a, with a stellar mass of (M_ / M _ )=8.14^ +0.21 _ 0.17 , and an age of (t_ age / yr )=7.61^ +0.21 _ 0.20 .

gmw_00008269.name: X-3
gmw_00008269.overview: X-3 is a background active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.4391 [arXiv:2303.12141]. It is visible through the foreground galaxy NGC 4536 and has an optical counterpart with the morphology of a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2303.12141].

sources.summary: X-3 is shown to be a background AGN seen through NGC 4536: its extended optical counterpart has high-redshift-galaxy morphology, redshifted oxygen lines at z=0.4391, and AGN-like X-ray/optical properties.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA, Dec; 12^ h 34^ m 32^ s .554 , + 2 10 54 84 and 12^ h 34^ m 31^ s .803 + 2 11 22 23 on the HST /WFC3 F555W image, respectively.

quotes.quote: The calculated redshift (z = 0.4391 0.0010) for one of the sources (X-3) indicates that the source is, in fact, a background active galactic nucleus (AGN). Within the positional error box for X-3 there is a single extended (FWHM = 0.18 0.16 ) optical source whose morphology and color indicates that it is a high redshift galaxy.

The optical counterpart of X-3 is an object sufficiently isolated from bright sources, which makes it easy to extract its spectrum. After subtracting the nebula contribution, we found several emission features in the residual spectrum which were identified as oxygen lines [ OII ] 3737, [ OIII ] 4959, [ OIII ] 5007, and weak H _ shifted at z = 0.4391 0.0010 Statistical errors in the wavelength measurements of the three oxygen lines observed in the AGN spectrum (about 40 km s ^ 1 ), possible off-centre position of the target in the slit (up to 45 km s ^ 1 ), and the systematic shift of narrow oxygen lines known for some AGNs relative to other lines (up to 200 km s ^ 1 ; Boroson2005 ) are taken into account for uncertainty calculation of the redshift. Thus, it is clear that X-3 is in fact an active galactic nucleus (AGN) that is visible through NGC 4536. The optical spectrum of X-3 is shown in Figure fig:ulx3_spec in the 4800--7800 \ range, where all detected object lines are located.

The spectrum of X-3 convincingly shows that this source is indeed an AGN with a redshift of z = 0.4391 0.0010 , and not an X-ray source in NGC 4536 galaxy. The ratio for X-3 is in agreement with the range that is derived from the majority of the AGNs (0.1 - 10) Aird2010 and also below the observed range for ULXs Avdan2016,Fabrika2021 .

gmw_00008270.name: Galaxy 17
gmw_00008270.overview: Galaxy 17 is a group galaxy located 51.2 kiloparsecs from a quasar's line-of-sight [arXiv:astro-ph/0603413]. It exhibits a passive elliptical spectrum and appears to be interacting with Galaxy 18, as evidenced by a flux bridge connecting the pair [arXiv:astro-ph/0603413].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy 17 as the nearest confirmed group galaxy to the quasar sightline, with halo-scale separation but a substantial velocity offset from the absorber.

quotes.quote: The nearest of the galaxies to \ is Galaxy 17, which lies at a distance of 51.2 kpc to the quasar's line-of-sight (hereinafter QLOS). This places the QLOS in the outer parts of the galaxy's halo. This separation is comparable to those seen in other absorption systems (see, for example, steidel97 ). This galaxy, however, does show a large velocity offset from the absorbing system, with velocity differences v v_ gal v_ abs with respect to the four components ranging from 430-610 .

An potential example of such an interaction, based on imaging data, is the pair 17 and 18. The velocity difference between the two ( 358 ), is comparable to the velocity dispersion of the group, and so is feasible for an interaction. A close-up of this pair of galaxies is shown in Fig. fig-gal-pair . There is evidence for a connection in flux between the two, with the flux in the ``bridge'' region reaching >7 above the background. There is certainly no spectroscopic sign of any strong interaction taking place, with both galaxies exhibiting simple passive elliptical spectra.

gmw_00008271.name: Galaxy 18
gmw_00008271.overview: Galaxy 18 is the brightest galaxy in its group at a redshift of approximately 0.66 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603413]. It exhibits a passive elliptical spectrum and shows potential signs of interaction with Galaxy 17, including a connecting bridge of flux [arXiv:astro-ph/0603413].

sources.summary: The paper presents Galaxy 18 as the brightest z~0.66 group member and a closer velocity match to the absorption system, with possible interaction evidence linking it to Galaxy 17.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 18 provides a much closer match to the velocities ( v = 74-253 ), and is only 61.2 kpc from the QLOS. It is also the brightest galaxy in the group, with i'=20.34 .

An potential example of such an interaction, based on imaging data, is the pair 17 and 18. The velocity difference between the two ( 358 ), is comparable to the velocity dispersion of the group, and so is feasible for an interaction. A close-up of this pair of galaxies is shown in Fig. fig-gal-pair . There is evidence for a connection in flux between the two, with the flux in the ``bridge'' region reaching >7 above the background. There is certainly no spectroscopic sign of any strong interaction taking place, with both galaxies exhibiting simple passive elliptical spectra.

gmw_00008272.name: VV 124
gmw_00008272.overview: VV 124 is classified as a transition dwarf galaxy that shares properties with both dwarf irregular and dwarf spheroidal galaxies [arXiv:1310.0814]. Its stellar and gas motions may be dominated more by dispersion than by rotation [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 124 as a transition dwarf sharing dSph-like properties, notes prior data analysis, and reports that two DEIMOS slitmasks were observed.

quotes.quote: Some dIrrs, like VV 124, Pegasus, and Leo T, share some properties with dSphs. Their stellar and gas motions may be less dominated by rotation than dispersion, and they may have lower gas fractions than typical dIrrs. In accordance with the theory that dIrrs transform into dSphs by interaction with a larger galaxy lin83,may01 , these galaxies are called transition dwarf galaxies (dTs or dIrr/dSphs).

Much of the spectroscopy and chemical analysis presented here has already been published. sim07 and kir10,kir13 published the details of the observations and the analysis of the MW dSphs. sim07 \ also included the Leo T transition dwarf galaxy in their sample. kir12 described the VV 124 data and analysis.

DEIMOS Spectroscopy We observed the new dIrr slitmasks with DEIMOS in the summers of 2010, 2011, and 2013. We observed two slitmasks for NGC 6822 and VV 124 and one slitmask each for the remaining dIrrs.

gmw_00008273.name: Pegasus dIrr
gmw_00008273.overview: The Pegasus dIrr is a dwarf irregular galaxy with a Cepheid-based distance modulus of 24.87 [arXiv:1310.0814]. Photometric observations of its stellar population show that the tip of its red giant branch is located close to the faint magnitude limit of the SDSS [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper presents new spectroscopy for Pegasus dIrr, building an SDSS DR7 target catalog and noting RGB-tip depth, photometric scatter, and Cepheid distance.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopy of red giants in NGC 6822, IC 1613, the Pegasus dIrr, Leo A, and Aquarius. These are the first published results from red giant spectroscopy in all of these galaxies except NGC 6822, which was studied by tol01 .

Pegasus dIrr We consulted SDSS Data Release 7 DR7, aba09 to generate a photometric catalog for Pegasus. We used CasJobs, the SDSS database server, to download ugriz magnitudes for all point sources classified as stars within 30' of Pegasus. The tip of the RGB in Pegasus is close to SDSS's faint magnitude limit. As a result, the photometric errors are large, as revealed by the large scatter of spectroscopic targets in color and magnitude (Figure fig:peg ). The Cepheid-based distance modulus is 24.87 tam11 .

gmw_00008274.name: Leo A
gmw_00008274.overview: Leo A is a dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1310.0814]. Spectroscopic observations of its red giant stars have been conducted using targets selected from the Isaac Newton Telescope Wide Field Survey [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper presents new spectroscopy for Leo A, selecting targets from the INT Wide Field Survey and applying a faint magnitude limit, Cepheid distance, and extinction corrections.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopy of red giants in NGC 6822, IC 1613, the Pegasus dIrr, Leo A, and Aquarius. These are the first published results from red giant spectroscopy in all of these galaxies except NGC 6822, which was studied by tol01 .

Leo A We selected spectroscopic targets in Leo A from the Isaac Newton Telescope Wide Field Survey mcm01 . A. \ Cole and M. \ Irwin kindly provided the photometric catalog to us. We imposed a faint magnitude limit of = 24.1 . We assumed the Cepheid-based distance of 24.59 tam11 . We corrected for extinction star by star based on the sch98 dust maps.

gmw_00008275.name: Aquarius
gmw_00008275.overview: Aquarius is classified as a dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1310.0814]. Photometric observations of the galaxy have been used to identify its stellar populations and select red giant stars for spectroscopic analysis [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper presents new Aquarius spectroscopy, describing Subaru/Suprime-Cam imaging, DAOPHOT photometry, and target selection similar to the other dwarf irregulars.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopy of red giants in NGC 6822, IC 1613, the Pegasus dIrr, Leo A, and Aquarius. These are the first published results from red giant spectroscopy in all of these galaxies except NGC 6822, which was studied by tol01 .

Aquarius We observed Aquarius with Suprime-Cam on 2010 May 25 in 0.8'' seeing. We obtained five 60 s exposures in each of the g and filters for a total of five minutes in each filter. The images were reduced with the SDFRED2 software ouc04 . We identified point sources and calculated photometric magnitudes using DAOPHOT ste87 within version 2.12.2 of IRAF tod86 . We selected targets for spectroscopy in a manner similar to the other dIrrs.

gmw_00008276.name: Leo T
gmw_00008276.overview: Leo T is classified as a transition dwarf galaxy that shares properties with both dwarf irregular and dwarf spheroidal galaxies [arXiv:1310.0814]. Its stellar and gas motions may be dominated more by dispersion than by rotation [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo T as a transition dwarf with dSph-like properties, cites prior inclusion in spectroscopy, and notes published metallicities for its stars.

quotes.quote: Some dIrrs, like VV 124, Pegasus, and Leo T, share some properties with dSphs. Their stellar and gas motions may be less dominated by rotation than dispersion, and they may have lower gas fractions than typical dIrrs. In accordance with the theory that dIrrs transform into dSphs by interaction with a larger galaxy lin83,may01 , these galaxies are called transition dwarf galaxies (dTs or dIrr/dSphs).

Much of the spectroscopy and chemical analysis presented here has already been published. sim07 and kir10,kir13 published the details of the observations and the analysis of the MW dSphs. sim07 \ also included the Leo T transition dwarf galaxy in their sample. kir12 described the VV 124 data and analysis.

Individual Stellar Metallicities We measured spectroscopic metallicities for individual stars in the dIrrs and the MW satellites. kir08b,kir09,kir10,kir12,kir13 published the metallicities for the MW satellites, VV 124, and Leo T .

gmw_00008277.name: Leo I
gmw_00008277.overview: Leo I is one of the most luminous dwarf spheroidal galaxies orbiting the Milky Way [arXiv:1310.0814]. Its stellar metallicity distribution function exhibits a sharp, metal-rich cut-off that can be explained by the rapid and efficient removal of gas [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper finds Leo I has a sharply cut-off MDF poorly fit by simple models; rapid gas removal can explain the cutoff, though Leo I prefers the Accretion Model.

quotes.quote: None of the models is a great fit to the MDFs of the four most luminous MW dSphs in our sample (Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II) . The observed MDFs approach a wall in [ Fe/H ] at the metal-rich end. None of the three models we presented so far can explain the sharpness of the wall. All three models overpredict the number of the most metal-rich stars.
Rapid, efficient removal of gas can explain the sharp, metal-rich cut-offs we observed for Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II .
Fornax, Leo I, and Leo II prefer the Accretion Model, although the difference between the two models for Leo II is tiny.

gmw_00008278.name: Leo II
gmw_00008278.overview: Leo II is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1310.0814]. Its stellar metallicity distribution function exhibits a sharp, metal-rich cut-off, which can be explained by the rapid and efficient removal of gas from the system [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper finds Leo II has a sharply cut-off MDF poorly fit by simple models; rapid gas removal can explain it, though Leo II only slightly prefers accretion.

quotes.quote: None of the models is a great fit to the MDFs of the four most luminous MW dSphs in our sample (Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II) . The observed MDFs approach a wall in [ Fe/H ] at the metal-rich end. None of the three models we presented so far can explain the sharpness of the wall. All three models overpredict the number of the most metal-rich stars.
Rapid, efficient removal of gas can explain the sharp, metal-rich cut-offs we observed for Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II .
Fornax, Leo I, and Leo II prefer the Accretion Model, although the difference between the two models for Leo II is tiny.

gmw_00008279.name: NGC 3115B
gmw_00008279.overview: NGC 3115B is classified as a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0705.0623]. It is considered a cluster-rich system, though its high globular-cluster specific frequency may be contaminated by a larger neighboring galaxy [arXiv:0705.0623].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3115B as an anomalous cluster-rich dwarf whose quoted globular-cluster specific frequency may be contaminated by a larger neighbor, making it a strong luminosity-frequency outlier.

quotes.quote: NGC 3115B If our understanding of the evolution in the Age, S_N plane is correct then NGC 3115B shouldn't end up where it is. Kundu Whitmore (2001b) kundu01b note that this is a ``cluster rich'' dwarf galaxy and perhaps some of its larger neighbour's systems have been mistakenly assigned to it. This would suggest that S_N should be lower than quoted. It is an outlier by over 3 magnitudes on the M _V - S_N relation.

gmw_00008280.overview: The radio galaxy 1607 + 26, located at a redshift of z = 0.473, is a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source with a compact symmetric morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0605312, arXiv:astro-ph/0701619]. High-resolution observations resolve the source region into two point-like X-ray sources and reveal an order of magnitude decrease in hard X-ray flux [arXiv:1603.00947].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 1607 + 26 into a Chandra source plus a secondary X-ray source, finds a large hard-flux decline, and rejects a simple changing-look explanation.

quotes.quote: 1607 + 26 Owing to the superior spatial resolution of Chandra we resolve the source region of 1607 + 26 observed with XMM-Newton by into two point-like X-ray sources (see Figure fig:images ). The location of 1607 + 26 matches the position of the upper left source visible in Figure fig:images with the detected 213 counts. The secondary source separated by 13.5 (lower right corner in Figure fig:images ) has 32 counts and its spectrum could be modeled with an absorbed power law (assuming only Galactic absorption since the redshift to the source is unknown) with a photon index = 1.4 0.3 (see Table tab:chandra-fit ). This means that the XMM-Newton spectrum obtained by represents a combination of emission from 1607 + 26 and from the secondary source.

We detect an order of magnitude decrease between the hard X-ray fluxes of XMM-Newton ( 4.2 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) and Chandra ( 4.8 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ). This change is larger than the sum of the fluxes from the two sources, and it is larger than the absolute calibration uncertainties between the Chandra and XMM-Newton satellites tsujimoto2011 . Therefore, we concluded that the X-ray flux of one or both of the sources decreased during the 3 years interval between the two observations.
Thus, we reject the possibility that X-ray variability observed in 1607 + 26 is due to the Compton thick/thin switches.

sources.summary: The paper treats CTD 93 as a young GPS compact symmetric radio source, measuring hot-spot separation, deriving kinematic and spectral ages, and favoring a CSO morphology.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the kinematic and spectral ages of the Gigahertz-Peaked Spectrum (GPS) source CTD 93. Measurements of the hot spot separation over 8.5 yr show evidence of an increase. The separation rate along the source axis is 0.34 0.11 (H _ 0 =72 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ), which results in a kinematic age of 2200 700 yr. Assuming that two hot spots are moving apart at equal speeds, we derive an advance speed of 0.17 0.06 . The radio lobe spectra show a high frequency steepening, as expected if energetic electrons lose energy by synchrotron radiation.

The luminous radio source CTD 93 (B 1607+268, J 1609+2641) is one of the earliest radio sources known to be a GPS source (Kraus et al. \ 1968). Its radio morphology is classified as CD (Phillips Mutel 1980), and it has a spectral peak around 1 GHz (Stanghellini et al. \ 1998). The radio luminosity is 3 10^ 27 h^ 2 W Hz ^ 1 at 1.6 GHz (Shaffer et al. \ 1999).

sources.summary: The paper describes 1607+268 as the galaxy counterpart of a two-component GPS source at z = 0.473 and reports two near-UV point sources in the HST image.

quotes.quote: 1607+268 : Also known as CTD93. VLBI maps e.g. Dallacasa98 show a two-component, 60 mas, GPS source. The counterpart is a galaxy at z = 0.473 O'Dea91 with m _ = 20.4 Stanghellini93 . We observe two point sources of magnitudes 24.38 and 23.92.

gmw_00008281.name: PDLA towards J0140 0839
gmw_00008281.overview: The PDLA towards J0140 0839 is a high-redshift absorber with a simple velocity structure consisting of two main low-ionization components [arXiv:1004.2715]. It exhibits the lowest metallicity ever detected in a high-redshift absorber and shows an unusually high silicon-to-iron ratio [arXiv:1004.2715].

sources.summary: The paper describes the J0140 0839 PDLA as a simple two-component, extremely metal-poor absorber with low-ionization structure, no detected high ions, and unusually high alpha-element ratios.

quotes.quote: J0140 0839, log \ = 20.75 270 The PDLA towards J0140 0839 has a simple velocity structure with two main components in the low ionization species at v= 0 and +50 \ (see Figure 0140_metals ). Three unsaturated transitions of SiII are detected ( _0= 1260, 1304, 1526 ), although the latter of these exhibits some blending. The column density is derived by fitting the two bluer transitions simultaneously in VPFIT . ArI 1048 is blended but the redder transition (ArI 1066) of the doublet coincides with a clean region of the spectrum and is used to determine an upper limit to the argon column density.

The PDLA towards J0140 0839 has the lowest metallicity ever detected in a high \ absorber (see Section 0140_sec for further discussion), although there may be an upward correction of up to a few tenths of a dex to be made for ionization.

Figure Si_Fe_HI shows that at low \ the PDLAs have fairly typical values of [ Si/Fe ] , with the exception of the very metal-poor PDLA towards J0140 0839 which has [ Si/Fe ] 0.72. This PDLA is even more of an outlier when its [ Si/Fe ] is considered relative to its metallicity. Although the high [ Si/Fe ] ratio could be due in part to ionization corrections and/or dust, it also raises the intriguing possibility of extreme alpha element enhancement in a chemically young object.

gmw_00008282.name: PDLA towards Q0151+048
gmw_00008282.overview: The PDLA towards Q0151+048 is a high-redshift system classified as a high-cooling absorber [arXiv:1004.2715]. Despite its high cooling rate, it exhibits a relatively narrow velocity structure and a low metallicity [arXiv:1004.2715].

sources.summary: The paper treats the Q0151+048 PDLA as a high-cooling but low-metallicity absorber whose interpretation depends on the QSO pair geometry and yields a large absorber-QSO distance limit.

quotes.quote: We calculate a cooling rate log l_c = -26.86 which qualifies this PDLA as a `high cool' (i.e. high cooling rate) system. Such systems typically have higher metallicities, dust-to-gas ratios and velocity spreads than the low cooling rate DLAs. However, the PDLA towards Q0151+048 has a relatively narrow velocity structure and apparently low metallicity ( [ S/H ] 2.03 ).

Q0151+048 may have J_ QSO contributions from Q0151+048B, introducing a second unknown distance. Fynbo et al. (1999) have argued that the most likely orientation of the system is that Q0151+048B is in front of Q0151+048 and the DLA and causes \ emission on the near face of the absorber. Modelling this system completely is complicated not just by uncertainty in the geometry of the QSO pair and absorber, but also by the anisotropy of quasar radiation and hence the flux `seen' by the DLA coming from Q0151+048B.

However, assuming that all the flux comes from the background QSO still yields a lower limit to the distance between Q0151+048 and the PDLA (just as we determine a lower limit under the assumption that internal star formation does not contribute). In Table lum_table we list the limiting value of the PDLA--QSO distance for a star formation rate _ = 0 and also the fiducial case of a Galactic star formation rate of log _ = -2.4 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 for the CNM solutions. For PDLAs with upper limits to N(CII ^ ) the CNM solutions are likely to be inappropriate and they are given in the Table only for completeness. More sensitive CII ^ limits could definitively rule out CNM solutions entirely as is the case for J1131+6044 which only has a WNM solution. The CII ^ analysis therefore only yields useful a distance constraint for Q0151+048 where the lower limit is 160 kpc (no star formation case), rising to just over 400 kpc for a Galactic star formation rate, a separation approximately 5 times larger than the only other PDLA in the literature has been analysed in this way (Q2343 BX415 by Rix et al. 2007).

gmw_00008283.name: PDLA towards J1131+6044
gmw_00008283.overview: The PDLA towards J1131+6044 is a high-redshift system characterized by a relatively high silicon-to-iron ratio [arXiv:1004.2715]. It exhibits high-ion absorption arising in gas outside the PDLA and possesses a warm neutral medium-only fine-structure solution [arXiv:1004.2715].

sources.summary: The paper describes the J1131+6044 PDLA as having relatively high Si/Fe, a WNM-only fine-structure solution, high-ion absorption outside the PDLA, and possible companion-driven ionization.

quotes.quote: J1131+6044 also has a relatively high value of [ Si/Fe ] =0.67, although Figure Si_Fe_HI shows that such values are not unknown in the intervening population.
More sensitive CII ^ limits could definitively rule out CNM solutions entirely as is the case for J1131+6044 which only has a WNM solution.

For J1131+6044 and Q0151+048 where the high ion velocity offset is very high, it is likely that the absorption is arising in gas outside the PDLA. As discussed in Section 0151_sec , Q0151+048 has a fainter companion separated by 27.5 \ and +120 . Although there is no confirmed companion for J1131+6044 in the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED), the SDSS image shows a bright ( g = 17.59 ) point-source 5.66 arcseconds to the north-west. The object has an almost identical g- colour to J1131+6044 (0.41 and 0.38 respectively), so that this may be a binary QSO.
