Out of these four objects, three are classified as transition-types, namely, KDG 61, KDG 64 and DDO 71 (Karachentsev Kaisin ; Boyce et al. ) based on HI detections and H emission. Also among the dwarfs that coincide with the LG dSph locus, one dwarf is classified as transition-type, namely HS 117, with HI associated with it (Huchtmeier Skillman ; Karachentsev et al. ).
The remaining three dwarfs classified as transition-types, namely KDG 64, DDO 71 and HS 117, lie in a deprojected distance of more than 100 kpc.

All of them show either a spatial variation of the centroids of the two stellar populations, as is the case of F12D1 and KDG 64, or that the metal-rich population is more centrally concentrated, as is the case of DDO 71, DDO 44 and F6D1. These findings agree well with the ones from the cumulative histograms, though we should keep in mind that the metal-poor and metal-rich stellar populations involved are differently selected. DDO 78 and IKN are clearly fairly metal-rich while KDG 61, KDG 64 and DDO 44 have prominent metal-poor populations.

sources.summary: The paper gives KDG 64 an optical velocity, finds HI emission offset from the optical galaxy, and considers but disfavors the idea that nearby clouds were expelled from it.

quotes.quote: KDG 64 = UGC 5442 The optical galaxy lies at 18 14 km s ^ 1 . We find HI emission offset in position and velocity, with HI detected at velocities ranging from 120 to 70 km s ^ 1 .

They could be: primordial material which is infalling towards the M 81 triplet; High Velocity Cloud (HVC) analogues belonging to M 81; material originally part of BK5N and KDG 64 which has been expelled; or tidal debris scattered into the SE quadrant.

The third option, that they represent material that has been expelled from BK5N and KDG 64 is also hard to defend. Not only do the clouds seem uncorrelated spatially with both dwarfs, but in the one case where we have velocity information for the dwarf, the velocities of the clouds are offset substantially.

sources.summary: The paper presents KDG 64 as a large M81-group dSph with resolved stellar kinematics, old metal-poor stars, high dark-matter fraction, and transitional dSph-UDG nature.

quotes.quote: Here we present the first measurements of spatially resolved stellar kinematics from deep integrated-light spectra of KDG 64 (UGC 5442), a large dSph galaxy in the M 81 group. From these data we infer stellar population properties and dark matter halo parameters using Jeans dynamical modelling. We find an old, metal-poor stellar population with no young stars and a dark matter mass fraction of 90 per cent within the half-light radius. These properties and the position of KDG 64 on the Fundamental Plane indicate that it is a local analogue of smaller UDGs in the Coma and Virgo clusters and is probably a transitional dSph-UDG object. Its evolutionary path cannot be uniquely established from the existing data, but we argue that supernovae feedback and tidal heating played key roles in shaping KDG 64.

sources.summary: The paper targets KDG 64, alias UGC 5442, as a luminous M81-group dwarf spheroidal and nearby UDG analog whose size, luminosity, and scaling-relation behavior bridge dwarf spheroidal and elliptical systems.

quotes.quote: In total, we observed 8 slitmasks: six in the Coma cluster, one in Abell 2147, and one in the M 81 group, where we targeted the dwarf spheroidal galaxy KDG 64 (UGC 5442). It is spatially extended and quite luminous, which makes it the best UDG analog we can find in the very nearby Universe.

In Figure fig3_chil we also display KDG 64 (Afanasiev et al. in prep.), a luminous dSph in the M 81 group, which exceeds by its size and luminosity all dwarf spheroidal satellites of the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxy and is almost as large as the Local Group dwarf ellipticals NGC 185 and NGC 147. It follows the same trends on both relations established by dwarf and giant early-type galaxies.

gmw_00008332.name: GOODSN-18574
gmw_00008332.overview: GOODSN-18574 is a galaxy at redshift z=1.25 that is considered an Andromeda-progenitor [arXiv:1801.02647]. Its bulge and disk are building simultaneously, with centrally concentrated dust-obscured star formation filling in ring-like structures observed in ultraviolet and H-alpha emission [arXiv:1801.02647].

sources.summary: The paper maps GOODSN-18574 as a z=1.248 Andromeda-progenitor galaxy whose centrally concentrated dust emission indicates dust-obscured bulge growth alongside disk growth.

quotes.quote: Here we map \ dust continuum emission in an \ at z=1.25 to probe where it is growing through dust-obscured star formation. Combining resolved dust measurements from the NOEMA interferometer with Hubble Space Telescope \ maps and multicolor imaging (including new UV data from the HDUV survey), we find a bulge growing by dust-obscured star formation: while the unobscured star formation is centrally suppressed, the dust continuum is centrally concentrated, filling in the ring-like structures evident in the \ and UV emission. Reflecting this, the dust emission is more compact than the optical/UV tracers of star formation with r_e(dust)=3.4 kpc, r_e(H )/r_e(dust)=1.4 , and r_e(UV)/r_e(dust)=1.8 . Crucially, however, the bulge and disk of this galaxy are building simultaneously; although the dust emission is more compact than the rest-optical emission ( r_e(optical)/r_e(dust)=1.4 ), it is somewhat less compact than the stellar mass ( r_e(M_*)/r_e(dust)=0.9 ). Taking the \ emission as a tracer of star formation, the expected structural evolution of this galaxy can be accounted for by star formation: it will grow in size by r_e/ M_* 0.3 and central surface density by _ cen / M_* 0.9 .

This suggests that in this galaxy, the ring observed in \ and UV emission is caused by dust-obscuration rather than centrally suppressed star formation. This is the main result of this paper: the bulge of this galaxy is building by dust-obscured star formation. To quantify this bulge building, we determine what fraction of the bulge growth underway at z 1 can be accounted for by the star formation we observe.

gmw_00008333.name: C
gmw_00008333.overview: C is a dwarf galaxy located to the east of a larger companion [arXiv:2008.08091]. Detected in both ultraviolet and optical wavelengths, it is likely undergoing a galactic interaction due to its close proximity to its neighbor [arXiv:2008.08091].

sources.summary: The paper assigns C to an eastern companion object, identifies ultraviolet and optical counterparts implying a dwarf galaxy, and highlights its proximity and possible interaction.

quotes.quote: To the east of , also 3 (24 kpc) away, garciaruiz02 reported the existence of an object. As they did not name it, we assigned a designation of C to it.

In NED and the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; sdssI,sdssIV ) it is listed as GALEXASC J090946.88+330840.4 and SDSS J090946.76+330837.7 , respectively. Its detections in the ultraviolet and optical imply it is also a dwarf galaxy, not just a gas cloud. The SDSS does not list a redshift for this galaxy http://skyserver.sdss.org/DR16//en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?id=1237664869211505073 .
The interaction with C is especially likely given its proximity to .

gmw_00008334.name: D
gmw_00008334.overview: The galaxy D is a southeastern companion galaxy to NGC 2770 [arXiv:2008.08091]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.00674, which is consistent with the redshift of its larger neighbor, and contains a relatively low mass of atomic gas [arXiv:2008.08091].

sources.summary: The paper names D as a southeastern companion galaxy, reports an SDSS spectroscopic redshift consistent with NGC 2770, and measures an atomic-gas mass far below the main galaxy.

quotes.quote: garciaruiz02 also reported a companion galaxy 12 (96 kpc) to the south-east. We named this galaxy D. It is listed in SDSS as SDSS J091001.70+325659.4 http://skyserver.sdss.org/DR16//en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?id=1237661384379924626 and has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.00674 0.00003 , consistent with the redshift and only 75 from the redshift of .
For B, C, and D we used circular apertures with radii of 60 .
The masses of B and D are around 20 times lower than that of , whereas C has 40 times less atomic gas than .

gmw_00008335.name: E
gmw_00008335.overview: The galaxy E is a nearby companion galaxy identified in the SDSS database [arXiv:2008.08091]. It is not detected in WHISP data, indicating a stringent upper limit on its atomic gas content [arXiv:2008.08091].

sources.summary: The paper discovers E as an additional nearby SDSS galaxy, names it locally, notes no WHISP detection, and reports stringent atomic-gas upper limits.

quotes.quote: Finally, we searched the SDSS http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr16/en/tools/search/sql.aspx for additional galaxies with redshifts 0.006<z<0.01 within the WHISP dataset and found SDSS J091031.75+330329.9 http://skyserver.sdss.org/DR16//en/tools/explore/summary.aspx?id=1237664869211570362 at z=0.00684829 . It is 107 and 12.8 (103 kpc; to the east) from . We named it E. It is not detected in the WHISP data.

In order to estimate the upper limit for E, we first calculated a typical root mean square of the cubes per nominal 20 channel of 1.9, 2.2, and 3.1 mJy beam ^ 1 for 18, 33, and 60 resolutions, respectively. Then we assumed that this galaxy is a point source and that its line spans 100 , and we reported 3 upper limits.
E has at least 100 times less atomic gas than .

gmw_00008336.name: FGC 1287
gmw_00008336.overview: FGC 1287 is a late-type spiral galaxy that belongs to a galaxy triplet [arXiv:2201.04763]. It features a truncated, warped neutral hydrogen disk and an exceptionally long 250 kpc neutral hydrogen tail containing star-forming clumps [arXiv:2201.04763].

sources.summary: The paper finds FGC 1287 has a 250 kpc HI tail, warped truncated disk, and star-forming tail clumps, but its stripping origin remains ambiguous under low ICM density limits.

quotes.quote: Received ; in original form We present \ and radio continuum, narrow--band H imaging, IFU spectroscopy, and X--ray observations of the FGC 1287 triplet projected 1.8 Mpc west of the galaxy cluster Abell 1367. One triplet member, FGC 1287, displays an exceptionally long, 250 kpc \ tail and an unperturbed stellar disk which are the typical signatures of ram pressure stripping (RPS).

Higher--resolution VLA \ data presented here show FGC 1287's \ disk is truncated and significantly warped whereas the \ tail is clumpy. TNG H imaging identified three star forming clumps projected within 20 kpc of FGC 1287's disk, with VIMOS--IFU data confirming two of these are counterparts to \ clumps in the tail. The triplet's \ kinematics, together with H and radio continuum imaging suggests an interaction may have enhanced star formation in FGC 1287's disk, but cannot readily account for the origin of the long \ tail.

FGC 1287 direct RPS scenario The long, narrow \ tail emanating from FGC 1287 black in the VLA moment 0 maps is consistent with a morphology predicted in simulations for strong ram pressure stripping, black ICM n_e = 10 ^ 3 cm ^ 3 and \ 1000 \ near a cluster centre lee20 . Given the XMM--Newton results indicate a significantly lower ICM density ( n_e < 2.6 10^ 5 cm ^ 3 ), it follows if RPS produced the FGC 1287 \ tail, it must have involved either an unusually high \ or an extremely weak gravitational restoring force binding the \ to the galaxy compared with the parameters in the simulations by . The dynamical mass (M _ dym ) within the \ disk radius is 8.8 10 ^ 10 \ and being within the canonical range for a late--type spiral galaxy, does not suggest abnormally weak gravitational restoring forces in the FGC 1287 disk.

gmw_00008337.name: IC 4518E
gmw_00008337.overview: IC 4518E is part of an interacting galaxy pair with IC 4518W [arXiv:1004.1364]. Its mid-infrared emission is dominated by a region southeast of the nucleus, while the deepest silicate absorption is located at the nucleus itself [arXiv:1004.1364].

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 4518 A needs strong reflection, including an inner Compton-thick ring in UXCLUMPY, but disagreement among variability tests leaves its N_H status undetermined.

quotes.quote: IC 4518 A Data reduction/fitting: For the second observation, MOS2 was not used as it was corrupted. Analysis of results: The data is well-fitted by and , with showing poorer statistics. This may be a result of the strong reflection seemingly needed to fit the data. In fact, this is the only source in our sample that requires the addition of an inner, CT reflection ring in . This component was introduced into the model precisely because of difficulty fitting sources with strong reflection with only a cloud distribution see Buchner2019 . and also yield large values of N_ H,av , which agrees with this interpretation.

The results obtained from our best fit are consistent with those of Zhao2020 , although we obtain higher values for N_ H,av ( 2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 in the mentioned work). While both the ^2_ red comparison for all models and the p-value obtained for suggest the need for N_ H,los variability, the p-values for and remain above the threshold. Therefore, we classify this source as `Undetermined'.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4518E as one member of an interacting pair whose mid-infrared emission is dominated by a southeastern region, with deepest silicate absorption at the nucleus.

quotes.quote: IC 4518E This galaxy is part of an interacting pair (IC 4518E/IC 4518W). Most of the mid infrared emission (PAH, fine structure line and 15 \ continuum) emission is dominated by an \ region located 7 arcsec to the south-east from the nucleus. The nuclear bright is similar to that of the southwestern \ region emission only for the molecular hydrogen ( 1 ) and the 5.5 \ continuum. The deepest silicate absorption is found at the nucleus.

gmw_00008338.name: NGC 6701
gmw_00008338.overview: NGC 6701 is a luminous infrared galaxy classified as a composite LINER based on optical spectroscopy [arXiv:1004.1364]. It features two symmetric extranuclear regions located in its spiral arms, and it exhibits deeper silicate absorption in its nucleus than in the rest of the galaxy [arXiv:1004.1364].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6701 as composite/LINER and identifies two symmetric extranuclear regions in its spiral arms, with deeper nuclear silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 6701 This LIRG is classified as composite( LINER) from optical spectroscopy AAH09PMAS . We can identify two extranuclear \ regions symmetrically located 10 arcsec away from the nucleus, one at each spiral arm. As is the case with the other extranuclear \ regions, the molecular hydrogen of these two regions is relatively low when compared with the \ nuclear emission. The silicate absorption is deeper in the nucleus than in the rest of the galaxy.

gmw_00008339.name: GB6 J1103+1158
gmw_00008339.overview: GB6 J1103+1158 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus and quasar located at a redshift of 0.912 [arXiv:1502.02380]. It features a compact core [arXiv:1502.02380], exhibits a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1502.02380], and shows evidence of long-timescale variability [arXiv:1502.02380].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GB6 J1103+1158 as a quasar/radio-loud AGN counterpart candidate to Fermi J1103.2+1145, citing its redshift, compact VLBI core, flat spectrum, and long-timescale variability.

quotes.quote: For J1105.2+1145, the closest GB6 source is GB6 J1103+1158, with an angular separation of 14 arcmin. The source has a 4.8 GHz flux density of 306 27 mJy, with the corresponding source, NVSS J110303+115816, having a flux density of 262.6 mJy, resulting in a spectral index of 0.1.

GB6 J1103+1158 corresponds to the quasar SDSS 110303.52+115816.5, which lies at a redshift of 0.912 . Furthermore, the quasar has been detected in the VLBA Calibrator Survey VLBI observations , confirming the presence of a compact core in this radio-loud AGN.

GB6 J1103+1158 is a little fainter than the catalogued GB6 value, however the ATCA observations confirm that the spectral index remains flat, at 0.1 , up to 38 GHz. There is no evidence of significant variability over the 4 months spanned by these observations, however comparison with the GB6 flux density indicates the presence of longer timescale variability.

gmw_00008340.name: northern BCG
gmw_00008340.overview: The northern BCG is a bright central galaxy located within the merging galaxy cluster Abell 370 [arXiv:2104.11781]. Strong gravitational lensing reconstructions show that the nearest massive mass peak is displaced approximately 35 kpc southeast of this galaxy [arXiv:2104.11781].

sources.summary: The paper treats the northern BCG in Abell 370 as a bright central galaxy whose nearby mass peak is displaced about 35 kpc southeast, a robust mass-light offset in lens reconstructions.

quotes.quote: The bright central galaxies (BCGs), associated with these central clumps are visible in the background BUFFALO image in Figure fig:massmap . As one can notice, the southern mass clump closely follows the southern BCG on the plane of the sky, but the northern mass clump is significantly displaced from the northern BCG (see Figure fig:bcg ). This possible example of mass not following light is consistent with the fact that A370 is an ongoing merger.

The offset we see between the northern BCG and the nearest massive mass clump is broadly consistent with the previous reconstructions performed by a number of groups using the HFF data Richard2014,Strait2018,Kawamata2018 . In our reconstruction, the displacement is 35 kpc South East (SE) of the northern BCG, comparable to that of the model, where the center of their DM3 halo is displaced by 50 kpc somewhat East of SE from that BCG. However, in another parametric model, , there are two cluster-scale halos near the northern BCG, one 20 kpc to the south, and the other located 100 kpc SE away from the northern BCG.

The fact that in our reconstructions the northern mass peak is displaced from the observed light peak of the northern BCG, could be a further indication of the disturbed state of the cluster. Though details vary, this displacement is also present in almost all the publicly available reconstructions of A370 (see Table tab:3massfeatures ), using parametric, free-form, and hybrid methods.

gmw_00008341.name: RFGC 3647
gmw_00008341.overview: RFGC 3647 is a thin, edge-on low surface brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0007242]. It features a small radial extent, a low stellar velocity dispersion, and an initially unstable gas disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0007242].

sources.summary: The paper deprojects RFGC 3647 as a thin edge-on LSB disk with low central surface brightness, small radial extent, unstable initial gas disk, and low stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: As we can see from the Table 2 the central surface brightness of the disk is about 22^m.2 in R that is in order of 1.^m5 lower than its value for HSB spirals. Total luminosity of the bulge is 2^m lower than the disk's one. So RFGC 3647 should be located close to UGC 11301 on Figures 1 and 2. The radial extension of the disk of RFGC 3647 (limited by the level of S/N = 3) is about 2.7 R_e which is lower than the tipical value for HSB galaxies.

Deprojecting "thin" edge-on galaxy RFGC 3647 we show that it has LSB disk (central surface brightness 22^m.2 in R) and high ratio of radial to vertical scale lengths. Surface density of initially gaseous disk was higher than critical density and gravitational instability slould be an essential factor affecting the fragmentation of the disk. At the same time the stellar disk of the galaxy was not heated significantly and its velocity dispersion is quite low this time.

gmw_00008342.name: UGC 11841
gmw_00008342.overview: UGC 11841 is a low-surface-brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0007242]. It features a large scale length exceeding 10 kpc and a flat, halo-dominated structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0007242].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 11841 as a low-surface-brightness disk with a scale length above 10 kpc, the sample's minimum vertical-to-radial scale ratio, and a halo-dominated flat structure.

quotes.quote: As one can see from Fig.1d, disks of three galaxies of our sample have low central SB. One of them, UGC 11841, have a scale length more than 10 Kpc what is tipically for giant LSB galaxies like Malin 1 (see Pickering, Impey, 1996). Note that this galaxy have a minimal value of Z_e/R_e in our sample. Such value is expected for "flat" halo-dominated galaxies (Zasov etc., 1991).

gmw_00008343.name: G1
gmw_00008343.overview: G1 is an early-type lensing galaxy located at a redshift of 0.523 [arXiv:2308.05181, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026]. It is physically associated with G2, a fainter spiral galaxy at a redshift of 0.526 that exhibits [OII] emission [arXiv:2308.05181, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026].

sources.summary: The paper describes G1 as the brighter lensing galaxy between the lensed quasar images, an early-type galaxy at z=0.523 that is probably dynamically associated with G2.

quotes.quote: The brightest lensing galaxy (G1) is located between the multiple lensed quasar images, while the other, fainter galaxy (G2) is slightly offset to the North of image D. The maximum image separation is 2.15 . Faure2004 found a galaxy overdensity about 40 south-west of the lens and proposed that G1 and G2 are two members of a physically related galaxy group. This was later confirmed spectroscopically by Eigenbrod2006 , with measured redshifts z_ G1 =0.523 0.001 and z_ G2 =0.526 0.002 . The spectrum of G1 matches well that of an ETG.

To estimate if G1 and G2 are potentially dynamically associated with each other we check if a typical peculiar velocity of cluster galaxies can account for the observed redshift difference of 0.003 Harrison1974 . We find that we need a peculiar velocity of around 1000 km/s, which is a typical value for cluster galaxies. Therefore G1 and G2 seem to be dynamically associated and dynamical interaction is possible to some extent.

sources.summary: The paper describes G2 as the fainter lensing galaxy north of image D, at z=0.526 with G1, and finds a round cored mass distribution can remove the fifth image.

quotes.quote: The brightest lensing galaxy (G1) is located between the multiple lensed quasar images, while the other, fainter galaxy (G2) is slightly offset to the North of image D. The maximum image separation is 2.15 . Faure2004 found a galaxy overdensity about 40 south-west of the lens and proposed that G1 and G2 are two members of a physically related galaxy group. This was later confirmed spectroscopically by Eigenbrod2006 , with measured redshifts z_ G1 =0.523 0.001 and z_ G2 =0.526 0.002 . The spectrum of G1 matches well that of an ETG.

We find that candidate models have a very round mass distribution of G2 with q_ G2,cand =0.97_ 0.05 ^ +0.02 , while the general distribution of the chain is more uniformly distributed over the whole prior range: q_ G2,all =0.77_ 0.12 ^ +0.15 . Similarly, the general distribution of r_ , G2 is skewed towards a vanishing core radius (i.e., a singular PL profile). In order to produce only four images, a core radius of r_ , G2, cand =(0.14_ 0.04 ^ +0.04 ) is needed.

sources.summary: For HE 0230 2130, G2 is a second lensing galaxy with prominent [OII] emission, redshift 0.526, and a spectrum resembling that of a Sa spiral galaxy.

quotes.quote: This spectrum has a lower signal-to-noise than our MOS spectra and less accurate flux calibration but it allows to measure well the redshift of lens G2, which displays prominent [ OII ] emission, contrary to lens G1. Fig. HE0230_OII shows that the emission comes from an object lying outside the region delimited by the quasar image. It therefore clearly corresponds to lens G2. The redshift of lens G2 is =0.526 0.002 . It is measured using the [ OII ] emission, the Ca II H K absorption lines, the G-band, and the hydrogen H and H absorption lines.
Although the flux calibration is not optimal without a good knowledge of the PSF, the spectrum resembles that of a Sa spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: For HE 0230 2130, G1 is the main lensing galaxy between the four quasar images, measured at redshift 0.523 and spectrally consistent with an early-type galaxy.

quotes.quote: HE 0230 2130 : This quadruply imaged z=2.162 quasar was discovered by Wisotzki et al. ( ). It has a maximum image separation of 2.15 \ and two lensing galaxies. The main lensing galaxy, G1, is located between the four quasar images. A second, fainter lens is located outside the area defined by the quasar images, close to the faint quasar image D (Fig. HE0230_slits_HST ). The spectrum of G1 is shown in Fig. HE0230_lens . Our redshift ( =0.523 0.001 ) is in very good agreement with the result =0.522 of Ofek et al. ( ). The spectrum matches well that of an early-type galaxy.

gmw_00008344.name: KK242
gmw_00008344.overview: KK242 is a transition-type dwarf galaxy located in a void environment [arXiv:2212.05624]. It features a faint star-forming complex containing two adjacent compact regions with detected hydrogen emission [arXiv:2212.05624].

sources.summary: The paper studies KK242 as a transition-type void dwarf, measuring its H-alpha velocity, analyzing its star-forming complex, and considering metallicity-driven evolutionary scenarios.

quotes.quote: KK242 is a LV dwarf of transition type residing in the void environment. Koda et al. present clear indications on its connection with Scd galaxy NGC6503. This implies the distance to KK242 of 6.3 Mpc and its M _ B = --10.5 mag. Its radial velocity, known from the Effelsberg radio telescope \ observations, reveals, however, the difference with that of NGC6503, V 400 .

To clear-up the issue of KK242 radial velocity, we obtained with the SAO 6-m telescope spectra of its faint star-forming (SF) complex. H and H emission is detected in two adjacent compact regions, the southern and northern, separated by 2 arcsec ( 60 pc). Their mean radial velocity is V _ hel = --66 , 100 \ lower than that of NGC6503. We use the HST Legacy Archive images and photometry of individual stars from the Extragalactic Distance Database, available for KK242, to identify in the SF complex the exciting hot stars, the probable BHeB and RHeB stars and a supernova remnant.

gmw_00008345.name: NGC 1373
gmw_00008345.overview: NGC 1373 is a compact, faint elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1906.08187] that exhibits a small degree of rotation [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331] and contains a central distinct component [arXiv:1906.08187]. Strong isophotal twisting suggests the presence of a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331], and the galaxy may be interacting with nearby NGC 1374 [arXiv:1906.08187].

sources.summary: FCC 143 is described as a compact faint elliptical near FCC 147, possibly interacting, with a central distinct component, enhanced dispersion, and redder colors.

quotes.quote: FCC 143 (NGC 1373) -- Fig. fig:FCC143map FCC 143 is one of the most compact and faint elliptical galaxies of the F3D sample. It is located on the western side of the cluster at R_ proj =0.8^ from the centre and it is close in projection to FCC 147 (NGC 1374). According to Blakeslee2009 , the difference in distance between FCC 143 and FCC 147 is only 0.3 Mpc. Deep FDS images show a possible ongoing interaction between the two galaxies Iodice2018 . FCC 143 is an ancient infaller in the north-south clump of galaxies.

The stellar kinematics reveals a central distinct component, which extends along the photometric major axis out to R 20 arcsec with a maximum rotation velocity v_ max 40 km s ^ 1 . Its stellar velocity dispersion is larger ( 80 km s ^ 1 ) than that in the surrounding regions ( 50 km s ^ 1 ). This feature is characterised by larger values of the Mg b and Fe5015 line-strength indices than in the outskirts, while the H index is almost constant at all radii.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1373 as the low-mass comparison in dynamical-friction models, assigning gas, halo, and external-field assumptions before exploring GC orbital evolution.

quotes.quote: We explored if dynamical friction can influence the distribution of GCs noticeably through simplistic models of the dynamics of the GCs of NGC 1399 and NGC 1373. These two are representatives of the most and the least massive galaxies in our sample, respectively.

The extended gaseous component of NGC 1373 was modeled by a S ersic sphere whose mass and S ersic index are the same as of the stellar component but the effective radius is four times larger. For the Newtonian models, we assumed the NFW dark matter halo derived for NGC 1399 by bil19 . It was deduced from an isotropic model of the kinematics of the GCS of the galaxy. For NGC 1373, we assumed a NFW dark matter halo of the virial mass of 10^ 11.35 M_ and a scale radius 13.4 kpc according to the stellar-to-halo mass relation behroozi13 and the halo mass-concentration relation diemer15 .

For NGC 1373, we explored various orbital eccentricities of the GCs and different apocentric distances in the range between 0.6 to 8 kpc. The friction came out to be the strongest near the center of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 1373 shows modest symmetric rotation, a wave-like asymmetric velocity-dispersion profile, and strong isophotal twisting interpreted as evidence for a bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 1373 The symmetric RC reveals a small degree of rotation, peaking at 15 km s ^ 1 . The inner 10-15 of the VDP has a wave-like appearance, with a 5 km s ^ 1 wave amplitude producing no obvious, and several local, maxima around 80 km s ^ 1 . The VDP is asymmetric: the E side is flat out to 14 from the center and then decreases to 30 km s ^ 1 at 20 , while the W side slowly decreases starting from 6 , to reach a minimum of 40 km s ^ 1 at 24 . The overall shape of the VDP and the presence of strong isophotal twisting (Caon et al. 1994) are, in this case, also indicative of the presence of a bar.

gmw_00008346.name: SDP.17
gmw_00008346.overview: SDP.17 is a hyper-luminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of 2.305 [arXiv:1311.5898]. The system features a clumpy structure with two distinct knots of rest-frame ultraviolet and optical emission, which may indicate an ongoing merger [arXiv:1311.5898].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.17 as a two-redshift lensing system whose clumpy lensed source is a hyper-luminous infrared galaxy and may trace an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: SDP.17 . At first glance, this system resembles a face-on spiral galaxy with two prominent spiral arms. However we know from spectroscopic follow-up observations that this system has an optical redshift of 0.9435 (N10) and a redshift of 2.305 from the detection of both CO Lupu12,Harris12 and H_ 2 0 Om11 lines, thus indicating the presence of two objects along the same line of sight. Follow-up observations with the SMA Buss13 show that the sub-mm/mm emission is relatively compact, concentrated within 0.6^ from the centre of the source, but fails to resolve the individual lensed images.

Therefore, in this work and in D13 we assume that the lensed object is made up of profiles 3-4-5-6-7 . The complicated clumpy structure of SDP.17 can be accounted for by a lensing event characterised by two distinct knots of rest-frame UV/optical emission in the source plane (D13).

All the galaxies in the sample are classified as Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxies (ULIRGs, 10^ 12 L_ L_ IR <10^ 13 L_ ) with the exception of SDP.17; its infrared luminosity, L_ IR 2 10^ 13 L_ , makes it an Hyper Luminous Infrared Galaxy (HyLIRGs). The latter comprises two distinct objects in the source plane, each one of 2-3 kpc in size, and separated by a few kpc (Fig.1 of D13). This morphology may be indicative of an on-going merger.

gmw_00008347.name: SDP.130
gmw_00008347.overview: In near-infrared Hubble Space Telescope images, the SDP.130 system resembles a lenticular foreground galaxy [arXiv:1311.5898]. Subtraction of this foreground galaxy's bulge is necessary to reveal the masked, gravitationally lensed background source [arXiv:1311.5898].

sources.summary: The paper says SDP.130 resembles a lenticular foreground system in HST images, but subtraction is needed to reveal the masked lensed background source.

quotes.quote: For SDP.130 no clear evidence of gravitational lensing can be claimed from the HST images alone, where the system resembles a lenticular galaxy.

Overall, the morphology of this system in the HST images is not suggestive of a lensing event. This is because, in the near-IR, the lensed object is masked by the prominent bulge of the foreground galaxy. The subtraction of the lens is therefore necessary to reveal the background source. SDP.130 presents the greatest challenge of the five lenses in terms of subtracting off the lensing galaxy light profile.

gmw_00008348.name: IC 3521
gmw_00008348.overview: IC 3521 is a member of the Virgo Cluster of galaxies [arXiv:1810.12768]. It resides in a dense local environment surrounded by forty-one neighboring galaxies, which include dwarf elliptical, dwarf irregular, spiral, and lenticular types [arXiv:1810.12768].

sources.summary: The paper places IC 3521 in the Virgo Cluster, describing a dense local environment with 41 nearby galaxies and a mix of dwarf elliptical, dwarf irregular, spiral, and lenticular neighbours.

quotes.quote: IC 3521 belongs to the Virgo Cluster of galaxies. A total of 41 galaxies are listed in the NED in the defined space volume, implying an average galaxy density of 80 Mpc ^ 3 . All the neighbour galaxies have velocities in the range of 439-833 km s ^ 1 . This indicates the presence of a galaxy-rich dense environment around IC 3521. Among these neighbour galaxies, a total of nine and three galaxies in the vicinity of IC 3521 are dE and dIrr types, respectively, and only nine galaxies are big spiral or lenticular types.

gmw_00008349.name: CGCG 041-023
gmw_00008349.overview: CGCG 041-023 is a member of the VV 462 galaxy group [arXiv:1810.12768]. It is situated near three dwarf galaxies that feature blue regions of ongoing star formation, together forming a small star-forming dwarf group [arXiv:1810.12768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 041-023 with the VV 462 galaxy group, where three nearby dwarf systems with blue star-forming regions form a small star-forming dwarf group.

quotes.quote: CGCG 041-023 CGCG 041-023 belongs to the VV 462 galaxy group. In the vicinity of CGCG 041-023, a total of 3 galaxies are listed in the NED which in turn gives an estimate of galaxy density as 8 Mpc ^ 3 within the defined volume. Two neighbour galaxies have velocity in a very narrow range of 1350-1359 km s ^ 1 , while one has velocity about 1482 km s ^ 1 . Interestingly, all these neighbour galaxies are dwarf systems, and their SDSS colour composite images show presence of blue regions extended over the galaxies extent representing an ongoing star formation activities in them. It seems that CGCG 041-023 together with neighbour galaxies forms a small group of star-forming dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00008350.name: 3CR 349
gmw_00008350.overview: 3CR 349 is a radio galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0412388]. The galaxy exhibits a noisy outer color profile that shows a gradient in its outer regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0412388].

sources.summary: For 3CR 349, the paper finds a noisy outer color profile, estimates K-band nuclear luminosity under flat and gradient-modeled assumptions, and obtains consistent values.

quotes.quote: 3CR 349 : The color profile is quite noisy, and the sky dominates the profile at radii > 1 75. Assuming a flat outer ( 0 75) color profile, we estimated L_ K^ = 10 ^ 43.09 0.10 . Although very noisy, the color profile shows a gradient in the outer part; we therefore studied the extreme case in which no IRE is present by modeling the color profile. The resulting L_ K^ = is 10 ^ 43.02 0.11 , consistent with the previous estimate.

gmw_00008351.name: Beetle galaxy
gmw_00008351.overview: The Beetle galaxy, also known as 2MASX J165315, is a radio-quiet type 2 quasar at a redshift of z=0.1 [arXiv:2103.06805]. It features ionized gas distributed in an eastern arc, a central lobe, and a western fan [arXiv:1802.06184], alongside faint, extended radio hot-spots that interact with circumgalactic gas [arXiv:2103.06805].

sources.summary: 2MASX J165315 has [O III] emission in an eastern arc, central lobe, and western fan, with mostly rotational kinematics plus a 370 pc outflow component.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J165315 The [ O III ] morphology in this target is distributed in three features; a faint arc 4 kpc east of the nucleus, a central lobe extending east from the nucleus, and a fan or conical shape west of the nucleus. [ O III ] kinematics are only obtained for the central lobe and western fan, with a largely rotational pattern. The maximum outflow radius is defined by a relatively broad ( > 500 km s ^ 1 ), systemic, (or blueshifted with respect to the narrow component), secondary component observed east of the nucleus for an R _ out = 370 pc. Single-component line emission with FWHM > 250 km s ^ 1 that follows a low amplitude rotation pattern is measured throughout the rest of the system, for an R _ dist of 1.1 kpc. The host galaxy of this target is moderately inclined, with no signs of a recent merger.

sources.summary: The Beetle galaxy is used as the motivating precedent for radio-induced feedback in radio-quiet QSO2, with deep VLA data revealing faint hot-spot radio structures interacting with circumgalactic gas.

quotes.quote: Recently, we discovered radio-induced feedback across large scales in the Beetle galaxy, which is a radio-quiet QSO2 at z=0.1 Villar2017 . Even though the radio emission in the Beetle was classified as compact based on archival survey data, deep VLA imaging revealed an extended radio structure with hot-spots stretching almost 50 kpc. The extended radio emission was detected only at the mJy level, and thus too faint to be detected in surveys. Still, this faint radio source interacts with the circumgalactic gas far outside the galaxy. The Beetle galaxy revealed that radio jets of modest power can be a relevant feedback mechanism acting across large scales, even in non-radio loud QSOs.

gmw_00008352.name: J0841+01
gmw_00008352.overview: J0841+01 is a merging system undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1504.01391] that hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.16437]. The system features tens-of-kiloparsec radio structures [arXiv:2103.06805] and ionized filaments located between the two merging galaxies [arXiv:2103.06805].

sources.summary: SDSS J0841+0101 is described as a major-merger dual/offset AGN candidate with two AGN-like emission components, matched stellar bulges, and a strong Chandra detection only in the more massive bulge.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0841+0101 was recognized as a Type 2 dual AGN candidate by . The system has two emission sources with AGN-like line ratios, nearly identical luminosities of L_ =10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , and a separation of 3 60 (7.28 kpc). The two emission components spatially coincide with the locations of two stellar bulges, J0841+0101NE and J0841+0101SW, to within 0.5 times the astrometric error. The merger ratio is 1.4:1, indicating that this system is undergoing a major merger. Our current Chandra observations show an X-ray AGN in the more massive stellar bulge, J0841+0101NE ( 237.2^ +0.0 _ 14.2 counts at 0.3-8 keV, for a >5 detection), but an upper limit of only 2.4 counts at 0.3-8 keV were detected in the less massive stellar bulge, J0841+0101SW.

sources.summary: J0841+01 is described as a merging QSO2 with tens-of-kiloparsec radio structure, 40 kpc AGN-related emission, tidal ionised features, and a candidate 8 kpc ionised bubble.

quotes.quote: The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc).

J0841+01 is a dubious case. A striking set of ionised filaments is located between the two merging galaxies ( Storchi2018 , Appendix results-objects2 , Fig. fig0841 ). Based on the morphology and filamentary appearance, we have proposed that this is a giant ( 8 kpc) ionised edge brightened bubble. The apparent spatial overlap and similar size as the radio source (Fig. overlay0841 ) suggests that it has been inflated by it.

sources.summary: The paper finds J0841+0101 is strongly detected in X-rays, with broadband spectra indicating a heavily obscured AGN and line-of-sight column density near 10^24 cm^-2.

quotes.quote: Only one dual AGN candidate, J0841+0101, is detected as a single, unresolved source in the and imaging, while the remaining three dual AGN candidates, J0122+0100, J1221+1137, and J1306+0735, are not detected with ; if these non-detections are due to obscuration alone, these systems are consistent with being absorbed by column densities of log( ) 24.9, 24.8, and 24.6, which are roughly consistent with previously inferred column densities in these merging systems. In the case of J0841+0101, the analysis of the 0.3-30 keV spectra reveal a line-of-sight column density of 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , significantly larger than the column densities previously reported for this system and demonstrating the importance of the higher signal-to-noise spectra and access to the >10 keV energies via . Though it is unclear if J0841+0101 truly hosts a dual AGN, these results are in agreement with the high obscuring columns expected in AGNs in late-stage mergers.

Our broad band spectroscopic analysis of J0841+0101, combining , , and observations, have provided more robust constraints on the X-ray spectral properties of the AGN in this merger. Our best fitting phenomenological model suggests a total line-of-sight column density of = 80.1_ 52.8 ^ +44.9 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , while our physical torus model suggests a torus obscuring column of log( )= 23.0_ 0.1 ^ +0.1 and a line-of-sight column density of = 121_ 18 ^ +20 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00008353.name: J0853+38
gmw_00008353.overview: J0853+38 is a type 2 quasar that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.06805]. The galaxy features a radio jet extending several tens of kiloparsecs, as well as a much larger active galactic nucleus-related radio structure spanning 472 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2103.06805].

sources.summary: J0853+38 is one of the QSO2 with suspected extended radio emission, showing a several-tens-of-kiloparsec jet and a much larger 472 kpc AGN-related radio structure.

quotes.quote: At the moment of the observations there was no evidence for extended radio emission on scales 5 for 4 out of 12 objects (J0802+25, J1316+44, J1356+10 and J1437+30), while radio emission was suspected or confirmed to be extended for 7 objects (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46, J0945+17, J1000+06, J1108+06, and J1517+33), as well as the Teacup.

The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc).

gmw_00008354.name: J0907+46
gmw_00008354.overview: The active galaxy J0907+46 is suspected to host extended radio emission [arXiv:2103.06805], which includes a radio jet spanning several tens of kiloparsecs [arXiv:2103.06805]. Hydrogen-alpha observations reveal that the system is undergoing a galaxy interaction [arXiv:2103.06805], with large-scale ionized gas features tracing its tidal debris [arXiv:2103.06805].

sources.summary: J0907+46 is described as having suspected extended radio emission, a several-tens-of-kiloparsec jet, H-alpha evidence for interaction, and large-scale ionised features tracing tidal debris.

quotes.quote: At the moment of the observations there was no evidence for extended radio emission on scales 5 for 4 out of 12 objects (J0802+25, J1316+44, J1356+10 and J1437+30), while radio emission was suspected or confirmed to be extended for 7 objects (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46, J0945+17, J1000+06, J1108+06, and J1517+33), as well as the Teacup.

The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc). The radio component at 11 kpc from the AGN in J0945+17 may be a jet or a lobe. The lobe emission from the 160 kpc radio source in J1356+10 is extremely faint, and additional observations are necessary to unambiguously confirm and accurately image this emission. The structure of the extended radio source in J1437+33 ( d_ max 2.5 kpc) is unknown, but, as we argue in Appendix results-objects2 , it is most likely related to the nuclear activity. Therefore, this sample shows a high fraction of AGN related radio sources that extend for at least typical effective radii of the spheroidal component of galaxies ( Urbano2019 ) and often much more. Size, line luminosity and nature of the large scale ionised gas We have found that QSO2 undergoing interaction/merger events appear to be invariably associated with ionised gas spread over large spatial scales, often well outside the main body of the galaxies, with maximum distances from the AGN in the range r_ max 12-92 kpc, with a median value of 46 kpc. The most spectacular case is the Teacup, which is associated with a 115 87 kpc nebula (Fig. figTeacup ). The H luminosities of the extended structures are in the range L_ H ^ ext = (2.5-235) 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (median 3.5 10 ^ 41 erg s ^ 1 ) (Table halum ) Some contamination by [ NII ] 6548,6583 is expected, although < 30 . We show in Fig. hist-ion the classification of the most interesting extended ionised features and the objects where they have been identified. For comparison, measured L_ H ^ ext for a sample of 37 3C low z radio galaxies. Six have nuclear [ OIII ] luminosities in the same range as our QSO2 sample. These have L_ H ^ ext = (15-389) 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (median 1.0 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ). Although this comparison sample is small, the H luminosities of the extended nebulae appear to be higher in the radio-loud sample. All QSO2 in our sample except J0907+46 show unambiguous evidence for mergers/interactions based on the distorted optical continuum morphologies and the presence of prominent tidal continuum features. The H image confirms this is also the case for J0907+46 (Fig. fig0907a ).

Many of the large scale ( 10 kpc) ionised features identified in our sample are remnants of the interaction/process. In some cases, they trace entire or partial tidal tails, bridges or shells (e.g. J0841+01, J0907+46, J1000+12, J1316+44 and J1356+10; see also Villar2010,Villar2017 ).

gmw_00008355.name: Leo P
gmw_00008355.overview: Leo P is a pristine nearby dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 1.5 to 2.0 Mpc [arXiv:1305.0272]. It contains a stellar counterpart with active star formation and exhibits an extremely low metallicity [arXiv:1305.0272].

sources.summary: Leo P, also AGC208583, is described as a pristine nearby dwarf with stellar counterpart, current star formation, very low metallicity, H I follow-up, and a 1.5-2.0 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: Follow-up optical observations reported by Rhode2013 : (i) confirmed that the HI source has a stellar counterpart, (ii) provided an accurate optical flux, partly resolved into individual stars, (iii) detected H emission indicative of current star formation activity and (iv) produced an independent estimate of the galaxy's distance. Later optical spectroscopy of the H 2 region revealed the galaxy's extremely low metallicity Skillman . We shall hereafter refer to it as Leo P because of its apparently pristine nature. It is also identified as AGC208583 in the catalog maintained by MPH and RG and reported in a number of on-line extragalactic data bases. The follow-up observations mentioned above include BVR broad-band imaging obtained in excellent seeing conditions (0.6 -0.8 ) with the WIYN 3.5-m telescope and more recently with the Large Binocular telescope (LBT); H imaging with the 2.1m KPNO telescope; optical spectroscopy with the 4m KPNO telescope and the Large Binocular Telescope (LBT); and HI synthesis imaging with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array ( VLA The National Radio Astronomy Observatory is a facility of the National Science Foundation operated under cooperative agreement by Associated Universities, Inc. ).

Both groups do however agree that the analysis of their respective data sets indicate that the distance to Leo P lies somewhere between 1.5 and 2.0 Mpc. These estimates are complemented by, and in agreement with that of D_ Mpc =1.3^ +0.9 _ 0.5 obtained using the baryonic Tully-Fisher relation, presented in Section 3.3. Optical spectroscopy data of Skillman obtained with the LBT shows Leo P to have the extremely low metallicity of 12 + log(O/H)=7.16 0.04 , and to exhibit essentially primordial Helium abundance.

gmw_00008356.name: MaNGAID: 1-245451
gmw_00008356.overview: MaNGAID: 1-245451 is an elliptical host galaxy [arXiv:1902.03754] classified as a radio-detected red geyser [arXiv:2109.02609]. It exhibits LINER-like central emission [arXiv:1902.03754], a misaligned stellar velocity field indicating a recent gas-rich merger [arXiv:1902.03754], and extreme ionized outflow kinematics within its inner radio contours [arXiv:2109.02609].

sources.summary: This compact red geyser example has LOFAR contours over MaNGA maps; its radio image lacks extended structures, but inner radio contours coincide with very high gas dispersion and outflow kinematics.

quotes.quote: MaNGAID: 1-245451 in Fig. fig:ionized shows an example of a radio detected red geyser belonging to the ``compact'' radio morphology class. The three columns in the figure correspond to SDSS optical image (first row), H EW (second row) and velocity dispersion (third row) extracted from the H emission line. The LOFAR radio contours (in green) are over-plotted on top of each map. The on-sky diameter of the MaNGA fiber bundles (overplotted in magenta hexagon in the optical image) generally ranges between 17 '' 32 '' , corresponding to a physical size of 10 - 30 \ kpc at median redshift of MaNGA observations ( z 0.03 ). The bi-symmetric pattern in the H EW map traces the ionized wind cone.

The absence of structures in the radio image makes it hard to associate the radio properties with any specific ionized features. However, we note very high gas dispersion within the inner radio contours detected with >20 , indicating extreme ionized outflow kinematics there.

sources.summary: The paper describes HX1 as an elliptical host with disturbed gas kinematics, LINER-like central emission, a misaligned stellar velocity field, and signs consistent with a recent gas-rich merger.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, which has an elliptical morphology G18 hereafter Graham18 , does not show any features of interaction in its light profile both at the location of the emission and elsewhere. The velocity map of the galaxy on the contrary shows very disturbed features. There appears to be some kind of rotation along the northeast-southwest diagonal axis, with the gas in the northeast direction moving away from us and the gas along the southwest direction coming towards us.
Moreover, the stellar velocity shown in the last panel is completely misaligned from the velocity flow of the emission. Such an misalignment is a signature of recent gas rich merger.
The host (HX1) also shows a corresponding LINER emission line ratio in its centre.

gmw_00008357.name: MaNGA ID 1-188530
gmw_00008357.overview: MaNGA ID 1-188530 is a red geyser galaxy classified as having an irregular morphology [arXiv:2109.02609]. It features a 30-kiloparsec radio structure with a central core, a diffuse emission plateau, and an elongated region of enhanced line width [arXiv:2109.02609].

sources.summary: MaNGA ID 1-188530 is an irregular red geyser with a 30 kpc LOFAR structure, central core, diffuse plateau, H-aligned radio extension, and enhanced line width.

quotes.quote: For the red geyser with MaNGA ID 1-188530 (belonging to the ``irregular'' morphology class), the LOFAR image has an unusual extended morphology spanning a distance of 30 kpc, with a central radio core and a plateau of diffuse emission. Interestingly, the radio structure is spatially extended in the direction of the H enhancement in the EW map, similar to the other sources in the "irregular" class. Additionally, we note that the galaxy shows an elongated region of enhanced line width ( > 200 km s ^ 1 ), spanning about > 7 kpc.

gmw_00008358.name: MaNGA ID: 1-23958
gmw_00008358.overview: MaNGA ID: 1-23958 is classified as a red geyser galaxy [arXiv:2109.02609]. Its radio emission features a one-sided, low surface brightness bubble that is detached from the central core and aligns with its ionized gas pattern [arXiv:2109.02609].

sources.summary: MaNGA ID 1-23958 has a detached one-sided radio bubble aligned with its emission-line pattern; the paper links such double-class systems to unresolved jet-driven ionized outflows.

quotes.quote: In red geyser with MaNGA ID: 1-23958, the radio image show a one-sided low surface brightness bubble, detached from the central bright core. Similar to the previous example, the direction of the radio bubble roughly aligns with the bi-symmetric pattern in the emission line map. This is the case for all sources classified as "double".

We find that for the galaxies in the ``irregular'' and ``double'' class, elongation along a specific direction or presence of one-sided bubbles in the radio emission (MaNGAID: 1-188530 and 1-23958, Fig. fig:ionized ) roughly aligns with the ionized gas features, marked by high gas dispersion. Such observations indicate that the large scale ionized outflows, stretching to > 10 \ kpc , are possibly driven by small scale radio jets, unresolved at the current LOFAR resolution. Often in these cases, the velocity dispersion map is clumpy and shows high values in distinct parts of the galaxy, and also in some cases, perpendicular to the bi-symmetric H feature.

gmw_00008359.name: MaNGAID: 1-378770
gmw_00008359.overview: MaNGAID: 1-378770 is a rare triple-class red geyser galaxy that hosts large-scale radio jets and lobes [arXiv:2109.02609]. These radio jets lie perpendicular to the galaxy's ionized wind, which is traced by H-alpha emission [arXiv:2109.02609].

sources.summary: MaNGAID 1-378770 is a rare triple-class red geyser whose large-scale radio jets and lobes lie perpendicular to the ionized wind, with velocity dispersion also enhanced perpendicular to H.

quotes.quote: Out of the three sources classified in the ``triple'' morphological category, two of them (Fig 12, MaNGA ID: 1-378770 and 1-595166) have radio jets lying perpendicular to the ionized gas traced by the H EW map (Fig. fig:ionized2 ). This is unlike what is observed in the above cases. The gas velocity dispersion is enhanced perpendicular to the H bi-symmetric feature in 1-378770, but is aligned in 1-595166.

However, among the three rare cases (classified as ``triple'') where we detect clear evidence of large scale radio jets and lobes, particularly notable are the galaxies with MaNGAID: 1-378770 and 1-595166 where we find that the radio jets, shown by LOFAR radio contours, and ionized wind, traced by the H emission map, lie perpendicular to each other (Fig. fig:ionized2 ). There can be several possible explanations for the perpendicular incidence of the radio jet and ionized broad angled wind.

gmw_00008360.name: MaNGAID: 1-595166
gmw_00008360.overview: MaNGAID: 1-595166 is a rare triple-class red geyser galaxy that hosts large-scale radio jets and lobes [arXiv:2109.02609]. These radio jets lie perpendicular to the ionized wind traced by H-alpha emission, while the gas velocity dispersion aligns with the H-alpha feature [arXiv:2109.02609].

sources.summary: MaNGAID 1-595166 is another rare triple-class red geyser with radio jets perpendicular to the H-traced ionized wind, though its gas velocity dispersion aligns with H.

quotes.quote: Out of the three sources classified in the ``triple'' morphological category, two of them (Fig 12, MaNGA ID: 1-378770 and 1-595166) have radio jets lying perpendicular to the ionized gas traced by the H EW map (Fig. fig:ionized2 ). This is unlike what is observed in the above cases. The gas velocity dispersion is enhanced perpendicular to the H bi-symmetric feature in 1-378770, but is aligned in 1-595166.

However, among the three rare cases (classified as ``triple'') where we detect clear evidence of large scale radio jets and lobes, particularly notable are the galaxies with MaNGAID: 1-378770 and 1-595166 where we find that the radio jets, shown by LOFAR radio contours, and ionized wind, traced by the H emission map, lie perpendicular to each other (Fig. fig:ionized2 ). There can be several possible explanations for the perpendicular incidence of the radio jet and ionized broad angled wind.

gmw_00008361.name: UGC 11794
gmw_00008361.overview: UGC 11794 is a disk galaxy that exhibits clear evidence of an extraplanar dust layer [arXiv:1511.03789]. Its vertical profile displays a halo emission excess that cannot be explained by the point spread function wing [arXiv:1511.03789].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 11794 as a clear extraplanar-dust case; despite its small angular size and lowest data quality, its fit is reasonable and its vertical profile shows halo excess.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3628, and UGC 11794) show clear evidence for the existence of extraplanar dust layer.
UGC 11794 has the smallest angular size, and hence the data quality is the lowest among our targets. However, the fit results give reasonable parameters.
The vertical profiles of NGC 891, NGC 3628, and UGC 11794 clearly show the halo emission excess which cannot be explained with the PSF wing.

gmw_00008362.name: ALMA-.02
gmw_00008362.overview: ALMA-.02 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy candidate detected at 3 mm [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was identified in the field of a target galaxy at redshift 2.24, but is likely unrelated to that target [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMA-.02 as a 3 mm galaxy candidate fainter than the targeted z=2.24 source and likely unrelated to that original target.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.02, ALMA-.10: Both sources were found in the same ALMA observations ( 2015.1.00862.S), which target the CO(3-2) transition in a z=2.24 galaxy selected from wide and deep narrow-band H_ surveys (RA = 02:16:45.8, Dec =- 05:02:44.7; Sobral2013a,Molina2017a ). The original source is not detected in the ALMA continuum image, from which we derive a flux density upper limit of S_ <21 mJy ( 3 ). This a factor of 6 and 3 fainter than our 3 mm candidates ALMA-.02 and ALMA-.10, which are located at 31 and 28 arcsec from the main target, respectively. All this information suggests that our source candidates are not related to the original one.

gmw_00008363.name: ALMA-.10
gmw_00008363.overview: ALMA-.10 is a 3 mm galaxy candidate detected in ALMA observations [arXiv:1810.12300]. It is likely an independent, high-redshift starburst galaxy that is unrelated to the nearby z=2.24 target [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper treats ALMA-.10 as an independent 3 mm galaxy candidate, likely unrelated to the z=2.24 target and possibly at higher redshift.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.02, ALMA-.10: Both sources were found in the same ALMA observations ( 2015.1.00862.S), which target the CO(3-2) transition in a z=2.24 galaxy selected from wide and deep narrow-band H_ surveys (RA = 02:16:45.8, Dec =- 05:02:44.7; Sobral2013a,Molina2017a ). The original source is not detected in the ALMA continuum image, from which we derive a flux density upper limit of S_ <21 mJy ( 3 ). This a factor of 6 and 3 fainter than our 3 mm candidates ALMA-.02 and ALMA-.10, which are located at 31 and 28 arcsec from the main target, respectively. All this information suggests that our source candidates are not related to the original one. Actually, ALMA-.10 is part of an independent ALMA follow-up of AzTEC sources at 1.1 mm ( Ikarashi2017a ), and despite having a similar flux density to other sources in the catalog whose redshift distribution peaks between z=2-3 , it lacks a photometric redshift estimation, suggesting a higher redshift solution.

gmw_00008364.name: ALMA-.04
gmw_00008364.overview: ALMA-.04 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy detected as a 3 mm source [arXiv:1810.12300]. It features a spectral line at 105.27 GHz, which indicates that the galaxy is not associated with the nearby z=1.91 cluster structure [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA-.04 as a 3 mm source whose 105.27 GHz line is inconsistent with the targeted z=1.91 cluster redshift.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.04: The source was detected in the ALMA program 2016.1.00698.S, which aims to detect the Sunyaev--Zeldovich effect in a galaxy cluster at z=1.91^ +0.19 _ 0.21 ( Mantz2014a ). A further analysis of the extracted spectrum of this candidate revealed a line at 105.27 GHz (Zavala et al. in preparation), which is inconsistent with the cluster redshift (the closest solution to the cluster's redshift is z=2.28 ). Therefore, this indicates that our galaxy is not associated with the cluster structure.

gmw_00008365.name: ALMA-.06
gmw_00008365.overview: ALMA-.06 is a high-redshift galaxy detected in the 3 mm continuum [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was observed within a proto-cluster field at a redshift of 1.6, but it does not show the expected carbon monoxide line emission for that structure [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ALMA-.06 as a 3 mm continuum detection in a proto-cluster field, with no expected CO feature at the target redshift.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.06, ALMA-.13: The ALMA observations ( 2015.1.01151.S) target the CO(2-1) transition in galaxies within a proto-cluster at z=1.6 . Despite being detected in the continuum maps, our detections do not show any features at 88.2 GHz, the expected frequency for the CO(2-1) line given the proto-cluster redshift. Indeed, one of our sources (ALMA-.13), shows a line at 99.8 GHz (Zavala et al. in preparation), which confirms that the source is not part of the targeted structure. The only galaxy found in the z=1.6 proto-cluster ( Noble2017a ; RA = 03:30:59, Dec =- 28:43:06) is actually not detected in the 3 mm continuum map.

gmw_00008366.name: ALMA-.13
gmw_00008366.overview: ALMA-.13 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy detected as a 3 mm continuum source [arXiv:1810.12300]. Although it was observed within a proto-cluster field at redshift z=1.6, a detected line at 99.8 GHz confirms that the galaxy is not part of that targeted structure [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper says ALMA-.13 is a 3 mm source in a z=1.6 proto-cluster field, with a line confirming it is not part of the targeted structure.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.06, ALMA-.13: The ALMA observations ( 2015.1.01151.S) target the CO(2-1) transition in galaxies within a proto-cluster at z=1.6 . Despite being detected in the continuum maps, our detections do not show any features at 88.2 GHz, the expected frequency for the CO(2-1) line given the proto-cluster redshift. Indeed, one of our sources (ALMA-.13), shows a line at 99.8 GHz (Zavala et al. in preparation), which confirms that the source is not part of the targeted structure. The only galaxy found in the z=1.6 proto-cluster ( Noble2017a ; RA = 03:30:59, Dec =- 28:43:06) is actually not detected in the 3 mm continuum map.

gmw_00008367.name: ALMA-.07
gmw_00008367.overview: ALMA-.07 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was serendipitously detected in the same field as two other galaxies, but it has a different color ratio and a photometric redshift of approximately 4.00 [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMA-.07 as a serendipitous, likely unrelated 3 mm galaxy with different color ratios and a photometric redshift near 4.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.07 (ALESS75.C in Wardlow2018a ), was serendipitously detected in the same field as the 870 m detected galaxies ALESS75.1 and ALESS75.2 (the original targets of the pointing), which lie at z_ spec =2.545 and 2.294, respectively ( Danielson2017a ). Our source does not show any emission line at the expected frequency of the CO(2-1) line at z=2.5 . Unfortunately, the ALMA tunings do not cover the frequencies of the expected CO lines for the z=2.29 solution. Nevertheless, this source shows a very different color ratio from the program's sources ( S_ 870 m /S_ 3 mm <17 vs 114 and >714 , respectively), and is even not detected at 870 m . Actually, Wardlow2018a reported that this source, ALMA-.07, has a photometric redshift of 4.00^ +0.07 _ 0.08 ( Taylor2009a,Cardamone2010a ), which is inconsistent with the redshifts of the two original targeted sources.

gmw_00008368.name: ALMA-.09
gmw_00008368.overview: ALMA-.09 is a dusty star-forming galaxy detected blindly at 3 mm [arXiv:1810.12300]. It is not detected at 870 micrometers, and its flux ratio is consistent with a high-redshift galaxy at z > 4 [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper describes ALMA-.09 as a blind 3 mm detection, unlike its 870 micron targets and consistent with a galaxy at redshift above 4.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.07, ALMA-.09, ALMA-.14: These three objects were found in the same project ( 2016.1.00754.S), which comprises spectroscopic observations towards multiple submillimeter galaxies selected based on previous 870 m ALMA continuum imaging. Given the nature of the targeted galaxies (multiple dusty star-forming galaxies), our 3 mm galaxies might be related to the 870 m selected multiple galaxies, introducing bias and breaking the blindness of our selection. However, here we show that these sources are most likely not related to the main sample and hence they can be considered blind detections.

Similarly, ALMA-.09 (ALESS87.C in Wardlow2018a ) shows no emission lines at the searched frequencies and is not detected at 870 m , contrary to the program's targets (ALESS87.1 and ALESS87.2). Its S_ 870 m /S_ 3 mm ratio is also very different from the project's sample ( <22 vs 28 and >54 , respectively) and, as mentioned by Wardlow2018a , is in better agreement with a z>4 galaxy.

gmw_00008369.name: ALMA-.14
gmw_00008369.overview: ALMA-.14 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was discovered as a blind 3 mm continuum source that does not exhibit any emission lines, suggesting a distinct redshift solution [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper treats ALMA-.14 as a blind 3 mm source outside the 870 micron primary beam, with no emission line and a different redshift solution.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.07, ALMA-.09, ALMA-.14: These three objects were found in the same project ( 2016.1.00754.S), which comprises spectroscopic observations towards multiple submillimeter galaxies selected based on previous 870 m ALMA continuum imaging. Given the nature of the targeted galaxies (multiple dusty star-forming galaxies), our 3 mm galaxies might be related to the 870 m selected multiple galaxies, introducing bias and breaking the blindness of our selection. However, here we show that these sources are most likely not related to the main sample and hence they can be considered blind detections.

Finally, ALMA-.14 is more than 30 arcsec away from the original 870 m selected objects, and consequently is outside of the ALMA primary beam at that frequency. This source does not show any emission line either, suggesting again a very different redshift solution.

gmw_00008370.name: ALMA-.15
gmw_00008370.overview: ALMA-.15 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy candidate detected at 3 mm [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was identified offset from the center of its observation map and is not associated with the targeted z~1.1 program sample [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMA-.15 as an unbiased 3 mm galaxy candidate, offset from the map center and not associated with the z~1.1 program targets.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.08, ALMA-.15, ALMA-.16: The three sources were detected in different observations of the same project ( 2016.1.00171.S), whose targets are galaxies at z 1.1 . As described in detail in the main text, our galaxies are expected to lie at z 2.5 , and therefore our detections can be considered unbiased. In fact, ALMA-.08 has a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =4.55 (see Table table:catalogue ), significantly higher than the original sample. Furthermore, ALMA-.15 and ALMA-.16 have been found 30 arcsec away from the center of the maps, where the main targeted sources are located. This further confirms that those galaxies are not associated with the program's sample.

gmw_00008371.name: ALMA-.16
gmw_00008371.overview: ALMA-.16 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy candidate detected at 3 mm [arXiv:1810.12300]. It was identified offset from the center of its observation map and is not associated with the targeted z~1.1 program sample [arXiv:1810.12300].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMA-.16 as an unbiased 3 mm galaxy candidate, offset from the map center and not associated with the z~1.1 program targets.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.08, ALMA-.15, ALMA-.16: The three sources were detected in different observations of the same project ( 2016.1.00171.S), whose targets are galaxies at z 1.1 . As described in detail in the main text, our galaxies are expected to lie at z 2.5 , and therefore our detections can be considered unbiased. In fact, ALMA-.08 has a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =4.55 (see Table table:catalogue ), significantly higher than the original sample. Furthermore, ALMA-.15 and ALMA-.16 have been found 30 arcsec away from the center of the maps, where the main targeted sources are located. This further confirms that those galaxies are not associated with the program's sample.

gmw_00008372.name: ESO 501 G075
gmw_00008372.overview: ESO 501 G075 is a spiral galaxy located within the Hydra I cluster [arXiv:2105.06014]. Although it has no nearby neighbors, its neutral hydrogen gas disk is disturbed, suggesting the galaxy is experiencing environmental processes that do not affect its stellar component [arXiv:2105.06014].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 501 G075 as a spiral near Hydra I with disturbed H I, no close neighbours, high H I mass, and likely ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: We find the WALLABY source J104059 270456 to be associated with the optical counterpart ESO 501 G075 (angular separation 6.5 arcsec). ESO 501 G075 is a spiral galaxy with morphological type SA(s) devaucouleurs1991 located at , = 10:40:59, 27 :04:59 (J2000). ESO 501 G075 lies to the North-East, NE, of the cluster centre at a projected distance of 1.05^ ( 1.12 Mpc), which is 0.3^ ( 0.32 Mpc) inside the virial radius and has no nearby neighbours with optical or H i detections within a projected distance of 0.35^ ( 0.37 Mpc, Figure fig:sky_map ) and velocity difference of 1 000 km s ^ 1 .

ESO 501 G075 has one of the highest stellar and H i masses of galaxies in the Hydra I cluster detected in H i with a stellar mass of (M_*/ [ M _ ] )=10.36 0.13 (derived from VHS VISTA Hemisphere Survey McMahon2013 . J and K band photometry, see Reynolds2019 Reynolds2019 for details) and an H i mass of (M_ HI / [ M _ ] )=9.75 0.02 . Table table:galaxy_properties summarises optical and H i derived galaxy properties of ESO 501 G075.

Unlike the optical disc, the H i gas disc (H i integrated intensity, moment 0, map) shows signs of a disturbed H i morphology through the compression (stretching) of the H i contours on the South-West, SW, (North-East, NE) side of the galaxy. This suggests that ESO 501 G075 may be experiencing one or more environmental processes that are disturbing the H i , but are not affecting the stellar component.

gmw_00008373.name: Q2225-403
gmw_00008373.overview: Q2225-403 is a quasar at a redshift of z=2.410 [arXiv:0903.2233]. It forms an apparent quasar pair with a nearby source, and both of their host galaxies have been resolved in the Ks-band [arXiv:0903.2233].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q2225-403 as quasar A in an apparent quasar pair, resolves its host galaxy in Ks imaging, and finds absorption at quasar B's redshift in its spectrum.

quotes.quote: In the framework of the study of the - relation throughout Cosmic Time (Decarli et al., 2009b in preparation), we collected high-resolution NIR imaging K09 kotilainen09 and optical spectroscopy (Decarli et al., 2009a, in preparation) of Q2225-403 (hereafter, quasar A), a z=2.410 quasar first reported by hewitt93 . We set the slit orientation so that we simultaneously observed both quasar A and the 10.5'' North-East source with similar magnitude (see figure fig_q2225_field ).

We report the discovery of an apparent quasar pair with angular separation of 10.5'' . Q2225-403A,B is the only apparent pair of quasars for which both the host galaxies have been resolved. Their Ks-band apparent magnitudes ( m_ A ( host) = 18.51 ; m_ B ( host) = 17.44 ) are consistent with those expected for typical quasar host galaxies at the distance indicated by their redshift. The discovery of an intervening absorption system in quasar A at the same redshift of B reveals an extended halo around the nearest object.

gmw_00008374.name: quasar B
gmw_00008374.overview: Quasar B is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.932 [arXiv:0903.2233]. Its resolved host galaxy is well modeled by a de Vaucouleurs profile and possesses an extended halo probed at a projected distance of 83 kpc [arXiv:0903.2233].

sources.summary: The newly found quasar B is at z=0.932, has a resolved host galaxy with an extended halo probed 83 kpc away, and its host is fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile.

quotes.quote: Quasar B (RA _ J2000 =22:28:50.4; Dec _ J2000 =-40:08:27) is not present in the catalogue by hereafter VCV06 vcv06 , nor in the NED or Simbad archives. It appears in the USNO-B1.0 catalogue (source ID: 0498-0814048, m_ R =20.0 , m_ B =20.4 , grossly consistent with the photometry of our R-band image, m_ =19.6 ). The optical spectrum clearly shows the broad line surrounded by multiplets (see Figure fig_spectra ), leaving no doubt on the source nature. The peak of the line is observed at 5406 , yielding z=0.932 .

The presence of this absorption reveals an extended halo around the host galaxy of quasar B at the projected distance of 83 kpc. From the analysis of NIR imaging data, we are able to detect the host galaxy of both quasars. For quasar B, the Ks-band roughly corresponds to the rest-frame J.
The galaxy is well resolved and modelled with a de Vaucouleurs profile with scale radius R_ eff =5.2 kpc (see Figure fig_aida ).

gmw_00008375.name: 2MASX J03584923-2925086
gmw_00008375.overview: 2MASX J03584923-2925086 is an Sc spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.058 [arXiv:1912.05986]. Diagnostic emission line ratios indicate that the galaxy is a star-forming system rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1912.05986].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J03584923-2925086 as an Sc spiral host offset from LSQ13ddu, measures a local host redshift and metallicity, and concludes it is star-forming rather than AGN-dominated.

quotes.quote: It was discovered by LSQ Baltay2013_LaSillaQuest on MJD 56622.3 at R.A. = 03:58:49.09 Decl. = 29:25:11.8 (J2000.0), which is offset by 4 kpc in projection from the centre of the galaxy, 2MASX J03584923-2925086 Jones2009 , an Sc spiral Makarov2014 at a redshift of z = 0.05845 0.00015 Retrieved from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) (Fig. 2MASXJ035849232925086 ). Using measurements of host galaxy emission lines in the spectra of LSQ13ddu at the position of SN, we find a slightly lower redshift of z = 0.057787 0.000125 , which we use throughout this paper.

The local metallicity of the host galaxy at the location of LSQ13ddu was estimated from measurements of the line strength ratios of key galaxy emission lines in the + 15 d WiFeS spectrum. The gas-phase metallicity, 12 + log( [ OIII ] /H), of the host galaxy was calculated to be 8.51 0.25 based on the O3N2 conversion ratio from . This value is equivalent to 0.7 times the solar metallicity value of and is consistent with previous metallicity studies of SNe Ibn that find sub-solar host metallicities for the class Pastorello2015e, Taddia2015 . Additionally, an archival 6DF spectrum Jones2009 of the nuclear region of the host of LSQ13ddu was used to identify any active galactic nucleus (AGN) activity. From this spectrum a log( [ NIII/H ] ) value of 0.425 and a log( [ OIII/H ] ) value of 0.177 were estimated, placing the host below both the Kewley theoretical dividing line Kewley2001 and the Kauffmann SDSS based empirical dividing line Kauffmann2003 on the `Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich' (BPT) diagram Baldwin1981 , we thus conclude that the galaxy is not the host of an AGN and has the properties of a starforming galaxy.

gmw_00008376.name: NGC 4827
gmw_00008376.overview: NGC 4827 is an elliptical [arXiv:1305.7228] or S0 galaxy [arXiv:2207.06624] classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) source [arXiv:2207.06624]. It hosts a radio source with a Z-shaped morphology [arXiv:1305.7228], featuring asymmetric jets [arXiv:1305.7228, arXiv:2207.06624] and disturbed lobes that extend up to 130.6 kpc [arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4827 with radio source 5C4.43, noting its elliptical host, location between Coma and the relic, Z-shaped morphology, lobes, and jets.

quotes.quote: The radio emission from 5C4.43 is associated with the elliptical galaxy NGC4827 at z = 0.025 2000MNRAS.313..469S . It is located in-between the Coma cluster and the radio relic, at 1^ from the cluster centre. The radio source was studied in detail by 1989A&A...213...49V : it has a Z-shaped morphology with some evidence of jets joining the core of the radio source to the diffuse emission of the lobes. The angular extent of the source at 1.4 GHz is 4'30'' 1989A&A...213...49V . Our observations show the core and two bright jets departing in the N and S direction.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4827 as an S0 LINER with similar two-band radio morphology, disturbed lobes, asymmetric jets, and a 130.6 kpc projected extent.

quotes.quote: 4: NGC 4827 The optical host is S0 and is a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region galaxy KentandGunn . The radio morphology at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is nearly identical. The north and south radio lobes show a disturbed morphology, extending toward the east, suggesting that the motion of the host galaxy in the ICM is possibly responsible for these extensions. Furthermore, the northern jet is of smaller extent than the southern radio jet, suggesting that projection effects are also at play. The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 4 66 ( 130.6 kpc).

gmw_00008377.name: Gal 364A
