gmw_00008377.overview: Gal 364A is a companion galaxy to the quasar QSO 364 [arXiv:2011.06448]. It exhibits strong [O II] emission and Ca II absorption lines, with a star formation rate typical of ultra-luminous infrared galaxies [arXiv:2011.06448].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Gal 364A as QSO 364’s companion, with strong [O II] and Ca II features, ultraLIRG-like star formation, and the highest SFR among observed companions.

quotes.quote: The SFR for candidate companion galaxies ranges from 0.1 to 55 M _ yr ^ 1 , except for Gal 364A and Gal 404A whose SFR is upwards of 10^2 M _ yr ^ 1 . The SFR for associated companions ranges from 0.6 to 215 M _ yr ^ 1 . Galaxy A associated to QSO 364 exhibits the highest SFR of all observed companion galaxies .
Gal. A (z=0.3467) is a companion galaxy with a prominent [ O ii ] emission line and Ca ii absorption lines. Its SFR is typical of ultraLIRGs.

gmw_00008378.name: Gal 404A
gmw_00008378.overview: Gal 404A is a foreground starburst galaxy located near the quasar QSO 404 [arXiv:2011.06448]. It exhibits strong calcium II absorption lines and has a high star formation rate exceeding 100 solar masses per year [arXiv:2011.06448].

sources.summary: The paper describes Gal 404A as a high-SFR foreground starburst galaxy near QSO 404, outside the projected-distance selection but included because its spectrum was obtained.

quotes.quote: The SFR for candidate companion galaxies ranges from 0.1 to 55 M _ yr ^ 1 , except for Gal 364A and Gal 404A whose SFR is upwards of 10^2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

QSO 404 Gal. A (z=0.4263) is a foreground starburst galaxy to QSO 404 (z=0.4601), showing a strong Ca ii absorption lines. Note that Gal. A of QSO 404 is at PD=574 kpc, which is > 250 kpc, our selection criterion. Our possible companion was a very faint object close to the QSO, for which we cannot obtain a good spectrum. Since the spectra of Gal. A with larger PD was obtained during the same exposure we included it in this work.

gmw_00008379.name: Candidate ID 2065
gmw_00008379.overview: Candidate ID 2065 is a spatially resolved galaxy with a spectroscopic confirmation at z=9.3 [arXiv:2305.01793]. Observations of the galaxy reveal a clear Lyman break, emission lines of oxygen, neon, and hydrogen, and an estimated stellar mass of log(M/Msun)=9.17 [arXiv:2305.01793].

sources.summary: The paper reports Candidate ID 2065 as a spatially resolved z=9.3 galaxy with spectroscopic confirmation, strong emission lines, a Lyman break, and stellar mass log(M/Msun)=9.17.

quotes.quote: Candidate ID 2065 in our sample has a spectroscopic confirmation at z=9.3 as reported by boyett23 , in good agreement with the estimated photometric redshift of z=9.50_ 0.08 ^ +0.34 . The NIRSpec observations of this spatially-resolved galaxy show prominent emission lines of O, Ne, and H, as well as a clear Lyman break. By combining the photometric and spectroscopic data, the best-fit SED provides a stellar mass of log( / 9.17).

gmw_00008380.name: PKS 2149-158C
gmw_00008380.overview: PKS 2149-158C is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio source associated with an elliptical galaxy in the Abell 2382 cluster [arXiv:0709.2652]. The galaxy features asymmetric double jets extending to a total radio size of approximately 210 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0709.2652].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2149-158C as a separate FR I elliptical radio galaxy in Abell 2382 with asymmetric double jets, 210 kpc extent, ordered magnetic-field structure, and wavelength-dependent depolarization.

quotes.quote: PKS 2149-158C The radio source (FR class I) is associated with a single elliptical galaxy and is unrelated to the dumb-bell system. Its total extent at 20 cm is about 3 ( 210 kpc). The source has a double asymmetric structure with the two jets emanating from the core to the north-east and south-west. The north jet and south jet extend out to 9.2 and 18 respectively from the core. Of course, the north-east jet may appear distorted by projection effects and probably bends along the line-of-sight.

The magnetic field configuration, as traced by the 6 cm images, is initially transverse to the jets, parallel to the southern lobe, and circumferential in the northern lobe. The mean fractional polarization is 7 at 20 cm and 25 at 6 cm. As for the dumb-bell system, the signal is affected by beam depolarization at longer wavelength.

gmw_00008381.name: DLA ( z_ abs =2.2298 )
aliases_external: DLA host toward Q 0338-0005 at z_abs=2.2298
gmw_00008381.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system DLA ( z_ abs =2.2298 ) is a high-redshift galaxy located along the line of sight to the quasar Q 0338-0005 [arXiv:1807.04189]. The host galaxy exhibits Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1807.04189] and has a measured zinc metallicity of -0.67 [arXiv:1807.04189].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Q 0338-0005 DLA at z_abs=2.2298, deriving hydrogen and metal column densities, a Zn metallicity of -0.67, and Ly emission from its host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We used the archival Very Large Telescope (VLT) instrument Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph (UVES) high-resolution data of Q 0338-0005 ( z_ em =3.049 ) to study abundances of the DLA ( z_ abs =2.2298 ) and sub-DLA ( z_ abs =2.7457 ) along the line of sight. We estimated column densities of \ and various elements present in the DLA and sub-DLA through Voigt profile fitting. The DLA throgh shows the Ly emission from the its host galaxy. We derive the metallicities of the DLA and sub-DLA with [ Zn/H ] =-0.67 0.18 and [ S/H ] =-1.45 0.17 , respectively.

We find the best-fit \ column densities for DLA ( z=2.2298 ) and sub-DLA ( z=2.7457 ) are log N( ) =21.09 0.10 cm ^ 2 and log N( ) =20.10 0.08 cm ^ 2 , respectively (Fig. lya ). Emission seen in the trough of the absorber is likely to correspond to the Ly emission from the DLA host see e.g., kulkarni12,rahmani16,zafar17 . The Ly emission from this DLA host galaxy is previously reported by krogager12 at impact parameters of b=0.49 0.12 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Q 0338-0005 sub-DLA at z_abs=2.7457, measures metal absorption over four components, derives log hydrogen column density 20.10, and reports S-based metallicity -1.45.

quotes.quote: We used the archival Very Large Telescope (VLT) instrument Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph (UVES) high-resolution data of Q 0338-0005 ( z_ em =3.049 ) to study abundances of the DLA ( z_ abs =2.2298 ) and sub-DLA ( z_ abs =2.7457 ) along the line of sight. We estimated column densities of \ and various elements present in the DLA and sub-DLA through Voigt profile fitting. The DLA throgh shows the Ly emission from the its host galaxy. We derive the metallicities of the DLA and sub-DLA with [ Zn/H ] =-0.67 0.18 and [ S/H ] =-1.45 0.17 , respectively.
The lines from \ ( 1253, 1259 ), \ ( 1302 ), \ ( 1608, 2374, 2382 ), \ ( 1304, 1526, 1808 ), and \ ( 1670 ) elements are identified for the sub-DLA spreading over four components.
We find the best-fit \ column densities for DLA ( z=2.2298 ) and sub-DLA ( z=2.7457 ) are log N( ) =21.09 0.10 cm ^ 2 and log N( ) =20.10 0.08 cm ^ 2 , respectively (Fig. lya ).

gmw_00008382.name: G13-1
gmw_00008382.overview: G13-1 is a clumpy galaxy that is identified as a major-merger system [arXiv:1703.00458]. It is more dispersion dominated than turbulent disk galaxies and deviates from the predictions of the self-gravitating disk instability model [arXiv:1703.00458].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G13-1 as one of two DYNAMO galaxies most consistent with a major-merger scenario, more dispersion dominated than turbulent disks and deviating from disk-instability expectations.

quotes.quote: In our sample 2 galaxies G13-1 and H10-2 appear most consistent with a major-merger scenario.

For the same average relative clump size DYNAMO merging galaxies (H10-2 and G13-1) are more dispersion dominated than the turbulent disks. Though the assumptions that are made for determining Q_ gas do not apply to merging galaxies, if one applies this formula to these two merging galaxies we find Q_ gas 6 and Q_ gas 15 . This is consistent with the expectation that those clumpy galaxies that are independently identified as merging systems, not disks, are also those systems that deviate from the predictions of the self-gravitating disk instability model.

gmw_00008383.name: H10-2
gmw_00008383.overview: H10-2 is a DYNAMO galaxy located at a redshift between 0.075 and 0.15 [arXiv:1405.7410]. It is classified as a perturbed rotator with a high star formation rate [arXiv:1405.7410], and its dispersion-dominated structure is highly consistent with a major-merger scenario [arXiv:1703.00458].

sources.summary: The paper observes H 10-2 as a DYNAMO galaxy with z=0.075-0.15 sample context, high star formation, perturbed-rotator classification, and gas fraction upper limit below 0.22.

quotes.quote: The systems observed were D 13-5, G 04-1, G 10-1 and H 10-2. Stellar masses for these systems range from 1-7 10^ 10 M _ and redshifts span the range z=0.075-0.15 . Star formation rates for D 13-5, G 10-1 and H 10-2 are 20 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . The star formation rate of G 04-1 is somewhat higher at 50 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . Galaxies G 04-1 and D 13-5 are classified as rotating disks, while H 10-2 and G 10-1 are classified as a perturbed rotators due to asymmetries in their velocity fields, and may be experiencing merging.
An upper-limit to the gas fraction of H 10-2 is f_ gas <0.22 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies H10-2 as one of two DYNAMO galaxies most consistent with a major-merger scenario, more dispersion dominated than turbulent disks and deviating from disk-instability expectations.

quotes.quote: In our sample 2 galaxies G13-1 and H10-2 appear most consistent with a major-merger scenario.

For the same average relative clump size DYNAMO merging galaxies (H10-2 and G13-1) are more dispersion dominated than the turbulent disks. Though the assumptions that are made for determining Q_ gas do not apply to merging galaxies, if one applies this formula to these two merging galaxies we find Q_ gas 6 and Q_ gas 15 . This is consistent with the expectation that those clumpy galaxies that are independently identified as merging systems, not disks, are also those systems that deviate from the predictions of the self-gravitating disk instability model.

gmw_00008384.name: Candidate id 1
gmw_00008384.overview: Candidate id 1 is a high-redshift Lyman continuum candidate classified as a first-quality source [arXiv:2004.00158]. It exhibits Lyman-alpha emitter features, a redward ultraviolet continuum excess, and an estimated absolute escape fraction between 5% and 73% [arXiv:2004.00158].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Candidate id 1 as a first-quality LyC candidate with Ly-emitter features, redward UV continuum excess, estimated escape fraction of 5-73 percent, and EW(Ly) below 50.

quotes.quote: Candidate id 1 has features of a Ly emitter, namely a relatively strong, asymmetric emission feature in the 1D and 2D spectrum typical of Ly lines at high redshift and a higher flux level in the UV continuum redward of Ly as compared to blueward (i.e., the Ly forest). This object will be noted as first quality (q1) candidate.

The estimated f_ esc ^ abs are in the range 5 73 for q1 candidate id 1 and 30 93 for q1 candidate id 368, where different metallicities can produce a variation in f_ esc ^ abs up to 10 . Both q1 LCG candidates have EW(Ly ) < 50 .

gmw_00008385.name: Candidate id 421
gmw_00008385.overview: Candidate id 421 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:2004.00158]. Its spectrum shows features consistent with its reported redshift, including a Lyman-alpha forest break, Lyman-alpha absorption, and potential interstellar medium absorption features [arXiv:2004.00158].

sources.summary: The paper treats Candidate id 421 as a second-quality LyC candidate whose VUDS spectrum shows features consistent with the reported redshift, though not a secure confirmation.

quotes.quote: Candidate id 421: the spectrum from the VUDS survey was available for analysis. From the spectrum we were able to identify several spectroscopic features consistent with the reported redshift from the VUDS survey, such as a Ly forest break, Ly absorption, and potential ISM absorption features, but conservatively cannot claim a solid redshift confirmation. The spectrum is presented in Appendix B. In Section 5 we will refer to the object id 421 as a second quality (q2) candidate.

gmw_00008386.name: id 326
gmw_00008386.overview: The object id 326 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy with a faint or undetected continuum [arXiv:2004.00158]. It exhibits somewhat asymmetric emission lines that are probably Lyman-alpha, though its reported redshift remains unconfirmed [arXiv:2004.00158].

sources.summary: The paper treats id 326 as a second-quality detection-close-pair candidate with asymmetric lines probably Ly, faint or undetected continua, and no secure redshift confirmation.

quotes.quote: Candidates id 326 and id 330 have somewhat asymmetric emission lines, consistent with those observed for Lyman break galaxies, and are probably Ly (see Appendix B). The continua are faint or not detected in the spectra. For these candidates we were not able to securely confirm their reported redshifts and in Section 5 these objects will be noted as second quality (q2) candidates.

gmw_00008387.name: id 330
gmw_00008387.overview: The galaxy candidate id 330 is classified as a close-pair system with probable Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2004.00158]. It is a potential merger system where Lyman-continuum photons may be produced by fast radiative shocks, clump interactions, or accretion processes [arXiv:2004.00158].

sources.summary: The paper treats id 330 as a q2 close-pair candidate with probable Ly emission; its very high inferred escape fraction may indicate contamination, lower redshift, or a merger system.

quotes.quote: Candidates id 326 and id 330 have somewhat asymmetric emission lines, consistent with those observed for Lyman break galaxies, and are probably Ly (see Appendix B). The continua are faint or not detected in the spectra. For these candidates we were not able to securely confirm their reported redshifts and in Section 5 these objects will be noted as second quality (q2) candidates.

For the candidate id 330 f_ esc ^ abs >>100 . This result can be an indication that the LyC flux from our candidate is contaminated by a low redshift interloper or that the estimated redshift is lower than 3.4 . Another interpretation, since candidate id 330 is classified as detection-close pairs , is that this is a merger system. Mergers are not taken into account by BPASS population synthesis models and in these scenarios, LyC photons could be produced by fast radiative shocks, the interaction of the clumps, or accretion processes Dopita2011, Wyithe2011a .

gmw_00008388.name: SDSS J142904+025142
gmw_00008388.overview: SDSS J142904+025142 is a candidate young radio galaxy [arXiv:2108.08122] that is unresolved in JVLA observations [arXiv:2108.08122]. High-resolution VLBI imaging reveals that the radio source consists of two lobes spanning 100 pc in projection [arXiv:2108.08122].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J142904+025142 has a broad, partly RFI-affected absorption profile, unresolved JVLA emission, two 100 pc radio lobes, and candidate young radio-galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: The \ absorption spectrum of SDSS J142904+025142 (hereafter J1429+0251) is shown in Fig. fig:det_spectra . The RFI affecting a big chunk of the channels right at the centre notwithstanding, the line appears to be very broad, with a maximum FWZI of 700 . The deeper component of the absorption appears to be 100 to 200 \ wide and centred at the systemic velocity. The spectrum also shows shallow wings on either side.

J1429+0251 is unresolved in our JVLA observations, and we measure a peak continuum flux density of 810 mJy beam 1 . The high-resolution C-band VLBI image is shown in Fig. fig:radio_cont . The radio source consists of two lobes and is 100 pc in projection. It has been classified as a peaked-spectrum source by Callingham17 suggesting that this source is a candidate young radio galaxy.

gmw_00008389.name: SDSS J145239+062738
gmw_00008389.overview: SDSS J145239+062738 is an unresolved flat-spectrum radio source and quasar [arXiv:2108.08122]. Its host galaxy exhibits a dust lane and a tidal tail, which suggests a past merger event [arXiv:2108.08122].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J145239+062738 as an unresolved flat-spectrum/quasar source in a dusty, tidal-tail merger with a wide high-opacity absorption profile.

quotes.quote: The \ absorption spectrum of SDSS J145239+062738 (hereafter J1452+0627) is shown in Fig. fig:det_spectra . The radio source is unresolved in our JVLA continuum image. It has been classified as a flat-spectrum source Healey07 and as a quasar Souchay12 . The Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical image shows that the host galaxy has a dust lane aligned along the radio source The HST image is available at the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). . The galaxy also exhibits a tidal tail suggesting that it may have undergone a merger.

The \ absorption profile is significantly wide with a FWZI 800 . We also note that the profile is centred on the systemic velocity with the peak absorption redshifted by 200 . A more shallow absorption component is seen on the blueshifted side. This happens to be a high-opacity absorber with a peak optical depth of 20 . We derive a high total \ column density of (13.2 0.2) 10^ 21 cm 2 , two orders of magnitude higher than the typical \ seen in absorption in radio galaxies.

gmw_00008390.name: SDSS J145845+372022
gmw_00008390.overview: SDSS J145845+372022 is classified as a blazar with a featureless optical continuum [arXiv:2108.08122]. At VLBI scales, the radio source displays a bright core alongside two extended features [arXiv:2108.08122].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J145845+372022 has a narrow blueshifted absorption profile, high optical depth, blazar classification, featureless optical continuum, and compact core-plus-extended VLBI structure.

quotes.quote: The \ absorption spectrum of SDSS J145845+372022 (hereafter J1458+3720) is shown in Fig. fig:det_spectra . The absorption profile is very narrow with FWZI 60 \ and is blueshifted by 40 . We measure a peak optical depth of 33 and an integrated \ column density of 1.2 0.1) 10^ 21 cm 2 . This absorption has been reported by Aditya18b .

J1458+3720 is classified as a blazar Massaro09, Dabrusco14 . Consistent with the classification as a blazar, the optical spectrum and, in particular, the continuum looks featureless, possibly due to the power-law emission from the nuclear region dominating over the stellar component. The redshift has been estimated from weak absorption lines which may have contributed to the uncertainty in the redshift. At VLBI scales, the radio source shows two extended features in addition to a bright core.

gmw_00008391.name: SDSS J234107+001833
gmw_00008391.overview: SDSS J234107+001833 is an active galactic nucleus with strong optical emission lines and a stellar continuum [arXiv:2108.08122]. These emission lines are significantly broad, indicating disturbed kinematics, while its absorption spectrum displays a broad, complex profile [arXiv:2108.08122].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J234107+001833 has a broad complex absorption profile and strong optical emission lines whose broad single-component fits indicate disturbed kinematics, unlike a blazar.

quotes.quote: The \ absorption spectrum of SDSS J234107+001833 (hereafter J2341+0018) is shown in Fig. fig:det_spectra . The profile is broad with an FWZI 550 . We find that the peak absorption is redshifted from the centre by 50 . The absorption profile is complex, consisting of relatively shallow red- and blueshifted components along with the deep absorption.

The SDSS spectrum of J2341+0018 shows strong emission lines on top of stellar continuum. Therefore, it is unlikely to be a blazar source, as has been suggested by Dabrusco14 . We find that all four emission lines (the \ doublet, \ lines, the \ doublet, and the \ doublet) are well fit with a single kinematic component. However, these lines are significantly broad (FWHM 1400 ), indicating disturbed kinematics.

gmw_00008392.name: FDF1337
gmw_00008392.overview: FDF1337 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:0805.3601]. It features an expanding shell modeled at 200 km/s, dusty outflow attenuation with a high neutral column density, and a Lyman-alpha escape fraction of 0.121 [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF1337 is fitted with a 200 km/s expanding shell, standard intrinsic Ly-alpha equivalent width, dusty high-column outflow attenuation, and an escape fraction of 0.121.

quotes.quote: FDF1337 and FDF5550 To model FDF1337 and FDF5550, we have therefore fixed = 600/3 = 200 , and we proceed to adjust the 3 remaining shell parameters. Our best fits and the corresponding parameters are presented in Fig. \ FDF1337_5550 . Note that both spectra are fitted with an intrinsic EW( ) _ int 60 , which corresponds to the equilibrium value reached by a galaxy with constant star formation after 50--100 Myr (cf. \ Fig. \ f_s04_sfr ). Even if the observed EW( ) _ obs is an order of magnitude lower, the intrinsic predicted EW( ) _ int seems to be ``standard'' in this sense.

Dust (we find _a=1.0 for 1337 and 1.5 for 5550, i.e. \ E(B-V) 0.1-0.15 ) in a high neutral column density ( =5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) outflow causes this attenuation. The derived \ escape fraction is f_e=0.052 for FDF5550 and f_e=0.121 for FDF1337.

gmw_00008393.name: FDF5550
gmw_00008393.overview: FDF5550 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:0805.3601]. It is modeled with a neutral outflowing shell expanding at 200 km/s, which exhibits a high dust optical depth and a low Lyman-alpha escape fraction of approximately 5.2 percent [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF5550 is fitted with the same 200 km/s expanding-shell picture as FDF1337, but with higher dust optical depth and a lower Ly-alpha escape fraction.

quotes.quote: FDF1337 and FDF5550 To model FDF1337 and FDF5550, we have therefore fixed = 600/3 = 200 , and we proceed to adjust the 3 remaining shell parameters. Our best fits and the corresponding parameters are presented in Fig. \ FDF1337_5550 . Note that both spectra are fitted with an intrinsic EW( ) _ int 60 , which corresponds to the equilibrium value reached by a galaxy with constant star formation after 50--100 Myr (cf. \ Fig. \ f_s04_sfr ). Even if the observed EW( ) _ obs is an order of magnitude lower, the intrinsic predicted EW( ) _ int seems to be ``standard'' in this sense.

Dust (we find _a=1.0 for 1337 and 1.5 for 5550, i.e. \ E(B-V) 0.1-0.15 ) in a high neutral column density ( =5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) outflow causes this attenuation. The derived \ escape fraction is f_e=0.052 for FDF5550 and f_e=0.121 for FDF1337.

gmw_00008394.name: FDF2384
gmw_00008394.overview: FDF2384 is a strong Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:0805.3601, arXiv:astro-ph/0702414] that exhibits an asymmetric emission line profile with a smooth red-wing bump [arXiv:0805.3601, arXiv:astro-ph/0702414]. It features a unique spatial structure consisting of a compact continuum-emitting component and a separate, diffuse Lyman-alpha emitting component [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414].

sources.summary: FDF2384 has a high observed Ly-alpha equivalent width, lower inferred neutral column than FDF1337/FDF5550, a smooth red-wing bump, and a best-fit escape fraction near 0.16.

quotes.quote: FDF2384, FDF4454, and FDF5812 The \ fits for these objects are shown in Fig. FDF5812 . The profiles differ from the former by their high EW( ) _ obs > 70 , and a less extended red wing ( 600 ). This leads to simulated neutral column densities an order of magnitude lower and higher escape fractions ( f_e > 0.15 , cf. \ Table t_summary ). To illustrate how well constrained the model parameters are we will present in detail the fitting of the object FDF2384.

The overall shape of the \ profile of FDF2384 is smooth, with a small bump visible in the extended red wing (at V 400 ); its position corresponds to 150 . Thanks to the ``extension'' of the line in FDF2384, we can fix (2-4) 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . Finally, the relative height of the bump compared to the main peak will determine correlated values for \ and _a , and two combinations are possible: ( =2 10^ 19 and _a=0.5 ) provides a reasonable fit, but the best fit is obtained with ( =3 10^ 19 and _a=1.0 ), as shown in Fig. \ FDF5812 . The derived best fit escape fraction is f_e 0.16 .

sources.summary: The paper describes FDF-2384 as a strong Lyman-alpha emitter with an asymmetric profile and unusual spatial structure: separate compact continuum and diffuse Lyman-alpha emitting components.

quotes.quote: The \ profile displays a wide variety of morphologies, from absorption (e.g., FDF-6063), to absorption with a weak emission (FDF-5903), and then to strong emission (e.g., FDF-2384). Most profiles show one asymmetric emission line with a sharp drop on the blue side and a pronounced red wing (e.g., FDF-2384, FDF-5550). This profile type is referred to hereafter as the ``asymmetric line profile''. FDF-4691, FDF-5215, and FDF-7539 show a secondary emission line blueshifted with respect to the main emission line. This profile type is called ``double-peak profile'' hereafter. FDF-1267 FDF-1337 FDF-2384 FDF-3389 FDF-4454 FDF-4691 FDF-5215 FDF-5550 FDF-5744 FDF-5812 FDF-5903 FDF-6063 FDF-6557 FDF-7539 FDF-7683 FDF-8304 The full width half maximum (FWHM) values of the \ emission lines are listed in Table def_lya2 . The distribution of the FWHM values corrected for the instrumental profile is shown in Fig. fig_as_fwhm . The observed values range from 200 \ up to 1500 . For FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, this value refers to the envelope of the profile. Most lines have widths below FWHM 600 , in agreement with Rhoads et al. ( ), Dawson et al. ( ), and Venemans et al. ( ), who analysed LAEs at a redshift of z > 5 and found line widths of FWHM < 500 . Figure fwhm_ewlya shows the \ as a function of the line width of the emission line. The values of the line width show a possible anti-correlation with the \ equivalent widths. The outliers with high line width are FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, both galaxies with double-peak profiles.

The HST/ACS images show the rest-frame UV of the starburst galaxies, which is dominated by the young stars. Moreover, as the surface brightness scales with ( z +1) ^ 4 , the extended regions of the galaxies are not easily detected. The observed diameters of the objects are of the order of 2 kpc, which is typical of Lyman-break galaxies at a redshift of three (Ferguson et al. 2004). The strong \ emitters appear compact (e.g., FDF-4691 or FDF-5215), while the galaxies with a weak (or no) \ emission line are elongated and irregular (e.g., FDF-5903 and FDF-6063). We detected neither (strong) spatial extension of the \ emissions in the two-dimensional spectra nor a spatial offset between the \ emission and the continuum for all of the members of the medium-resolution sample, excepting FDF-2384. Figure images_hst b shows that FDF-2384 has two components, a compact one and a diffuse component, north east of the compact component. The two-dimensional spectrum shows that the continuum is emitted by the compact object, while the \ emission is emitted by the diffuse object.

gmw_00008395.name: FDF4454
gmw_00008395.overview: FDF4454 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a high Lyman-alpha equivalent width [arXiv:0805.3601]. Its spectral profile is modeled with an expanding shell moving at 150 km/s, which exhibits a low neutral column density and a high escape fraction of approximately 0.42 [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF4454 shares the high-EW, shorter red-wing profile class; its fit uses a 150 km/s expanding shell, lower column density, similar dust, and a high escape fraction.

quotes.quote: FDF2384, FDF4454, and FDF5812 The \ fits for these objects are shown in Fig. FDF5812 . The profiles differ from the former by their high EW( ) _ obs > 70 , and a less extended red wing ( 600 ). This leads to simulated neutral column densities an order of magnitude lower and higher escape fractions ( f_e > 0.15 , cf. \ Table t_summary ).

We proceed the same way to fit FDF4454 and FDF5812, which appear quite similar: the expanding shell has the same velocity =150 , the column density in the shell is an order of magnitude lower than in FDF1337 and FDF5550, but the dust amount, _a , remains the same. Since the intrinsic EW( ) _ int is EW _ obs and the latter values are already relatively large, one obtains quite large intrinsic \ equivalent widths, between 100 and 280 , for FDF2384, 4454, and 5812. The highest values require fairly young ages (see Fig. \ f_s04_sfr ); however since these objects are quite faint, their continuum placement may be uncertain and EW _ int may therefore be overestimated. The \ escape fraction of FDF4454 is high, f_e 0.42 , because the shell is less dusty.

gmw_00008396.name: FDF5812
gmw_00008396.overview: FDF5812 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a high observed Lyman-alpha equivalent width [arXiv:0805.3601]. Its spectral profile is modeled with an expanding shell velocity of 150 kilometers per second and a relatively low neutral column density [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF5812 shares the high-EW, shorter red-wing profile class; the authors fit it with a 150 km/s shell, lower column density, similar dust, and uncertain large intrinsic equivalent width.

quotes.quote: FDF2384, FDF4454, and FDF5812 The \ fits for these objects are shown in Fig. FDF5812 . The profiles differ from the former by their high EW( ) _ obs > 70 , and a less extended red wing ( 600 ). This leads to simulated neutral column densities an order of magnitude lower and higher escape fractions ( f_e > 0.15 , cf. \ Table t_summary ).

We proceed the same way to fit FDF4454 and FDF5812, which appear quite similar: the expanding shell has the same velocity =150 , the column density in the shell is an order of magnitude lower than in FDF1337 and FDF5550, but the dust amount, _a , remains the same. Since the intrinsic EW( ) _ int is EW _ obs and the latter values are already relatively large, one obtains quite large intrinsic \ equivalent widths, between 100 and 280 , for FDF2384, 4454, and 5812. The highest values require fairly young ages (see Fig. \ f_s04_sfr ); however since these objects are quite faint, their continuum placement may be uncertain and EW _ int may therefore be overestimated.

gmw_00008397.name: FDF3389
gmw_00008397.overview: FDF3389 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.5 [arXiv:0805.3601]. Its spectrum displays a complex, multi-peaked red extension of the Lyman-alpha emission line and exhibits a high escape fraction of 0.45 [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF3389 is a z~4.5 object with medium Ly-alpha equivalent width, a complex multi-peaked red extension, near-canonical intrinsic equivalent width, and high escape fraction.

quotes.quote: FDF3389 and FDF6557 These two objects are both at z 4.5 . They have medium observed equivalent widths (EW( ) _ obs =30-40 ), compared to the former asymmetric profiles, and rather small extensions ( 600-700 ). We treat them separately, because their spectra seem more complex than a smooth asymmetric emission line. They present multi-peaks on the red extension of the line.

However, they are also more noisy, and these secondary features are only twice the noise amplitude. Nevertheless, we assume that they are significant, and we derive best-fits, taking these features into account (see Fig. \ FDF3389 ). The parameters derived from our fitting are close to the 3 narrow asymmetric cases ( =150 , 2-4 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 , _a=0.5-1 ) except for the intrinsic EW, which have ``canonical'' values --- compatible with expectations for SFR=const --- again, because the observed EW are lower for these objects. The escape fraction is high for FDF3389 ( f_e=0.45 ), and lower for FDF6557 ( f_e=0.17 ) because of a higher column density and so a higher dust content.

gmw_00008398.name: FDF6557
gmw_00008398.overview: FDF6557 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.5 [arXiv:0805.3601]. It exhibits a medium observed Lyman-alpha equivalent width, a complex multi-peaked red extension, and a low escape fraction of 0.17 due to its high dust content and column density [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF6557 is a z~4.5 object with medium Ly-alpha equivalent width, complex red-extension peaks, near-canonical intrinsic equivalent width, and lower escape fraction from higher column and dust.

quotes.quote: FDF3389 and FDF6557 These two objects are both at z 4.5 . They have medium observed equivalent widths (EW( ) _ obs =30-40 ), compared to the former asymmetric profiles, and rather small extensions ( 600-700 ). We treat them separately, because their spectra seem more complex than a smooth asymmetric emission line. They present multi-peaks on the red extension of the line.

However, they are also more noisy, and these secondary features are only twice the noise amplitude. Nevertheless, we assume that they are significant, and we derive best-fits, taking these features into account (see Fig. \ FDF3389 ). The parameters derived from our fitting are close to the 3 narrow asymmetric cases ( =150 , 2-4 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 , _a=0.5-1 ) except for the intrinsic EW, which have ``canonical'' values --- compatible with expectations for SFR=const --- again, because the observed EW are lower for these objects. The escape fraction is high for FDF3389 ( f_e=0.45 ), and lower for FDF6557 ( f_e=0.17 ) because of a higher column density and so a higher dust content.

gmw_00008399.name: FDF7539
gmw_00008399.overview: FDF7539 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a double-peaked Lyman-alpha emission line profile [arXiv:0805.3601, arXiv:astro-ph/0702414]. This profile exhibits a high line width [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414] and indicates a high neutral hydrogen column density as well as the presence of dust [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF7539 has a double-peaked Ly-alpha spectrum with small LIS-emission velocity shift, high column density, dust, canonical intrinsic spectrum, and escape fraction 0.28.

quotes.quote: FDF7539 For this object the velocity shift between the LIS in absorption and \ in emission was measured: =80 , which implies a shell velocity =80/3 27 , i.e. almost static as in the case of FDF4691, or at maximum 40 , in case of a low column density. Indeed, the spectrum is also double-peaked as for FDF4691. The large peaks separation ( V_p 500 , larger than for FDF4691) implies a high column density. Presumably, the rather low observed EW( ) _ obs also implies the presence of dust in the shell.

Indeed, the best fit shown in Fig. \ FDF4691_7539 has a high column density ( =5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ), and dust ( _a=0.3 ). It is compatible with the canonical value for the intrinsic \ spectrum. The resulting escape fraction is f_e=0.28 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies FDF-7539 as a high-redshift galaxy with a double-peaked Lyman-alpha line, high line width, and modelled profile compared against finite-element radiative-transfer calculations.

quotes.quote: The \ profile displays a wide variety of morphologies, from absorption (e.g., FDF-6063), to absorption with a weak emission (FDF-5903), and then to strong emission (e.g., FDF-2384). Most profiles show one asymmetric emission line with a sharp drop on the blue side and a pronounced red wing (e.g., FDF-2384, FDF-5550). This profile type is referred to hereafter as the ``asymmetric line profile''. FDF-4691, FDF-5215, and FDF-7539 show a secondary emission line blueshifted with respect to the main emission line. This profile type is called ``double-peak profile'' hereafter. FDF-1267 FDF-1337 FDF-2384 FDF-3389 FDF-4454 FDF-4691 FDF-5215 FDF-5550 FDF-5744 FDF-5812 FDF-5903 FDF-6063 FDF-6557 FDF-7539 FDF-7683 FDF-8304 The full width half maximum (FWHM) values of the \ emission lines are listed in Table def_lya2 . The distribution of the FWHM values corrected for the instrumental profile is shown in Fig. fig_as_fwhm . The observed values range from 200 \ up to 1500 . For FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, this value refers to the envelope of the profile. Most lines have widths below FWHM 600 , in agreement with Rhoads et al. ( ), Dawson et al. ( ), and Venemans et al. ( ), who analysed LAEs at a redshift of z > 5 and found line widths of FWHM < 500 . Figure fwhm_ewlya shows the \ as a function of the line width of the emission line. The values of the line width show a possible anti-correlation with the \ equivalent widths. The outliers with high line width are FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, both galaxies with double-peak profiles.

Therefore, we restricted the finite element modelling to the double-peaked profiles of FDF-4691, FDF-5215, and FDF-7539. A spherical two-component model with a central line emission region surrounded by a shell of neutral HI gas was assumed. For the emission from the central region, we assumed a Gaussian emission profile. The model is mainly characterised by the velocity dispersion of the intrinsic emission line, as well as the velocity dispersion, the neutral column density, and the outflow velocity of the shell. The exact geometrical properties of the model are of minor importance for the computed line profiles (Richling et al. ). In Fig. FDF5215_lyatheo the comparison of the \ profiles of FDF-5215 and FDF-7539 is shown with the theoretical model. The results for FDF-4691 have already been described by Tapken et al. ( ). The derived fit parameters are listed for the three galaxies in Table tab_fem . While N _ HI could not be constrained well for FDF-4691, an upper limit can be found for FDF-5215: N _ HI cannot exceed N _ HI = 2 10^ 16 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00008400.name: FDF1267
gmw_00008400.overview: FDF1267 is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits a strong asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission peak with a blue-side bump [arXiv:0805.3601]. This spectral profile is best explained by a quasi-static shell with a low dust content [arXiv:0805.3601].

sources.summary: FDF1267 shows a strong asymmetric Ly-alpha peak plus blue bump; the favored interpretation is a low-dust quasi-static shell, with the fast dusty solution disfavored.

quotes.quote: FDF1267 This object presents an asymmetric emission peak plus a bump on the blue side of this peak with a strong observed EW( ) _ obs =129 . The \ profile, shown in Fig. \ FDF1267 , can be fitted by different scenarii. On the left panel of Fig. \ FDF1267 , the bump is considered as a blue peak in an almost static ( =50 ) shell with a small column density ( =2 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ) and a small amount of dust ( _a=0.1 ). Values of b higher than b=20 lead to too separated peaks.

Our favoured solution is the ``quasi-static shell'' picture. Indeed, the high EW _ int inferred in the second fit seems unlikely. Furthermore, the large observed EW _ obs of 1267 would imply a rather low column density as discussed in Sect. s_obsEW . Finally, the SFR values derived from uncorrected UV and \ fluxes ( SFR( )>SFR(UV) ) indicate a low dust content.

gmw_00008401.name: FDF5215
gmw_00008401.overview: FDF5215 is a compact, high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414]. It exhibits a double-peaked Lyman-alpha emission line profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414] with a small bump on the blue side of its main emission [arXiv:0805.3601, arXiv:astro-ph/0702414].

sources.summary: FDF5215 resembles FDF1267 with a blue-side bump, but neither low- nor high-column fit is fully satisfactory; the low-column solution implies an unusually huge intrinsic FWHM.

quotes.quote: FDF5215 FDF5215, shown in Fig. \ FDF5215 presents the same spectral shape as FDF1267: a small bump on the blue side of the asymmetric strong emission, but the noise level is much lower, and this small bump has to be taken into account. Again, two different scenarii can reproduce the spectral shape. The bump is either considered as a blue peak in an almost static shell, or as the first red peak of \ emission. The two solutions, differing by more than 5 orders of magnitude in , are listed in Table t_summary . In passing we note that FDF5215 was also modeled by Tapk07 ; their set of parameters is similar to our solution at low , except for a higher value of b .

None of our fits are really satisfying. The solution at low- \ (left panel) reproduces well the observed profile, but the derived column density is very low: at least 4 orders of magnitude lower than the rest of the sample. As a consequence, the FWHM of the intrinsic \ emission is huge (FWHM=700 ) to reproduce a broad profile without efficient broadening due to radiation transfer in an almost transparent medium.

sources.summary: The paper treats FDF-5215 as a compact high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitter with a double-peaked profile, modelled radiative-transfer line formation, and an upper limit on neutral hydrogen column density.

gmw_00008402.name: NGC 5330
gmw_00008402.overview: NGC 5330 is a nearly round elliptical galaxy embedded in the outer halo of the nearby giant elliptical NGC 5328 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. The galaxy exhibits a faint ripple structure, but its line-strength indices suggest no recent episodes of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: NGC 5330 is treated as a nearly round elliptical embedded in NGC 5328's halo, with contaminated profiles, a faint ripple, and no recent star-formation signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 5330 The surface brightness profile of this nearly round elliptical was fitted by a Sersic power law with a high structural parameter of n=3.16 , with the isophotes showing no deviations from pure ellipses (see Figure fig4 , top right). The average ellipticity is 0.1, while the position angle variation outside the region affected by the seeing is less than 10 ^ . Like 2MASX J13524838-2829584, NGC 5330 is embedded in the outer halo of NGC 5328 from which it is separated by 35 kpc in projection. The luminosity and geometric profiles of NGC 5330 are contaminated by the light of the nearby giant elliptical. The contamination is probably the cause for the observed increase of the (B--R) colour profile at radii > 16 \ .
Only minor fine structures have been detected: a faint ripple is found in NGC 5330, however, no recent star formation episodes are suggested for this galaxy by the H vs. MgFe line-strength indices.

gmw_00008403.name: MCG --5--33--29
gmw_00008403.overview: MCG --5--33--29 is an SBa spiral galaxy located in an outer group environment [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. The galaxy features a central bar, tightly wound and bifurcated spiral arms, and a type 2 Seyfert active nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG --5--33--29 as an outer-group SBa spiral with a bar, tightly wound arms, patchy bifurcated structure, strong forbidden lines, and type 2 Seyfert activity.

quotes.quote: MCG --5--33--29 The SBa spiral is located at about 124 kpc projected distance from NGC 5328. The arms start from a bar at 10 \ and appear tightly wound. The bar can be seen in the flattening of the surface brightness profile at 11 . The (B--R) colour profile in the bar region is flat and red ( 1.75 mag) and drops to 1.4 mag in the position of the arms and the ring. The model subtracted image (Figure fig3 ) reveals the irregular patchy structure of the spiral arms which both appear bifurcated.
The spectrum of the spiral MCG --5--33--29 presents narrow but strong forbidden lines of [ O III ] , [ N II ] and [ S II ] . H is weak in emission and H is weak in absorption.
Nuclear activity is present in MCG --5--33--29: the strong forbidden lines of [ O III ] and [ N II ] ratios indicate a type 2 Seyfert activity.

gmw_00008404.name: 2MASX J13524838-2829584
gmw_00008404.overview: The surface photometry of the small galaxy 2MASX J13524838-2829584 is strongly contaminated by the nearby galaxy NGC 5328 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. It features a slightly discy inner region, boxy outskirts likely connected to a merging event, and line-strength indices that suggest recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: The paper finds 2MASX J13524838-2829584 strongly affected by NGC 5328's light, with discy inner parts, boxy outskirts, and line-strength evidence for recent star formation.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J13524838-2829584 The surface photometry of this small galaxy is strongly contaminated by the vicinity of NGC 5328. The inner parts are slightly discy while the outskirts are quite boxy ( b_4 = -0.05 ). The position angle variation of about 10 ^ is only marginally due to the light contamination caused by NGC 5328. The colour profile is red and flat at (B--R)=1.65.

The extremely boxy isophotes of 2MASX J13524838-2829584 are believed to be connected to a merging event sch96 . Line strength indices suggest that this object may have experienced a recent star formation episode.

gmw_00008405.name: 2MASX J13523852-2830444
gmw_00008405.overview: 2MASX J13523852-2830444 is an S0-type lenticular galaxy viewed almost edge-on [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. It features a central major-axis dust lane, a stellar disc, and central star formation characteristic of HII regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 2MASX J13523852-2830444 as an edge-on S0 with a disc and dust lane, and its emission-line ratios indicate HII-region-like central star formation.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J13523852-2830444 The morphology suggests a S0-type galaxy seen almost edge-on. The presence of a disc is supported by the increasing ellipticity 0.2 0.6 and the constant position angle profiles with a variation lower than 5 ^ . The results from the surface photometry are affected by a central major-axis dust lane, whose signature is evident in the residual image (Figure fig2 ) and in the positivity of the b_4 parameter.
Figure fig7 shows the emission features in 2MASX J13523852-2830444 in the region around H featuring narrow emission lines of H , [ N II ] and [ S II ] .

According to these diagrams, the spiral MCG --5--33--29 has emission properties typical of an AGN while both the S0s 2MASX J13523852-2830444 and 2MASX J13525393-2831421 present emissions features characteristic of HII regions, suggesting the presence of ongoing or recent star formation in their centers.

gmw_00008406.name: 2MASX J13525393-2831421
gmw_00008406.overview: 2MASX J13525393-2831421 is a barred lenticular galaxy classified as an SB0 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. It exhibits a bar-like isophotal twist [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056] and strong hydrogen emission lines characteristic of H II regions, indicating ongoing or recent central star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 2MASX J13525393-2831421 as an SB0 with a bar-like twist, strong H emission, and HII-region-like line ratios signaling ongoing or recent central star formation.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J13525393-2831421 We classify this galaxy as an SB0. This classification is based upon the surface brightness profile showing two components and the sudden isophotal twisting of about 20 ^ , indicating the presence of a bar component as suggested also in the isophotal image of Figure fig3 . The (B--R) colour profile has an average value of 1.5 mag.
2MASX J13525393-2831421 shows strong H emission and only weak [ N II ] and [ S II ] lines.

According to these diagrams, the spiral MCG --5--33--29 has emission properties typical of an AGN while both the S0s 2MASX J13523852-2830444 and 2MASX J13525393-2831421 present emissions features characteristic of HII regions, suggesting the presence of ongoing or recent star formation in their centers.

gmw_00008407.name: 2MASX J13530016-2827061
gmw_00008407.overview: 2MASX J13530016-2827061 is an edge-on S0 lenticular galaxy featuring a weak, warped disk component [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. The galaxy hosts an old, metal-rich stellar population and is likely undergoing an active interaction with nearby group members [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J13530016-2827061 as an edge-on S0 with a weak warped disc, old metal-rich stellar population, and likely ongoing interaction with nearby group members.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J13530016-2827061 This galaxy is an edge--on S0 galaxy with a weak disc component, merely distinguishable in the surface brightness profile, but clearly visible in ellipticity, position angle and the b_4 profile. The residual image in Figure fig3 shows the typical pattern of positive 4 ^ th Fourier--coefficients indicative of strong discy isophotes. The disc is thickening with increasing radius and is warped in its outer parts.

From the combined analysis of the two planes the following considerations can be done: NGC 5328, NGC 5330 and 2MASX J13530016-2827061 have quite old stellar populations, super-solar metallicities and super-solar [ /Fe ] ratios.

A warped disc component emerges from the model subtracted image of 2MASX J13530016-2827061. Since nel95 conclude that warps cannot be primordial structures but must have either a recent or a continuous excitation mechanism, we suggest an ongoing interaction of this galaxy with nearby group members.

gmw_00008408.name: A1139-BCG
gmw_00008408.overview: A1139-BCG is a brightest cluster galaxy with a central body that formed at an early epoch [arXiv:1706.06107]. The galaxy has experienced considerable tidal events more recently than other similar systems, resulting in a bluer and more scattered pixel color-magnitude distribution [arXiv:1706.06107].

sources.summary: The paper finds A1139-BCG has bluer, more scattered pCMD behavior than A2589-BCG, a smaller early-formed central body, and evidence for more recent tidal events.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: BCG RA & 10 ^ h 58 ^ m 11.0 ^ s; BCG Dec & +01 ^ d 36 ^ m 16 ^ s

quotes.quote: (1) While the overall shapes of the pCMDs are similar to those of typical early-type galaxies, the A2589-BCG tends to have redder mean pixel color and smaller pixel color deviation at given surface brightness than the A1139-BCG. (2) The mean pixel color distribution as a function of pixel surface brightness (pCMD backbone ) indicates that the A2589-BCG formed a larger central body ( 2.0 kpc in radius) by major dry mergers at an early epoch than the A1139-BCG (a central body 1.3 kpc in radius), while they have grown commonly by subsequent minor mergers. (3) The spatial distributions of the pCMD outliers reveal that the A1139-BCG experienced considerable tidal events more recently than the A2589-BCG, whereas the A2589-BCG has an asymmetric compact core possibly resulting from major dry merger at an early epoch. (4) The A2589-BCG shows a very large faint-to-bright pixel number ratio compared to early-type non-BCGs, whereas the ratio for the A1139-BCG is not distinctively large.

gmw_00008409.name: A2589-BCG
gmw_00008409.overview: A2589-BCG, also known as NGC 7647, is a cD galaxy located at the center of the Abell 2589 cluster [arXiv:1101.3375]. It features a red envelope [arXiv:0808.1521], an asymmetric compact core [arXiv:1706.06107], a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0808.1521], and a large population of small companion galaxies [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7647 as A2589's central cD galaxy, compares its SDSS and BATC SEDs, notes aperture effects from its extended core, and links cluster elongation to its orientation.

quotes.quote: Figure 3(a) shows the relative SDSS and BATC SEDs of the central cD galaxy in A2589, NGC 7647. It is clear that there is a systematic offset between two SEDs which is called zero point. Figure 3(b) gives the combined SED of NGC 7647 after zero point correction.

For the most extended central cD galaxy, NGC 7647, our aperture just covers its core region, which leads to a large zero point of nearly 2.0 mag. The contour of surface density shows an north-south elongation, which agrees with the X-ray brightness profile and the orientation of central cD galaxy, NGC 7647.

sources.summary: The paper finds A2589-BCG is redder and more relaxed, with a larger central body from early dry mergers, an asymmetric compact core, and a large faint-to-bright pixel ratio.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: BCG RA & 23 ^ h 23 ^ m 57.4 ^ s; BCG Dec & +16 ^ d 46 ^ m 38 ^ s

quotes.quote: (1) While the overall shapes of the pCMDs are similar to those of typical early-type galaxies, the A2589-BCG tends to have redder mean pixel color and smaller pixel color deviation at given surface brightness than the A1139-BCG. (2) The mean pixel color distribution as a function of pixel surface brightness (pCMD backbone ) indicates that the A2589-BCG formed a larger central body ( 2.0 kpc in radius) by major dry mergers at an early epoch than the A1139-BCG (a central body 1.3 kpc in radius), while they have grown commonly by subsequent minor mergers. (3) The spatial distributions of the pCMD outliers reveal that the A1139-BCG experienced considerable tidal events more recently than the A2589-BCG, whereas the A2589-BCG has an asymmetric compact core possibly resulting from major dry merger at an early epoch. (4) The A2589-BCG shows a very large faint-to-bright pixel number ratio compared to early-type non-BCGs, whereas the ratio for the A1139-BCG is not distinctively large.
These results are consistent with the idea that the BCG in the dynamically older cluster (A2589) formed earlier and is relaxed better.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7647 as a cD with a red envelope and many small companions, and finds a clear central kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: NGC7647: This is a cD galaxy with a red envelope and a large number of small companions (Vitores et al. \ 1996). The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile of this galaxy shows a clear KDC in the centre.

gmw_00008410.name: J092455.24+051052.0
gmw_00008410.overview: J092455.24+051052.0 is a dual active galactic nucleus system with a nuclear separation of 0.43 arcseconds [arXiv:2011.09961]. Spectral decomposition of the system reveals a shoulder in its Gaussian profile, and it exhibits weak, AGN-like diagnostic features [arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper includes J092455.24+051052.0 as a recently identified DAGN with 0.43 arcsec nuclear separation, a spectral shoulder revealed by decomposition, and weak AGN-like diagnostics.

quotes.quote: In addition, we have included a recently identified DAGN SDSS J092455.24+051052.0 that has a nuclear separation of 0.43" (Liu et al 2018).

For the remaining 3 sources (J092455.24+051052.0, J095207.62+255257.2, J162345.20+080851.1), we see a shoulder in a Gaussian profile due to one of the double-peaked components that can be revealed from spectral decomposition.

Interestingly, the most AGN-like source (J112659.54+294442.8: upper-left data point) in this plot corresponds to the most AGN-like source in BPT-VO diagram (top data point), and the least AGN-like source (J092455.24+051052.0: lowest data point) in this plot corresponds to the least AGN-like source in BPT-VO plot (bottom data point).

gmw_00008411.name: J102325.57+324348.4
gmw_00008411.overview: J102325.57+324348.4 is a known double active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2011.09961]. Radio observations of the source reveal a faint, jet-like structure stretching from the center in the north-south directions [arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper treats J102325.57+324348.4 as a known DAGN whose blended spectral shape is ambiguous at low velocity offset, with a faint VLASS jet-like structure and VLA confirmation data.

quotes.quote: If the velocity offset is 4 69 \ (4th panel in Fig. 1), we can still visually identify double-peaked lines for 5 out of 12 sources (J112659.54+294442.8, J114642.47+511029.6, J115822.58+323102.2, J142507.32+323137.4, J171544.05+600835.7; FWHM of them are narrower than that of the average value and their peak fluxes are comparable), but it is difficult to tell if the spectral shape is composed of a single or double Gaussian lines for 4 of them (J102325.57+324348.4, J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3).
The VLASS images do not show apparent radio jets, except a faint jet-like structure stretching from center to the north south directions in J102325.57+324348.4.

For the confirmation of two AGNs in our known DAGN sample, radio observations (VLA: J102325.57+324348.4, J115822.58+323102.2, J150243.10+111557.3; VLBI: J142507.32+323137.4) were made use of for 4 out of 6 radio-loud AGNs.

gmw_00008412.name: J112659.54+294442.8
gmw_00008412.overview: J112659.54+294442.8 is a Type 2 active galactic nucleus host galaxy that is undergoing an extreme minor merger [arXiv:1504.01391]. The system features a secondary, faint point-like source located southeast of the central nucleus, which may represent a remnant black hole from the interaction [arXiv:1504.01391, arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1126+2944 as a Type 2 dual AGN in an extreme 460:1 minor merger, with paired emission/X-ray components and a faint pointlike SE source detected by HST.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1126+2944 is a Type 2 AGN with double-peaked emission lines WA09.1,LI10.1 , and follow-up observations showed that the double peaks are produced by two emission components separated by 0 94 , or 1.76 kpc CO12.1 . These two sources, J1126+2944NW and J1126+2944SE, spatially coincide with two components in the HST /F160W observations, to within 0.9 times the astrometric error. J1126+2944NW is detected at the >5 level in 0.3-8 keV X-rays, while J1126+2944SE is detected at 2.3 .

The fascinating component of this galaxy is J1126+2944SE. J1126+2944SE is faint in the F160W image (460 times fainter than J1126+2944NW), and was not identified in a 120 second image taken in the near-infrared ( K_p ) with the Keck laser guide star adaptive optics system FU12.1 . However, it is also detected in our F814W and F438W images. We detect J1126+2944SE in the HST /F160W data with a significance of 2.4 above the primary galaxy background, and we have marked its location with an arrow in Figure fig:images .

sources.summary: The paper describes J112659.54+294442.8 as a known DAGN with the smallest velocity offset, ambiguous single-versus-double [O III] fits, strong AGN-like diagnostics, and a possible remnant black hole.

quotes.quote: Except for J112659.54+294442.8, we can visually identify double-peaked [ O III ] lines in the spectra. The J112659.54+294442.8 has the smallest velocity offset (216 3 instrumental resolution) and spectral fitting with a single [ O III ] narrow emission line reduced ^2 =1.16) is equally good as the fit with two [ O III ] narrow emission components (reduced ^2 =0.91). Thus, it is impossible to tell if this source is a DAGN or a single AGN without other details.

Interestingly, the most AGN-like source (J112659.54+294442.8: upper-left data point) in this plot corresponds to the most AGN-like source in BPT-VO diagram (top data point), and the least AGN-like source (J092455.24+051052.0: lowest data point) in this plot corresponds to the least AGN-like source in BPT-VO plot (bottom data point).

Note that J092455.24+051052.0 (Liu et al. 2018) and J132323.33-015941.9 (Woo et al. 2014) are very close doubles, and J112659.54+294442.8 has a point-like source 1 \ SE of the central nucleus which could be the remnant black hole from a minor merger interaction with the main galaxy (Comerford et al. 2015).

gmw_00008413.name: J115822.58+323102.2
gmw_00008413.overview: J115822.58+323102.2 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:2011.09961]. Its optical spectrum exhibits double-peaked [O III] emission lines, which are best fit by combining two [O III] components with a broad-line component [arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper notes J115822.58+323102.2 is an exception where two [O III] components initially fit poorly, but adding a broad-line component improves the fit and double peaks remain visible.

quotes.quote: Visual inspection of the resulting spectral decompositions, as well as ^2 values, suggest that, except for J115822.58+323102.2, the spectral decomposition with two [ O III ] components fits significantly better than that with one [ O III ] component.
It also improves the fit significantly for J115822.58+323102.2; it decreases the reduced ^2 value from 9.28 (two component fit) to 0.73 (two component plus broad-line component fit).

If the velocity offset is 4 69 \ (4th panel in Fig. 1), we can still visually identify double-peaked lines for 5 out of 12 sources (J112659.54+294442.8, J114642.47+511029.6, J115822.58+323102.2, J142507.32+323137.4, J171544.05+600835.7; FWHM of them are narrower than that of the average value and their peak fluxes are comparable), but it is difficult to tell if the spectral shape is composed of a single or double Gaussian lines for 4 of them (J102325.57+324348.4, J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3).

gmw_00008414.name: J132323.33-015941.9
gmw_00008414.overview: J132323.33-015941.9 is a type-2 quasi-stellar object located at a redshift of z=0.35 [arXiv:1401.3042]. The system is a candidate sub-kiloparsec binary active galactic nucleus in the late stage of a galaxy merger [arXiv:1401.3042], displaying very close double stellar cores [arXiv:2011.09961].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J132323.33-015941.9/J1323 as a type-2 QSO and candidate sub-kpc binary AGN in a late-stage galaxy merger.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the discovery of a sub-kpc scale binary AGN candidate in a type-2 QSO, SDSS J132323.33-015941.9 (hereafter J1323), based on Very Large Telescope (VLT) spectroscopy combined with Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging. At the redshift of the QSO, z=0.35 , 1 corresponds to 4.9 kpc, assuming H _ o = 70 \ Mpc ^ 1 , _ =0.7 and _ m =0.3 .

In this paper, we presented a binary AGN candidate based on the spatial and kinematic decomposition of the double-peaked [ ] line profile using VLT IFU spectroscopy and HST imaging. Although the spatial resolution of the IFU image is limited to the seeing ( 0.7 ), the coincidence of the separation and the PA between the two stellar cores, with those between the two flux centroids of the [ ] kinematic components suggests that a binary AGN harbors double NLRs. As the velocity separation of 200 \ between blue and red components is typical of two cores in a galaxy merger, the sub-kpc scale spatial separation may indicate a binary AGN in a late stage of a galaxy merger.
If confirmed, J1323 is one of the rare cases of sub-kpc scale binary AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes J132323.33-015941.9 as having asymmetric Gaussian spectral profiles that are hard to classify at low velocity offset, and identifies it as a very close double.

quotes.quote: For the remaining 3 sources (J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3), we notice asymmetric Gaussian profiles in the spectral shapes that can be fitted with two Gaussian profiles.

If the velocity offset is 4 69 \ (4th panel in Fig. 1), we can still visually identify double-peaked lines for 5 out of 12 sources (J112659.54+294442.8, J114642.47+511029.6, J115822.58+323102.2, J142507.32+323137.4, J171544.05+600835.7; FWHM of them are narrower than that of the average value and their peak fluxes are comparable), but it is difficult to tell if the spectral shape is composed of a single or double Gaussian lines for 4 of them (J102325.57+324348.4, J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3).

Note that J092455.24+051052.0 (Liu et al. 2018) and J132323.33-015941.9 (Woo et al. 2014) are very close doubles, and J112659.54+294442.8 has a point-like source 1 \ SE of the central nucleus which could be the remnant black hole from a minor merger interaction with the main galaxy (Comerford et al. 2015).

gmw_00008415.name: zC400528
gmw_00008415.overview: zC400528 is a massive, main-sequence galaxy at redshift z=2.3 that is currently in the process of quenching [arXiv:1807.07074]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus that drives a lopsided, potentially fossil molecular outflow extending at least 10 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1807.07074, arXiv:2306.00518].

sources.summary: The paper studies zC400528 as a massive z=2.3 main-sequence galaxy whose AGN-driven molecular outflow dominates the energetics, cannot escape, and may drive central quenching.

quotes.quote: In this work we use new deep, high-spatial resolution ALMA CO(3-2) and archival VLT/SINFONI \ observations to study the molecular and ionized components of the AGN-driven outflow in zC400528 ---a massive, main sequence galaxy at z=2.3 in the process of quenching. We detect a powerful molecular outflow that shows a positive velocity gradient and extends for at least 10 kpc from the nuclear region, about three times the projected size of the ionized wind. The molecular gas in the outflow does not reach velocities high enough to escape the galaxy and is therefore expected to be reaccreted. Keeping in mind the various assumptions involved in the analysis, we find that the mass and energetics of the outflow are dominated by the molecular phase. The AGN-driven outflow in zC400528 is powerful enough to deplete the molecular gas reservoir on a timescale at least twice shorter than that needed to exhaust it by star formation.

sources.summary: The paper interprets zC400528 as a possible fossil-outflow host because its weak outflow has one detached component and is strongly lopsided in position and velocity.

quotes.quote: zC400528 : this galaxy has a rather weak outflow, with momentum and energy rates consistent with continuous momentum driving Herrera-Camus2019ApJ . Fig. 7 of that paper, however, shows one detached component of the outflow (component 3); in general, the whole outflow is very lopsided: it extends only in one direction from the nucleus both in the plane of the sky (northwest) and radially (all components have a positive velocity relative to the nucleus). All of these properties suggest it is a fossil, perhaps comprised of remnants of several previous outflows.

gmw_00008416.name: J0943+3532
gmw_00008416.overview: J0943+3532 is a gravitationally lensed system containing a quasar at a redshift of z=1.21 [arXiv:2110.09535]. Gaia resolves the system into two point-source components, while image modeling reveals an additional intervening extended component between them [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0943+3532 as a new lens: its blended quasar spectrum is at z=1.21, Gaia resolves two point sources, and CFIS modelling requires an intervening component.

quotes.quote: J0943+3532 (lens) Given the small separation of 0.831 between the two components, the spectrum shows a single blended component, with emission lines of a quasar at z=1.21 . Gaia detects two point-source components (with small excess noise and proper motion) and the CFIS image modelling requires an additional extended component between these two point sources (see fig:cand_G8NOT ). We therefore classify this system as a lens.

gmw_00008417.name: J1111+3804
gmw_00008417.overview: J1111+3804 is a strongly lensed quasar system at a redshift of z=3.02 [arXiv:2110.09535]. The system features a visible lensing galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.6 and has a strong radio detection [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J1111+3804 as a z=3.02 lensed quasar with a visible lensing galaxy, redder upper component, z~0.6 absorption features, and radio detection.

quotes.quote: J1111+3804 (lens) Quasar source at high z=3.02 and lensing galaxy can be seen in light modelling. An existing SDSS spectrum centred on the lower quasar confirms the source redshift at z=3.018 . The top component is redder in the spectrum (blue in fig:spec ), which suggests some light leaking from a galaxy. We can see the 4000 break at H and K lines of CaII and G at z 0.6 . We also note that there is a strong radio detection for this system from VLASS.

gmw_00008418.name: J1146+3254
gmw_00008418.overview: J1146+3254 is a gravitationally lensed quasar system [arXiv:2110.09535]. Its counter-image, which is blended with a lensing galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.3, was identified using CFIS imaging and resolved spectra [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1146+3254 as a new lens revealed by CFIS depth and seeing, with NOT spectra detecting a counter-image CIV line blended with a z~0.3 lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1146+3254 (lens) This new lens is an example of the power provided by the improved depth and seeing of CFIS in finding lensed quasars. A single point source and galaxy subtracted from Pan-STARRS or DECaLS The DECaLS image of J1146+3254 can be seen in https://www.legacysurvey.org/viewer?ra=176.720&dec=32.903&zoom=16 images does not show any counter image, whereas in CFIS it is immediately apparent. Our resolved spectra detect the CIV line of the counter image blended with the lensing galaxy, which we measure to be at z 0.3 .

gmw_00008419.name: J1201+4013
gmw_00008419.overview: J1201+4013 is a gravitationally lensed system containing a quasar at a redshift of 1.936 [arXiv:2110.09535]. High-resolution data resolved two point sources, revealing a counter-image and a lensing galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.4 [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies J1201+4013 as a lens after CFIS modelling required a counter-image and lensing galaxy and NOT spectra recovered quasar emission and z~0.4 lens absorption.

quotes.quote: J1201+4013 (lens) This system was originally considered by the SDSS quasar lens search as a potential lens, SDSS J120118.92+401318.1, and it was selected as a morphological candidate with an SDSS QSO spectrum at z=1.936 . InadaEtal10 ruled this out as a possible lens based on I band imaging from Tek2k CCD at UH88 which was fit as a single point source+galaxy model. Thanks to the deeper and higher resolution spectra provided by Gaia , we selected this system as having two Gaia detections, and thus highly likely to have two point sources present in the system. Modelling of the CFIS data requires a counter image and a detection of the lensing galaxy as well (see fig:cand_G8NOT ). Resolved NOT spectra clearly show CIV and CIII emission from the fainter component, with a redder continuum, which is likely due to the lensing galaxy.

gmw_00008420.name: J1555+3118
gmw_00008420.overview: J1555+3118 is a lensed quasar at a redshift of z=3.360 [arXiv:2110.09535]. The system is characterized as a u-band dropout and features a detected foreground lensing galaxy [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J1555+3118 as a new lens, describing it as a z=3.360 u-band dropout recovered by selection with a detected lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: In this work, we confidently confirm five new lensed quasars: J0943+3532, J1111+3804, J1146+3254, J1201+4013, and J1555+3118.

J1555+3118 (lens) This system is a u band dropout recovered by our selection at z=3.360 with a lensing galaxy detected. It was originally selected in the Gaia double- WISE cross-match catalogue and not in the MILLIQUAS sample because of its marginally bluer WISE colours of W1-W2=0.67 and W2-W3=3.32 , typical of higher redshift quasars SecrestEtal15 .

gmw_00008421.name: J1559+3839
gmw_00008421.overview: J1559+3839 is a probable gravitationally lensed quasar with a redshift of z=1.334 [arXiv:2110.09535]. Modeling of the system's residuals indicates the presence of an intervening lens galaxy situated between its two point sources [arXiv:2110.09535].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1559+3839 as a probable lensed quasar: SDSS spectra favor z=1.334, modelling needs an intervening galaxy, and the system has reddening and X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: J1559+3839 (probable lens) Although we do not obtain the NOT follow-up spectra for this system, there is an SDSS spectrum with a QSO pipeline redshift of z=4.37 . However, closer inspection of the spectrum reveals the main emission line is likely MgII with apparent CIII, NeV, and NeVI at z=1.334 (see fig:spec_others ). This is also the classified redshift of the improved classification of Hewett&Wild10 . The modelling residuals require a galaxy between the two point sources. We therefore classify it as a probable lensed quasar.

gmw_00008422.overview: 1640.8+398 is a faint quasar that exhibits a variability range of approximately two magnitudes [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241]. Historical observations recorded three distinct maxima during periods of activity lasting about two months, though the object was not detected after 1977 [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1640.8+398 as a faint quasar with no prior detailed study, finding about two magnitudes of variability, three maxima, month-scale activity, and nondetection after 1977.

quotes.quote: 1640.8+398 (Q1) We have not found in the literature any detailed study of this faint object that shows a variability range of approximately 2 magnitudes. We report three maxima occurring at MJD 39618 (B = 18.64), MJD 41122 (B = 18.82) and MJD 42534 (B = 18.88). The first and the second happen during a period of activity lasting about 2 months, during which the object seems to oscillate around the maximum with an amplitude of 0.5 mag (see fig. 6). After 1977 the QSO was too faint and not detected any longer.

gmw_00008423.overview: The quasar 1641.8+399 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibited historical variability between 1967 and 1985 [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241]. During this period, its luminosity fluctuated by about two magnitudes, reaching two distinct minima before the object went undetected after 1985 [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241].

sources.summary: The paper reports first variability results for quasar 1641.8+399, covering 1967-1985, with about two magnitudes of change, two faint minima, and nondetection after 1985.

quotes.quote: 1641.8+399 (Q2) As for Q1, we did not find data about this object in the literature. Our data cover the period from 1967 to 1985 with a total of 42 observations in B. We find a variability of about 2 magnitudes, with a minimum (B=20.24) at MJD 40760 and a second minimum (B=20.19) at MJD 41119. Apart from this minima, the luminosity oscillates between B = 18 and B = 19.6 mag (see fig. 7). The object was not seen after 1985.

gmw_00008424.name: E1641.7+3988
gmw_00008424.overview: E1641.7+3988 is a spectroscopically confirmed quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241]. Although it shows B-band brightness fluctuations of about one magnitude, it displays no indications of violent activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241].

sources.summary: The paper treats E1641.7+3988 as a spectroscopically confirmed quasar not previously studied for variability, finding about one magnitude of B-band fluctuation but no violent activity.

quotes.quote: E1641.7+3988 (Q3) This object was first identified and spectroscopically confirmed as quasar by Crampton et al. (1988, 1989). Never studied for variability, at least to our knowledge, this object shows fluctuations of about 1 mag in B between 17.5 and 18.5 (see Fig. 8). We do not find any indication of violent activity.

gmw_00008425.name: NGC 6212
gmw_00008425.overview: NGC 6212 is an early-type [arXiv:1805.08655] Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1805.08655, arXiv:astro-ph/0409241]. It emits in the optical, radio, and X-ray bands [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241], displaying a homogeneous stellar population [arXiv:1805.08655] alongside suspected optical nuclear variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0409241].

sources.summary: Paper presents MaNGA ID 1-95092 as an early-type AGN example with homogeneous stellar populations, larger extinction than controls, flatter age profiles, low outer gradient values, and younger mean ages across most of one effective radius.

quotes.quote: As an example, we have selected to show two typical sets of results in Figs. fig:late-comp and fig:early-comp , the results for one ``late-type" AGN (MaNGA ID 1-44379) and one ``early-type" AGN (MaNGA ID 1-95092) and their control galaxies.

The early-type AGN case of Fig. fig:early-comp shows higher contribution of the old component in the central regions of the galaxies for the AGN and its controls. In this case, however, the AGN's RGB map shows a more homogeneous population throughout the galaxy, whilst the control galaxies' RGB maps show an older central region surrounded by a younger population outwards. The extinction for the AGN is larger compared with its control galaxies as can be seen in the A_ V profiles.

The population profiles show a more constant distribution of ages for the AGN inside the R 1.0 R_ e , specially between 0.5 and 1.0 R_ e . This behavior is reflected in the low gradient values of the 0.5-1.0 R_ e bin compared to the control galaxies. The mean age <t> profile of the AGN shows a younger population over almost all of the 0-1.0 R_ e range, similar to the late-type case (Fig. fig:late-comp .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6212 as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy with optical, radio, and X-ray emission, and finds suspicious low-level optical nuclear variability requiring linear-detector confirmation.

quotes.quote: NGC 6212 (Q4) Q4 is a type 1 Seyfert galaxy known as NGC 6212, which emits non only in the optical band but also in the Radio and in the X bands. A strong outburst (a factor of ten) in X-ray was reported by Hailey and Helfand (1980). In 1985 Biermann et al. identified a strong variable X-ray source within the galaxy; this variability covers a period of one month.

The residual variability never exceeds 0.3 for the 122 and 182 telescope and 0.5 for the S67, and we regard it as highly suspicious. In order to provide additional confirmation of the reality of the variability we also performed the surface photometry of the galaxy with the Ellipse package of STSDAS. The comparison of the magnitudes of the nuclear region obtained by the two methods shows good agreement because the nuclear region of NGC 6212 is always well visible in our images, even in those obtained by the Schmidt telescope. At the same time, our results underline the need of further observations of this galaxy in the optical band with linear detectors.

gmw_00008426.name: HAH J002348.6 244149.6
gmw_00008426.overview: HAH J002348.6 244149.6 is a moderately inclined spiral galaxy that acts as a gravitational lens [arXiv:2207.06997]. This system features a quadruple configuration of compact images, including a bright, blue point-like image and a southern thin arc [arXiv:2207.06997].

sources.summary: The paper describes HAH J002348.6 244149.6 as a new grade-A quadruple lens with a moderately inclined spiral deflector, compact images, and modeling potential for bulge-mass constraints.

quotes.quote: HAH J002348.6 244149.6 also shows compact images, but in a quadruple configuration.

Firstly, the new A grade HAH J002348.6 244149.6 comprises a moderately inclined lens spiral galaxy with a bright, blue point-like image in the north and three images distributed evenly along a southern thin arc. This system extends the small number of known spiral deflectors with prominent discs see also, the SWELLS survey, Treu2011 and, due to the relative positions of multiple images, its modelling has the potential to tightly constrain the galaxy bulge mass.

gmw_00008427.name: HAH J083420.3 + 452506.9
