gmw_00008427.overview: HAH J083420.3 + 452506.9 is a distant, isolated gravitational deflector galaxy located at a redshift of 0.6495 [arXiv:2207.06997]. Its spectrum is blended with a background star-forming source galaxy that has a probable redshift of 1.667 [arXiv:2207.06997].

sources.summary: The paper highlights HAH J083420.3 + 452506.9 as a distant isolated deflector with SDSS spectral evidence for blended lens and source galaxies and a probable source redshift of 1.667.

quotes.quote: Systems with the most distant, isolated deflectors (e.g. HAH J083420.3 + 452506.9 at z_ spec = 0.65 ) are very useful in that regard, as well as future spectroscopic follow-up of lenses with z_ phot > 0.6 (e.g. HAH J005403.4 + 394712.1, HAH J125709.5 + 282239.7).

We nonetheless inspected the SDSS spectra of all grades A, and identified multiple emission lines inconsistent with the lens redshift of 0.6495 for HAH J083420.3 + 452506.9 ( _* = 269 48 km s ^ 1 ). Our estimate r_ arc 0.6 suggests that the lens and source galaxies are blended in the SDSS spectrum, and that the background source is a star-forming galaxy with detections of CIV- 1549 , HeII- 1640 , OIII- 1664 , and CIII ] 1908 emission lines (see Fig. fig:specJ083420 ). The [ OII ] 3727 \ doublet is also tentatively detected at 9942.6 \ and 9949.47 . By fitting the smoothed spectrum, we inferred a probable source redshift of 1.667.

gmw_00008428.name: NGC 4915
gmw_00008428.overview: NGC 4915 is a relatively isolated E0 elliptical galaxy that is thought to have formed from a disk-disk merger [arXiv:2404.13459]. The galaxy exhibits a boxy fine structure with an apparent X-shape and hosts a young stellar population at its center [arXiv:2404.13459].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4915 as an isolated E0 early-type galaxy with a young central population, boxy X-structure, likely disk-disk merger history, and chemically normal stellar populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4915 is a relatively isolated, E0 galaxy thought to have formed < 7 Gyr ago 1992AJ....104.1039S . At its center, NGC 4915 hosts a young stellar population that has a strong H absorption feature 1992AJ....104.1039S . Strong Balmer features, such as the H line, point to A or F stars added to an old stellar population, which is consistent with an aging starburst population that formed during the merger. 1992AJ....104.1039S describes the fine structure around NGC 4915 to be extremely boxy, with an apparent X-structure.

NGC 4915 Finally, in the case of NGC 4915, we find chemical abundance ratios similar to NGC 3818 and other ETGs, however NGC 4915 is fit with a younger stellar population (relative to other ETGs of a similar mass) at its center and it possesses a fine structure constant of =5.5 . 1990ApJ...364L..33S suggests that NGC 4915 was a product of a disk-disk merger, and if NGC 4915 experienced enough gas inflow to the center of the galaxy spurring star formation, it would explain the presence of a younger stellar population. Assuming that the stellar and gaseous abundance patterns are the same in the progenitor galaxies that formed NGC 4915, the gas present at the time of the merger was likely chemically similar to the gas found in ETGs without fine structure at the same epoch. As a result, the resulting stellar population that formed during the merger would then be chemically similar to that of ETGs without fine structure.

gmw_00008429.name: WKK 6204
gmw_00008429.overview: WKK 6204 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2102.12721]. It has a nearby bright galaxy in its field, which likely causes sky gradients and a highly uncertain K_s band magnitude [arXiv:2102.12721].

sources.summary: WKK 6204 dominates the scatter in the IRSF-NTT photometry comparison because its IRSF K_s magnitude is uncertain; excluding it barely changes the Fundamental Plane fit.

quotes.quote: This scatter is dominated by WKK 6204 with a very uncertain IRSF K_s band magnitude. When this galaxy (WKK 6204) is excluded from the comparison between the IRSF and NTT magnitudes, the mean difference is 0.0125 0.0180 mag and the rms reduces from 0.095 to 0.080. However, the effect on the IRSF Ks-band Fundamental Plane analysis is not very significant -- the rms from the FP fit decreases slightly from 0.0778 to 0.0776 when WKK 6204 is included and excluded, respectively. The source of the large uncertainty on the IRSF K_s band magnitude for this galaxy (WKK 6204) is not very clear since the error/uncertainty in the IRSF J band magnitude is significantly lower than the IRSF K_s band magnitude for this same galaxy (0.07 mag in J and 0.28 mag in K_s band).

These sky gradients are most likely due to a nearby bright galaxy in the field of WKK 6204. Therefore, the large uncertainty on the IRSF magnitude for WKK 6204 could be due to the error associated with the measured sky background in the K_s band as a result of this nearby galaxy.

gmw_00008430.name: NGC 4294
gmw_00008430.overview: NGC 4294 is a spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that features a quiescent nucleus and two faint flocculent arms [arXiv:0909.1816]. It forms a pair with NGC 4299, sharing a common neutral hydrogen envelope [arXiv:1304.1279] and exhibiting a high global star formation rate [arXiv:0909.1816, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4294 as a paired galaxy sharing an H I envelope with NGC 4299, with symmetric radio continuum but highly asymmetric 6 cm polarized emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4294 NGC 4294 is part of a galaxy pair. Together with NGC 4299, it shares a common H i envelope (Chung et al. 2009). However, none of these galaxies shows a significantly perturbed optical disk, reminiscent of a tidal interaction. The radio continuum disk of this highly inclined disk is symmetric at both frequencies. It is considerably more extended in the vertical direction at 6 cm than at 20 cm.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4294 as an interacting Virgo spiral with high star formation, faint flocculent arms, streaming motions, and a derived pattern speed and corotation radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 4294 appears to interact with NGC 4299 with a projected separation of 27 kpc. Both galaxies are similar in morphology, mass, luminosity, gas content, and both exhibit a high global star formation rate e.g., KoopmannKenney2004 . Moreover, Chungetal2007 found that due to its position in the Virgo cluster (0.7 Mpc from M 87) interaction with the intra-cluster medium could be plausible. NGC 4294 hosts many \ regions, a quiescent nucleus, and two rather faint flocculent spiral arms.

sources.summary: NGC 4294 is described as NGC 4299's close, similar-mass pair companion, sharing high global star formation but lacking NGC 4299's outer HII ridge; tidal interaction may drive both galaxies' enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4299 is in an apparent pair with NGC 4294, which is projected 5.8 \ (27 kpc) away, and has a similar line-of-sight velocity. NGC 4294 is similar to NGC 4299 in mass, luminosity, morphology, HI content, and a relatively high global star formation rate. Unlike NGC 4299, there is no clear ridge of HII regions at the outer edge of the H disk. The high rates of star formation in these galaxies might be due a tidal interaction, perhaps enhanced in NGC 4299 due to an ongoing ICM-ISM interaction.

gmw_00008431.name: NGC 4532
gmw_00008431.overview: NGC 4532 is an irregular, peculiar Sm-type galaxy located in the direction of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2006.10442, arXiv:1304.1279]. It exhibits vigorous star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], a shared neutral hydrogen distribution with DDO 137 [arXiv:1304.1279], and a radio-bright polar region likely driven by a galactic wind or starburst outflow [arXiv:2307.09154].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4532 as PS17cke’s nearby irregular star-forming host, adopts a Virgo-region distance, and notes its high surface brightness affects transient detection.

quotes.quote: PS17cke lies in the nearby star-forming host galaxy NGC 4532. It is in a high surface brightness region of the irregular host, making image subtraction difficult. NGC 4532 has a heliocentric velocity recorded in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Databse (NED) of 2061 \ or z = 0.00671 0.00001 , which would correspond to a distance of 27 Mpc for our adopted cosmology. However, it lies in the direction of the Virgo galaxy cluster and is either in the cluster or its recessional velocity is significantly perturbed by Virgo's mass.

NGC 4532 is toward the subcluster B around M49 in the southern regions of Virgo, to which Mei2007 estimate a distance of 16 Mpc. We hence adopt 15 5 Mpc, an equivalent distance modulus of = 30.9 0.8 as the distance to NGC 4532, and will propagate the distance uncertainty through the discussions of the nature of the transient. In the weeks surrounding the discovery of PS17cke, the field was observed a total of 36 times across 7 epochs by PS1. Typical 5 limiting magnitudes of the individual 45 second images in this period were between 21< <22 , but the high surface brightness of NGC 4532 reduces the sensitivity of the difference images at the location of the transient.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4532 as an H I-rich peculiar Sm galaxy with strong star formation, a shared H I distribution, extended radio continuum, and vertical magnetic-field structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4532 NGC 4532 is an H i rich, optically peculiar Sm galaxy with strong H emission indicating a high star formation rate. NGC 4532 shares a common H i distribution with DDO 137 (Koopmann et al. 2008). In addition, a 500 kpc H i extension was discovered by Koopmann et al. (2008) southwest of the galaxy pair. In particular, an H i filament is running in projection across NGC 4532 in east-west direction. The 20 and 6 cm continuum emission is vertically extended.

sources.summary: NGC 4532 has the Virgo sample's highest normalized massive star formation rate, vigorous galaxy-wide H emission, moderate HI richness, association with DDO137 and a large HI cloud, and likely tidal or accretion-driven enhancement.

quotes.quote: The Sm/Im galaxy NGC 4532 has the highest NMSFR in the Virgo sample, with an H equivalent width of 88 . Its H image (Figure enhanced3 ) shows vigorous star formation throughout the galaxy, with no sharp outer boundary in H that would indicate ICM pressure. The galaxy is moderately HI-rich (HI def=-0.35), and both NGC 4532 and the nearby DDO137 are associated with a large extended, bi-lobal, HI cloud (Hoffman et al. 1993). Much of the HI is concentrated around the 2 optical galaxies, but about one-third seems kinematically and spatially distinct from the individual galaxies (Hoffman et al. 1999). The enhanced star formation in NGC 4532 might therefore be caused by tidal forces and/or gas accretion.

sources.summary: The paper attributes NGC 4532 radio-bright emission to a polar, magnetized region produced by a nuclear starburst outflow or galactic wind.

quotes.quote: The radio-bright region in NGC 4532 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_3 ) is extended in the polar direction and shows a strong poloidal magnetic field component visible in polarized emission (Vollmer et al. 2013). Therefore, this region is most probably due to a nuclear starburst outflow.

Based on our knowledge of the Virgo spiral galaxies, we identified the physical causes of radio-bright regions: CR advection transport via gravitational tides in M 51, CR transport via a galactic wind in NGC 4532, CR transport via ram pressure stripping in NGC 4330, and NGC 4522.

gmw_00008432.name: IC 3392
gmw_00008432.overview: IC 3392 is a spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster that exhibits a severely truncated star-forming disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243]. It features a symmetric ring of star formation near this truncation radius and regular ionized-gas rotation, likely resulting from interstellar medium stripping [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243].

sources.summary: IC 3392 is grouped with severely truncated Virgo galaxies that look fairly normal inside the truncation radius and show symmetric rings of star formation, regular ionized-gas rotation, and likely ICM stripping.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 4580, NGC 4405, and IC 3392, have severely truncated H disks with enhanced star formation at the H truncation radius, but appear fairly normal inside the truncation radius. These galaxies were assigned `mixed' Hubble-type classifications of Sc/Sa and Sc/S0 by Binggeli, Sandage, Tammann (1985; hereafter BST) because of their peculiar morphology. The R images (Figure stmix ) show that the stellar disks beyond the edge of the star-forming disk are mostly regular and featureless, except for NGC 4580, which has spiral arms. NGC 4580 and IC 3392 have symmetric rings of star formation near the truncation radius, containing a large fraction of the H emission. NGC 4405 has a knottier and asymmetric HII region distribution, with an arc or partial ring to the southwest.

H major axis spectra of all three galaxies show regular, rising rotation curves for the ionized gas (Rubin, Waterman, Kenney 1999). The truncated H distribution, combined with the regular stellar isophotes and regular gas kinematics, strongly suggest that these galaxies are victims of ICM-ISM stripping.

gmw_00008433.name: NGC 4351
gmw_00008433.overview: NGC 4351 is a Virgo Cluster spiral galaxy located near the cluster center [arXiv:0803.3638, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243]. It exhibits asymmetric H-alpha and neutral hydrogen emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], which may be caused by interstellar medium stripping [arXiv:0803.3638, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4351 has an SKB distance around 20 Mpc, discrepant with Solanes’s estimate, and attributes the mismatch plausibly to HI-disk truncation by ICM-ISM stripping.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

NGC 4351 and NGC 4606 Consider now the cases of NGC 4351, and NGC 4606. Both galaxies belong to Rubin's sample but they were not classified as ``low rotators". Solanes's estimation gives distances of 11.2 1.3 Mpc, and 12.7 0.3 Mpc respectively which again differ from our results of 20.3 _ 1.2 ^ 0.8 Mpc, and 19.9 _ 0.5 ^ 1.0 Mpc. In NGC 4351 the discrepancy, could be due to truncation of the HI disk via ICM-ISM stripping, a very likely effect on this galaxy considering its high relative velocity with respect to the cluster mean ( 1200 ). NGC 4606 has a star formation class ``Truncated/Compact", same as NGC 4064, and NGC 4424, and as these galaxies it has features that suggest gravitational interactions (Cort es 2006), so again its small line-width could be due to the action of environmental effects.

sources.summary: NGC 4351 is described as a cluster-center, high-velocity Virgo galaxy with asymmetric H and HI emission but more regular R isophotes, making it a candidate for ongoing ICM-ISM interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4351, also shown in Figure trunnorm1 , has a strongly asymmetric, off-center, patchy distribution, with a high surface brightness in the circumnuclear region. The HI emission is highly asymmetric in the same direction as the H emission (Warmels 1988). NGC 4351 is one of the galaxies closest to the cluster center, only 1.7 ^ from M87, and it has a high line-of-sight velocity of 2310 km s ^ 1 . The location of the galaxy and the combination of more regular R isophotes and asymmetric H and HI emission make this galaxy a good candidate for an ongoing ICM-ISM interaction.

gmw_00008434.name: UGCA 292
gmw_00008434.overview: UGCA 292 is classified as an extremely low-metallicity dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509677]. Direct abundance measurements of its observed regions yield a low oxygen abundance of approximately 7.32 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509677].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGCA 292 as an extremely low-metallicity dwarf irregular: multiple regions were observed, five regions trace a tight abundance locus, and direct measurements give 12+log(O/H) around 7.32.

quotes.quote: In this sample, UGC 1175 -004+007, UGC 2023 -028+023, UGCA 292 +026+004, and UGC 9992 +007+010 all lie within this region of high uncertainty; however, due to the lack of detailed age information for these particular regions, we do not include age effects in the present analysis. Fortuitously, several regions were observed in all four of these galaxies, and thus the tabulated mean abundances (Table tab:gals ) do not rely heavily on the results from these four extremely low ionization parameter regions.

For example, in Figure fig:r23 , the five regions in UGCA 292 form a tight locus around the 12+log(O/H) = 7.35 iso-metallicity contour while direct abundance calculations for 3 of these regions yield 12+log(O/H) 7.32 0.03.
The regions in this sample range from those with extremely low metallicity (e.g., UGCA 292, 12 + log(O/H) = 7.32) to those that are a modest fraction of solar (e.g., UGC 3647, 12 + log(O/H) = 8.07).

gmw_00008435.name: CGCG 007-025
gmw_00008435.overview: CGCG 007-025 is a dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509677]. Spectroscopic observations of its H II regions reveal that one specific region exhibits a high ionization parameter [arXiv:astro-ph/0509677].

sources.summary: The paper includes CGCG 007-025 spectroscopy from a prior observing run, notes one region has unusually high ionization parameter, and finds its abundance measurements agree with high-quality literature observations.

quotes.quote: We also include observations of CGCG 007-025, which were obtained in 1997 January, during an observing run described in vSHOB98 .

Similar to the regions in vHS97a , the regions in the present sample tend to have low average ionization parameters, U 0.001 -- 0.01; only one region in this sample, CGCG 007-025 -004+000, has an ionization parameter comparable to regions in ITL97,IT98 .

In general, the abundance measurements agree well for those galaxies with high signal--to--noise ratio observations both here and in the literature [ UGC 4483, STKGT94 , CGCG 007-025, KPGLP04,IT04 , and UGCA 292, vZ00a ] .

gmw_00008436.name: PKS 1622 297
gmw_00008436.overview: PKS 1622 297 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.815 [arXiv:astro-ph/0203231, arXiv:astro-ph/0304405]. The galaxy hosts a weak kiloparsec-scale radio jet, a fast parsec-scale jet, and optical emission likely dominated by an accretion disc [arXiv:astro-ph/0304405, arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The paper presents COMPTEL and EGRET observations of the gamma-ray blazar PKS 1622-297, highlighting its 1995 flare, hard MeV spectrum, multiwavelength detections, and flat-spectrum radio-quasar nature.

quotes.quote: We report results of observations and analyses on the \ blazar PKS 1622-297, with emphasis on the COMPTEL data (0.75 - 30 MeV) collected between April 1991 and November 1997. PKS 1622-297 was detected as a source of \ by the EGRET experiment aboard CGRO in 1995 during a \ outburst at energies above 100 MeV lasting for five weeks. In this time period the blazar was significantly ( 5.9 ) detected by COMPTEL at 10-30 MeV. At lower COMPTEL energies the detection is marginal, resulting in a hard MeV spectrum.

The quasar PKS 1622-297 has a redshift of z = 0.815 (Wright Otrupcek, 1990) and is located at (l,b) = (348.82 ,13.32 ). It is a known radio source with a flat radio spectrum (flat-spectrum radio quasar) (K uhr et al., 1981; Steppe et al., 1993), showing polarization of 4.6 at 5 GHz (Impey Tapia, 1990). In the optical band PKS 1622-297 is a weak source with typical visual magnitudes around 20 to 21 mag (e.g. Torres Wroblewski, 1984; Saikia et al., 1987). X-rays of PKS 1622-297 were detected by ROSAT with a flux of (3.2 0.8) 10 ^ 13 erg cm ^ 1 s ^ 1 in the 0.1-2.4 keV energy band (Mattox et al., 1997).

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1622 297 as a COMPTEL quasar with uncertain redshift, difficult photometry, blue optical slope, and optical emission probably dominated by an accretion disc.

quotes.quote: We note that the redshift for PKS 1622 297 ( z=0.815 ) is taken from the Parkes Catalogue (Wright Otrupcek 1990, hereafter PKSCAT90), wherein no reference is given. No other determination of its redshift has been made in the literature, and, while we use this value herein, it should be regarded as potentially suspect.
Poor weather on the first night allowed us to observe only PKS 1622 297 and two standard stars, but the second night had good conditions, allowing us to observe the remaining sources.

For instance, the K band flux of PKS 1622 297 is approximately equal (on a F_ scale) to the 20 GHz flux from Kovalev (1999), while the spectral index in the two regimes is approximately the same. This, however, should not be surprising if we attribute the optical emission to the accretion disc, as it will then not be the same component as the radio emission. In such a picture, the synchrotron emission from the jet has turned over somewhere in the infrared, leaving the accretion disc emission to dominate the optical.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 1622 297 as an observed AGN case where C and F tests disagree: C finds no variability, while the F second-night detection is interpreted as a noisy false positive.

quotes.quote: Another interesting case is PKS 1622 297, observed during two nights with N=13 and N=22 , respectively. Using the C parameter, the AGN DLC resulted non-variable with all the possible combinations of the field stars. The F test, however, classified the DLC as variable for the second night. Although the second night had more points, the seeing was better during the first one. Thus, according to the results of the present work, the C criterion results are more reliable than those of the F test, interpreting the last behaviour as a false positive due to a combination of N 20 and a noisy DLC.

sources.summary: The note describes 1622 297 as a weak kpc radio jet with no Chandra detection, a fast pc-scale jet, and no associated HST features.

quotes.quote: 1622 297 (PKS B1622 297) The kpc radio jet is weak and not detected with Chandra . There is a pc-scale jet at a PA of -70 with maximum apparent speed of 18.6 2013AJ....146..120L . There are no apparent features in the HST image that are associated with radio or X-ray emission.

gmw_00008437.overview: The galaxy 8940-12702 is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.11336]. It has exhibited a changing-look transition from an "off" to an "on" state, with broad Balmer emission lines persisting for approximately two years [arXiv:2008.11336].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 8940-12702 as an off-to-on changing-look AGN followed with Palomar; broad Balmer lines persisted about two years later, disfavoring a SNe IIn origin.

quotes.quote: We observed two of these CL-AGNs (8940-12702 and 9048-1902, both moving from ``off'' to ``on'' states) with Double Spectrograph (DBSP) mounted on the Palomar Hale Telescope (P200) on April 6, 2019. The slit widths we used is 1.5''. The average seeing is 1.5''. The total exposure time for 8940-12702 is 8400s with the average S/N for continuum is about 50. For the 9048-1902, the total exposure time is 9000s with the average S/N for continuum is about 30.

Figure fig:samp (blue lines of left panels) shows the spectra of these two objects. Figure fig:samp (right panels) presents the emission line fitting results to these spectra. The rest frame time difference from MaNGA epoch to P200 epoch is about 2 years. The broad Balmer emission lines are still detected, implying that the Type II supernovae (SNe IIn) scenario for these two objects can be ruled out.

gmw_00008438.overview: The active galactic nucleus 9048-1902 is classified as an off-to-on changing-look active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.11336]. Broad Balmer emission lines were detected in its spectrum two years after its initial observation, which disfavors a Type II supernova origin for its transition [arXiv:2008.11336].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 9048-1902 as an off-to-on changing-look AGN followed with Palomar; broad Balmer lines persisted about two years later, disfavoring a SNe IIn origin.

quotes.quote: We observed two of these CL-AGNs (8940-12702 and 9048-1902, both moving from ``off'' to ``on'' states) with Double Spectrograph (DBSP) mounted on the Palomar Hale Telescope (P200) on April 6, 2019. The slit widths we used is 1.5''. The average seeing is 1.5''. The total exposure time for 8940-12702 is 8400s with the average S/N for continuum is about 50. For the 9048-1902, the total exposure time is 9000s with the average S/N for continuum is about 30.

Figure fig:samp (blue lines of left panels) shows the spectra of these two objects. Figure fig:samp (right panels) presents the emission line fitting results to these spectra. The rest frame time difference from MaNGA epoch to P200 epoch is about 2 years. The broad Balmer emission lines are still detected, implying that the Type II supernovae (SNe IIn) scenario for these two objects can be ruled out.

gmw_00008439.name: NGC 6861
gmw_00008439.overview: NGC 6861 is a fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:2303.01495] hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1001.2567] and a rotating, ring-like molecular gas disk with a central hole [arXiv:1707.08229, arXiv:2206.09043]. It shows signs of recent interactions [arXiv:1001.2567] and features a hot, bifurcated X-ray tail [arXiv:1001.2567].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6861 as a second-brightest SA0 group galaxy with unusually high stellar velocity dispersion, hot X-ray tails, and merger-driven sloshing or stripping signatures.

quotes.quote: Both the brightest group member, elliptical galaxy NGC 6868 ( 20^h09^m54.1^s , 48^ 22'46 4 , B_T=11.66 , NED) and the second brightest group galaxy, SA0 galaxy NGC 6861 ( 20^h07^m19.5^s , 48^ 22'12 8 , B_T = 12.12 , NED), 25 7 to the west, show signs of recent interactions and are likely to merge. Surprisingly, Wegner (2003) measured a central stellar velocity dispersion of the less luminous galaxy (NGC 6861) of 414 , a factor 1.6 greater than the 250 central stellar velocity dispersion of the more luminous elliptical galaxy NGC 6868. We summarize the galaxy properties and estimates of the central black hole masses for both NGC 6868 and NGC 6861 in Table tab:bhmass .

X-ray surface brightness and temperature maps for NGC 6861, 206 kpc to the west of NGC 6868, show a complex morphology also suggestive of a subgroup merger. While spectra of the central 5 kpc of NGC 6861 containing the bright stellar disk is well described by a cool 0.66^ +0.11 _ 0.07 keV thermal plasma model plus a power law with photon index 1.87 for the central active galactic nucleus (AGN), the temperature of gas in the 5-14 kpc `sheath' outside NGC 6861's central region is a factor 2 higher. A hot ( 0.9 - 1.2 keV), bifurcated tail is seen trailing NGC 6861 38 kpc to the west and northwest. These features may suggest that the NGC 6861 subgroup is undergoing ram-pressure or turbulent-viscous stripping during supersonic infall towards NGC 6868. However, comparing the density and temperature morphologies of NGC 6868 and NGC 6861 taken together with simulations from the literature, the more likely explanation is that the hot features in NGC 6861 are the consequence of gas sloshing caused by the ongoing merger of two subgroups, whose respective hot gas halos have not yet been stripped.

sources.summary: ALMA finds NGC 6861 is CO-bright with a rotating dust-coincident molecular disk, a double-horned profile, and a wide central CO hole.

quotes.quote: The bright CO(2 1) emission seen in NGC 1332 , NGC 1380 , NGC 3258 , NGC 3268 , and NGC 6861 directly coincides with optically thick dust disks as seen in the structure maps of the corresponding optical HST images. Inspecting their image cubes reveals clear rotation about the nuclear continuum sources. To visualize their rotational properties, we integrated the flux densities in each channel over the elliptical regions coinciding with the optical dust disks. These CO velocity profiles (Figure  pvd_velchan ) exhibit nearly symmetric double-horned features characteristic of rotating disks with emission ranging between 300 and 500 km s ^ 1 from v_ sys .

We also constructed a position-velocity diagram (PVD; also shown in Figure  pvd_velchan ) for each galaxy by extracting a slice along the major axis as determined from the average disk position angle (measured in kin ). The extraction widths were set to the projected CLEAN beam FWHM along the disk major axis. A central rise in the PVD envelope to high velocity at small radius, which indicates a massive, compact object at the disk center, is unambiguously observed only in NGC 1332 and NGC 3258 , with a marginal detection in NGC 1380 within the central 0 1 . The PVD for NGC 6861 shows a 2 wide hole in CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6861 as a fast-rotating ETG with strong higher-order kinematic patterns, large central h4 values partly linked to dust, and an outer envelope component.

quotes.quote: This is particularly visible for NGC 6861, were the h_ 2n+1 (anti-) correlations with v_ rot become much steeper for the transition from the dynamically hot to the dynamically cold regime, |v_ rot / | > 1 . This is also clearly visible in the kinematic maps (see e.g. NGC 6861 in Fig. fig:FRkinmap ), where spatial regions with the strongest odd order Hermite coefficients trace out the major axis of the galaxy.

We note that NGC 6861 seems to have particularly large values of h_4 within |v_ rot / | 0.5 , compared to other fast rotators. This could be indicative of a larger, dynamically hot component in the galaxy contrasting against a narrower, more flattened component by way of h_4 . However, this is likely also partly due to dust, as the h_4 map of the galaxy (see Fig. fig:FRkinmap ) shows its largest h_4 values in spatial regions associated with patchy dust bands within the galaxy's disk.

Yet, for several of the galaxies in our sample, photometric decompositions require an outer stellar envelope component: This was the case for NGC 1407, 5516 Rusli2013b and NGC 6861 (Mehrgan et al. in prep.). The latter has been noted for being surrounded by an especially rich and dense globular cluster system Escudero2015 .

sources.summary: For NGC 6861, ALMA reveals a rotating ring-like gas disk, but a central CO hole makes its black-hole mass imprecise, with models favoring roughly 1-3e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: NGC 6861 is located at a luminosity distance of 27.3 Mpc in the Telescopium galaxy group, which corresponds to an angular scale of 129.9 pc arcsec ^ 1 when using a redshift of z = 0.00944 from our initial dynamical models. This galaxy is classified as an E/S0 in Hyperleda and as an S0A-(s) in RC3. note that the main body of NGC 6861 does not deviate significantly from an n 2 Sérsic-function profile, although the galaxy has extra central light; we adopt that paper's classification as an extra-light elliptical.

In NGC 6861, the presence of a rotating gas disk with ring-like structure is observed with peak LOS velocities of 500 km s^ 1 , but a central 1 hole in the CO distribution precludes a precise measurement of .

In the case of NGC 6861, we optimize dynamical modeling fits to the ALMA CO data using two different host galaxy models, and find that the results for differ by a factor of 3 due to the lack of dynamical tracers within the innermost 1 , and to the structural differences in the shape of the dust-corrected surface brightness and stellar mass profiles of the host galaxy. Given the large difference between the two results, the value of in NGC 6861 cannot be precisely constrained, although we find that our models suggest a plausible range of = (1-3) 10^9 M_ and a lower limit of 1 10^8 M_ derived by assuming an unlikely amount of central dust extinction.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6861 is a regularly rotating, high-dispersion galaxy whose inferred black-hole mass decreases when a dark-matter halo is included, though within similar confidence intervals.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies exhibit regular rotation: NGC 1374, NGC 4751, NGC 6861 and NGC 7619. In these galaxies, h_3 is anti-correlated with the rotational velocity, matching a widely observed trend (e.g. Bender-94,Krajnovic-08,Krajnovic-11,Fabricius-12 ).
NGC 4751 and NGC 6861 show the fastest rotation and the highest dispersion.

In one galaxy, NGC 6861, \ decreases (but still being slightly above the upper limit of Beifiori2009 ). However, the confidence intervals for models with and without DM are nearly identical for this galaxy.

gmw_00008440.name: host galaxy of AT2019pev
gmw_00008440.overview: The host galaxy of AT2019pev is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:2205.05097]. It is located at a redshift of z = 0.096 [arXiv:2205.05097].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AT2019pev as occurring in a z=0.096 narrow-line Seyfert 1 host galaxy and repeatedly uses that NLSy1 classification when discussing the transient.

quotes.quote: AT2019pev is a nuclear transient in a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy at z=0.096 .
Based on the width of the broad Balmer lines and the [ O III ] / H line ratios, they classified the host galaxy of AT2019pev as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1) galaxy at z = 0.096 .
The host galaxy of AT2019pev was classified as a NLSy1 galaxy Frederick2021 , consistent with its relatively soft X-ray spectra.

gmw_00008441.name: Q2348 011-1
gmw_00008441.overview: Q2348 011-1 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.425 [arXiv:1704.08075]. Its emission has been detected at an impact parameter of 0.7 arcseconds, though its position angle remains poorly constrained [arXiv:1704.08075].

sources.summary: The paper reports emission from Q2348 011-1 at z=2.425, detected in one slit spectrum at 0.7 arcsec impact parameter, with its position angle poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: The remaining two systems are the =2.425 DLA towards Q2348 011 (hereafter Q2348 011-1) and the =1.794 DLA towards Q1313+1441. The emission from Q2348 011-1 is only detected in one spectrum (for PA =0 ) at an impact parameter of b=0.7  arcsec. We are therefore not able to firmly constrain the position angle; however, given the fact that the emission is not detected in the two other slits, the most likely position angle would be 180 \ east of north, since this angle minimizes the overlap between the source and the two other slits.

gmw_00008442.name: DLA towards Q1313+1441
gmw_00008442.overview: The DLA towards Q1313+1441 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:1704.08075]. Its main counterpart has an impact parameter of 1.3 arcsec and may reside within a small group of galaxies [arXiv:1704.08075].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the DLA counterpart toward Q1313+1441 as a likely small group, choosing the more significant PA3 detection as the main counterpart at 1.3 arcsec.

quotes.quote: The emission for Q1313+1441 is detected in two slit positions. For PA2 ( +60 ), we measure an impact parameter of b_ +60 =-0.5 0.2 arcsec, and for PA3 ( 60 ), we measure an impact parameter of b_ 60 =-1.3 0.2 arcsec. The detection in PA2 at smaller impact parameter cannot be the same object as observed in PA3 due to the relative orientation of the slits. We therefore propose that the detection in PA2 could be a neighbouring member of a small group, however, this will require further follow up to confirm.

The detection in PA3 is the most significant detection and in the following we thus quote this as the main counterpart for the DLA towards Q1313+1441. For the main counterpart of this DLA, we infer an impact parameter of b=1.3 0.2  arcsec and a position angle of 120 27 \ east of north.

gmw_00008443.name: DLA 2222--0946
aliases_local: the galaxy counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.354 DLA towards the z=2.926 quasar Q 2222 0946
counterpart of the absorber towards Q 2222--0946
gmw_00008443.overview: DLA 2222--0946 is a compact, isolated high-redshift field galaxy [arXiv:1805.05960] that acts as the counterpart to a damped Lyman-alpha absorber [arXiv:1002.4626, arXiv:2011.04557]. It is a vigorously star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1002.4626] with a smooth morphology [arXiv:1805.05960] and a likely galactic wind [arXiv:1002.4626].

sources.original_name: the galaxy counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.354 DLA towards the z=2.926 quasar Q 2222 0946
sources.summary: The paper reports the DLA counterpart detected in Ly, [OIII], and H emission at 6 kpc, with star formation, compact line emission, inferred disk geometry, and a likely galactic wind.

quotes.quote: In this first paper resulting from the survey, we report on the discovery of the galaxy counterpart of the z_ abs = 2.354 DLA towards the z=2.926 quasar Q 2222 0946.

The galaxy is detected in the Ly , [ OIII ] 4959, 5007 and H emission lines at an impact parameter of about 0.8 arcsec (6 kpc at z_ abs = 2.354 ). Based on the H line, we infer a star-formation rate of 10 M_ yr ^ 1 , which is a lower limit due to the possibility of slit-loss. Compared to the recently determined H luminosity function for z=2.2 galaxies the DLA-galaxy counterpart has a luminosity of L 0.1L^*_ H .

The DLA-galaxy counterpart is a vigorously star-forming galaxy with a SFR of at least 10 M_ yr ^ 1 . The line-of-sight probes the neutral gas associated with this galaxy at an impact parameter of about 6 kpc. The kinematical information inferred from emission and absorption suggests that we observe the galaxy with a line-of-sight that is close to parallel with its rotation axis. From the profile of Ly , we can infer that there most likely is a galactic wind causing the blueshifted part of the line to be absorbed.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Q2222 0946 DLA counterpart as a compact, isolated high-redshift field galaxy with smooth morphology and a direct ALMA molecular-gas upper limit.

quotes.quote: The Q2222 0946 Field: We find this object to be the most compact and isolated within our sample. It does not show any companions or a specific structure. It might therefore be a fully evolved field galaxy, although it lies at high redshift . Since it is also the one with the highest redshift ( z =2.35) it is not as highly resolved as the other ones at lower redshift and we might not be able to resolve individual components.

For one of the absorbing galaxies studied here (Q2222-0946) we have a direct 5-sigma upper limit on the molecular mass from CO(3-2) ALMA non-detection of log( M_ mol / M_ ) < 9.89, which corresponds to < 50 percent of the gas being molecular.

sources.original_name: counterpart of the absorber towards Q 2222--0946
sources.summary: The paper describes the Q 2222--0946 absorber counterpart with X-shooter and HST detections, impact parameters, multiple emission lines, H-based SFR, and SED-based stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The counterpart of the absorber towards Q 2222--0946 was detected by Fynbo2010 as part of the VLT/X-shooter campaign. The authors report a detection of the emission counterpart at an impact parameter of 0.8 arcsec, at a predicted position angle of 40 based on triangulation from the observations with different slit orientations. Using HST data, confirm the detection at an impact parameter of 0.74 arcsec. Using deep VLT/X-shooter data obtained with the slit aligned towards the emission counterpart, Krogager2013 detect emission from Ly , [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , H , and H lines. Further data was obtained from Keck/Osiris Jorgenson2014 and VLT/SINFONI Peroux2012 .

sources.summary: The paper uses DLA 2222--0946 as a benchmark counterpart, noting consistent dust estimates and H flux/continuum detections that agree with the model predictions.

quotes.quote: Two DLAs in our X-shooter campaign have precise measurements of H via deep ( 10 hr) follow-up observations: namely 2222--0946 and 0918+1636-2. By applying the same modelling approach as described above for the H flux, we find an expected flux ranging from 3.1 to 10 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 (1 ) for DLA 2222--0946 in perfect agreement with the 5.7 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 observed Peroux2012, Krogager2013 .

For the DLA 2222--0946, the amount of dust inferred from the SED of the emission counterpart ( A_V=0.08_ 0.07 ^ +0.29 mag) is fully consistent with the extinction derived from the depletion observed in absorption ( A_V=0.10 0.03 mag).

Their detection (Q2222--0946) was also reported as a continuum detection by Krogager2013 , and in the figure the two points are seen both to fall close to the predicted relation; i.e., both the continuum and the H detection follow the predicted relation with only a small difference.

gmw_00008444.name: NGC 4116
gmw_00008444.overview: NGC 4116 is a barred spiral companion galaxy to NGC 4123, classified as an SB(rs)dm type [arXiv:astro-ph/0008204]. It features an undisturbed neutral hydrogen disk with kinematics consistent with circular rotation, showing no clear signs of tidal interaction with its neighbor [arXiv:astro-ph/0008204].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4116 as NGC 4123's fainter companion, with similar systemic velocity, an undisturbed H I disk, and possible but unproven tidal influence.

quotes.quote: NGC 4123 has a companion galaxy, NGC 4116, which lies 14 \ southwest of NGC 4123 and at nearly the same systemic velocity, 1312 . NGC 4116 is of SB(rs)dm type and is fainter than NGC 4123, with magnitude B_T = 12.41 , from the RC3 via NED. At 22.4 Mpc, the separation of the galaxies in the plane of the sky is just 91 kpc. However, neither shows much sign of tidal interaction (see Section sec-vlaobs ) and it is possible that the real separation is much greater.

NGC 4116 has an \ disk just over half the angular size of the \ disk of NGC 4123, and kinematics consistent with circular rotation and a slowly rising rotation curve. Tides from NGC 4116 could be responsible for the arm of NGC 4123, but if this is the explanation, it is curious that the counter-arm pointing away from the companion is the more pronounced; furthermore, the larger NGC 4123 does not appear to have distorted the smaller galaxy.

gmw_00008445.name: MRC0222-224
gmw_00008445.overview: MRC0222-224 is a quasar that exhibits two radio lobes with no apparent core [arXiv:0910.0704]. This active galactic nucleus is consistent with an intrinsically small source viewed at a large angle to its accretion disk axis [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper reports sky-subtraction artifacts in MRC0222-224, selects a Lorentzian plus accretion-disk model, and later interprets its radio lobes as a small source seen at large disk angle.

quotes.quote: MRC0222-224 : This spectrum contains noise spikes from poor sky subtraction which affects most of the H-band spectra. The apparent absorption line in the broad line is an artifact, and is excluded from the emission line fitting.

MRC0222-224 : The model including the Lorentzian line, accretion disk and all narrow lines (Model 1) was selected with higher evidence by ( Evidence ) = 6.4 than the next best model (Model 10), which includes the accretion disk and narrow lines only.

The radio map for MRC0222-224 (see molonglo3 ) shows two radio lobes with no hint of a core (a possible weak core is present between these lobes in a higher resolution MERLIN map, Down et al., in preparation); this quasar is consistent with an intrinsically small source viewed at a large angle to the accretion disk axis. The Balmer decrement for this quasar is estimated as / 23 , more than a factor of two higher than for any of the other sources in this Molonglo sub-sample (Janssens et al., in preparation).

gmw_00008446.name: MRC0327-241
gmw_00008446.overview: MRC0327-241 is a quasar classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. It exhibits a BL Lacertae-like continuum and shows strong evidence for accretion disk emission [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes MRC0327-241 as having a BL-Lac-like continuum and strong evidence for disk emission across several acceptable Bayesian models.

quotes.quote: MRC0327-241 : The continuum slopes with an index of 1.7 , where F_ ^ . This is a BL-Lac type continuum, as these have spectral indices of > 1 (e.g. brown89 ).

MRC0327-241 : The best-fit model includes the Lorentzian line, the accretion disk emission and narrow (Model 3). Four other models are within the ( Evidence ) = 5 bound of the selected model; however, all these models include disk emission, so this quasar has strong evidence for disk emission.

gmw_00008447.name: MRC0413-210
gmw_00008447.overview: MRC0413-210 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its spectral profile is best described by a model that incorporates a Lorentzian broad line, disk emission, and all narrow lines [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper notes an unfitted separate narrow line in MRC0413-210 and favors a Lorentzian broad-line, disk-emission, all-narrow-lines model.

quotes.quote: MRC0413-210 : The narrow line at a wavelength of 7322 (observed 13230 ) is . This line was not fitted in the analysis, as it is well-separated from the region.
MRC0413-210 : Model 1, which includes the Lorentzian broad line, the disk emission and all narrow lines, is preferred, with ( Evidence ) = 6.3 over the next best-fit model.

gmw_00008448.name: MRC0413-296
gmw_00008448.overview: MRC0413-296 is classified as an anomalous quasar [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its spectrum contains narrow lines and exhibits more than two components of broad emission, with strong evidence against the presence of an accretion disk [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper treats MRC0413-296 as an anomalous quasar: its narrow features are problematic, broad emission needs more than two components, and evidence argues against disk emission.

quotes.quote: MRC0413-296 : The apparent narrow features to the right of the broad profile are artifacts of the sky subtraction.

MRC0413-296 : This quasar is a special case, since there are obviously narrow lines present in the spectrum, but these did not constrain properly in the fit as the narrow parameters attempted to fit a broad component of emission. There appears to be more than two components of broad emission in this spectrum. From the evidence, the best model is Model 7, which has the broad Lorentzian, the broad Gaussian and all the narrow lines, although the narrow fitted a third broad component. ( Evidence ) = 10.8 between the best fit, and the next best-fit model which includes a disk, so there is strong evidence against the presence of a disk.

gmw_00008449.name: MRC0430-278
gmw_00008449.overview: MRC0430-278 is classified as a quasar [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its spectrum exhibits a low signal-to-noise ratio and is modeled as a possible accretion disk source [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC0430-278 as a noisy, low-signal-to-noise possible-disk quasar best fit by Lorentzian and Gaussian broad-line components plus narrow lines.

quotes.quote: MRC0430-278 : This spectrum has a large number of noise spikes remaining from the sky subtraction process.

MRC0430-278 : This spectrum has a reasonably low signal-to-noise ratio. The preferred model is Model 7, with the Lorentzian broad line plus the Gaussian broad line and all the narrow lines, though many models fall within the Jeffreys' criterion. The margin in logarithmic evidence of Model 7 over Model 1, which includes the accretion disk emission, is only ( Evidence ) = 2.2 , so this is a ``possible disk'' quasar.

gmw_00008450.name: MRC0437-244
gmw_00008450.overview: MRC0437-244 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. Spectral modeling of the object provides strong evidence for the presence of an accretion disk with an unusually blueshifted disk component [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper finds strong disk evidence for MRC0437-244, notes an unusually blueshifted fitted disk component, and reports stable parameters in Bayesian robustness tests.

quotes.quote: MRC0437-244 : Model 1, with the Lorentzian broad line, accretion disk emission and narrow lines, is preferred over the next best-fit model by ( Evidence ) = 1.4 , though the second best-fit model also includes accretion disk emission. There is strong evidence ( ( Evidence ) = 92.0 ) for the presence of a disk.

The distribution of fitted disk shifts is relatively evenly distributed between -2000 and +2000 , with the exception of MRC0437-244 with a disk shift of -2660 relative to the narrow lines. This distribution does not change significantly if only the reliable shifts are considered.
All parameters were stable in the tests for MRC0437-244.

gmw_00008451.name: MRC0450-221
gmw_00008451.overview: MRC0450-221 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. There is strong evidence for the presence of an accretion disk within the system [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper excludes a flux-calibration artifact in MRC0450-221, finds strong accretion-disk evidence, and uses the source in parameter-stability tests.

quotes.quote: MRC0450-221 : The drop in flux longwards of 1350 is not a real feature, but an artifact introduced during the flux calibration. This part of the spectrum is excluded from the emission line fitting.

MRC0450-221 : The evidence is strong ( ( Evidence ) = 40.1 ) for the presence of an accretion disk, with the selected best-fit model being Model 1, including the accretion disk plus Lorentzian line and the full complement of narrow lines.
The variation in velocity dispersion was similar through all of the parameters tested: all other parameters were shown to be stable in each of the tests for MRC0450-221.

gmw_00008452.name: MRC0549-213
gmw_00008452.overview: MRC0549-213 is a low-signal-to-noise ratio quasar [arXiv:0910.0704]. It is classified as a possible-disk source, although it appears to have weak or absent accretion disk emission [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper classifies MRC0549-213 as a possible-disk, low-signal-to-noise quasar best fit by a single broad Lorentzian, with weak or absent disk emission.

quotes.quote: MRC0549-213 : Most of the models fall within the Jeffreys' criterion of the best-fit model, including all of the models with an accretion disk plus a standard BLR, so this quasar is a ``possible disk'' source. The preferred model for this low signal-to-noise ratio spectrum is Model 6, with one single Lorentzian line only. This is preferred over a disk emission model by ( Evidence ) = 2.3 .

MRC0549-213 has a posterior probability distribution for the sine of the disk angle which extends over the entire range. This source appears to have weak or absent disk emission, as it is well-fit by a Lorentzian line, but is classed as a PD source, as there is no strong evidence against a disk.

gmw_00008453.name: MRC1019-227
gmw_00008453.overview: The active galactic nucleus MRC1019-227 is classified as a weak accretion disk candidate [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its spectrum contains artifacts from poor sky subtraction, which resemble narrow lines and must be excluded from emission line fitting [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper excludes sky-subtraction artifacts in MRC1019-227 and identifies it as a weak disk candidate with a Model 1 fit only slightly favored over Model 7.

quotes.quote: MRC1019-227 : The sky subtraction is poor, leading to artifacts which resemble narrow lines. The apparent narrow line at the wavelength of is a sky line defect, and is excluded from the emission line fitting.

MRC1019-227 : This spectrum contains artifacts from poor sky subtraction which were excluded from the Bayesian fitting process, including the region surrounding narrow . As a result, the sub-set of models with the narrow line only were not constrained, although the models with all narrow lines present could converge, since the narrow parameters were fixed with reference to the other narrow lines. The best-fit model is Model 1, with the broad Lorentzian, the accretion disk emission and all the narrow lines, though this is only favoured over Model 7 (which includes the Lorentzian, the Gaussian and all the narrow lines) by ( Evidence ) = 2.5 , so this source is a ``weak disk'' candidate.

gmw_00008454.name: MRC1114-220
gmw_00008454.overview: MRC1114-220 is a quasar that exhibits strong evidence of an accretion disk [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its radio map reveals a strong radio core and a single-sided jet, suggesting the source lies at a small angle to the line of sight [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper finds strong disk evidence for MRC1114-220 and flags it as a compact steep-spectrum source whose radio core and one-sided jet make its geometry puzzling.

quotes.quote: MRC1114-220 : The selected model is Model 1, with the Lorentzian line plus accretion disk emission and the full complement of narrow lines. There is strong evidence for a disk, since the best-fit model without an accretion disk has lower evidence by ( Evidence ) = 7.9 .

The radio map of MRC1114-220 silva1 reveals a strong radio core and single-sided jet, indicating that this source probably lies at a small angle to the line of sight, so that it is unlikely to be as intrinsically small as it appears. Possible explanations are that the accretion disk and radio jet are misaligned in this source following a merger event, or that the jet is precessing; however, this quasar merits further investigation.

gmw_00008455.name: MRC1217-209
gmw_00008455.overview: MRC1217-209 is a quasar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. Spectral analysis of the object shows weak evidence for a model that includes a Lorentzian line and an accretion disk with a poorly constrained distribution [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC1217-209 as a noisy, poor-seeing spectrum with weak evidence for a Lorentzian plus accretion-disk model and poorly constrained disk angle.

quotes.quote: MRC1217-209 : This spectrum is very noisy due to poor seeing. The structure of is not readily apparent.

MRC1217-209 : Model 5, which includes the Lorentzian line and the accretion disk, is preferred, but the evidence for this model is weak. Most of the models fall within the Jeffreys' criterion of the best-fit model, with an evidence difference between Model 5 and the best-fit model without a disk of ( Evidence ) = 1.4 . The lack of fitted narrow lines is likely to be due to the poor signal-to-noise ratio of the spectrum.
MRC1217-209 has a poorly-constrained distribution, due to the low signal-to-noise ratio of the spectrum.

gmw_00008456.name: MRC1301-251
gmw_00008456.overview: The active galactic nucleus MRC1301-251 exhibits weak broad emission lines compared to its strong narrow lines [arXiv:0910.0704]. Fitting this spectrum requires complex broad emission, which may indicate opposing outflows [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper reports weak broad emission in MRC1301-251, inconclusive preferred models, large plausible broad-component shifts, and a need for complex broad emission.

quotes.quote: MRC1301-251 : Broad emission is weak in this spectrum compared to the strong narrow lines.

MRC1301-251 : Model 1 (the Lorentzian and the disk emission plus narrow lines), Model 7 (the Lorentzian and Gaussian plus narrow lines), and Model 10 (accretion disk emission plus narrow lines), all have probabilities within the Jeffreys' criterion of each other and are clearly preferred above the other models. Model 7 is preferred by a margin of ( Evidence ) = 0.5 over Model 1, so the results are inconclusive. The wavelength shifts of the two broad components relative to the narrow line for Model 7 are 4200 for the Lorentzian line and +4500 for the Gaussian line; these are within a plausible range for opposing outflows. It is clear, however, that more complex broad emission than a single broad line is required to fit this spectrum.

gmw_00008457.name: MRC1349-265
gmw_00008457.overview: MRC1349-265 is a compact steep-spectrum source with a deprojected size of less than 100 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0910.0704]. The active galaxy has a measured redshift of 0.924, and its emission is best modeled with two broad components plus narrow lines [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper corrects MRC1349-265's redshift, favors a two-broad-component fit over disk emission, and lists it among compact steep-spectrum sources with small deprojected size.

quotes.quote: The only exception to this was MRC1349-265, whose redshift was given as z = 0.934 in molonglo4 , but for which z = 0.924 was a solid measurement, even in the absence of narrow ; a change of this size is likely to result from a typographical error in the original paper.

MRC1349-265 :The preferred fits are those with two broad components plus the full set of narrow lines. Of these, Model 7 with the Lorentzian line plus Gaussian line and narrow lines is preferred, but only with weak to moderate evidence ( ( Evidence ) = 2.5 ) over Model 1, which includes emission from an accretion disk in addition to the Lorentzian line and narrow lines.
Four of the CSS sources, MRC0222-224, MRC1114-220, MRC1349-265 and MRC1359-281, have deprojected sizes of less than 100 kpc.

gmw_00008458.name: MRC1355-215
gmw_00008458.overview: MRC1355-215 is a quasar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.0704]. Its spectrum is characterized by narrow emission lines and two distinct components of broad emission [arXiv:0910.0704].

sources.summary: The paper treats MRC1355-215 as affected by sky-subtraction artifacts and strongly favors two broad emission components over an accretion-disk alternative.

quotes.quote: MRC1355-215 : The apparent narrow absorption features are artifacts from imperfect sky subtraction.

MRC1355-215 : The preferred models for this spectrum are overwhelmingly those with two components of broad emission and with all the narrow lines. Of these, Model 7, which includes the Lorentzian line and the Gaussian line in addition to the narrow lines, is weakly preferred to Model 1, with the Lorentzian line and the accretion disk emission as well as the narrow lines, by ( Evidence ) = 1.61 .

gmw_00008459.name: G
gmw_00008459.overview: The galaxy G is a massive elliptical cD galaxy located at a redshift of 0.36 [arXiv:1405.1529, arXiv:astro-ph/0308397]. It acts as the primary gravitational lens for the double quasar system Q0957+561 and resides at the center of a rich galaxy cluster [arXiv:1405.1529, arXiv:astro-ph/0010232].

sources.summary: The paper describes G as the lensing elliptical galaxy in Q0957+561, uses its HST-derived de Vaucouleurs profile in the model, and finds its extracted spectrum too noisy.

quotes.quote: Q0957+561 (our science target) consists of two quasar images, A and B, separated by 6 1 with identical redshifts ( z_ Q = 1.41), and a lensing elliptical galaxy ( z_ L = 0.36) placed between A and B walsh79,young80 . The G (lensing) galaxy is only 1 \ apart from B. Accurate positions of the B image and G relative to the A image, and the optical structure of G, i.e., its de Vaucouleurs profile, are known from Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations bernstein97,keeton98,kochanek13 .

The positions of the B image and the lensing galaxy (G) relative to the A image, and the de Vaucouleurs profile of G are set to accurate values from HST observations bernstein97,keeton98,kochanek13 . Therefore, each monochromatic frame of q0957 is initially modelled by a ten-parameter distribution of light.
The galaxy spectrum is very noisy and is not shown in Fig. Fig11 . However, averaging over independent intervals of 400 , red-arm fluxes of the galaxy are comparable to quasar fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper treats Q0957+561 as a special CASTLES lens whose cluster environment affects image separation, mass-to-light placement, and modeling with both two-double and single-double configurations.

quotes.quote: Q0957+561 is a special case, as there is a doubly imaged galaxy component in addition to the famous double quasar. The lensing galaxy is part of a cluster that contributes significantly to the large image separation ga92 . Consequently a position below the general trend in a mass-to-light analysis is expected. This lens is an excellent example for the consequences of possibly yet unknown image systems. The considerations following in Section sec:svs for are carried out for two different image configurations: on one hand the 2 double image system shown in Figure fig1 and on the other hand a single double system.

For each lens an ensemble of 100 mass maps with 21 21 pixels each has been computed, from which the mass-profile and therewith the formal velocity dispersion is derived with an 90 uncertainty, as one can see e.g. for the lens Q0957+561 in Figure fig1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the massive cD lensing galaxy near image B and models its extended light so that galaxy contamination can be subtracted from the quasar photometry.

quotes.quote: A massive, elliptical cD galaxy (named G1) at z=0.36 , located only 1'' from the center of the B image, seems to be the principal lensing object. The closely juxtaposed quasar images and the extended brightness profile of the lens galaxy make accurate photometry a challenge. During the 1980s and mid-1990s, standard aperture photometry was performed without any corrections for the light contamination between the quasar components (crosstalk) or from the lens galaxy, see e.g. \ Schild86, Schild90, Kundic95 .
To correct for the galaxy's light contamination, we decided to subtract from each frame a synthetic model of G1.

G1 is an extended object whose profile is much broader and totally different from that of the stars. For this reason it is important to calibrate a resolved object like G1 with the ``total'' light from the comparison point sources, here taken to be the flux in apertures of radius 12'' .

sources.summary: The paper tests whether the halo of lensing galaxy 0957+561 is made of MACHOs, using Q0957+561 light curves to constrain halo compact-object masses.

quotes.quote: If the halo of the lensing galaxy 0957+561 is made of massive compact objects (MACHOs), they must affect the lightcurves of the quasar images Q0957+561 A and B differently. We search for this microlensing effect in the double quasar by comparing monitoring data for the two images A and B -- obtained with the 3.5m Apache Point Observatory from 1995 to 1998 -- with intensive numerical simulations. This way we test whether the halo of the lensing galaxy can be made of MACHOs of various masses. We can exclude a halo entirely made out of MACHOs with masses between 10^ 6 M_ and 10^ 2 M_ for quasar sizes of less than 3 10^ 14 h_ 60 ^ 1/2 cm, hereby extending previous limits upwards by one order of magnitude.
The lensing galaxy at z_ G = 0.36 is the central galaxy of a rich cluster, whose weak lensing effects on background galaxies have been seen (Barkana et al. 1999).

sources.summary: The paper models G1 as the lensing galaxy contaminating QSO 0957+561 photometry, subtracting its convolved brightness profile and fitting its brightness with the quasar images.

quotes.quote: The reference magnitudes encountered in the literature (e.g. \ Keel82 , Vander89 , and Serra99 ), may differ by as much as 0.4 mag for a single star Photometry of the quasar images The distinctive feature of QSO 0957+561 is the seeing-dependent contamination by the lensing galaxy, denoted G1, particularly of the closely juxtaposed B image. To correct for this influence, we construct and subtract from each frame a galaxy model. Using a composite R band brightness profile derived by Bern97 and the appropriate ellipse parameters, a seeing-free G1 model is synthesised and convolved with the PSF of the actual frame (see Ovaldsen02 for details, especially as regards the magnitude calibration).

Note that the brightness level is adjusted by an amount estimated by a three-component (A, B, G1) simultaneous fitting in the entire data set. Without better options (see, however, Teuber03 ), we use the same galaxy profile in the V band, decreasing the brightness level accordingly.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0957 as the most massive lens, a cD galaxy in a cluster, with two image-pair data sets and a high inferred dark-matter dependence.

quotes.quote: The system Q0957 -- the most massive one -- is particularly interesting in this analysis. It consists of two independent image pairs, allowing its lensing mass to be calculated with two separate data sets. Not only are the lensing mass estimates from the different image pairs consistent, but this galaxy shows a particularly high dependence on dark matter. However, it is noted that Q0957 is a cD galaxy in the centre of a cluster , which means that an additional component clustering over larger scales, such as a 2 eV neutrino , could accommodate the results.

gmw_00008460.name: massive quenching galaxy at z=4.01
gmw_00008460.overview: The massive quenching galaxy at z=4.01 is a quiescent galaxy identified with suppressed star formation [arXiv:1909.10721]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy, also known as SXDS-27434 [arXiv:2307.06994], reveal strong Balmer absorption lines [arXiv:1909.10721] and a Sersic index of 1.23 [arXiv:2307.06994].

sources.summary: The paper reports HAWK-I/GRAAL structural measurements for the z=4.01 quiescent galaxy SXDS-27434, deriving its effective radius, Sersic index, and axis ratio.

quotes.quote: Here we report the size estimate at rest-frame 4200 \ of the galaxy SXDS-27434 at z=4.01 2019ApJ...885L..34T, 2020ApJ...889...93V . This is based on observations with the High Acuity Wide-field K-band Imager HAWK-I, Pirard2004, Kissler-Patig2008 coupled with the Ground Layer Adaptive Optic system GRAAL Paufique2010 (Program ID 0104.B-0213, PI: F. Valentino). The target ( R.A., Dec. = 34.29871, -4.98987 ) was observed with the K_ s filter for 2040 s on source in service mode on October 22nd, 2019.

We model the surface brightness of the target as a single S e rsic profile with galfit. We create a natural PSF by stacking unsaturated stars falling in the HAWK-I footprint, selected in the stellar locus in the FLUX RADIUS-MAG AUTO plane generated with Source Extractor Bertin1996 and visually inspected. The final average seeing is of FWHM =0 34 . The best-fit model and the residuals are shown in Figure fig:hawki . We measure an effective semi-major axis of r_ e =0.74^ +0.20 _ 0.09 kpc and a S e rsic index n= 1.23 ^ +0.65 _ 0.91 with an axis ratio q=0.88^ +0.06 _ 0.26 .

sources.summary: The paper presents the first stellar-velocity-dispersion study of a massive z=4.01 quenching galaxy, identifies Balmer absorption at a secure redshift, and describes it as the most distant known quiescent galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present the first stellar velocity dispersion measurement of a massive quenching galaxy at z=4.01 . The galaxy is first identified as a massive z 4 galaxy with suppressed star formation from photometric redshifts based on deep multi-band data. A follow-up spectroscopic observation with MOSFIRE on Keck revealed strong multiple absorption features, which are identified as Balmer lines, giving a secure redshift of z=4.01 . This is the most distant quiescent galaxy known to date.

gmw_00008461.name: z19863
gmw_00008461.overview: Located in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field, z19863 is an extreme emission line galaxy [arXiv:2403.13285]. Its stellar morphology classifies it as a galaxy merger, whereas its gas emission shows no merger signs and extends toward a region of Lyman continuum photon escape [arXiv:2403.13285].

sources.summary: The paper identifies z19863 as a HUDF EELG in MOSEL, with stellar morphology classed as a merger and gas emission extending toward the LyC-detection position, suggesting localized photon escape.

quotes.quote: The galaxy z19863 (RA=53.1699, DEC=-27.7684) was identified as an EELG by using photometric data from the FourStar Galaxy Evolution survey ; straatman_2016_FourStarGalaxyEvolution . It is part of the Multi-Object Spectroscopy of Emission Line ; tran_2020_MOSELStrongOiii, gupta_2022_MOSELSurveyExtremely survey of galaxies, with aims to analyse the properties of EELGs at 2.5<z<4 . The galaxy lies in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field (HUDF), providing us access to a multitude of ancillary data from both HST and JWST (Figure fig:fresco_imaging ).

The Gini-M20 statistics lotz_2008_EvolutionGalaxyMergers classify z19863 as a merger based on the stellar emission (F182M) but a non-merger based on the gas emission (F210M-F182M). The peak gas emission (F210M-F182M) is coincident with the peak non-ionizing UV emission (F606W), whereas the peak stellar emission (F182M) is 0.18'' SW of the non-ionizing UV emission (F606W). The lack of merger signs in the gas emission could be because hydrodynamical cooling results in faster settling time for the gas compared to stars after a merger event (See Gupta et al., in prep. for more discussion). The gas emission (F210M-F182M) seems to be extended towards the position of the LyC detection, indicating that LyC photons could be escaping from a recently star-forming part of the galaxy.

gmw_00008462.name: NGC 4294/9
gmw_00008462.overview: NGC 4294/9 is an interacting galaxy pair with a projected separation of 5.6 arcminutes [arXiv:1206.6916]. The system exhibits extended neutral hydrogen emission from both galaxies and has a measured HIPASS flux of approximately 44 Jy [arXiv:1206.6916].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4294/9 as an interacting pair with extended H I from both galaxies, giving their projected separation and a HIPASS flux measurement for the system.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1221+11 : NGC 4294/9 is an interacting galaxy pair with extended \ emission from both galaxies (Chung et al. 2009). The projected separation between NGC 4294 ( \ = 363 ) and NGC 4299 ( \ = 227 ) is 5.6 arcmin. Our HIPASS \ measurement of approximately 44 Jy \ for the system (HIPASS J1221+11; Wong et al. 2006) agrees well with the \ values (27 and 18 Jy ) given by Chung et al. from VLA \ data, but is likely an underestimate.

gmw_00008463.name: NGC 4523
gmw_00008463.overview: NGC 4523 is a Magellanic dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 17 Mpc [arXiv:1206.6916]. It has a measured neutral hydrogen mass of 1.4 billion solar masses [arXiv:1206.6916].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4523 as a Magellanic dwarf galaxy, states that its HIPASS value agrees with previous H I measurements, and derives its H I mass.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1233+15 : NGC 4523 is Magellanic dwarf galaxy at a distance of 17 Mpc. Our \ value agrees well with previous \ measurements. We derive an \ mass of 1.4 10^9 .

gmw_00008464.name: NGC 4633/4
gmw_00008464.overview: NGC 4633/4 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of the galaxies NGC 4633 and NGC 4634 [arXiv:1206.6916]. The pair has been detected in H I emission and has a projected separation of 3.8 arcminutes [arXiv:1206.6916].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4633/4 as an interacting pair detected in H I emission, gives the pair separation, and notes HIPASS underestimates blue-shifted emission from NGC 4634.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1242+14 : NGC 4633/4 is an interacting galaxy pair detected with the WSRT in \ emission over a velocity range from --50 to +410 \ by Oosterloo Shostak (1993). The projected separation between NGC 4634 ( \ = 115 ) and NGC 4633 ( \ = 297 ) is 3.8 arcmin. Our HIPASS \ measurement of 12 Jy \ is slightly lower than the sum of the \ values (7.4 and 5.8 Jy ) given by Oosterloo Shostak. Our underestimate is due to Galactic \ emission at velocities below +140 \ affecting our ability to estimate the blue-shifted \ emission from NGC 4634.

gmw_00008465.name: SDSSJ1100 + 5329
gmw_00008465.overview: SDSSJ1100 + 5329 is a strong gravitational lens system featuring a large circular arc [arXiv:1512.00462, arXiv:0904.1454] that is modeled with a bulge and disk mass distribution [arXiv:0904.1454]. The lens galaxy is in a close encounter with a companion galaxy to the northeast, which displays an extending tidal arm [arXiv:1512.00462].

sources.summary: SDSS J1100+5329 is a cusp-quad lens with one of SLACS's largest Einstein radii, a close companion, and a tidal arm that may contaminate the arc light.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1100+5329 is a cusp quad configuration lens with a large 180 ^ arc in which several distinct features can be found. The arc is about 1.8 ^ from the centre of the lens, which corresponds to 8 kpc at its redshift. This is certainly an extreme value, being one of the largest Einstein radii in SLACS auger09 . We masked out several nearby, small objects.
The lens is in a close encounter with another galaxy just 3.9 ^ towards the North-East. A tidal arm extends from the companion in a way that might contribute light to the arc.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ1100 + 5329 as a disk-plus-bulge strong lens with a broken large circular arc, using it to motivate catastrophe modeling.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ1100 + 5329 presents a large circular arc relatively far from the lens ( 1.5 ''), broken at its centre. We were able to model this system with two SIE profiles of different ellipticities but with the same orientations, representing a mass distribution comprising both a bulge and a disk. While a detailed modeling will be presented elsewhere, we take a cue from SDSSJ1100 + 5329 and study in sec:simplemodels the catastrophe points present behind disk-plus-bulge lenses.

gmw_00008466.name: SDSSJ0808 + 4706
gmw_00008466.overview: SDSSJ0808 + 4706 is a massive elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.2195 [arXiv:0904.1454]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens, with a nearby satellite galaxy distorting its Einstein ring [arXiv:0904.1454].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ0808 + 4706 as a massive elliptical lens with a nearby satellite distorting its Einstein ring, modeling the system to reproduce its arcs and caustic structure.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ0808 + 4706 is a massive elliptical galaxy with a large satellite galaxy lying just outside the Einstein ring and distorting its shape.
Model We model a system comparable to SDSSJ0808 + 4706, a main galaxy with a small satellite, by two NIE profiles (with very small cores).

The redshifts of the lens and source planes were set at =0.2195 and = 1.0215 (to match those of SDSSJ0808 + 4706). We arbitrarily chose the velocity dispersion of the satellite to be one third that of the main galaxy, and used a double-peaked source to better reproduce the arcs seen in SDSSJ0808 + 4706.

gmw_00008467.name: SL2SJ1405 + 5502
gmw_00008467.overview: SL2SJ1405 + 5502 is a binary galaxy pair that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:0904.1454]. This binary system produces an asymmetric Einstein Cross image configuration and is modeled to have complex caustic structures [arXiv:0904.1454].

sources.summary: The paper describes SL2SJ1405 + 5502 as a binary strong lens producing an asymmetric Einstein Cross; simple binary models reproduce its images and predict complex caustics, including deltoid features.

quotes.quote: In SL2SJ1405 + 5502 we have an interesting binary system leading to an asymmetric ``Einstein Cross'' quad configuration (Figure fig:real140533 ). Binary systems, as we will see in sec:complexmodels below can develop exotic caustic structures (as discussed by S+E08a ). We are able to reproduce the image configuration fairly well with a simple binary lens model, and present its critical curves and caustics in sec:complexmodels below.

Binary lenses Model Given the asymmetry of the pair of lens galaxies visible in SL2SJ1405 + 5502 ( ssec:sl2s ), we expect the critical curves and caustics to be quite complex. We first make a qualitative model of the lens, using the following mass components: where is in km s ^ 1 , in kpc, in arcseconds and in radians. The redshifts chosen are = 0.6 and = 1.2 , reasonable estimates for the SL2S objects. As fig:140533 shows, the model-predicted image configuration matches the observed images fairly well.

Even with isothermal spheres instead of the ellipsoids used in modelling SL2SJ1405 + 5502 above, the binary system can produce an inner astroid caustic with two ``deltoid'' (three-cusped) caustics 2006MNRAS.366...39S .

gmw_00008468.name: SL2SJ0859 0345
gmw_00008468.overview: SL2SJ0859 0345 is a complex gravitational lens system consisting of four or five lens components situated in a compact group of galaxies [arXiv:0904.1454]. The system features an oval-shaped configuration of arcs, with models indicating an elliptic umbilic catastrophe near a redshift of 2.4 [arXiv:0904.1454].

sources.summary: The paper presents SL2SJ0859 0345 as a compact-group lens with four or five components, oval arcs, and modeled caustics including an elliptic umbilic catastrophe near z=2.4.

quotes.quote: SL2SJ0859 0345 is more complex, consisting of 4 or 5 lens components in a compact group of galaxies Lim++08 . The image configuration is very interesting: an oval-shaped system of arcs, with 6 surface brightness peaks visible in the high resolution (low signal-to-noise) /WFPC2 (Wide-Field and Planetary Camera 2) image. It is in this single-filter image that the fifth and faintest putative lens galaxy is visible; it is not clear whether this is to be considered as a lens galaxy, or a faint central image. In fig:real085914 we show the WFPC2 and CFHTLS images, and in the central panel a version of the latter with the four brightest lens components modelled with (in each filter independently) and subtracted.
Then we illustrated a four-lens component system based on the exotic SL2SJ0859 0345 lens.
For example, our model of SL2SJ0859 0345 shows an elliptic umbilic catastrophe at around z=2.4 .

gmw_00008469.name: NSA 50
gmw_00008469.overview: NSA 50 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits He II emission [arXiv:2108.09307]. It displays Seyfert-like optical emission, although it is unclear whether this is driven by an active galactic nucleus or shocks from a Type IIn supernova [arXiv:2108.09307].

sources.summary: The paper treats NSA 50 as an ambiguous dwarf-galaxy nuclear-activity case, noting He II emission, radio and X-ray alternatives, Seyfert-like diagnostics, and possible supernova-shock contamination.

quotes.quote: NSA 50: This galaxy is a known He 2 emitting galaxy Shirazi2012 , and the high-resolution VLA data taken by could not rule out star formation as the cause of the observed radio emission. We confirm the result from using the FIRST survey radio detections in this work. Additionally, we could not rule out high-mass XRBs as the cause of the observed X-ray emission.

Furthermore, the \ emission seen in the SDSS spectrum is consistent with that of SN2005ip. We do note that the He 2 /H ratio indicates the presence of an AGN, and this galaxy appeared Seyfert-like in the [ O 3 ] /H vs. [ S 2 ] /H and [ O 1 ] /H diagrams. Given the low \ detection, it is unclear whether the optical emission is driven by AGN activity or a very energetic Type IIn SNe driving strong shocks into the circumstellar medium.

gmw_00008470.name: NSA 275961
gmw_00008470.overview: NSA 275961, also known as J1351+4012, is a composite galaxy with a compact, blue central region that suggests active star formation [arXiv:1408.4451, arXiv:2108.09307]. Spectral evidence, emission-line ratios, and archival Chandra data strongly indicate the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.4451, arXiv:2108.09307].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1351+4012/Mrk 462 as a Seyfert-starburst composite candidate with compact blue star formation but spectral evidence for an accreting black hole.
