quotes.quote: J1351+4012 : As discussed above, this object is a good candidate for a Seyfert/starburst composite galaxy based on its emission-line flux ratios. Similar to J0949+3213, it is a UV-excess Markarian galaxy (Mrk 462) with a compact, blue central region that suggests active star formation. However, as noted in 5.1, other spectral evidence points strongly to the presence of an accreting black hole. Its only published spectral classification is that of ``emission spectrum galaxy'' (Petrosian et al. \ 2007).

sources.summary: The paper recovers NSA 275961 as a composite object and finds AGN-like indicators from He II, emission-line diagrams, and archival Chandra data.

quotes.quote: NSA 275961: This galaxy was designated as a composite object by their ID 105 reines2013 . In this work, we recover the designation of composite using the VO87 diagrams, and find that the object appears AGN-like via the He 2 /H ratio and the [ S 2 ] /H vs. [ O 2 ] 7320,7330/H diagram. Additionally, the archival Chandra data indicate that this object is also AGN-like (see Figure fig:expxrbalt ).

gmw_00008471.name: NSA 300542
gmw_00008471.overview: NSA 300542 is a dwarf galaxy that was previously labeled as a supernova candidate [arXiv:2108.09307]. However, line diagnostics reveal that the galaxy is Seyfert-like, exhibiting active galactic nucleus characteristics and strong iron emission [arXiv:2108.09307].

sources.summary: The paper revises NSA 300542 from a supernova-candidate context, finding Seyfert-like line diagnostics and Fe X emission substantially stronger than SN2005ip.

quotes.quote: NSA 300542: This galaxy was labeled as a SNe candidate by via the detection of a P Cygni profile in H (NSAv0 61339 in their work). However, the P Cygni profile was very subtle in this object, as shown in Figure 7 in . In this work, we found that NSA 300542 appears Seyfert-like in the [ O 3 ] /H vs. [ S 2 ] /H and [ O 1 ] /H diagrams as well as the [ S 2 ] /H vs. [ O 2 ] 7320,7330/H diagram. Additionally, the \ emission is about 6 times stronger than SN2005ip.

gmw_00008472.name: NGC 4810
gmw_00008472.overview: NGC 4810 is a dwarf galaxy that has interacted with its neighbor, NGC 4809 [arXiv:2308.01128]. This interaction likely drove gas inflows into NGC 4810, triggering efficient star formation, younger stellar ages, and mixed ionized-gas velocities [arXiv:2308.01128].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4810 as having mixed ionized-gas velocities, younger stellar ages, efficient stellar mass assembly, and gas inflow-driven star formation after interaction with NGC 4809.

quotes.quote: As seen in these maps, the ionized gas displays an obvious northeast-southwest velocity gradient in NGC 4809, while it is approximately oriented along the northwest-southeast direction in NGC 4810. We observe that the ionized gas in NGC 4809 is clearly separated into different velocity ranges, while it appears to be mixed in NGC 4810. Moreover, the southwest regions of NGC 4809 (e.g., knots ID: 48, 49, 50, 53, 56) exhibit similar velocities to those in NGC 4810 (knot 39).

However, the power index in NGC 4810 is 1.87, which is much larger than 1.0 and inconsistent with a constant star formation history. This difference in power index indicates that the stellar mass assembly process in NGC 4810 is more efficient than in NGC 4809.

It is important to note that the stellar ages in NGC 4810 are consistently smaller than those in NGC 4809, with median log(age/yr) values of 7.3 and 7.8, respectively. We suggest a possible scenario in which the interaction first destabilized the molecular gas cloud in NGC 4809, triggering star formation and then enriching the metallicity of ISM by stellar activities (e.g., supernovae, winds) in the past 100 Myr to 30 Myr. Subsequently, the interaction likely transported significant amounts of metal-enriched molecular and ionized gas into NGC 4810, thereby stimulating the more efficient creation of new stars at a shorter timescale of 30 Myr to 10 Myr. Then these stellar activities create and eject metal into the surrounding ISM. The metal-richer ISM in NGC 4810 (shown at the N2S2-based metallicity map in Fig. fig:zoh ), and the more efficient stellar mass assembly process in NGC 4810 (see right panel of Fig. fig:mass_age ) support this scenario.

gmw_00008473.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 67843 has a Prospector redshift estimate of 3.58 [arXiv:2311.05394]. It exhibits signs of possible residual or ongoing star formation, which may explain why its measured rest-frame near-infrared size is method-dependent [arXiv:2311.05394].

sources.summary: The paper flags candidate 67843 as having a lower Prospector redshift than BAGPIPES, possible residual star formation, and method-dependent evidence for a larger F444W size.

quotes.quote: In general, we find that our Prospector- redshift and stellar mass estimates show good agreement with those reported in , with the exception of candidate 67843, which we predict to be at z=3.58 0.14 (as opposed to the BAGPIPES estimation of z=4.19 0.12 ). We additionally note that the median sSFR estimate of this candidate is log(SFR/M _ )=-8.73 0.6 yr ^ 1 , which is 0.33 dex above the log(SFR)-log(M _ ) relation at z=3.6 e.g., Speagle_2014 and thus indicative of possible residual or ongoing star formation.

Additionally, seven out of ten (70 ) candidates in the sample exhibit rest-frame NIR (F444W) sizes smaller than their rest-frame optical sizes (see Figure fig:svs ), with the exception of candidates 67843 and 71124 (65713 does not change). Of the two candidates with larger F444W sizes (67843 and 71124), only one candidate (71124) appears larger in F444W when measured with both GALFIT and imcascade (see Figure fig:galim ). Candidate 67843 appears larger in F444W when measured with imcascade, but does not appear larger when measured with GALFIT . This candidate also exhibits signs of possible residual or ongoing star formation, which may explain this discrepancy.

gmw_00008474.overview: Galaxy 71124 is a massive, quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z=3.434 [arXiv:2311.04867] that exhibits an extremely compact, bulge-dominated morphology [arXiv:2311.04867, arXiv:2311.05394]. It features a post-starburst star formation history [arXiv:2311.04867] and a tidal tail that indicates a merger [arXiv:2311.04867].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy A as a massive quiescent z=3.434 Cosmic Vine member with post-starburst history, a tidal tail, compact size, and bulge-dominated merger morphology.

quotes.quote: Remarkably, the two most massive galaxies in the Cosmic Vine, Galaxy A and Galaxy E (Fig. fig:img ), are found to be quiescent. Galaxy A is located in the densest region of the Cosmic Vine, i.e., Peak A. Being classified as a quiescent galaxy in multiple studies Schreiber2018quiescent,Valentino2023quiescent,Carnall2023sample , it was confirmed at z=3.434 with Keck/MOSFIRE spectroscopy in . With state-of-the-art JWST and HST photometry, as shown in Fig. fig:spec , the Bagpipes SED fitting yields a stellar mass of log (M_*/M_ )=10.82 0.02 and an upper limit of SFR <0.5 M_ yr ^ 1 (95th quantile), confirming its massive and quiescent nature. The inferred SFH suggests a post-starburst picture with a peak of star-formation 350 M_ yr ^ 1 occurred at z 4.5 and being quiescent by z=4 (Fig. fig_SFH ).

Coincidentally, a tidal tail associated with Galaxy A is robustly detected in NIRCam F200W and LW images (Fig. fig_morph ), indicating a merger morphology. Our morphology analysis gives a S ersic index of n 2.7 and a Bulge-to-Total ratio of B/T>0.7 , revealing a bulge-dominated morphology. Moreover, the size of Galaxy A is extremely compact with a effective radius of r_ eff =622 3 pc.

sources.summary: The paper treats candidate 71124 as a redshift-estimate exception, the only larger-F444W case confirmed by both size-fitting methods, and one of three extremely compact massive candidates.

quotes.quote: Our median redshift estimates fall within 1 errors reported by carnall23 for seven out of ten candidates, with the exception of candidates 67843, 82419, and 71124 all candidate IDs listed in this letter correspond with our publicly available photometric catalog .

Additionally, seven out of ten (70 ) candidates in the sample exhibit rest-frame NIR (F444W) sizes smaller than their rest-frame optical sizes (see Figure fig:svs ), with the exception of candidates 67843 and 71124 (65713 does not change). Of the two candidates with larger F444W sizes (67843 and 71124), only one candidate (71124) appears larger in F444W when measured with both GALFIT and imcascade (see Figure fig:galim ).
We find that three of the most massive galaxies in the sample (71124, 82632, 47006) at log(M_ /M_ ) 10.74-10.95 are surprisingly compact, with rest-frame optical sizes of 0.7 kpc.

gmw_00008475.name: GRB 210610B host
gmw_00008475.overview: The GRB 210610B host is a low-mass dwarf galaxy characterized by active star formation and low extinction [arXiv:2403.02312]. It is located near a more massive companion galaxy that exhibits a lower star formation rate [arXiv:2403.02312].

sources.summary: The GRB 210610B host is detected under the burst position and modeled as a low-mass, actively star-forming dwarf galaxy with low extinction, near a more massive companion.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy We observed GRB 210610B location 58 days after the burst using GTC/HiPERCAM and 253 days after with GTC/EMIR. In the HiPERCAM images we detect an underlying object at the GRB position and we consider this object to be the host. We also find a putative companion galaxy towards North-West of the host candidate (see Fig. Fig:FC ) at a distance of 2 7 , which would correspond to a distance of 22 kpc at the redshift of the system. The photometry of the host galaxy is shown in Tab. Tab:phot as well as the values for the companion. We perform a SED analysis for both galaxies assuming both of them are at the GRB redshift.

Host galaxy The SED analysis of GRB 210610B host reveals a galaxy with a low stellar mass, consistent with a dwarf galaxy that is actively forming stars and has low extinction. At the same redshift, the companion is more massive than the GRB host, with a higher stellar mass but lower SFR. The extinction for this galaxy is also higher than the one of the GRB host assuming the same extinction law.

gmw_00008476.name: SDSS J1100+4421
gmw_00008476.overview: SDSS J1100+4421 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.840 [arXiv:1409.1309, arXiv:1707.05416] that is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1409.1309]. It exhibits strong, rapid optical and near-infrared variability driven by synchrotron emission from its relativistic jets [arXiv:1707.05416], which extend into a large 100 kpc radio structure [arXiv:1409.1309].

sources.summary: The paper reports rapid optical brightening of SDSS J1100+4421, classifying it as a peculiar radio-loud NLS1 with strong narrow lines, relativistic jets, and a large radio structure.

quotes.quote: We present our discovery of dramatic variability in SDSS J1100+4421 by the high-cadence transient survey Kiso Supernova Survey (KISS). The source brightened in the optical by at least a factor of three within about half a day. Spectroscopic observations suggest that this object is likely a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1) at z=0.840 , however with unusually strong narrow emission lines. The estimated black hole mass of 10^7 implies bolometric nuclear luminosity close to the Eddington limit. SDSS J1100+4421 is also extremely radio-loud, with a radio loudness parameter of R 4 10^2 - 3 10^3 , which implies the presence of relativistic jets.

The 1.4 GHz radio image of the source shows an extended structure with a linear size of about 100 kpc. If SDSS J1100+4421 is a genuine NLS1, as suggested here, this radio structure would then be the largest ever discovered in this type of active galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper monitors SDSS J110006.07+442144.3/J1100+4421, a z=0.840 radio-loud AGN, finding strong optical-NIR variability, a steady steep SED, and synchrotron emission from a relativistic jet.

quotes.quote: We present monitoring campaign observations at optical and near-infrared (NIR) wavelengths for a radio-loud active galactic nucleus (AGN) at z=0.840 , SDSS J110006.07+442144.3 (hereafter, J1100+4421), which was identified during a flare phase in late February, 2014. The campaigns consist of three intensive observing runs from the discovery to March, 2015, mostly within the scheme of the OISTER collaboration. Optical-NIR light curves and simultaneous spectral energy distributions (SEDs) are obtained. Our measurements show the strongest brightening in March, 2015. We found that the optical-NIR SEDs of J1100+4421 show an almost steady shape despite the large and rapid intranight variability.

Given the lack of absorption lines and the steep power-law spectrum of _ 1.4 , where f_ ^ _ , synchrotron radiation by a relativistic jet with no or small contributions from the host galaxy and the accretion disk seems most plausible as an optical-NIR emission mechanism. The steep optical-NIR spectral shape and the large amplitude of variability are consistent with this object being a low _ peak jet-dominated AGN.

gmw_00008477.name: 3C9
gmw_00008477.overview: 3C9 is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.02 [arXiv:2101.08291, arXiv:2305.05173] that features a 151 kiloparsec radio jet [arXiv:2011.03130] and a ring-like host galaxy disk [arXiv:2101.08291]. This merging system hosts a one-sided conical outflow [arXiv:2106.08337] that is likely driven by star formation [arXiv:2106.08337].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C9 as a luminous quasar host merger with a ring-like disk, an outflow, a star-forming companion, and resemblance to Arp 148.

quotes.quote: 3C9 is a luminous quasar at z = 2.019922 with a prominent blue rest-frame UV continuum. For this source, we identify three distinct regions. ``SE-SW component A" is a region with a ring-like morphology associated with the 3C9 quasar host galaxy. We measure a velocity dispersion from a Gaussian fit to the nebular emission lines of 407.6 12.9 and the kinematics resembling a rotating disk. ``SE component A" is classified as an outflow region with a very high emission line FWHM of 1362.7 60.5 and elevated and ratios relative to the rest of the system.

The 3C9 system best resembles the local galaxy merger system Arp 148 (z=0.036), also known as Mayall's Object. The outflow in this system appears to be emanating from the ring of the galaxy with the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C9 as a radio-jet QSO lensing example, noting its redshift, flux, jet extent, nearby foreground galaxies, and poor prospects for detectable SMBH lensing images.

quotes.quote: As an illustrative example of the lensing that we expect to occur due to a SMBH within a galaxy, we consider a radio-jet QSO that is known to have nearby foreground galaxies. The radio and optical observations of 3C9 are discussed in detail by 1991ApJ...367L...1K . This source lies at a redshift of z_S=2.012 , has total flux of approximately 190 mJy, and its radio jets are approximately 10 arcsec across.
The especially interesting feature of 3C9 is that there are two foreground galaxies near the jets.

These faint images of 3C9 would not be detectable, even with the anticipated sensitivity of the SKA, and are presented merely as examples of how structure could be identified. 3C9, therefore, is not a realistic candidate for future SMBH lensing studies.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 9 as a radio-loud quasar with a 151 kpc jet, weak 5 GHz core, broken-power-law radio emission, and little line-of-sight torus obscuration.

quotes.quote: -3C 9 has a jet of 151 kpc (as noted in Section sec:radio de-projected length, measured lobe to lobe) and a core contribution of less than 1 at 5 GHz. The radio emission in this source is best described as a broken power-law (with an exponential cutoff at submm wavelengths, see Table tab:best_radio_fit ) which is consistent with the presence of multiple features i.e., jet, counter jet, bright hot spots based on 4.9 GHz VLA data, Bridle1994 . The emission from the torus is best described with a combination of clumps with negligible opacity and a homogeneous disk with the highest allowed optical depth (see Table tab:sieb_par ). The filling factor and optical depth of the clumps indicate that there is not much obscuration from the dust clouds along the line of sight. Therefore, a comparison of the gray squares with the black plus signs indicates the correction of the optical-UV emission from the torus obscuration is negligible.

sources.summary: The paper finds a one-sided conical outflow in 3C9, likely star-formation driven because it follows the disk minor axis and arises from a recent star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: We find bi-conical outflows in 3C318, 3C298 and 4C05.84 while we see one-sided conical outflows in 3C9, 4C04.81, 3C268.4 and 4C 09.17.

For 3C9, 4C09.17, and 3C268.4 the outflow can either be driven by star formation, by an isothermal shock, or through radiation pressure by the AGN. The most likely driving source for 3C9 is star formation, given the geometry of the outflow and the fact that it does not extend to the quasar. The outflow propagates along the minor axis of the rotation disk, similar to star formation driven outflow in M82. Furthermore, the wind in 3C9 is emanating from the location where star formation has recently occurred based on nebular emission line ratios and the presence of extended UV emission in a galactic ring vayner19b .

sources.summary: SDSS J002025.22+154054.7 is a radio-loud quasar with radio/X-ray jets; ALMA detects south-east jet knots plus the optical quasar, while X-ray emission mechanisms need multiwavelength interpretation.

quotes.quote: SDSS J002025.22+154054.7 (3C 9) is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of 2.019, with radio and X-ray jets pointing to the north-west and to the south-east of the quasar. The X-ray emission of this object exceeds the radio emission by a factor five. Several mechanisms have been suggested to explain the X-ray emission, namely non-thermal synchrotron, non-thermal inverse Compton and thermal emission fabian03 . Compared to the 4.9 GHz map bridle94 , only the knots in the south-east jet are detected on the ALMA band 3 image labelled as F, G, H, and I in bridle94 , in addition to the central feature matching the optical quasar (feature D). Interestingly, the knot with the highest polarization fraction (G with 30 ) is also the only one that is resolved on the ALMA image.

gmw_00008478.name: 7C 1354+2552
gmw_00008478.overview: 7C 1354+2552 is a luminous, radio-loud quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.006 [arXiv:2101.08291, arXiv:2110.00019]. The host galaxy features an extended star-forming disk [arXiv:2101.08291], perpendicular radio jets [arXiv:2110.00019, arXiv:2305.05173], and a merging companion galaxy located 6 to 7 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2101.08291].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7C 1354+2552 as a luminous quasar host with extended disk emission, rotational kinematics, and a separate merging galaxy several kiloparsecs away.

quotes.quote: 7C 1354+2552 is a luminous quasar at z=2.0064 with a blue UV continuum. For this target, we identify two distinct regions. ``Component A" is the extended emission associated with the quasar host galaxy. The kinematics show a smooth velocity gradient, indicating the presence of a galactic disk. The size, morphology, and kinematics of the disk are similar to that of star-forming galaxies on the more massive end of the star formation main sequence at z 2 Forster18 .

In Table tab:fluxes we provide values integrated over the entire galactic disk. ``E component B " is a region associated with the merging galaxy at a projected separation of 6-7 kpc. The kinematics are consistent with a dispersion dominated galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 7C 1354+2552 as a radio-loud quasar with perpendicular large-scale jets, compact ionized outflow, northeast molecular gas emission, and no detected cold molecular gas outflow.

quotes.quote: 7C 1354+2552 is a radio-loud quasar at z=2.0064. The system contains two jets that are perpendicular to each other. The east-west jet has a length of about 24 kpc, while the north-south jet has a length of 86 kpc. Only the east-west jet is detected in the continuum in our ALMA observations due to limited sensitivity to low-surface brightness emission on scales > 6 .

An ionized outflow is detected on a spatial scale of <1 kpc. In the ALMA band 4 observations, we detect molecular gas emission towards the northeast. The narrow emission line resides near the quasar's systemic redshift. We do not detect any broad emission from a turbulent molecular gas outflow.

sources.summary: SDSS J135706.53+253724.4 is a radio-loud quasar with perpendicular jet/lobe structures; ALMA traces a bent east-west jet and molecular gas possibly linked to a merging galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135706.53+253724.4 (7C 1354+2552), is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of 2.007, with two jets that are perpendicular to each other lonsdale93 . While the north-south direction comprises two isolated lobes (detected only at 1.4 GHz) whose intermediate point does not coincide with the quasar position, the east-west oriented component is a one-sided jet (to the west of the quasar) and shows remarkable resemblance in 1.4, 5, and 146 GHz. As reported in lonsdale93 , the east-west jet is bent (also visible on the ALMA image) and potentially connects with the southern lobe, hinting at a complex nature of the north-south lobes. Molecular gas emission towards the north-east is reported by vayner21a, vayner21b . As there is no evidence for cold outflows at the sensitivities of the observations, the molecular gas emission might be related to a merging galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper places 7C 1354+2552 at the low-radio-luminosity end of the sample, maps its jet, and finds no extended outflow beyond 1 kpc.

quotes.quote: Our observed sources span a wide range of radio luminosities from the initial parent sample, ranging from the low end (7C1354) to the high end (3C9).

The quasar jet still dominates the continuum emission at these wavelengths and for sources 4C05.84, 3C318, 4C09.17, 4C22.44, and 7C 1354+2552 we can produce high-quality continuum maps of the quasar jets.
For 7C 1354+2552, 4C22.44, and 4C57.29, no extended outflows are detected at separations >1 kpc.

gmw_00008479.name: 4C57.29
gmw_00008479.overview: 4C57.29 is a luminous quasar host galaxy at a redshift of z=2.1759 [arXiv:2101.08291]. The system features a broad nuclear outflow, though no extended outflows are detected at distances greater than 1 kiloparsec [arXiv:2106.08337].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C 57.29 as a luminous quasar host with marginally resolved host emission and a second northern region of uncertain association.

quotes.quote: 4C 57.29 is a luminous quasar at z=2.1759 with a blue UV continuum. For this target, we identify two regions. Region ``NE component A" belongs to the host galaxy of the quasar. The relatively high value indicates that this region is consistent with being photoionized by the quasar. The 500.7 nm is the only emission line detected for this region.

The region is marginally resolved, making it hard to measure the kinematic structure. We require a double Gaussian fit to the emission in this region to obtain a good fit, and we measure an FWHM of 474.3 and 502.5 with offsets of 35.0 and -1050.1 relative to the redshift of the quasar. We identify a second region north of the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper detects a broad nuclear outflow in 4C57.29, finds no extended outflow beyond 1 kpc, and considers shock or high-column radiation-pressure driving.

quotes.quote: For 4C09.17, 4C57.29, 3C268.4, 4C04.81, 4C57.29, 4C22.44. and 7C 1354+2552 we detect broad spatially-unresolved asymmetric emission lines.
For 7C 1354+2552, 4C22.44, and 4C57.29, no extended outflows are detected at separations >1 kpc.

The nuclear outflows in 4C04.81, 4C57.29 and 4C22.44 are consistent with either being driven by an adiabatic shock or from radiation pressure in a very optically thick environment with a high column density ( N_ H >10^ 24 cm^ 2 ).

gmw_00008480.name: 4C22.44
gmw_00008480.overview: 4C22.44 is a luminous, radio-loud quasar at redshift 1.5492 with a reddened UV continuum [arXiv:2101.08291, arXiv:2110.00019]. The host galaxy features a marginally resolved disk-like region ionized by star formation [arXiv:2101.08291], an east-west radio jet [arXiv:2110.00019, arXiv:2305.05173], and a compact nuclear outflow [arXiv:2110.00019, arXiv:2106.08337].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C22.44 as a reddened luminous quasar host with one marginally resolved disk-like region ionized by star formation and quasar photoionization.

quotes.quote: 4C22.44 is a luminous quasar at z=1.5492 with a reddened UV continuum. Similar to 3C318, we do not detect any evidence for a merging galaxy for this system. For this target, we identify a single region, ``N, S component A". The kinematics of this region may be consistent with a galactic disk belonging to the quasar host galaxy. We see evidence for a smooth gradient in the radial velocity map, however, the region is marginally resolved.
We measure an emission line FWHM of 434.8 . The region is consistent with being ionized by star formation with some contribution from quasar photoionization.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 22.44 as a radio-loud quasar with an east-west radio jet, compact ionized outflow, and no detected ALMA CO(3-2) emission.

quotes.quote: 4C 22.44 is a radio-loud quasar at z=1.5492. The radio jet in this system extends along the east-west direction with a length of 7 \ and a bright core emission associated with the quasar's optical emission location. With Keck/OSIRIS we detected extended ionized gas in the , , and the emission lines with an extent of 2 kpc. An ionized gas outflow is detected on a spatial scale of <1 kpc. We detect no emission in the CO (3-2) line with ALMA.
We place a limit on the CO (3-2) line luminosity of 0.08 Jy \ for an aperture matching the beam with a line velocity dispersion of 250 , which converts to a molecular gas mass limit of < 1 10^ 9 .

sources.summary: SDSS J170955.01+223655.7 is a radio-loud quasar whose extended radio jets and lobes dominate VLASS emission; ALMA sees the core and a northern source that may be a companion.

quotes.quote: Finally, SDSS J170955.01+223655.7 (4C 22.44) is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of 1.549. Its radio jets extend out to 7 along the east–west direction and the two lobes dominate the emission at 3 GHz as seen in the VLASS Quicklook image lacy20 . In the ALMA band 4 image one can also see a bright core emission at the location of quasar's optical coordinates vayner21a . The detected source to the north of the quasar does not have a counterpart on the SDSS image and could be a close companion or a chance projection.

sources.summary: The paper maps the 4C22.44 quasar jet, finds no extended outflow beyond 1 kpc, and interprets its nuclear outflow as shock or high-column radiation-pressure driven.

quotes.quote: The quasar jet still dominates the continuum emission at these wavelengths and for sources 4C05.84, 3C318, 4C09.17, 4C22.44, and 7C 1354+2552 we can produce high-quality continuum maps of the quasar jets.
For 7C 1354+2552, 4C22.44, and 4C57.29, no extended outflows are detected at separations >1 kpc.

The nuclear outflows in 4C04.81, 4C57.29 and 4C22.44 are consistent with either being driven by an adiabatic shock or from radiation pressure in a very optically thick environment with a high column density ( N_ H >10^ 24 cm^ 2 ).

gmw_00008481.name: 4C04.81
gmw_00008481.overview: 4C04.81 is a reddened, luminous quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.5883 [arXiv:2101.08291]. The system hosts galaxy-wide outflows driven by energy-conserving shocks [arXiv:2106.08337], with gas photoionized primarily by the central quasar [arXiv:2101.08291].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C04.81 as a reddened luminous quasar host where the only identified region is a broad blue- and red-shifted outflow photoionized by the quasar.

quotes.quote: 4C04.81 is a luminous quasar at z=2.5883 with a reddened UV continuum. For this target, we identify a single region, ``E component A outflow". The kinematics show blue and red-shifted broad (FWHM 800 ) emission. The quasar mainly photoionizes the gas. We do not identify any narrow extended emission in this object.

sources.summary: The paper reports connected unresolved and galaxy-wide outflows in 4C04.81, a one-sided conical morphology, and energy-conserving shock consistency.

quotes.quote: Only 4C04.81 and 4C22.44 have their bolometric luminosities computed from BOSS spectra and thus could have larger uncertainties.
In 4C09.17, 4C05.84, 3C268.4, and 4C04.81 we believe the unresolved outflows are connected to the galaxy-wide outflows since they show similar emission line profiles.
The galaxy-scale outflows in 3C318, 3C 298, and 4C04.81 are consistent within the error bars on the ratio between and with an energy conserving shock as the driving mechanism.

gmw_00008482.name: The gravitational lens
gmw_00008482.overview: The gravitational lens is an edge-on S0 lenticular galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.4656 [arXiv:0809.4125]. It features a gravitational arc located 0.42 arcseconds from its center, perpendicular to the galaxy's minor axis [arXiv:0809.4125].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a L* edge-on S0 gravitational lens at z=0.4656, measuring its arc geometry, total mass, and dark matter fraction inside the Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: We analyze a new gravitational lens , serendipitously found in a deep I _ 814 band image of the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS). The lens is a L^* , edge-on S0 galaxy at z_ l =0.4656 . The gravitational arc has a radius of 0''.42 1.74 h^ 1 kpc . We have determined the total mass and the dark matter (DM) fraction within the Einstein radius \ as a function of the lensed source redshift, which is presently unknown.

Both the photometric model and the spectroscopic data (discussed below) confirm that the lensing galaxy is an edge-on S0. The gravitational arc, subtending 150 degree , is located at 0''.42 from the lens centre, perpendicular to the minor axis of the galaxy. A candidate counter-image appears on the opposite side at 0''.65 .

gmw_00008483.name: 500R COSMOS T1 x08
gmw_00008483.overview: 500R COSMOS T1 x08 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 9.0 [arXiv:2112.13779]. It is classified as an infrared starburst galaxy with an extremely high infrared luminosity and an inferred star formation rate of 10,000 solar masses per year [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: The paper keeps 500R COSMOS T1 x08 as a high-z candidate, using VLA localization, optical/NIR counterpart limits, z_ph=9.0, and extreme inferred IR luminosity and SFR.

quotes.quote: 500R COSMOS T1 x08 As mentioned earlier, its ALMA detection has only S/N =2.8 . This is largely because the source is at the very edge of the field. However the separation of ALMA and radio position is only 0 .58, and this lends strong support to the identification and the localization of the source. We adopt the VLA position as its final position, as the ALMA position has much larger uncertainty of 0 .85 (as compared to the uncertainty of 0 .08 of the VLA position).

While the z_ ph estimates cannot be taken as the confirmations, they do lend support that the Hi z Idx(500) criterion works: 500R COSMOS T1 x08 has z_ ph =9.0 ; x31 has z_ ph =8.0 and 7.5 in the scenarios with and without the IRAC photometry, respectively; x10 has a rather chaotic PDF whose first peak is at z_ ph =2.5 , but it still has the second peak at z_ ph =9.5 .

Without exception, all the three high- z candidates have L_ IR >10^ 13 L_ : 500R COSMOS T1 x08 , x31 and x10 have L_ IR =1.0 10^ 14 , 6.3 10^ 13 and 1.3 10^ 14 L_ , respectively. Applying the standard conversion given by Kennicutt1998b , which is SFR_ IR =1.0 10^ 10 L_ IR after adjusting for a Chabrier IMF, the inferred instantaneous SFRs are 10^4 , 6.3 10^3 and 1.3 10^4 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

gmw_00008484.name: 500R COSMOS T1 x24
gmw_00008484.overview: The source 500R COSMOS T1 x24 is an inconclusive high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:2112.13779]. It has no visible counterpart in optical, near-infrared, or S-COSMOS IRAC images, though its ALMA and VLA positions are in agreement [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: The paper treats 500R COSMOS T1 x24 as an inconclusive high-z candidate: its ALMA/VLA positions agree, but optical and near-IR images show no secure counterpart.

quotes.quote: There is no visible I_ 814 source at the ALMA position. Unfortunately, it has no H_ 160 image because it is out of the COSMOS-DASH coverage. This source is invisible in the HSC rizy images.

On the other hand, there seems to be a weak detection in g . In our extraction, it has 28.7 0.4 mag at the exact location. However, due to its low S/N, it is highly likely that this ``detection'' is a false positive. The source is also invisible in the UltraVISTA images and the S-COSMOS IRAC images. We cannot rule out the possibility that it is at high- z .

gmw_00008485.name: 500R COSMOS T1 x31
gmw_00008485.overview: 500R COSMOS T1 x31 is a high-redshift infrared starburst galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 7.5 to 8.0 [arXiv:2112.13779]. It exhibits extreme infrared luminosity and an inferred instantaneous star formation rate of 6,300 solar masses per year [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint H160/Ks counterpart for 500R COSMOS T1 x31, considers IRAC contamination, and finds z_ph=8.0 or 7.5 with extreme IR luminosity.

quotes.quote: 500R COSMOS T1 x31 The optical-to-NIR images of this source are shown in Figure fig_500R_x31 . At the exact ALMA location, there is a faint blob in H_ 160 , which should be the counterpart. By our own photometry, it has H_ 160 = 24.61 0.25 mag. It is invisible in I_ 814 .

While the z_ ph estimates cannot be taken as the confirmations, they do lend support that the Hi z Idx(500) criterion works: 500R COSMOS T1 x08 has z_ ph =9.0 ; x31 has z_ ph =8.0 and 7.5 in the scenarios with and without the IRAC photometry, respectively; x10 has a rather chaotic PDF whose first peak is at z_ ph =2.5 , but it still has the second peak at z_ ph =9.5 .

Without exception, all the three high- z candidates have L_ IR >10^ 13 L_ : 500R COSMOS T1 x08 , x31 and x10 have L_ IR =1.0 10^ 14 , 6.3 10^ 13 and 1.3 10^ 14 L_ , respectively. Applying the standard conversion given by Kennicutt1998b , which is SFR_ IR =1.0 10^ 10 L_ IR after adjusting for a Chabrier IMF, the inferred instantaneous SFRs are 10^4 , 6.3 10^3 and 1.3 10^4 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

gmw_00008486.name: 500R COSMOS T1 x10
gmw_00008486.overview: 500R COSMOS T1 x10 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy [arXiv:2112.13779] that is visible in UltraVISTA J, H, and Ks bands but undetected in optical and H160 imaging [arXiv:2112.13779]. It has a high inferred infrared luminosity exceeding 10^13 solar luminosities [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: The paper keeps 500R COSMOS T1 x10 as a revised high-z candidate, with no I814/H160 detection, UltraVISTA/IRAC visibility, secondary z_ph=9.5, and high inferred IR luminosity.

quotes.quote: 500R COSMOS T1 x10 This is one of the sources whose Hi z Idx have been revised because of the new ALMA identifications, and yet still survives the criterion of Hi z Idx(500) 0.7 . The optical-to-NIR images are shown in Figure fig_500R_x10 . At the exact ALMA location, there is no detection in either I_ 814 or H_ 160 . The 500 m riser counterpart is invisible in all the HSC images. It is visible in the UltraVISTA JHK_s images, especially in K_s and H .

While the z_ ph estimates cannot be taken as the confirmations, they do lend support that the Hi z Idx(500) criterion works: 500R COSMOS T1 x08 has z_ ph =9.0 ; x31 has z_ ph =8.0 and 7.5 in the scenarios with and without the IRAC photometry, respectively; x10 has a rather chaotic PDF whose first peak is at z_ ph =2.5 , but it still has the second peak at z_ ph =9.5 .

Without exception, all the three high- z candidates have L_ IR >10^ 13 L_ : 500R COSMOS T1 x08 , x31 and x10 have L_ IR =1.0 10^ 14 , 6.3 10^ 13 and 1.3 10^ 14 L_ , respectively. Applying the standard conversion given by Kennicutt1998b , which is SFR_ IR =1.0 10^ 10 L_ IR after adjusting for a Chabrier IMF, the inferred instantaneous SFRs are 10^4 , 6.3 10^3 and 1.3 10^4 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

gmw_00008487.name: 500R COSMOS T1 x02
gmw_00008487.overview: The infrared galaxy 500R COSMOS T1 x02 is located at a redshift of less than 4 [arXiv:2112.13779]. Its K_s band image is more extended than the point spread function and elongated in the north-south direction, suggesting the galaxy may consist of two components [arXiv:2112.13779].

sources.summary: The paper reidentifies 500R COSMOS T1 x02 with an ALMA/VLA counterpart, revises it below the high-z criterion, and notes optical detections implying z<4 and possible two-component structure.

quotes.quote: Counterparts of the contaminators 500R COSMOS T1 x02 The counterpart identified by the ALMA data corresponds to a bright VLA source at exactly the same location, which is 6 .13 away from the HerMES centroid. The VLA source was noted in YMHF20, but it was not taken as the right counterpart due to the large positional separation. With the new identification, its Hi z Idx(500) is revised to 0.13 and no longer satisfies Hi z Idx(500) >0.7 .

However, it is clearly detected in the HSC g and bands, which means that it must be at z<4 . It is prominent in the other HSC images as well as in the UltraVISTA images. Its photometry, which is from the team catalogs, is listed in Table 2. The K_s image is significantly more extended than the PSF, and is elongated in the N-S direction. This suggests that it might have two components.

gmw_00008488.name: KKR 17
gmw_00008488.overview: KKR 17 is a gas-rich, low-surface-brightness irregular galaxy whose mass is dominated by neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1411.5469]. The galaxy features a bright H II region with recent star formation, alongside a diffuse stellar region dominated by medium-age stars [arXiv:1411.5469].

sources.summary: The paper presents KKR 17 as a gas-rich low-surface-brightness irregular galaxy dominated by H I, with distinct stellar populations, low metallicity, and recent star formation in its H II region.

quotes.quote: We present new narrow-band (H and [ O 3 ] ) imagings and optical spectrophotometry of H 2 regions for a gas-rich low surface brightness irregular galaxy, KKR 17. The central surface brightness of the galaxy is _0(B) = 24.15 0.03 mag sec ^ 2 . The galaxy was detected by Arecibo Legacy Fast ALFA survey (ALFALFA), and its mass is dominated by neutral hydrogen (HI) gas. In contrast, both the stellar masses of the bright H 2 and diffuse stellar regions are small. In addition, the fit to the spectral energy distribution to each region shows the stellar populations of H 2 and diffuse regions are different.

The bright H 2 region contains a large fraction of O-type stars, revealing the recent strong star formation, whereas the diffuse region is dominated by median age stars, which has a typical age of 600 Myrs. Using the McGaugh's abundance model, we found that the average metallicity of KKR 17 is 12 + (O/H) = 8.0 0.1. The star formation rate of KKR 17 is 0.21 0.04 M _ /yr, which is 1/5 of our Milky Way's.

gmw_00008489.name: MACS0744-064
gmw_00008489.overview: MACS0744-064 is a high-redshift galaxy that has been observed as a Lyman-alpha and Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1901.09001]. It is also identified as a gravitationally lensed source with a securely detected emission line [arXiv:1901.09001].

sources.summary: The paper reports a secure emission-line detection for MACS0744-064, seen on two observing dates with S/N 10.3 in the full-depth stack and no skyline overlap.

quotes.quote: We observed (Figure fig:m0744det ) on 2016 Feb 22, 2016 Feb 23 and 2016 Mar 20 with slit-masks MACS0744 M, MACS0744 M, and MACS0744 ATH4Yband mt M, respectively (see Table tab:obs_conditions ). The emission line is significantly detected on 2016 Feb 23 and 2016 Mar 20, but hardly detected on 2016 Feb 22. While the exposure time was comparable for 2016 Feb 22 and 2016 Feb 23, the seeing was much worse on 2016 Feb 22, explaining the difference in S/N . The seeing and attenuation were both good on 2016 Feb 22 and 2016 Mar 20, the two dates where the emission line is significantly detected.

ID= is confidently detected, with an integrated signal-to-noise ratio of S/N_ int = 10.3 in the full-depth stack. The two negative residuals are also clearly detected in the stack at the correct locations and can also be seen on multiple nights of observation. The line is completely separated from sky emission lines. As a result, this line is considered secure.

gmw_00008490.name: RXJ1347-018
gmw_00008490.overview: RXJ1347-018 is a highly magnified Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 7.161 [arXiv:2309.11548]. Although it is classified as a high-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1901.09001], ALMA observations yielded non-detections for its expected emission lines [arXiv:2309.11548].

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ1347-018 as a lower-confidence emission-line detection: split-half spectra support a real line, but a nearby sky feature could introduce spurious signal.

quotes.quote: ID= is detected at S/N_ int =5.1 . Unlike , we only observed this object during a single night of observation, so we do not have multiple nights to corroborate the detection. To test the robustness of the detection, we split the data in half, re-reduced each half, and re-extracted the spectra to see if the emission line was present in both halves We split the data by using every even dither pair for one half and every odd pair for the other pair. This is opposed to dividing the dataset in half temporally, which could introduce differences in the two halves of the data due to variable weather.

Despite this evidence in support of the line, there are other aspects of the line that are concerning. A sky emission line is present just blueward of the line-peak emission wavelength of 9924 . Part of the 6 spectral bandpass used to calculate the integrated signal-to-noise falls on this sky emission line. While the noise spectrum in principle takes into account the noise from the sky emission line, the sky subtraction is imperfect and could introduce spurious features at this level of S/N .

sources.summary: The paper observes RXJ1347-018 as a highly magnified z=7.161 Lyman-alpha emitter, obtains no line detection, and estimates upper limits from matched channels and an HST-centered aperture.

quotes.quote: We observed three highly magnified ( 4-20 ) Lyman alpha emitting (LAEs) galaxies with ALMA at z_ Ly =6.323 0.003 (MACS0454-1251), 6.846 0.001 (MACS2129-1412), and 7.161 0.001 (RXJ1347-018).

The remaining two galaxies, MACS2129-1412 and RXJ1347-018, yielded non-detections for emission (see Fig fig:contour_figure ). Because their intrinsic line widths are unknown, we estimate their L_ ( 3 ) upper limits by integrating over the same width of channels ( 9 channels, 225 km s ^ 1 ) as was done in MACS0454-1251. In the absence of known systemic redshifts, we use redshifts to predict expected emitting frequency of each object This assumes there is no velocity offset between and emission for these two galaxies . The RMS uncertainty was calculated using the 1 intensity value extracted from a 0. 5 aperture centered to the HST imaging centroid of the target.

gmw_00008491.name: Eagle
gmw_00008491.overview: The Eagle galaxy is a highly star-forming galaxy at a redshift of 0.77 [arXiv:1107.2931]. It is likely undergoing a major merger and is caught in the early stage of a starburst, exhibiting a low metallicity for its size [arXiv:1107.2931].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Eagle as a z=0.77 AEGIS galaxy with disturbed merger morphology, high star formation, blue optical colors, low metallicity, and measured SWIFT kinematics.

quotes.quote: The `Eagle' galaxy at a redshift of 0.77 is studied with the Oxford Short Wavelength Integral Field Spectrograph (SWIFT) and multi-wavelength data from the All-wavelength Extended Groth strip International Survey (AEGIS). It was chosen from AEGIS because of the bright and extended emission in its slit spectrum. Three dimensional kinematic maps of the Eagle reveal a gradient in velocity dispersion which spans 35-75 10 km s ^ 1 and a rotation velocity of 25 5 km s ^ 1 uncorrected for inclination. Hubble Space Telescope images suggest it is close to face-on.

In comparison with galaxies from AEGIS at similar redshifts, the Eagle is extremely bright and blue in the rest-frame optical, highly star-forming, dominated by unobscured star-formation, and has a low metallicity for its size. This is consistent with its selection. The Eagle is likely undergoing a major merger and is caught in the early stage of a star-burst when it has not yet experienced metal enrichment or formed the mass of dust typically found in star-forming galaxies.

Selection The Eagle galaxy, DEEP2 ID 13019195 ( 14:19:39.3, +52:55:48.8, J2000), was selected from the AEGIS Survey for which the DEEP2 Survey provides redshifts and spectra; davi to be observed with the Oxford Short Wavelength Integral Field Spectrograph SWIFT; swift . The Eagle galaxy is so named for its distinctive appearance in Hubble Space Telescope images (Figures fig:SWIFT and fig:DEIMOS ).

gmw_00008492.name: VCC 1183
gmw_00008492.overview: VCC 1183 is a flattened, nucleated dwarf early-type galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1401.7336, arXiv:0812.3272]. The galaxy hosts a bar [arXiv:0812.3272] and a kinematically decoupled core characterized by an inverted rotation and a younger stellar population [arXiv:1401.7336].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 1183 as a Virgo dwarf early-type galaxy with a kinematically decoupled core, inverted rotation, and a younger, more metal-rich central population.

quotes.quote: Box 3000, FI-90014 University of Oulu, Finland ^ 11 Finnish Center for Astronomy with ESO (FINCA), University of Turku, Finland ^ 12 Laboratoire AIM Paris-Saclay, CNRS/INSU, Universit e Paris Diderot, CEA/IRFU/SAp, 91191 Gif-sur-Yvette Cedex, France ^ 13 Kapteyn Astronomical Institute, Postbus 800, 9700 AV Groningen, The Netherlands We present evidence for kinematically decoupled cores (KDCs) in two dwarf early-type (dE) galaxies in the Virgo cluster, VCC 1183 and VCC 1453, studied as part of the SMAKCED stellar absorption-line spectroscopy and imaging survey. These KDCs have radii of 1.8 '' (0.14 kpc) and 4.2 '' (0.33 kpc), respectively. Each of these KDCs is distinct from the main body of its host galaxy in two ways: (1) inverted sense of rotation; and (2) younger (and possibly more metal-rich) stellar population. The observed stellar population differences are probably associated with the KDC, although we cannot rule out the possibility of intrinsic radial gradients in the host galaxy.

The KDC of VCC 1183 is 2.0 0.5 Gyr old and has a metallicity of M/H = -0.3 0.3 , while the main body is 10 Gyr old (this value is outside the grid so we assume the age in the models that is consistent with the 2 _G Gaussian width, _G uncertainty of the measurement) with M/H = -1.1 0.3 .
The stellar populations for VCC 1183 and VCC 1453 are younger and possibly more metal-rich in the KDC than in the main body of the galaxy.

sources.summary: VCC 1183 is a flattened nucleated dE galaxy with a reported bar, some rotation, and intermediate-age, relatively metal-rich stellar populations without obvious radial changes.

quotes.quote: VCC 1183 = IC 3413 (Fig figvcc1183 ). This flattened nucleated dE galaxy harbours a bar reported by . We observe some rotation. Low signal-to-noise ratio of the data did not allow the detailed studies of the stellar populations, however we notice the intermediate age ( 4 Gyr) and relatively high metallicity ( 0.3 dex) without evident changes along the radius.

gmw_00008493.name: VCC 1453
gmw_00008493.overview: VCC 1453 is a dwarf early-type galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1401.7336]. It hosts a kinematically decoupled core with an inverted rotation, a central velocity-dispersion dip, and a younger stellar population than its main body [arXiv:1401.7336].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 1453 as a Virgo dwarf early-type galaxy with a kinematically decoupled core, inverted rotation, central velocity-dispersion dip, and younger central stars.

quotes.quote: Box 3000, FI-90014 University of Oulu, Finland ^ 11 Finnish Center for Astronomy with ESO (FINCA), University of Turku, Finland ^ 12 Laboratoire AIM Paris-Saclay, CNRS/INSU, Universit e Paris Diderot, CEA/IRFU/SAp, 91191 Gif-sur-Yvette Cedex, France ^ 13 Kapteyn Astronomical Institute, Postbus 800, 9700 AV Groningen, The Netherlands We present evidence for kinematically decoupled cores (KDCs) in two dwarf early-type (dE) galaxies in the Virgo cluster, VCC 1183 and VCC 1453, studied as part of the SMAKCED stellar absorption-line spectroscopy and imaging survey. These KDCs have radii of 1.8 '' (0.14 kpc) and 4.2 '' (0.33 kpc), respectively. Each of these KDCs is distinct from the main body of its host galaxy in two ways: (1) inverted sense of rotation; and (2) younger (and possibly more metal-rich) stellar population.
The velocity dispersion profile of VCC 1453 has a dip in the center of the galaxy with respect to the values at larger radii.

The KDC of VCC 1453 is 4.5 1.1 Gyr old, and has a metallicity of M/H = -0.2 0.1 . The main body of the galaxy has an age of 10.3 5.7 Gyr and a metallicity of M/H = -0.6 0.3 . The stellar populations for VCC 1183 and VCC 1453 are younger and possibly more metal-rich in the KDC than in the main body of the galaxy.

gmw_00008494.name: VCC 917
gmw_00008494.overview: VCC 917 is a flattened dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1401.7336]. It features a kinematically decoupled core with a younger, metal-rich stellar population rotating in the opposite direction of the main galactic body [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 917 is a flattened dwarf with little rotation and a counter-rotating kinematically decoupled core tied to a dispersion drop and younger, metal-rich central population.

quotes.quote: VCC 917 = IC 3344 (Fig figvcc0917 ). No substructures were revealed by in VCC 917. This flattened object with a very little rotation was considered as one of the examples of the galaxies supported by the anisotropic velocity dispersions. We see a kinematically decoupled central component with the radius of about 3 arcsec ( 0.25 kpc) rotating in the other sense compared to the main galactic body. It is associated with the drop in the velocity dispersion profile from 40 to 20 km s ^ 1 and younger and much more metal-rich stellar population compared to the periphery.

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 917 as a Virgo dE with a literature KDC, giving its radial velocity and showing that its projected irregular-galaxy neighbor is probably unbound.

quotes.quote: VCC 917 VCC 917 has a radial velocity of 1258.9 0.2 km s ^ 1 (Paper II). This dE is located at 72 kpc ( 15.1' ) from VCC 1001, an irregular galaxy with a radial velocity of 340 km s ^ 1 . The large difference between these two radial velocities suggests that they are not gravitationally bound.

Combined with the KDCs found in VCC 510 by Thomas06 , VCC 917 and VCC 1261 by Chil09 , and in VCC 1475 by Koleva11 , this makes a total of six dEs (defined as early-type galaxies in the luminosity range 19 < M_r < -16 ) with a significant or marginal detection of a KDC in the Virgo cluster.

gmw_00008495.name: VCC 1475
gmw_00008495.overview: VCC 1475 is a bright early-type dwarf galaxy in the Virgo cluster classified as E-S0 [arXiv:1105.4809, arXiv:1401.7336]. The galaxy features slightly discy inner parts and a counter-rotating core [arXiv:1105.4809], and it is located near a likely unbound blue compact dwarf neighbor [arXiv:1401.7336].

sources.summary: NGC 4515 is classified as E-S0, with slightly discy inner parts, a counter-rotating core, and a steep metallicity decline.

quotes.quote: NGC 4515 = VCC 1475 We classify this galaxy as E-S0 ( = 0.2), based on the images and on the slightly discy inner parts. The core, within one arcsec, is counter-rotating by about 2 , with respect the the bulk of the galaxy peaking at V_ max = 16 at = 5.5 arcsec. The presence of the KDC is not reflected in the stellar population. The metallicity steeply decreases from [ Fe/H ] _0 = 0.15 dex to about -0.7 dex at one .

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1475 as a bright Virgo dE with a literature KDC, radial velocity, and a projected blue-compact-dwarf neighbor that is likely unbound.

quotes.quote: VCC 1475 VCC 1475 has a radial velocity of 951 km s ^ 1 . This bright dE M_r=-19.0 , Janz09 is close in projection (85.3 kpc, 17.9' ) to VCC 1427, an Im/blue compact dwarf with a radial velocity of 132 km s ^ 1 . The large difference between the radial velocities of these galaxies suggests that they are not gravitationally bound.

Combined with the KDCs found in VCC 510 by Thomas06 , VCC 917 and VCC 1261 by Chil09 , and in VCC 1475 by Koleva11 , this makes a total of six dEs (defined as early-type galaxies in the luminosity range 19 < M_r < -16 ) with a significant or marginal detection of a KDC in the Virgo cluster.

gmw_00008496.name: CID-413
gmw_00008496.overview: CID-413 is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1512.04551]. The source is characterized by a relatively weak broad emission line, although simulations indicate the emission can also be modeled with stronger components [arXiv:1512.04551].

sources.summary: The paper treats CID-413 as an AGN with a relatively weak broad emission line; simulations allow stronger components, but the ambiguity drives unusually large parameter uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Another \ source, CID-413, has a relatively weak broad \ line, with =31 . Our simulations, however, show that the \ emission can be accounted for with significantly stronger components, reaching 70 . Indeed, this ambiguity regarding the broad component of CID-413 is reflected in the atypically large uncertainties on \ and \ (see tab:fit_pars ). We chose, however, to include this source in the analysis that follows, since even the most extreme realizations present >25 .

gmw_00008497.name: LID-205
gmw_00008497.overview: LID-205 is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1512.04551]. The galaxy exhibits strong, unambiguous emission lines dominated by an AGN ionization field, though its broad emission lines are extremely weak or undetectable [arXiv:1512.04551].

sources.summary: The paper flags LID-205 as a weak broad-line AGN: its broad component can be absent or very weak, but strong emission lines support AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: Two of the \ sources, LID-205 and LID-721, have extremely weak or indeed undetectable broad \ emission lines. Our fitting procedure suggests that the rest-frame equivalent widths of these components are approximately 10-15 . More importantly, a series of (manual) fitting attempts demonstrated that the data can be adequately modeled without any broad \ components. We also verified that these low \ values are not due to measurement-related uncertainties. For LID-205, 90 \ (99 ) of the resampling simulations resulted in <18 \ (30 , respectively).

We stress that the two `` weak'' sources we identified have strong and unambiguous \ emission lines, with flux ratios / 3 , further supporting the identification of the sources as emission line systems dominated by an AGN ionization field .

gmw_00008498.name: LID-721
gmw_00008498.overview: LID-721 is an active galactic nucleus characterized by an ionization field dominated by AGN activity [arXiv:1512.04551]. It is classified as a weak broad-line source because its broad emission lines are extremely weak or entirely undetectable [arXiv:1512.04551].

sources.summary: The paper flags LID-721 as a weak broad-line AGN: its broad component can be absent or very weak, but strong emission lines support AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: Two of the \ sources, LID-205 and LID-721, have extremely weak or indeed undetectable broad \ emission lines. Our fitting procedure suggests that the rest-frame equivalent widths of these components are approximately 10-15 . More importantly, a series of (manual) fitting attempts demonstrated that the data can be adequately modeled without any broad \ components. We also verified that these low \ values are not due to measurement-related uncertainties.
For LID-721, the corresponding quantiles are <20 and 25 , respectively. The best-fit values are lower, by at least a factor of 4, than the median value of \ we find for the rest of the \ sources.

We stress that the two `` weak'' sources we identified have strong and unambiguous \ emission lines, with flux ratios / 3 , further supporting the identification of the sources as emission line systems dominated by an AGN ionization field .

gmw_00008499.overview: 1137+3000 is a wide-angle tail radio source hosted by a bright elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0303554]. Located in a galaxy cluster at a redshift of z=0.96, the radio source exhibits a bent morphology with two lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0303554].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1137+3000 as a WAT radio source in a z=0.96 cluster, with bent FIRST morphology and a bright elliptical host galaxy.

quotes.quote: Here we present optical and NIR observations taken at the position of the WAT radio source, 1137+3000. A combination of imaging and spectroscopy confirms that this source is located in a distant cluster of galaxies with a redshift z=0.96 .
The radio source is detected in the FIRST survey as a three component object, including a core and two lobes. The source is clearly bent with an opening angle between the two lobes of 80^ .

The aperture magnitudes, measured as described above, for the radio source counterpart are 22.90 0.04 , 19.46 0.1 , and 17.78 0.1 , in R , J , and K , respectively. This gives colors of ( R-K ) = 5.12 0.11 , ( J-K ) = 1.68 0.14 , and ( R-J ) = 3.44 0.11 . The absolute magnitude of the host galaxy is M_ V = -22.29 , assuming no evolution, a redshift z=0.96 (see sec:spectra ), and a K correction from Coleman, Wu, Weedman (1980). This is typical of a bright elliptical galaxy, but fainter than a cD.

gmw_00008500.name: TDG 10
gmw_00008500.overview: TDG 10 is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0410014]. Its continuum is reasonably reproduced by an 8 Myr starburst model, though deeper Balmer absorption lines suggest a non-negligible contribution from an older stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0410014].

sources.summary: The paper models TDG 10 as a tidal-dwarf candidate whose continuum is reproduced by an 8 Myr burst, while Balmer absorption favors older stars.

quotes.quote: While the total flux of the continuum of TDG 7+8 is not significantly different from that measured on slit spectra, for TDG 10 and TDG 3+9 we measured fluxes a factor of 2.0 and 1.6 higher than in the slit-spectra taken at positions 10 and 9, respectively.
The spectrum of TDG 10 was prepared in a similar way.

The same 8 Myr model reproduces the continuum of TDG 10 reasonably well (with mass 2.0 10 ^7 M _ ). However this object shows deeper Balmer absorption lines than the model, suggesting a non-negligible contribution by an older stellar population. In fact, a 50 Myr model better reproduces the continuum in the red part of the spectrum and the Balmer absorption lines (although not the continuum at the blue end of the range, nor the H equivalent width).

gmw_00008501.name: SDSS J0753 + 3847
gmw_00008501.overview: SDSS J0753 + 3847 is a well-resolved quasar located at a redshift of z < 0.1 [arXiv:1902.02377]. The galaxy has a companion source, which contaminates its PACS and potentially its SPIRE band flux measurements [arXiv:1902.02377].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J0753+3847 as a quasar whose PACS fluxes are companion-contaminated, notes it is well resolved at z<0.1, and flags possible SPIRE contamination.

quotes.quote: Comparing the aperture photometry before and after companion removal, we find that the extracted flux densities of 10 quasars SDSS J0753 + 3847, J0936 + 5924, and J1356 + 4304 are contaminated in all three PACS bands.
SDSS J0753 + 3847, at z<0.1 , is well-resolved, and its photometry is measured using the extended aperture.

Except for those undetected in the SPIRE bands, the following objects are possibly contaminated: SDSS J0753 + 3847, J1102 + 6459, J1109 + 4233, J1258 + 5239, J1356 + 4259, J1356 + 1026, J1358 + 4741, and J1405 + 4026. The potentially contaminated measurements are marked in Table tab:herschel . However, since the companion sources are always much fainter than the quasar in PACS maps, we believe that the contamination is not significant.

gmw_00008502.name: SDSS J0936 + 5924
gmw_00008502.overview: SDSS J0936 + 5924 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1904.06734] that hosts a quasar [arXiv:1902.02377] and features an inner lens structure [arXiv:1904.06734]. The galaxy has at least one physical companion [arXiv:1902.02377], while a projected foreground galaxy group lies along its line of sight [arXiv:1902.02377, arXiv:1904.06734].

sources.summary: The paper flags SDSS J0936+5924 as a quasar in a complex field, with a physical companion, foreground group projection, uncertain centroid, and possible extended 70 micron emission.

quotes.quote: Comparing the aperture photometry before and after companion removal, we find that the extracted flux densities of 10 quasars SDSS J0753 + 3847, J0936 + 5924, and J1356 + 4304 are contaminated in all three PACS bands.

SDSS J0936 + 5924 resides in a complex region. It has at least one physical companion at z 0.096 , and a group of galaxies at z 0.04 is projected against it. Since the center of the aperture cannot be reliably determined by fitting the source with a Gaussian profile in the companion-subtracted maps, we fix the center to the nominal optical position. Weak, extended emission may be present at 70 . We use a large aperture to enclose all the probable emission, even at the expense of incurring larger noise.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J093625.36+592452.7 as a lenticular galaxy with an apparent close companion that is actually a foreground galaxy, and notes that it exhibits an inner lens.

quotes.quote: Both the merging/disturbed system SDSS J162436.40+334406.7 and the lenticular galaxy SDSS J093625.36+592452.7 have an apparent close companion.
However, the apparent companion of SDSS J093625.36+592452.7 is a foreground galaxy at z=0.04 .
Three lenticular galaxies (SDSS J075940.95+505024.0, J093625.36+592452.7, and J130038.09+545436.8) exhibit an inner lens.

gmw_00008503.name: UGC 8255
gmw_00008503.overview: UGC 8255 is a galaxy that hosted the supernova SN 2009nr [arXiv:1008.4126]. Its structure contains a nucleus and at least six off-center H II regions, which exhibit a radial oxygen-abundance gradient of 0.06 dex/kpc [arXiv:1008.4126].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes UGC 8255, the host of SN 2009nr, through SDSS and GALEX photometry, absolute magnitude and color estimates, stellar-population fits, and a radial oxygen-abundance gradient.

quotes.quote: Host Galaxy Properties Table table:host_properties summarizes the basic parameters of the host galaxy of SN 2009nr, UGC 8255. We derived total magnitudes of UGC 8255 from SDSS in the ugriz bands using Sextractor ref:Bertin_1996 . The total SDSS gr magnitudes were used to infer the absolute magnitude in B , correcting for Galactic extinction ref:Schlegel_1998 , and K-corrections ref:Blanton_2007 , and using the distance modulus derived from the light curve fits (see Table table:fit_results ). The derived absolute magnitude in B and the rest-frame B-V color of UGC 8255 are shown in Table table:host_properties . We used the total magnitudes of UGC 8255 in the SDSS bands and GALEX UV photometry (FUV and NUV), estimated from aperture photometry, to fit stellar population synthesis models (SPS) with the FAST code ref:Kriek_2009 .

Figure fig:host_oh shows the radial metallicity dependence of the SN 2009nr host galaxy, UGC 8255, along the slit. Oxygen abundances of the nucleus and 6 off-center H II regions were estimated using the [ N II ] /H calibration described in . The linear fit to the Oxygen abundance measurements has a gradient of 0.06 dex/kpc, which is consistent with other observations for similar galaxy type ref:Zaritsky_1994 .

gmw_00008504.name: Cetus III
gmw_00008504.overview: Cetus III is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy candidate located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 251 kpc [arXiv:1704.05977]. It is identified as a stellar overdensity with a half-light radius of 90 pc and an absolute magnitude of -2.4 mag [arXiv:1704.05977].

sources.summary: The paper presents Cetus III as a new ultra-faint dwarf-galaxy candidate, a 10.7-sigma stellar overdensity at 251 kpc with M_V about -2.4 and a 90 pc half-light radius.

quotes.quote: This satellite, Cetus III, has been identified as a statistically significant (10.7 ) spatial overdensity of star-like objects, which are selected from a relevant isochrone filter designed for a metal-poor and old stellar population. This stellar system is located at a heliocentric distance of 251 ^ +24 _ 11 kpc with a most likely absolute magnitude of M_V = -2.4 0.6 mag estimated from a Monte Carlo analysis. Cetus III is extended with a half-light radius of r_h = 90^ +42 _ 17 pc, suggesting that this is a faint dwarf satellite in the MW located beyond the detection limit of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey. Further spectroscopic studies are needed to assess the nature of this stellar system.

Based on a maximum likelihood analysis, the half-light radius of Cetus III is estimated to be 90 pc. This is significantly larger than MW globular clusters with the same luminosity of M_V 2.5 mag, thereby suggesting that it is a dwarf galaxy. Deeper imaging of Cetus III with space telescopes will be worthwhile for getting further constraints from its faint RGB/MS members against background faint galaxies.

gmw_00008505.name: Virgo I
gmw_00008505.overview: Virgo I is an extremely faint satellite dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1704.05977]. Depending on the stellar initial mass function used, its total stellar mass is estimated to be between approximately 313 and 625 solar masses [arXiv:1704.05977].

sources.summary: The paper revisits Virgo I with improved HSC band selection, updating its detection significance, absolute magnitude estimates, completeness corrections, and total stellar mass under several IMFs.

quotes.quote: This new satellite already reported in Homma2016 shows the highest signal of S= 12.8 with n =1.87 and S/S_ th =1.90 . In contrast to our previous paper Homma2016 , we have adopted the i band data for the removal of contaminations in addition to the g and band data, so that the final results for the physical parameters of stellar system are slightly changed. We thus list the revised values for Virgo I in Table tab: 2 , although the differences are well within the 1 uncertainty. For the median (mean) value of M_V with completeness correction, we obtain M_V = -0.33^ +0.75 _ 0.87 mag ( M_V = -0.76^ +1.18 _ 0.45 mag) In Homma2016 , we reported only the mean M_V of 0.8 mag. .

When we correct for unobserved faint stars below the limit of i=24.5 , we obtain M_V = -0.70^ +0.55 _ 0.69 mag ( M_V = -1.04^ +0.80 _ 0.38 mag). The total stellar mass of Virgo I is estimated as M_ =323^ +129 _ 108 M_ (Kroupa), 625^ +255 _ 211 M_ (Salpeter), and 313^ +128 _ 101 M_ (Chabrier).

gmw_00008506.name: System R
gmw_00008506.overview: System R is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.7645 [arXiv:2006.10700]. It is a Lyman-alpha-emitting source that is strongly gravitationally lensed into three multiple images [arXiv:2006.10700].

sources.summary: The paper identifies System R as a z=3.7645 Ly-alpha-emitting multiple-image system in northern MS 0451, with three images that improve lens-model constraints in a weakly constrained region.

quotes.quote: We report the identification of two new systems of multiple images at high redshifts. System R ( z=3.7645 , QF = 3) is composed of three multiple images, and is located in the poorly-constrained Northern region of the cluster. System S ( z=4.4514 , QF = 3) is predicted to be quadruply-imaged but only two multiple images could be identified. These sources are Ly- emitters identified thanks to the blind-search carried out directly in the datacube with muselet . These two systems are located within two poorly-constrained regions of the cluster, and are playing a substantial role in the improvement of the accuracy of the model as described in Sect. section5 .

gmw_00008507.name: System S
gmw_00008507.overview: System S is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.4514 [arXiv:2006.10700]. It is a strongly lensed source predicted to be quadruply imaged, with two of its highly magnified images currently identified [arXiv:2006.10700].

sources.summary: The paper identifies System S as a z=4.4514 Ly-alpha lensed source in northern MS 0451, predicted as quadruply imaged with two highly magnified MUSE-detected images.

quotes.quote: We report the identification of two new systems of multiple images at high redshifts. System R ( z=3.7645 , QF = 3) is composed of three multiple images, and is located in the poorly-constrained Northern region of the cluster. System S ( z=4.4514 , QF = 3) is predicted to be quadruply-imaged but only two multiple images could be identified. These sources are Ly- emitters identified thanks to the blind-search carried out directly in the datacube with muselet . These two systems are located within two poorly-constrained regions of the cluster, and are playing a substantial role in the improvement of the accuracy of the model as described in Sect. section5 .

Although spectroscopically confirmed, System \ S is not well reproduced by the model, with an rms of 1.3 . Both Images \ S.1 and S.2 are in the high magnification region of the cluster, close to the radial critical line at the redshift of the source, z = 4.45 , with a measured magnification for S.1 of > 300 .

gmw_00008508.name: System \ 8
gmw_00008508.overview: System 8 is a lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.41 [arXiv:2006.10700]. It is a Lyman-alpha emitter that is gravitationally lensed into five confirmed images and may be physically connected to the fainter System 10 [arXiv:2006.10700].

sources.summary: The paper treats System 8 as a z=4.41 Ly-alpha-emitting lensed galaxy in MACS J2129, confirming five images and noting possible physical connection to fainter System 10.

quotes.quote: We measure a redshift of z=4.41 (QF = 3, Ly- emitter) for Images \ 8.1, 8.2 and 8.3, in agreement with the redshift of 8.1 measured by monna , and confirmed by . The fourth image of System \ 8, Image 8.4, is outside the MUSE field of view. We also confirm the counter-image \ 8.5 identified by . We show in Fig. detail_spec_810 composite colour HST stamps of the five images, narrow-band images, and their spectra extracted from the MUSE datacubes.
This could imply that the two systems highlight two physically connected lensed galaxies at z=4.41 , System \ 8 being more UV bright and detected in the HST images.

gmw_00008509.name: System \ 10
gmw_00008509.overview: System 10 is a lensed, Lyman-alpha-emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 4.41 [arXiv:2006.10700]. It is predicted to be quadruply imaged, and it may be physically connected to another lensed galaxy known as System 8 [arXiv:2006.10700].

sources.summary: The paper reports System 10 as a newly identified z=4.41 Ly-alpha-emitting lensed galaxy in MACS J2129, predicted as quadruply imaged, MUSE-confirmed in two images, and possibly linked to System 8.

quotes.quote: System \ 10 ( z = 4.41 , QF = 3, Ly- emitter) is not identified by . Our model predicts it as quadruply-imaged, although only two multiple images could be confirmed with MUSE. This system seems to be linked to System \ 8, composed of 5 multiple images. Image \ 10.1 appears to be located close to the Ly- emission of Image \ 8.3, although the two emission regions are well separated. This could imply that the two systems highlight two physically connected lensed galaxies at z=4.41 , System \ 8 being more UV bright and detected in the HST images.

gmw_00008510.name: J1708+0035
gmw_00008510.overview: J1708+0035 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.449 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410104]. It exhibits an arcsecond-scale triple structure and a milliarcsecond-scale core-jet, which reveal a large misalignment between its radio axes [arXiv:astro-ph/0410104].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1708+0035 as a z=0.449 galaxy whose arcsecond-scale triple structure and VLBA core-jet reveal a strong radio-axis misalignment.

quotes.quote: As expected, quite a few had been included and one of them --- J1708+0035 --- seemed to be particularly interesting. J1708+0035 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.449 . In the JVAS map jvas2 it appears as a core-dominated aligned triple at P.A. 25 (Fig. JVAS_map ). Its projected linear size is 10 h^ 1 kpc.

Therefore, we observed J1708+0035 with the VLBA at two frequencies (5 GHz and 15 GHz) and, using standard reduction techniques with phase referencing followed by self-calibration, produced the maps shown in Fig. VLBA_maps . At 15 GHz the source is a core-jet whereas at 5 GHz an additional steep-spectrum part of the jet is also visible. The whole milliarcsecond structure points to P.A. 25 confirming the existence of a large misalignment between the arcsecond and milliarcsecond structures, tentatively suggested by the VCS maps.

gmw_00008511.name: Arp 284
gmw_00008511.overview: Arp 284 is an interacting system formed by an off-centre collision between the disc galaxies NGC 7714 and NGC 7715 [arXiv:0908.2619]. This interaction has created a tidal bridge containing H II regions and star-forming clusters [arXiv:1801.03981, arXiv:0908.2619].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 284 as an interacting system with NGC 7714 and NGC 7715, bridge H II regions, gas transfer fueling the primary starburst, and mixed inflow/outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: In the secondary galaxy (NGC 7715, east) we do not detect \ emission in the western side of the edge-on disc which is toward the primary galaxy. This can be seen is shown in Figure fig:appendix:arp284group1 . There are HII regions in the tidal bridge between the galaxies. found an HI bridge in the same position as the optical bridge. The primary galaxy (NGC 7714, west) displays smooth rotation in the star-forming components, which is scattered throughout the tilted disc.

Dynamical modeling by indicates that a significant amount of gas has been transferring from the secondary companion to the the primary galaxy since the first passage, which occurred 170 Myr ago. This gas transfer is fueling the starburst in the core of NGC 7714. It seems that tidal striping of gas and lack of inflows is why the secondary galaxy has quenched star formation.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 284 as an early-stage collision between NGC 7714 and NGC 7715, using HST cluster photometry to trace recent star formation during a >100 Myr interaction.

quotes.quote: In this paper we focus on the early-stage interacting system Arp 284, which at a distance of 38.6 2.7 Mpc ( 1 = 190 pc) according to the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) has nearly 175 detected star forming regions in archival HST images. We use HST colours and the evolutionary synthesis code starburst99 ( sb99 ) to obtain age and reddening estimates for star forming regions. We also use lower-resolution UV data from the Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ; mar05 ) and ground-based H to obtain age estimates for larger regions, and mid-IR colours from the Spitzer Space Telescope Infrared Array Camera (IRAC; wer04,faz04 ) to compare the regions to IR emission clumps found in other interacting systems. These age results are used to aid in the interpretation of the X-ray data of smi05a ,which was obtained using Chandra wei02 . The peculiar morphology of the Arp 284 system results from an off-centre collision between two disc galaxies, with the primary (NGC 7714) having roughly three times the mass of the companion (NGC 7715). The time of closest approach was 100 Myr ago. For a discussion of the interaction history of the system, the reader is directed to the models of str03 . In this section we summarize previous observations of the Arp 284 system that relate to its star formation history.

We have used broadband colours to obtain age estimates for 174 proto-globular cluster candidates in the Arp 284 system. The populations detected in the tidal features of NGC 7714 are generally quite young ( 20 Myr old). However, when the sample is limited by a mass cut, the dominance of young clusters becomes unclear in the NGC 7714 disc and vanishes in the bridge. Due to the small number of clusters above the mass cut, this latter result is highly uncertain. Deeper imaging is required to better define the cluster mass function and push down the completeness limit, particularly in the bridge, which has higher photometric uncertainties and less F380W coverage than the rest of the system. No substantial differences are found between the populations within NGC 7714. The clusters in the nucleus do not generally differ in age from those in the tidal structures. It therefore appears that the early-stage dissolution processes, if present, operate via internal processes, rather than local environmental effects, but we cannot rule out the possibility of weaker dissolution in the bridge. Using larger apertures to study region complexes, we find in several cases that the region as a whole appears to be older than the clusters detected inside it. This suggests an older, redder population that is unresolved, possibly the remnants of star clusters that have already dissolved or which were not detected as discrete sources because of evolutionary fading. The large aperture results suggest that star formation has been ongoing in regions B and D, located in the northern tail, for a dynamical time-scale, like that in the nucleus. In both cases, the star formation history, whether continuous or in discrete bursts, is unknown. We also do not know if regions B and D are gravitationally bound, or simply regions where successive waves or infall events have triggered star formation. Region C is similar, but we have insufficient data to comment on its nature. If in the future these regions are found to be bound, they could be interpreted as nearby analogs to the high redshift disc clumps studied by elm09 . The Arp 284 interaction has been in progress for over 100 Myr, yet we find little evidence for a significant cluster population older than 20 Myr.

gmw_00008512.name: W1814 + 3412
gmw_00008512.overview: W1814 + 3412 is a hot dust-obscured galaxy that hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1406.2506]. The galaxy exhibits a minimum dust temperature of 53 K and has a rest-frame dust extinction value of A_V = 48 [arXiv:1406.2506].
