sources.summary: The paper uses W1814 + 3412 as an empirical Hot DOG SED template, assigning 53 K coolest dust, high IR luminosity, significant extinction, and Compton-thick AGN-level gas columns.

quotes.quote: The W1814 + 3412 template shown is entirely empirical, and assumes a single-temperature dust spectrum representing the minimum dust temperature present (53 5 K), with an emissivity index of = 1.5 at longer wavelengths, smoothly interpolated to a power-law spectrum instead of a Wien law at shorter wavelengths, with an opacity factor imposed at the shortest mid-IR wavelengths, to match the WISE data, corresponding to a finite total luminosity L_ 8-1000 m = 4.6 10 ^ 13 L _ for W1814 + 3412.

Converting to a V band extinction implies an extra dust extinction A_ V 6.8 mag. eisenhardt12 found significant obscuration in the SED of W1814 + 3412 with a dust extinction value of A_ V = 48 4 in the rest-frame from optical SED fitting.

This is consistent with stern14 , who observed three Hot DOGs, including W1814 + 3412 in common with this paper, with NuSTAR and XMM-Newton, and found that the three targets have gas column densities N_ H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , which implies the targets are Compton-thick AGNs.

gmw_00008513.name: SDSS J140909.60+275325.8
gmw_00008513.overview: SDSS J140909.60+275325.8 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.459 to 0.469 [arXiv:2204.12771, arXiv:2206.10710]. Its spectrum displays narrow emission lines that indicate a high star formation rate [arXiv:2206.10710].

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSS J140909.60+275325.8 (G2) as an alternative host at z=0.459, but rejects it because its chance-coincidence probability and physical/normalized offsets are too large.

quotes.quote: In order to avoid this apparent issue, Ref. invokes another galaxy, SDSS J140909.60+275325.8 (hereafter G2) at redshift z=0.459 , in the GRB field of view as the GRB host galaxy. However, the chance coincidence probability P_ cc, G2 =0.15 between GRB 211211A and G2 is much higher than that between GRB 211211A and SDSS J140910.47+275320.8 (G1), the host galaxy candidate adopted in our paper and all other papers on GRB 211211A. Furthermore, if G2 is the host galaxy, the location of GRB 211211A has a physical offset of 61.81 kpc and a normalized offset of 36.78 from the host. These numbers are inconsistent with long GRBs and even short GRBs. At such a large offset, it is essentially impossible to have a massive star and a dusty environment to support the suggested dust echo model.

sources.summary: The paper treats galaxy B as a z=0.469 alternative environment for GRB 211211A, with the burst possibly 62 kpc away and its SDSS spectrum showing narrow star-forming emission lines.

quotes.quote: Indeed galaxy C , D have photometric redshift of about 0.5 and galaxy B has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.469 . The chance-coincidence probability to find such a galaxy within 10'' from a random position is of order unity. Therefore, this is not an evidence for an association of GRB 211211A with a galaxy group at z 0.5 , but only a consistency test for this hypothesis. If indeed GRB 211211A resides in the halo of galaxy B at z=0.469 , than it is located at projected distance of about 62 kpc from this galaxy -- A reasonable distance between a dwarf galaxy and its main halo. Another hint that shows the consistency of this solution is that the SDSS spectrum of galaxy B display narrow emission lines indicating of high star formation rate in this environment.

gmw_00008514.name: DOG 12
gmw_00008514.overview: DOG 12 is a galaxy that hosts a strong central point source [arXiv:0904.1231]. While a single Sersic fit suggests an elliptical galaxy structure, accounting for this central point source reveals an extended component with a disk-like profile [arXiv:0904.1231].

sources.summary: The paper finds DOG 12 hosts a strong central point source; adding a PSF changes the inferred host from elliptical-like to a disk-like extended component.

quotes.quote: DOG 12 also appears to host a strong central point source. For DOG 12 the point source has a PSF/Sersic flux ratio of 80 . The single Sersic fit for DOG 12 had a Sersic index of 3.47, consistent with an elliptical galaxy. However, after including a PSF in the fit, GALFIT finds that the extended component has a Sersic index of 0.55, more consistent with a disk. This is the only galaxy in the sample for which including a point source in the fit, fundamentally changes the Sersic index from elliptical-like to disk-like.

gmw_00008515.name: DOG 13
gmw_00008515.overview: DOG 13 is a diffuse galaxy characterized by a bump-like spectral energy distribution [arXiv:0904.1231]. Its structure is best fit by a three-component model dominated by a low-surface-brightness main component, with a faint nearby structure located to the south-east [arXiv:0904.1231].

sources.summary: DOG 13 is fit by three components; its low-surface-brightness main component dominates the flux, its PSF contribution is small, and nearby structures may be additional components.

quotes.quote: DOG 13 is also best fit by a 3 component model, Sersic 1 + PSF + Sersic 2 (Figure fig:merger2 ). In this system, the component that contains the bulk of the light is low surface brightness, and not obvious in the image until the higher surface brightness component is subtracted off (to best see the low surface brightness component look at the residuals in the bottom row of Figure fig:merger2 ). It has a large half-light size, 0.49 , and contains about 5 times the total flux of the higher surface brightness component. The central PSF component contains only 2 of the flux of the main galaxy.

There also appear to be several additional nearby structures not included in the fit -- e.g. a faint structure appears about 0.8 to the south-east of the main galaxy. It is not clear if these are additional components in the same system or just background noise.
DOG 13 has a bump-like SED and is very diffuse.

gmw_00008516.name: SDSS J1608+2716
gmw_00008516.overview: SDSS J1608+2716 is a quadruply lensed quasar located at a redshift of z=2.575 [arXiv:2306.12502]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals four point-like components in a cusp configuration, which are lensed by an intervening edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:2306.12502].

sources.summary: HST imaging changes SDSS J1608+2716 from a triple-quasar candidate to a quadruply lensed quasar, revealing a fourth point source, an intervening disk galaxy, and a reproducible cusp lens configuration.

quotes.quote: We report Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) deep IR (F160W) imaging of SDSS J1608+2716. This system, located at a redshift of z=2.575 , was recently reported as a triple quasar candidate with subarcsecond separations ( 0.25'' ) based on selection from Gaia astrometry and follow-up Keck adaptive optics-assisted integral field unit spectroscopy. Our new HST deep IR imaging reveals the presence of a fourth point-like component located 0.9'' away from the triple system.

Additionally, we detect an edge-on disk galaxy located in between the four point sources. The entire system exhibits a characteristic cusp structure in the context of strong gravitational lensing, and the observed image configuration can be successfully reproduced using a lens model based on a singular isothermal ellipsoid mass profile. These findings indicate that this system is a quadruply lensed quasar.

gmw_00008517.name: NGC 6945
gmw_00008517.overview: NGC 6945 is a galaxy characterized by its disk structure [arXiv:1501.05498]. Visual inspection of the galaxy's band image has been used to resolve discrepancies in its reported position angle [arXiv:1501.05498].

sources.summary: The CALIFA analysis finds NGC 6945 has a large PA discrepancy with LEDA, rejects the catalog PA by visual inspection, and excludes it from the median PA comparison.

quotes.quote: There is a galaxy (NGC 6945) with a large difference between PA _ CALIFA and PA _ LEDA . For this galaxy the value of the PA reported by LEDA is 32^ 66^ , which is 94 ^ different from our value. A simple visual inspection of the band galaxy image showed in Fig. f1 ruled out the LEDA PA value. The median value is, excluding NGC 6945, PA=|PA_ LEDA PA_ CALIFA |=4.0^ .

gmw_00008518.name: NGC 3267
gmw_00008518.overview: NGC 3267 is a bright lenticular galaxy projected near NGC 3268 [arXiv:1510.04274]. It belongs to a high-velocity trio of undisturbed S0 galaxies that are likely located in the background of NGC 3268 [arXiv:1510.04274].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 3267 in a high-velocity trio of bright lenticulars projected near NGC 3268, arguing the undisturbed S0s likely lie behind Antlia.

quotes.quote: To the last group belong the three bright lenticulars NGC 3267 (FS90-168), NGC 3269 (FS90-184), and NGC 3271 (FS90-224), near to NGC 3268 in projected distance.

Strikingly, all three S0's around the central galaxy (FS90-224, FS90-168, and FS90-184) have positive velocity offsets in the range of 900-1000 km s ^ 1 . If these velocities were due to the potential of NGC 3268 (and its associated dark matter), very radial orbits would be needed for surpassing the circular velocity that is about 300 km s ^ 1 and probably constant cas14 . The high velocity offsets are, moreover, related to fairly large projected spatial offsets (which in the case of FS90-224 is about 65 kpc), whereas the highest velocities are found at the bottom of the potential well. Furthermore, the galaxies do not show any morphological indication of being tidally disturbed.
We therefore prefer the interpretation that the three S0s have to be placed in the background of NGC 3268.

gmw_00008519.name: NGC 3271
gmw_00008519.overview: NGC 3271 is a bright lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1510.04274]. It is part of a high-velocity trio of undisturbed lenticular galaxies projected near NGC 3268, which likely lie in the background of that system [arXiv:1510.04274].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 3271 in a high-velocity trio of bright lenticulars projected near NGC 3268, arguing the undisturbed S0s likely lie behind Antlia.

quotes.quote: To the last group belong the three bright lenticulars NGC 3267 (FS90-168), NGC 3269 (FS90-184), and NGC 3271 (FS90-224), near to NGC 3268 in projected distance.

Strikingly, all three S0's around the central galaxy (FS90-224, FS90-168, and FS90-184) have positive velocity offsets in the range of 900-1000 km s ^ 1 . If these velocities were due to the potential of NGC 3268 (and its associated dark matter), very radial orbits would be needed for surpassing the circular velocity that is about 300 km s ^ 1 and probably constant cas14 . The high velocity offsets are, moreover, related to fairly large projected spatial offsets (which in the case of FS90-224 is about 65 kpc), whereas the highest velocities are found at the bottom of the potential well. Furthermore, the galaxies do not show any morphological indication of being tidally disturbed.
We therefore prefer the interpretation that the three S0s have to be placed in the background of NGC 3268.

gmw_00008520.name: R.A. (J2000)=08:48:35.975 and Dec (J2000)= -02:44:06.93
gmw_00008520.overview: The galaxy at R.A. (J2000)=08:48:35.975 and Dec (J2000)= -02:44:06.93 has a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.228 [arXiv:2201.06395]. It is identified as the likely host galaxy of the gamma-ray burst GRB 211227A [arXiv:2201.06395].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a coordinate-specified galaxy at the Swift/XRT error-circle border as GRB 211227A's likely host, with z=0.228 and an estimated 20.47 kpc burst offset.

quotes.quote: However, there exists a known source at the border of the Swift/XRT error circle at coordinates: R.A. (J2000)=08:48:35.975 and Dec (J2000)= -02:44:06.93, which has R 19.13 0.09 mag from our images, classified as a galaxy with a photo-redshift of z=0.244 0.089 from SDSS (Figure fig:Host ). Moreover, Malesani et al. (2021) reported that 5 emission lines are observed in the putative host galaxy (e.g., [ O II ] , Hbeta, [ O III ] , Halpha, [ N II ] , and [ S II ] ), and they proposed that a possible host galaxy of GRB 211227A with redshift z =0.228 was detected by the X-shooter spectrograph. The redshift z=0.244 0.089 what we measured is consistent with that reported in Malesani et al. (2021) with z= 0.228 via the X-shooter spectrograph, and the position of this source for LCOGT observations is the same with GCN report in Malesani et al. (2021). In our calculations, we adopt z= 0.228 which is the spectroscopic redshift. In addition, we also calculate the physical distance from the center of GRB 211227A to the host galaxy center as 20.47 14.47 kpc if we assume that the galaxy of z= 0.228 is intrinsic host of GRB 211227A.

gmw_00008521.name: P144+50
gmw_00008521.overview: P144+50 is a luminous high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=5.66 [arXiv:2201.11724]. It exhibits a point-source structure across broad-band photometry and displays a strong Lyman-alpha spectral feature with a clear Gunn-Peterson trough [arXiv:2201.11724].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J144128.715+502239.463/P144+50 as a newly discovered luminous z=5.66 quasar with point-source photometry, low i-z color, no LoTSS counterpart, and Ly/Gunn-Peterson spectral features.

quotes.quote: The other source, PSO J144128.715+502239.463 (shortened to P144+50 hereafter) was convincingly classified as a previously undiscovered, luminous quasar at a redshift of z=5.66 , and in the following section we describe the observed properties of this newly discovered HzQ. P144+50 -- a luminous quasar at z 5.7 The most luminous and promising source of the candidate sample, P144+50 has a very clear point-source like structure across the available broad band photometry, as illustrated in Figure fig:quasarbands . This luminous quasar was most likely missed by earlier searches owing to its relatively low i_ P1 z_ P1 colour of 1.4 , which may be excluded by traditional binary colour cuts.

Although no radio counterpart for this quasar was identified within the LoTSS DR1 catalogues, P144+50 was still assigned a probability of P_q = 0.01 from our method, demonstrating that our novel HzQ selection method is capable of assigning realistically high probabilities even to non-radio detected HzQs. In Table table:p144_mag we give the apparent magnitudes of P144+50 in the available optical and infrared filters. The 1D spectrum of P144+50, shown in Figure fig:quasarspec displays a bright and broad strong Ly feature, with a clear break in the spectrum blueward of the line, showing the Gunn-Peterson trough. The peak of the Ly line suggests a redshift of z=5.66 .

gmw_00008522.name: SDSS J160536+134838
gmw_00008522.overview: SDSS J160536+134838 is a quasar-like active galactic nucleus at a redshift of approximately 0.44 [arXiv:2402.04861]. It is a potential tidal disruption event candidate, featuring a unique optical flare and double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines that suggest disk-like broad-line regions [arXiv:2402.04861].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J160536+134838/SDSS J1605 as a z 0.44 quasar-like AGN and potential TDE candidate, with a CSS flare, stable ASAS-SN light curves, disk-like broad-line regions, and discrepant BH-mass estimates.

quotes.quote: In this letter, motivated by double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines probably related to tidal disruption events (TDEs), a potential TDE candidate is reported in SDSS J160536+134838 (=SDSS J1605) at z 0.44 having quasar-like spectrum but with double-peaked broad H . The long-term CSS light curve can be naturally described by a main-sequence star of 2.82_ 0.19 ^ +0.20 M_ disrupted by the central black hole (BH) of 144_ 21 ^ +26 10^6 M_ in SDSS J1605. Meanwhile, the ASAS-SN light curves afterwards show none apparent trend variability, indicating the bright CSS flare in SDSS J1605 unique and different enough from variability of normal AGN.

Furthermore, there is a consistency between the TDE model determined sizes of debris with the sizes of emission regions for the double-peaked broad H described by the accretion disk model, supporting the disk-like BLRs probably related to a central TDE in SDSS J1605. And the virial BH mass 7 times higher than the TDE model determined value can be naturally explained by R-L relation determined BLRs sizes very larger than the actual distance of emission regions related to TDEs debris in SDSS J1605.

gmw_00008523.name: NGC 2730
gmw_00008523.overview: NGC 2730 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a broken exponential disk structure [arXiv:1709.00595]. This disk structure is evidenced by a break in its surface brightness profile and a flatter abundance gradient in its outer region [arXiv:1709.00595].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2730 as an example whose outer abundance gradient is flatter and whose surface-brightness profile has a prominent break better fit by a broken exponential disk.

quotes.quote: The left column panels of Fig. figure:sn5 show the properties of the galaxy NGC 2730, where the gradient in the outer part of the disc is flatter than that in the inner part.

A comparison between the left column panels a and b of Fig. figure:sn5 shows that the purely linear gradient in the disc of the galaxy NGC 2730 traced by spaxels with high-quality spectra (1883 data points) is in agreement with the purely linear gradient traced by the spaxels with moderate-quality spectra (718 data points). However, the scatter of the abundances in spaxels with high-quality spectra around the O/H -- R_ g relation, _ OH,PLR = 0.0283 , is lower than that for the spaxels with moderate-quality spectra, _ OH,PLR = 0.0510 .

The lower panel of Fig. figure:galaxy-sb shows the measured surface brightness profile of the galaxy NGC 2730, which is an example of a galaxy where the surface brightness profile shows a prominent break. The purely exponential disc is in significant disagreement with the observed surface brightness profile; the value of the scatter _ SB,PED is high. In contrast, the broken exponential disc adequately reproduces the observed surface brightness profile, and the value of the scatter _ SB,BED is low.

gmw_00008524.name: NGC 5320
gmw_00008524.overview: NGC 5320 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a distinct stellar disk [arXiv:1709.00595]. The oxygen abundance gradient in the inner part of its disk is flatter than the gradient observed in the outer region [arXiv:1709.00595].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5320 as an example where the inner abundance gradient is flatter, data-quality cuts preserve the gradient, and different calibrations give different break radii.

quotes.quote: The right column panels of Fig. figure:sn5 show the same but for the galaxy NGC 5320, where the gradient in the inner part of the disc is flatter than that in the outer part.

A similar behaviour is seen in the galaxy NGC 5320 (right column panels a and b of Fig. figure:sn5 ). While the purely linear gradients traced by the spaxels with high-quality spectra (2045 data points) and by the spaxels with moderate-quality spectra (890 data points) are in agreement, the scatter of the abundances in spaxels with high-quality spectra around the O/H -- R_ g relation, _ OH,PLR = 0.0297, is lower than that for the spaxels with moderate-quality spectra, _ OH,PLR = 0.0410.

The middle column panels show the galaxy NGC 5320 where the break radii in the (O/H) _ PG16 and (O/H) _ M13,O3N2 distributions are close to each other, but differ from that in the (O/H) _ M13,N2 distribution.

gmw_00008525.name: dwarf galaxy hosting PTF10qaf
gmw_00008525.overview: The dwarf galaxy hosting PTF10qaf is a star-forming system that exhibits galaxy-centered radio emission [arXiv:2108.10445]. This galaxy shows a radio-based star formation rate excess and is likely interacting with a large spiral galaxy [arXiv:2108.10445].

sources.summary: The paper describes the PTF10qaf host as a star-forming dwarf with galaxy-centered radio emission, radio-SFR excess, and optical evidence for interaction with a large spiral.

quotes.quote: The radio emission of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, and SN 2006gy are dominated by the galaxy center rather than the SN positions.

The hosts of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, PTF12dam, and SN 1999bd have an excess of radio-based SFRs over SED-based SFRs by a factor of 3--12. The radio excess in the hosts of SN 1999bd, PTF10qaf, and PTF10uhf are consistent with the results by hats18 .
Optical observations show that a dwarf galaxy hosting PTF10qaf is likely to be interacting with a large spiral galaxy perl16 .

gmw_00008526.name: PTF10uhf host
gmw_00008526.overview: The PTF10uhf host is a massive galaxy with a stellar mass exceeding $10^{11}$ solar masses [arXiv:2108.10445]. Optical imaging suggests the galaxy is a merger, which may drive its dusty star formation and its observed excess of radio-based star formation [arXiv:2108.10445].

sources.summary: The paper describes the PTF10uhf host as a massive SLSN host with galaxy-centered radio emission, radio-SFR excess, and optical evidence suggesting a merger.

quotes.quote: The radio emission of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, and SN 2006gy are dominated by the galaxy center rather than the SN positions.

The hosts of PTF10qaf, PTF10uhf, PTF12dam, and SN 1999bd have an excess of radio-based SFRs over SED-based SFRs by a factor of 3--12. The radio excess in the hosts of SN 1999bd, PTF10qaf, and PTF10uhf are consistent with the results by hats18 .

The optical image of the PTF10uhf host is also likely to represent a merger perl16 . The interaction or merger may cause dusty star formation in the hosts. The stellar masses of the hosts of SN 1999bd, SN 2006gy, PTF10qaf, and PTF10uhf are in the largest range among the sample of SLSNe lelo15, perl16, schu18 . In particular, the hosts of SN 2006gy and PTF10uhf have the largest stellar mass with M_*/M_ > 11 perl16, schu18 .

gmw_00008527.name: PMN J1230 1139
gmw_00008527.overview: PMN J1230 1139 is a highly radio-loud, high-redshift quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0601037] classified as a blazar [arXiv:2209.10760, arXiv:astro-ph/0601037]. The source exhibits variability [arXiv:2209.10760] and features a curved radio jet [arXiv:2209.10760] alongside an offset radio core [arXiv:2209.10760, arXiv:astro-ph/0601037].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1230 1139 as a high-redshift blazar with a curved pc-scale jet, an offset radio core, measurable superluminal motion, and significant variability.

quotes.quote: J1230 1139 The 8.4-GHz image of this source shows a prominent curved jet that bends from the west to the southwest at a projected distance of about 45 pc. Two components, J1 and J2, are detected in the curved southwest jet section, and J3 is located where the jet bends. The brightest component is C, but it is not a radio core. The extension east of C shows a flat spectrum and should host the core. The maximum proper motion is 0.10 mas yr ^ 1 , corresponding to an apparent transverse speed of 12 .

The estimated viewing angle of the innermost jet is around 10 , derived from the Doppler factor and maximum apparent jet speed, classifying it as a blazar. The source also shows significant variability, consistent with its blazar identification. It shares similar high-resolution jet properties with other high- z blazars e.g. 2020SciBu..65..525Z,2020NatCo..11..143A .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PMN J1230 1139 as a highly radio-loud high-redshift quasar with no X-ray extension; its X-ray emission is attributed to the quasar core.

quotes.quote: Two of our targets are not as representative: PMN J1230 1139 and PMN J2219 2719 are highly radio-loud with R 7400--9500 . However, these targets are important as they enlarge the radio-loud ``blazar'' population with X-ray observations (only 5 blazars have been detected in X-rays previously).
Using this method, three of the targets (PMN J0214 0518, PMN J0324 2918, and PMN J1230 1139) showed no evidence of X-ray extension.

In the case of PMN J1230 1139, the larger offset of the X-ray and optical cores (0.35 ) may be attributed to inaccuracy in the optical astrometry. To confirm this assessment, we considered the 4-cm and 13-cm images of PMN J1230 1139 taken for the Very Long Baseline Array Calibrator Survey (VCS1; Beasley et al. 2002). The flux seen in these images appears almost certain to be from the radio core, given its morphology and intensity (A. J. Beasley and D. Dallacasa 2005, private communication). The VLBA core position is consistent to within 0.26 \ with the X-ray core (see Table 1). Thus, we conclude the X-ray emission originates from the quasar core and is not from extended X-ray jets.

gmw_00008528.name: NGC 7768
gmw_00008528.overview: NGC 7768 is an elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:0808.1521, arXiv:1310.5310]. The galaxy exhibits major-axis rotation of approximately 114 km/s [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: The paper finds significant major-axis rotation in NGC7768, about 114 km/s, consistent with earlier measurements near 100 km/s from previous studies.

quotes.quote: NGC7768: Surface brightness profiles were published by Schombert (1986), Malumuth Kirshner (1985) and Jord an et al. \ (2004) and HST imaging was published by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile of this galaxy shows significant rotation of the order of 114 11 km s ^ 1 along the major axis. This agrees with the 101 5 km s ^ 1 and the 99 km s ^ 1 rotation found previously by Fisher et al. \ (1995a) and Prugniel Simien (1996), respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports a large transition parameter for NGC 7768, fixes it to approximate infinity, and describes its appendix light-profile treatment.

quotes.quote: The best-fit parameter for NGC 7768 is found to be 16.6.
Since for large ( > 10) the profile becomes insensitive to the exact value of , we prefer to use the latter fit (fixing to 100) to the light profile of NGC 7768.
The derivation of light profiles of NGC 3379, NGC 3842, NGC 6086 and NGC 7768 is described in the Appendix.

gmw_00008529.name: GAMA-622744
gmw_00008529.overview: GAMA-622744 is a star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:1807.01522]. It possesses a negative ionization parameter gradient, which likely drives a correlation between its metallicity and ionization parameter [arXiv:1807.01522].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GAMA-622744 as the only sample galaxy with significant ionization-parameter behavior, including a negative gradient and metallicity-ionization correlation.

quotes.quote: GAMA-622744 appears to be the only galaxy with a significant ionization parameter gradient (PCC magnitude = 0.73). kaplan16 found significant ionization parameter gradients in their sample of 8 galaxies using VENGA data.

However the strength of these gradients is weak with only GAMA-622744 having a PCC magnitude of greater than 0.6. We also investigate how the ionization parameter varies with metallicity (Figure metiongrid ). We do this by plotting the KD02 metallicity determined from the N2O2 diagnostic against the KK04 ionization parameter measurements. We use the KD02 N2O2 metallicity diagnostic instead of the KK04 R23 metallicity diagnostic to try and exclude any possible dependencies between the two parameters caused by the iterative method used to calculate the ionization parameter. Again we find that only GAMA-622744 produces a significant Pearson correlation coefficient. The correlation between metallicity and ionization parameter for GAMA-622744 is likely driven by the fact that it is the only galaxy in our sample with a significant negative ionization parameter gradient and not necessarily because of an intrinsic correlation between metallicity and ionization parameter. dopita14 found a strong positive trend between the metallicity and ionization parameter, which is not seen in either this work or kaplan16 . dopita06 provide a theoretical relationship between gas-phase metallicity and ionization parameter, q [ cm/s ] Z [ O/H ] ^ 0.8 .

GAMA-622744 is the only galaxy that displays a significant correlation between metallicity and ionization parameter. However we believe this is not necessarily caused by an intrinsic relationship between metallicity and ionization parameter, but rather because GAMA-622744 is the only galaxy which possess a significant ionization parameter gradient.

gmw_00008530.name: ESO 582-G12
gmw_00008530.overview: ESO 582-G12 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a flat-to-declining rotation curve and high shear [arXiv:astro-ph/0603622]. Its dark matter halo is constrained to a relatively low mass with a high concentration [arXiv:astro-ph/0603622].

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 582-G12 as a worked spiral-galaxy example, finding a flat-to-declining rotation curve, high shear, adiabatic-contraction preference, high NFW concentration, and substantial stellar baryon fraction.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies we chose are chosen such that one galaxy, ESO 582-G12, has a flat rotation curve, with a shear, S=0.52 0.05 in the middle of the range, and the other galaxy, IC 2522, has a continually rising rotation curve, with a shear, S=0.39 0.01 , towards the low end of the range of shears.

Once we constrain the models by forcing the predicted shear to be consistent the observed range S=0.47-0.57 , we favor models that include adiabatic contraction. The dark matter of halo of ESO 582-G12 is constrained to be of relatively low mass, M_ vir 5-8 10^ 11 M _ , and to have a fairly high NFW concentration, c_ vir > 16 . This is consistent with the high-end of the the c_ vir distribution predicted for LCDM bullock01b . The stellar mass in the galaxy is between 15 and 40 of the the baryonic mass associated with its dark matter halo.

gmw_00008531.name: IC 2522
gmw_00008531.overview: IC 2522 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a continually rising rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0603622]. It features a low-concentration, massive dark matter halo and a low stellar baryon fraction [arXiv:astro-ph/0603622].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 2522 as the low-shear comparison case, with a rising rotation curve, low-concentration massive halo, low stellar baryon fraction, and preference for non-adiabatic contraction.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies we chose are chosen such that one galaxy, ESO 582-G12, has a flat rotation curve, with a shear, S=0.52 0.05 in the middle of the range, and the other galaxy, IC 2522, has a continually rising rotation curve, with a shear, S=0.39 0.01 , towards the low end of the range of shears.

Figure ic2522_m shows analogous results for IC 2522. Unlike ESO 582 G12, this galaxy prefers a more massive, low-concentration halo: M_ vir 1.5-6 10^ 12 M _ with c_ vir 2-8 , at the low end of the c_ vir distribution bullock01b . The implied stellar mass is quite small: < 10 of the universal baryon budget has ended up on stars. Furthermore, the measured shear rate of this galaxy favors our non-AC model. This is in qualitative agreement with results presented by Dutton et al. \ (2005).

gmw_00008532.name: galaxy A
gmw_00008532.overview: Galaxy A is a member of the compact spectroscopic triplet J0848+1644 [arXiv:1512.02439]. The galaxy has an armless spherical shape and exhibits modest current star formation activity triggered by interactions within the system [arXiv:1512.02439].

sources.summary: Galaxy A is a member of the compact triplet J0848+1644; its photometric redshift differs from galaxy B, it falls outside the main SDSS sample, and the members share similar colors and modest star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy system SDSS J084843.45+164417.3 (hereafter J0848+1644), which contains galaxy A, B and C (see Fig. Fig:plot1 for details), is the spectroscopic triplet we report and study in this paper. This triplet is not included in the A-sample because these three galaxies have very small angular distances and only galaxy B has spectrum in SDSS, z_ spec =0.078829 0.000015 ( SpecObjID: 2565950139160094720) Abazajian2009 .

Also, it is excluded by the O-sample since the z_ phot of the other two galaxies ( z_ phot =(0.32, 0.08, 0.19) for galaxy A, B and C respectively) are all much more different from z_ spec of galaxy B.

Overall, members of J0848+1644 are similar. They all have armless spherical shapes with similar luminosity and color. This similarity is regarded as another evidence of the long time co-evolution. All three members show significant but not strong activities of current star formation, that means the interaction may continue to play the role of star forming trigger.

gmw_00008533.name: galaxy B
gmw_00008533.overview: Galaxy B is a member of the spectroscopic galaxy triplet SDSS J084843.45+164417.3 [arXiv:1512.02439]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.078829 and exhibits active star formation, as indicated by its strong H-alpha and O II emission lines [arXiv:1512.02439].

sources.summary: Galaxy B is the SDSS-spectroscopic member of J0848+1644; BFOSC confirms its SDSS redshift, its H-based and OII-based star-formation estimates agree, and the triplet members look co-evolved.

quotes.quote: The galaxy system SDSS J084843.45+164417.3 (hereafter J0848+1644), which contains galaxy A, B and C (see Fig. Fig:plot1 for details), is the spectroscopic triplet we report and study in this paper. This triplet is not included in the A-sample because these three galaxies have very small angular distances and only galaxy B has spectrum in SDSS, z_ spec =0.078829 0.000015 ( SpecObjID: 2565950139160094720) Abazajian2009 .
For galaxy B, the BOSFC redshift is in excellent agreement with the SDSS value ( z_ SDSS =0.078829 ).

For galaxy B or C of J0848+1644, we see significant H emission line in their respective SDSS or LAMOST spectrum, which indicates their current star forming activities. Unfortunately, the LAMOST spectrum of galaxy C may be seriously affected by unreliable flux calibration and causes an unexpected stronger H emission which may lead to the wrong value of star formation rate (SFR). Alternatively, we use the OII flux measured from the BFOSC spectra of all three galaxies to calibrate their SFR, following the relation of Kennicutt1998 , SFR( M _ yr ^ 1 ) 1.4 10^ 41 L ( OII )( ergs s ^ 1 ), The SFR results, together with the (SFR/M_*) , are calculated and listed in Table tabel1 . For galaxy B, these two values are all consistent with those derived from the H flux.

gmw_00008534.name: galaxy C
gmw_00008534.overview: Galaxy C is a member of the J0848+1644 galaxy triplet and belongs to the SDSS main galaxy sample [arXiv:1512.02439]. It exhibits current star-forming activity, as indicated by H-alpha emission in its LAMOST spectrum [arXiv:1512.02439].

sources.summary: Galaxy C is the LAMOST-detected member of J0848+1644; BFOSC agrees with its LAMOST redshift, it belongs to the SDSS main sample, and its emission-line calibration drives the triplet SFR analysis.

quotes.quote: The galaxy C is compiled into the complementary galaxy sample of the LAMOST spectral survey and its spectra has been released in the LAMOST DR1 with z_ spec = 0.07929 (Obsid:78907168) Luo2015 Based on SDSS DR7 photometry, the galaxy C ( =17.06) belongs to the main galaxy sample whereas the galaxy A ( =18.45) does not. .

For galaxy C, the BOSFC redshift is also consistent with the LAMOST value ( z_ LAMOST =0.0793 ). Considering the internal consistence of the BOSFC spectra, we only take the BOSFC redshifts to study the kinematics and dynamics of J0848+1644 below.

For galaxy B or C of J0848+1644, we see significant H emission line in their respective SDSS or LAMOST spectrum, which indicates their current star forming activities. Unfortunately, the LAMOST spectrum of galaxy C may be seriously affected by unreliable flux calibration and causes an unexpected stronger H emission which may lead to the wrong value of star formation rate (SFR). Alternatively, we use the OII flux measured from the BFOSC spectra of all three galaxies to calibrate their SFR, following the relation of Kennicutt1998 , SFR( M _ yr ^ 1 ) 1.4 10^ 41 L ( OII )( ergs s ^ 1 ), The SFR results, together with the (SFR/M_*) , are calculated and listed in Table tabel1 . For galaxy B, these two values are all consistent with those derived from the H flux.

gmw_00008535.name: DD J022050.38 053714.1
gmw_00008535.overview: DD J022050.38 053714.1 is a very red galaxy located at a photometric redshift of 2.02 [arXiv:2011.15030]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus that powers both a strong mid-infrared continuum and a high 3 GHz radio luminosity [arXiv:2011.15030].

sources.summary: This very red DeepDrill source is described as an AGN at photometric redshift 2.02, with Spitzer IRS features and a high 3 GHz radio luminosity inconsistent with star formation alone.

quotes.quote: For one of the very red sources, DD J022050.38 053714.1 (5MUSES-033), the Spitzer IRS spectrum from the CASSIS database 2011ApJS..196....8L shows a strong mid-infrared continuum and spectral features consistent with an AGN at its photometric redshift (derived from SWIRE data) of 2.02 2013MNRAS.428.1958R . This source is also detected in the VLA Sky Survey (VLASS; 2020PASP..132c5001L ) with a 3 GHz flux density S_ 3 GHz =2.0 0.2 mJy. The correspondingly high radio luminosity of L_ 3 GHz =5 10 ^ 25 W Hz^ 1 is much more consistent with the radio emission being powered by an AGN rather than by star formation e.g. 2011ApJ...739L..29K .

gmw_00008536.name: DD J033401.66 265017.0
gmw_00008536.overview: DD J033401.66 265017.0 is a very red galaxy located in the Chandra Deep Field South [arXiv:2011.15030]. It features a compact radio counterpart detected in deep VLITE data, and it is likely powered by an active galactic nucleus if it lies at a redshift above one [arXiv:2011.15030].

sources.summary: This very red CDFS source has a compact VLITE counterpart, lacks a VLASS detection implying a steep spectral index, and is judged likely AGN-powered if it lies at redshift above one.

quotes.quote: Of the 12 very red sources in CDFS that fall within the currently imaged portion (spanning a width of 2 deg) of the deep VLITE data, one source (DD J033401.66 265017.0) has a compact counterpart in VLITE with a primary-beam-corrected flux of 1.0 0.3 mJy at a SNR of 4.8. The lack of detection in VLASS implies a moderately-steep spectral index, ^ 0.34 GHz _ 3.0 GHz <-0.9 . Unfortunately, the source currently lacks a photometric redshift, though assuming it is at z>1 it is also more likely to be powered by an AGN.

gmw_00008537.name: DLS01
gmw_00008537.overview: DLS01 is a strong galaxy-galaxy lens candidate with a high-arcness arc centered relative to an elliptical lens galaxy [arXiv:0712.3063]. The system has a large estimated Einstein radius of 4.9 arcseconds, which is likely due to its proximity to nearby galaxy clusters [arXiv:0712.3063].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DLS01 as a strong galaxy-galaxy lens candidate with a high-arcness arc, nearby elliptical lens galaxy, large Einstein radius, and possible cluster influence.

quotes.quote: One interesting candidate (DLS01) is discovered at this arcness level shown on the Left in Fig. fig:arccands (North is to the left, East is down). The candidate arc has a measured arcness a=0.18 , ellipticity e=0.73 , and magnitude R=23.5 . The arc also appears centred relative to an elliptical lens galaxy located southwest of the arc with magnitude R=20.37 .

The candidate system DLS01 has a large estimated Einstein radius ( 4.9 arcsec) which is likely due to its proximity to nearby galaxy clusters. The preliminary weak lensing convergence map for the DLS F2 field (Wittman et. al 2006) reveals a large structure with three overlapping peaks. A redshift survey of this field has identified these peaks to be Abell 781, CXOU J092053+3029880, and CXOU J092110+302751 geller05 . Relative to the centre of the peak in the convergence map DLS01 lies 2.9 arcmin away.

gmw_00008538.name: DLS02
gmw_00008538.overview: DLS02 is a faint elliptical lens galaxy with an R-band magnitude of 21.96 [arXiv:0712.3063]. It is associated with a candidate arc that has the smallest estimated Einstein radius of 3.4 arcseconds, though the arc may alternatively be a galaxy with high intrinsic arcness from a tidal interaction [arXiv:0712.3063].

sources.summary: The paper presents DLS02 as a lower-arcness lens candidate centered on a faint elliptical lens, with the smallest estimated Einstein radius and possible non-lensing explanations.

quotes.quote: Another interesting arc candidate (DLS02) is detected in this lower arcness space shown on the Right in Fig. fig:arccands (North is to the left, East is down). This arc candidate has a measured arcness a=0.13 , ellipticity e=0.80 , and magnitude R=23.9 . The arc also appears centred on an elliptical lens galaxy located directly north of the arc with magnitude R=21.96 .
The remaining candidate DLS02 has the smallest Einstein radius ( 3.4 arcsec) and is separated by at least 20 arcmin from any peak in the convergence map.
It is possible that the candidate arcs in DLS01 and DLS02 could be galaxies which are not strongly lensed but instead have a high intrinsic arcness, for instance due to a tidal interaction.

gmw_00008539.overview: The active galactic nucleus 1150+812, also classified as the blazar 5BZQ J1153+8058, exhibited transient gamma-ray activity during 2014 [arXiv:1804.03703]. Its radio jet displays edge-offset polarization and monotonic transverse rotation measure gradients [arXiv:1410.6653].

sources.summary: The paper reports 1150+812 has edge-offset jet polarization, orthogonal core field behavior, and monotonic transverse RM gradients in the core and inner jet.

quotes.quote: 1150+812 Figure fig:pmaps2 a shows our 7.9 GHz polarization map for 1150+812. The jet EVPAs are roughly perpendicular to the jet and clearly offset toward the western edge of the jet, indicating a longitudinal magnetic field, while the core EVPAs are oriented roughly parallel to the jet direction, suggesting an orthogonal field.

The core region and jet both show evidence of RM gradients transverse to the jet, in the same direction. Figure fig:1150RM also shows a slice taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with its errors. The line drawn across the RM map indicate the location of the slice, in the core region. This gradient is monotonic, and has a significance of approximately 5 . A transverse gradient that is essentially, but not strictly, monotonic can also be observed in the inner jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes 5BZQ J1153+8058 as active only in 2014, with transient gamma-ray behavior washed out in long catalog integrations but localized by a TS map.

quotes.quote: 5BZQ J1153+8058. From the light curve (bottom panel of Fig. bzq1153 ) this source shows \ activity only in 2014, which is consistent with its noninclusion in the 3FGL catalog (3FGL covers the period of 08/2008-08/2012). A likelihood analysis with 3FGL setup results in null detection. For this case, we are likely probing the peak of a transient \ activity during the year 2014, and smoothing out the signal along 7.5 years of binned analysis. The detection of a steady high-energy component (non-flaring state) is currently limited by the Fermi-LAT sensitivity.

We built a TS map (top left in Fig. bzq1153 ) integrating along the whole year of 2014, showing that the blazar position is compatible with the \ signature within the 68 containment region. This case in particular allowed us to use E > 3.0 GeV photons instead of E > 500 MeV, providing improved localization.

gmw_00008540.name: CLASS J1333+5056
gmw_00008540.overview: CLASS J1333+5056 is a high-redshift blazar located at a redshift of z=1.362 [arXiv:1101.2379]. It exhibits a broad-band spectral energy distribution reminiscent of a flat-spectrum radio quasar and displays gamma-ray variability [arXiv:1101.2379].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CLASS J1333+5056 as a z=1.362 high-redshift blazar coincident with Suzaku source D, with an FSRQ-like SED, radio/X-ray/gamma-ray ratios, variability, and steep gamma-ray photon index.

quotes.quote: Within the error circle of 1FGL J1333.2 + 5056, our Suzaku /XIS observations revealed the presence of several weak X-ray flux maxima with possibly diverse spectral properties (as indicated by the spectral analysis hampered by the limited photon statistics). One of the detected X-ray sources (src D) coincides with the high-redshift blazar CLASS J1333+5056 ( z=1.362 ). The broad-band spectral energy distribution of 1FGL J1333.2 + 5056/CLASS J1333+5056/src D is presented in Figure fig:specJ1333-SED , including the LAT ray, Suzaku X-ray, archival radio, and newly analyzed Swift /UVOT data for the blazar. The constructed SED reveals two distinct radiative components, consisting of a low-energy synchrotron bump and an (energetically dominant) high-energy inverse-Compton continuum, reminiscent of typical broad-band spectra for blazars of the FSRQ type ghisellini98 .

Note that the X-ray--to-- ray flux ratio 10^3 implied by Figure fig:specJ1333-SED , as well as the relatively large radio flux, would be both in agreement with the blazar identification of 1FGL J1333.2 + 5056. In addition, we note that the discussed Fermi object is the most variable in rays out of all four Fermi targets studied in this paper, with the variability index of 38 which indicates a <1 probability of a steady flux; see 1FGL . The additional support for the blazar association is offered by the fact that the ray continuum of 1FGL J1333.2 + 5056 is the steepest among the four Fermi objects observed by us, with the photon index 2.5 0.1 , which is compatible with the mean ray photon index of the FSRQ population reported in the 1FGL, namely 2.47 0.19 abdo10-FSRQ-BLLac .

gmw_00008541.name: CLASS J2017 + 0603
gmw_00008541.overview: CLASS J2017 + 0603 is a high-redshift blazar located at a redshift of z=1.743 [arXiv:1101.2379]. It has been detected in both radio and X-ray observations, and it is situated within a Fermi gamma-ray error circle [arXiv:1101.2379].

sources.summary: The paper reports Suzaku and Swift detected the z=1.743 blazar CLASS J2017 + 0603 in the Fermi error circle, with radio/X-ray properties compatible with a blazar but pulsations favoring a pulsar association.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, in our Suzaku /XIS exposure we have only detected the high-redshift blazar ( z=1.743 ) CLASS J2017 + 0603, but not the pulsar. The same is true for the Swift /UVOT observation cognard10 , which resulted in analogous flux and upper limit measurements in the optical for the blazar and pulsar, respectively. The constructed radio to X-ray SEDs for the pulsar and blazar systems are shown in Figure fig:specJ2017-SED together with the LAT spectrum.

On the other hand, the relatively large radio flux of CLASS J2017 + 0603, together with the X-ray--to-- ray flux ratio 300 for the 1FGL J2017.3+0603/CLASS J2017 + 0603 system, are in some level of agreement with the blazar interpretation. The ray photon index of 1FGL J2017.3 + 0603 is however rather flat for a FSRQ and represents a 3 deviation from the distribution observed for FSRQs mean =2.47 , =0.19 ; see abdo10-FSRQ-BLLac thus making the association with the FSRQ less likely. Although the detected pulsations in radio and rays is key to the identification of the ray source with a pulsar, there may be some contaminating flux from the blazar. Indeed, the chance probability of finding a CLASS-like background blazar in the Fermi error circle of this source is 0.003 .

gmw_00008542.name: AS1063-885
gmw_00008542.overview: AS1063-885 is a high-redshift galaxy with a measured redshift of z=3.1171 [arXiv:1801.05816]. This extremely blue and young object exhibits physical characteristics similar to a proto-globular cluster [arXiv:1801.05816].

sources.summary: The paper notes AS1063-885 is extremely blue and young with proto-globular-cluster-like properties, and confirms its high redshift with a measured z=3.1171.

quotes.quote: The source AS1063-885 has been studied in detail by Vanzella2016 and Vanzella2017 . They report in their work that this object is extremely blue and young, showing characteristics similar to a proto-globular cluster. They find a redshift of z=3.1169 , which is consistent with the redshift of z=3.1171 we measure in this work.

gmw_00008543.name: MACS1206-1472
gmw_00008543.overview: MACS1206-1472 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.038 [arXiv:1801.05816]. It is gravitationally lensed, with MACS1206-820 representing a multiple image of only its top-left clump [arXiv:1801.05816].

sources.summary: The paper treats MACS1206-1472 and MACS1206-820 as multiple images of the same z=3.038 galaxy, noting MACS1206-820 images only the top-left clump.

quotes.quote: The sources MACS1206-1472 and MACS1206-820 are also known as multiple images of the same galaxy Zitrin2012 . The redshift of 3.03 reported is also in good agreement with our measurement of z=3.038 .
MACS1206-820 is an image of only the top left clump of MACS1206-1472 (see Fig. image3 in Appendix appA ).

gmw_00008544.name: 3C 66 A
gmw_00008544.overview: The active galaxy 3C 66 A is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0603268]. Its X-ray spectrum is best fit by a broken power-law model, and no X-ray variability was detected during monitoring in 2003 and 2004 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603268].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 66 A/0219+428 as a BL Lac counterpart to an EGRET source, analyzes its XMM-Newton spectrum, and finds a broken power-law fit better than a simple absorbed power law.

quotes.quote: The counterpart of this EGRET source is the BL Lac 0219+428 , although Kuiper et al. (2000) have proposed that the flux below 300 MeV is significantly contaminated by the nearby pulsar PSR J 0218+4232 . 3C 66 A was extensively monitored from radio to very high rays during 2003-2004 (B ottcher et al. 2005) and no significant X-ray variability was detected. The present XMM-Newton data set was affected by a high background, but it is possible to use more than 70 of the observation. The fit of the spectrum of this BL Lac object with an absorbed power law model is acceptable (see Table tab:srcdata ), although there are residuals for energies greater than 4 keV. The broken power law model provides the best fit with a confidence >99.99 with a f-test.

gmw_00008545.name: SMMJ141802.87+523011.1
gmw_00008545.overview: SMMJ141802.87+523011.1 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy characterized by hot dust temperatures exceeding 60 Kelvin [arXiv:0905.4499]. The system likely originated from a major merger of gas-rich galaxies, which triggered an ongoing starburst that formed the majority of its stellar population [arXiv:0905.4499].

sources.summary: The paper singles out SMMJ141802.87+523011.1 among extreme SMGs with very high SSFRs, hot dust, most stellar mass formed in the current starburst, and likely major-merger origins.

quotes.quote: At the extreme there are three high- z SMGs SMMJ131201.17+424208.1, SMMJ141802.87+523011.1, SMMJ221806.77+001245.7 plus a low-mass, low- z case, SMMJ030238.62+001106.3 with very high SSFRs >10 Gyr ^ 1 (Column of Table tab:grasilres ). They are all hot ( T_d>60 K, Column ) and formed the majority of their stellar populations during the ongoing starburst ( M_ burst /M_*>60 , Column ). Therefore they are likely the most powerful cases of SMGs formed in major mergers of galaxies with huge gas reservoirs that were subsequently converted into stars.

gmw_00008546.name: SMMJ221806.77+001245.7
gmw_00008546.overview: SMMJ221806.77+001245.7 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy with hot dust [arXiv:0905.4499]. It exhibits a very high specific star formation rate, having formed the majority of its stellar population during its ongoing starburst, and likely originated from a major merger [arXiv:0905.4499].

sources.summary: The paper singles out SMMJ221806.77+001245.7 among extreme SMGs with very high SSFRs, hot dust, most stellar mass formed in the current starburst, and likely major-merger origins.

quotes.quote: At the extreme there are three high- z SMGs SMMJ131201.17+424208.1, SMMJ141802.87+523011.1, SMMJ221806.77+001245.7 plus a low-mass, low- z case, SMMJ030238.62+001106.3 with very high SSFRs >10 Gyr ^ 1 (Column of Table tab:grasilres ). They are all hot ( T_d>60 K, Column ) and formed the majority of their stellar populations during the ongoing starburst ( M_ burst /M_*>60 , Column ). Therefore they are likely the most powerful cases of SMGs formed in major mergers of galaxies with huge gas reservoirs that were subsequently converted into stars.

gmw_00008547.name: SMMJ030238.62+001106.3
gmw_00008547.overview: SMMJ030238.62+001106.3 is a low-mass, low-redshift submillimeter galaxy characterized by hot dust [arXiv:0905.4499]. It exhibits a very high specific star formation rate and likely originated from a major merger of gas-rich galaxies [arXiv:0905.4499].

sources.summary: The paper singles out SMMJ030238.62+001106.3 as a low-mass, low-redshift extreme SMG with very high SSFR, hot dust, dominant current-starburst mass, and likely major-merger origin.

quotes.quote: At the extreme there are three high- z SMGs SMMJ131201.17+424208.1, SMMJ141802.87+523011.1, SMMJ221806.77+001245.7 plus a low-mass, low- z case, SMMJ030238.62+001106.3 with very high SSFRs >10 Gyr ^ 1 (Column of Table tab:grasilres ). They are all hot ( T_d>60 K, Column ) and formed the majority of their stellar populations during the ongoing starburst ( M_ burst /M_*>60 , Column ). Therefore they are likely the most powerful cases of SMGs formed in major mergers of galaxies with huge gas reservoirs that were subsequently converted into stars.

gmw_00008548.name: And XVI
gmw_00008548.overview: And XVI is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1606.01207, arXiv:1710.09038] that lies almost perfectly on the M31 satellite plane [arXiv:1904.03192]. The galaxy ceased all star formation by a lookback time of 6 Gyr [arXiv:1606.01207] and hosts a sparse population of only eight RR Lyrae stars [arXiv:1710.09038].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry for And XVI, using resolved stellar populations to compare its CMD coverage, horizontal-branch features, and early star-formation history with other M31 dwarf spheroidals.

quotes.quote: The positions of the observed fields are shown in Figure f4 . As can be seen in Figure f4 , for And I and And II the ACS field covers only part of the galaxy within the half light radius and the parallel WFC3 field contains many member stars. Some member stars were detected in the WFC3 field of And III, but not nearly enough to produce a SFH with reasonable uncertainties. For And XV, And XVI, and And XXVIII, the ACS field of view covers most of the galaxy out to the half light radius and the WFC3 field is distant enough that a minimal number of member stars are expected.

Figure f5 shows that the ACS photometry reaches below the oldest main-sequence turn-offs allowing for very strong constraints on the oldest epochs of star formation. These observations of And I, And II, and And III are 2.5 mag fainter than the deepest CMDs previously obtained for these galaxies dacosta1996, dacosta2000, dacosta2002 . There are several notable features that are common to all six ACS CMDs. All six galaxies show the presence of blue horizontal branch stars (BHBs). This is generally taken as evidence of the presence of stars with ages comparable to the Milky Way GC population see discussion in gallart2005 . To date, all galaxies that have been observed with sufficient photometric depth have shown at least some star formation at the earliest times.

Figure f7 shows a variety of SFHs. However, all six SFHs have the common properties of starting their star formation early (there is significant star formation in the earliest time bin for all six galaxies), having at least 50 of their stars formed by a lookback time of 9 Gyr, and then ceasing all star formation by a lookback time of 6 Gyr. And XV and And XXVIII show the oldest mean stellar populations of the sample, but both are clearly not consistent with a single age population and are clearly not consistent with forming all of their stars before the epoch of reionization (as indicated in Figure f7 by the shaded region).

sources.summary: The HST census treats And XVI as an M31 dSph, detects only eight RRLs, finds no anomalous Cepheids, and links the sparse RRL population to its star-formation history.

quotes.quote: Under the ISLAndS Initial Star formation and Lifetimes of Andromeda Satellites project (based on very deep, multi-epoch HST ACS and WFC3 data), six M31 dSph satellite companion galaxies were observed: And I, And II, And III, And XV, And XVI and And XXVIII.
We detect 870 RR Lyrae (RRL) stars in the fields of And I (296), II (251), III (111), XV (117), XVI (8), XXVIII (87).

RRLs were detected in all six galaxies with as few as 8 (in And XVI Excluding the RRL star AndXVI-V001 (V0 in Monelli2016 ) as it is a candidate M31 halo field star not belonging to And XVI. ) and as a many as 296 (in And I). The striking difference in the number of RRL between And XVI and And XV, despite having a similar mass, can be explained as a consequence of their different SFHs: the mass fraction already in place at old ages (10 Gyr ago) was only about 50 in And XVI, while it was 90 in And XV (see Figure 7 in Skillman2017 ). A few (3-4) ACs are present in And II, III, XV, and XXVIII, but none have been detected in And I nor in And XVI.

sources.summary: The paper notes Andromeda XVI lies almost exactly on the M31 satellite plane, leaving a relatively broad band of proper motions consistent with remaining near it.

quotes.quote: IC10, LGS3, IC1613 and Andromeda XVI are very far from M31, with distances 252kpc, 269kpc, 520kpc and 279kpc respectively. As a result, only a thin strip of PM's, which correspond to angular momenta very closely aligned to the plane normal, produce trajectories which remain closer than 3 _ D from the plane. IC1613 is so far from the origin of M31 that no PM's correspond to planar orbits, so we don't include it in Figs. theplot and theotherplot . Andromeda XVI on the other hand currently lies almost perfectly on the plane, so there is a relatively wide band of PM's consistent with it remaining less than _ D from the plane. IC10, LGS 3 and IC1613 are more likely accidentally aligned with the plane.

sources.summary: The paper presents robust kinematic and metallicity measurements for And XVI, deriving member-star velocities, velocity dispersion, and average metallicity, and finds its properties consistent with Local Group dSph trends.

quotes.quote: In order to create a more uniform sample for analysis of the in-plane M31 dSphs, we have presented robust kinematic properties for the M31 dSphs, And XVI and XVII. From samples of 20 and 16 member stars respectively, we derive v_r=-369.1^ +1.1 _ 1.3 and _v=5.8^ +1.1 _ 0.9 for And XVI and v_r=-264.3 2.5 and _v=6.5^ +3.3 _ 2.7 for And XVII. We measure average spectroscopic metallicities for both dSphs, finding =-2.0 0.1 for And XVI and =-1.7 0.1 for And XVII. When comparing their properties to those of other LG dSphs, we find they are consistent with established trends between size, luminosity, chemistry, and mass.

gmw_00008549.name: SDSS J113152.98+295944.8
gmw_00008549.overview: SDSS J113152.98+295944.8 is a red sequence galaxy characterized by an old stellar population [arXiv:2310.20408]. It hosts a dormant active galactic nucleus with a supermassive black hole and has exhibited historical quiescence for at least a century [arXiv:2310.20408].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J113152.98+295944.8 as AT2017bcc's host, modeling it as a red, old-stellar-population galaxy with a dormant AGN, massive SMBH, and century-scale nuclear quiescence.

quotes.quote: We retrieved archival magnitudes of the host galaxy, SDSS J113152.98+295944.8 (or WISEA J113152.97+295944.9), from a number of catalogs.

Modelling archival SDSS magnitudes with Prospector showed the host to be a red galaxy with an old stellar population, and suggested the presence of a dormant AGN with a SMBH mass of a few 10^7-10^8 M _ . Before it was discovered in 2017 the galaxy showed very little activity, varying in luminosity by < 0.1 mags in survey photometry. This historical quiescence was also shown in the weak [ O III ] emission in recent spectra, which implied that the narrow-line emitting regions of the AGN had not been illuminated for at least 100 years.

gmw_00008550.name: NGC 4790
gmw_00008550.overview: NGC 4790 is a nearby flocculent spiral galaxy [arXiv:1304.0095] located approximately 23.6 megaparsecs away [arXiv:1306.5490]. The galaxy features a bright central nucleus [arXiv:1304.0095, arXiv:1306.5490] and exhibits a super-solar metallicity environment [arXiv:1304.0095].

sources.summary: NGC 4790 is identified as SN 2012au’s nearby host galaxy; the supernova lies within 600 pc of its bright nucleus, and host emission indicates a super-solar, flocculent-spiral environment.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:lcurves (left) shows the region around the SN and its host galaxy NGC 4790 ( D = 23.5 0.5 Mpc; Theureau07 ). SN 2012au is located at coordinates = 12^ h 54^ m 52 18 and = -10 14'50 2 (J2000.0), which is is less than 600 pc away in projection from the center of NGC 4790's bright central nucleus.

The relative strengths of narrow lines from coincident host galaxy emission in the nebular spectra were measured to estimate the explosion site metallicity using the method described in Sanders12 . From the N2 diagnostic of PP04 , we meausure an oxygen abundance of ( O/H )+12=8.9 with uncertainty 0.2 dex. Adopting a solar metallicity of ( O/H )_ +12=8.7 Asplund05 , the measurement indicates that SN 2012au exploded in an environment of super-solar metallicity around Z 1-2 Z_ .

These properties are very different from SN 2012au, which was far less luminous ( M_B 18 mag), produced an order of magnitude less ^ 56 Ni, and was located in a nearby flocculent spiral we estimate to have super-solar metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4790 as the 23.6 Mpc host of SN 2012au, notes its nucleus may contaminate late photometry, and finds host-galaxy extinction negligible.

quotes.quote: SN 2012au was discovered by Catalina Real-Time Transient Survey SNHunt project on 2012 Mar 14 UT 2012CBET.3052....1H in NGC 4790 d=23.6 0.5 Mpc; 1988Sci...242..310T,2007A&A...465...71T , and subsequently classified as SN Ib 2012CBET.3052....2S,2012ATel.3968....1S,2013arXiv1304.0095M .
It is noted that the bright nucleus of the host galaxy exists only 5'' east from this SN and it may contaminate our photometry in the latest phase.

For the extinction within the host galaxy, we place a limit as E(B-V)_ host 0.035 mag from the upper-limit of the equivalent width of Na 1 D absorption line 2012MNRAS.426.1465P in the averaged spectra. The extinctions in the host galaxy is thus negligibly small ( 0.1 mag in V band)

gmw_00008551.name: TXS 1515 273
gmw_00008551.overview: TXS 1515 273 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176] located at a redshift of z = 0.1285 [arXiv:2012.05176]. Radio observations reveal a core-jet morphology pointing toward the east [arXiv:2310.10206] and a one-sided jet structure [arXiv:2310.10206].

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 1515 273 as a BL Lac with unknown prior redshift and obtains a high-S/N spectrum showing z=0.1285 absorption and emission lines.

quotes.quote: TXS 1515 273 has recently been recognised as a BL Lac Lef17 . An upper limit to its redshift, z 1.1 has been established from the lack of detection of the Ly break in UVOT and SARA photometry Kau18 . Moreover a low S/N spectrum taken by Pen17 with the Goodman Spectrograph at the SOAR telescope, did not show any feature, leaving its redshift unknown.

We obtained a high S/N spectrum of TXS 1515 273 (see Fig. fig_spec1 , fourth row, left) in which absorption and emission lines at z =0.1285 are visible along with a strong continuum. In absorption we detect with good confidence CaHK, CaIG, Mgb and NaID.

sources.summary: The paper says TXS 1515 273 was detected by MAGIC during a Fermi flare, has a one-component MOJAVE jet, and shows an eastward TANAMI core-jet morphology.

quotes.quote: 1515 273: TXS 1515 273 was detected by MAGIC mirzoyan19 during a ray flare by Fermi cutini19 . MOJAVE images reveal a one-sided jet structure that can be modeled by one component with an apparent speed of 0.73 0.18 lister21 . Our S-band images also show a core-jet morphology pointing towards the east.

gmw_00008552.name: WNB0722.4+4129
gmw_00008552.overview: WNB0722.4+4129 is a radio galaxy located near the center of the Abell 580 galaxy cluster [arXiv:0705.3209]. The source is characterized as a small, asymmetric double radio source that lacks a detected core [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: The source is described as a small asymmetric double with no detected core at high frequency, fully resolved out in B-array maps, and lying near the centre of Abell A 580.

quotes.quote: WNB0722.4+4129: a small asymmetric double without a core. It is completely resolved out in the B-array observations at 5 and 8.4 GHz.
The radio source is located near the centre of the Abell cluster A 580, which has also been detected in the RASS.

gmw_00008553.name: WNB0951.9+6037
gmw_00008553.overview: WNB0951.9+6037 is a radio source featuring structured northern and southern lobes with steep spectral indices [arXiv:0705.3209]. It also contains a central component, though its steep spectrum makes it unlikely to be an active core [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: The paper describes steep, structured lobes and a central component whose spectrum makes it unlikely to be an active core; RASS shows faint coincident X-ray emission but no known nearby cluster.

quotes.quote: In WNB0951.9+6037 the northern lobe (or tail) has _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 1.2 in the centre, and steepens going outwards in both directions to values of _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 1.9 and _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 2.2 at the respective ends. The south lobe has a uniform spectral index of _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 1.7 . Note that also the central component of this source has a steep spectrum, with 0.9 .

WNB0951.9+6037: the central component is detected in the B-array observation at 8.4 GHz but not at 4.8 GHz due to the higher rms noise on the 4.8 GHz map. Since it has _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 0.9 and _ 1.4 ^ 8.4 = 1.2 it is difficult to consider this an active core. At 327 MHz and 1.4 GHz the source structure is very similar, the northern component being slightly more elongated at 327 MHz.

gmw_00008554.name: WNB1023.3+2959
gmw_00008554.overview: WNB1023.3+2959 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a small, asymmetric double structure without a core [arXiv:0705.3209]. The galaxy appears to be isolated, with no known cluster within a ten-arcminute radius and no detected X-ray emission [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: The source is a small asymmetric double without a core, resolved out at high frequency, with no RASS X-ray emission or known cluster within 10 arcmin and appearing isolated.

quotes.quote: WNB1023.3+2959: a small asymmetric double without core. It is completely resolved out in the B-array observations at 5 and 8.4 GHz.
No X-ray emission is seen down to the sensitivity level of the RASS; no cluster is known within a 10 radius from the radio galaxy.
Although about half of the dying sources are located in clusters, there are some (WNB1023.3+2959, WNB1150.0+3749 and WNB1127.5+4927) which appear to be isolated.

gmw_00008555.name: WNB1127.5+4927
gmw_00008555.overview: WNB1127.5+4927 is a classical double radio galaxy with no detected core [arXiv:0705.3209]. Its radio lobes exhibit a spectral index that steepens outward from the core region, and modeling indicates a high relic-age fraction [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1127.5+4927 is treated as a classical double with no detected core; its lobes have nearly constant spectral indices, and CI_OFF modeling finds a high relic-age fraction.

quotes.quote: WNB1127.5+4927: a classical double source with a compact (apparently background) source superimposed on the western lobe. No core has been detected. At 5 and 8.4 GHz (B-array) only the background source is detected. The double source structure is very similar at 327 MHz and 1.4 GHz, but the background source is not detected at 327 MHz due to its flat spectral index ( 0.2 ).

On the other hand, in WNB1127.5+4927 and WNB1150.0+3749 the spectral index steepens going from the regions near the core outward. Thus, it is plausible that the electrons were injected near the core and subsequently flowed outward. We fitted the spectral index trend in WNB1127.5+4927 with the CI _ OFF model and obtained a minimum break frequency of _ b (1800 250) MHz with t_ OFF /t_ s >0.79 .

gmw_00008556.name: WNB1150.0+3749
gmw_00008556.overview: WNB1150.0+3749 is a radio galaxy characterized by an amorphous, coreless radio structure [arXiv:0705.3209]. Its spectral index steepens from the center toward its outer regions, indicating the galaxy is in an aged relic phase [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1150.0+3749 has amorphous, coreless radio structure, steepening spectral indices toward its outer parts, and an MJP fit indicating an aged relic phase comparable to the active phase.

quotes.quote: WNB1150.0+3749: a source with amorphous structure. It may be a small double source without a core and is completely resolved out in the B-array observations at 5 and 8.4 GHz. At 327 MHz the radio structure is very similar to the one at 1.4 GHz.
In WNB1150.0+3749 the spectral index varies from _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 1.6 in the source centre to _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 2.2 in the outer parts.

In WNB1150.0+3749 the fit of the MJP model to the observed spectral index trend gives _ b (260 25) MHz and t_ OFF /t_ s =0.55 0.05 . For this source the CI _ OFF model produces a significantly worse fit of the spectral trend than the MJP model, in good agreement with the fit obtained for the integrated spectrum.

gmw_00008557.name: WNB1257.4+3137
gmw_00008557.overview: WNB1257.4+3137 is a dying radio galaxy with an amorphous structure at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:0705.3209]. Only its central component is visible at 4.8 GHz, and it is associated with a faint X-ray source [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: The paper describes WNB1257.4+3137 as amorphous at 1.4 GHz, with only its central component visible at 4.8 GHz, plus faint RASS X-ray emission and a nearby cluster at matching redshift.

quotes.quote: WNB1257.4+3137: a source with amorphous structure at 1.4 GHz. At 4.8 GHz only the central component is still visible, which, however, is completely resolved out at 8.4 GHz.

A faint X-ray source is present in the RASS image at the position of the dying galaxy. NED lists a cluster (NSCJ125947+312215) at 1 7 from the radio source, with a photometric redshift of z= 0.0585; this is in agreement with the spectroscopic redshift of the radio source, z=0.05172.

gmw_00008558.name: WNB1328.3+6520
gmw_00008558.overview: WNB1328.3+6520 is a compact radio source that resembles a compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:0705.3209]. It exhibits a steep power-law spectrum, which may indicate the aged emission of a continuous injection phase rather than a dying phase [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1328.3+6520 is compact, detected in all B-array observations, and resembles an unusually steep-spectrum CSS source; its steep power-law spectrum may indicate aged continuous-injection emission rather than a dying phase.

quotes.quote: WNB1328.3+6520: a compact radio source with a very steep spectrum. It is detected in all B-array observations. This source resembles a Compact Steep Spectrum (CSS) source, but with an unusually steep spectrum.

The spectra of WNB1328.3+6520 and WNB1458.0+4959 are steep power laws. According to the adopted model we see the aged spectrum of the continuous injection phase. Thus, these sources may not yet be in the dying phase.

gmw_00008559.name: WNB1408.8+4630
gmw_00008559.overview: WNB1408.8+4630 is a radio galaxy with a one-sided morphology where the galaxy's position coincides with a bright, compact component [arXiv:0705.3209]. This bright component is likely a flat-spectrum radio core, while fainter emission extending to the southwest suggests a core-jet or small head-tail structure [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1408.8+4630 is described as one-sided, with the galaxy at a bright compact component, fainter southwest emission, a probable flat-spectrum radio core, and extended steep low-frequency emission.

quotes.quote: WNB1408.8+4630: apparently this source has one-sided morphology. The galaxy position coincides with the bright compact component. Much fainter emission extends to the south-west. Perhaps this is a core-jet radio source or, alternatively, a small head-tail source. The bright component has _ 5.0 ^ 8.4 = 0.2 and is probably the radio core.

The very steep spectrum low frequency emission probably comes from an extended component that is not seen at frequencies >1 GHz. However, the integrated spectrum shows flattening at high frequencies due to the presence of the flat-spectrum core.

gmw_00008560.name: WNB1438.0+3720
gmw_00008560.overview: WNB1438.0+3720 is a small, bent radio galaxy that may possess a wide-angle tail morphology [arXiv:0705.3209]. High-resolution observations reveal a detected core component, which causes the galaxy's integrated spectrum to flatten at high frequencies [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1438.0+3720 shows high-frequency spectral flattening from a core-jet component; it is described as a small bent radio source with a detected core and possible WAT morphology.

quotes.quote: Finally, WNB1408.8+4630, WNB1438.0+3720 and WNB2317.4+4234 show evident flattening of the integrated spectrum at high frequency. This flattening is due to the presence of a core-jet component that dominates the spectrum at the highest frequencies. This is confirmed by the images at higher resolution (not shown), in which only the core is visible.
WNB1438.0+372: a small bent radio source at 1.4 GHz. A core ( _ 5.0 ^ 8.4 = 0.4 ) is detected at higher frequencies. This radio source may be a small wide angle tail (WAT).

gmw_00008561.name: WNB1458.0+4959
gmw_00008561.overview: WNB1458.0+4959 is a very compact radio galaxy that is detected at 1.4 and 5.0 GHz but remains undetected at 8.4 GHz [arXiv:0705.3209]. The source resembles a compact steep-spectrum galaxy, displaying a steep power-law spectrum that may represent the aged emission of a continuous injection phase [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB1458.0+4959 is very compact, undetected at 8.4 GHz, and resembles an unusually steep CSS source; its steep power-law spectrum may be aged continuous-injection emission rather than a dying phase.

quotes.quote: WNB1458.0+4959: a very compact radio source at 1.4 and 5.0 GHz. It is not detected at 8.4 GHz. This source resembles a CSS source, but with an unusually steep spectrum.

The spectra of WNB1328.3+6520 and WNB1458.0+4959 are steep power laws. According to the adopted model we see the aged spectrum of the continuous injection phase. Thus, these sources may not yet be in the dying phase.

gmw_00008562.name: WNB2317.4+4234
gmw_00008562.overview: WNB2317.4+4234 is a head-tail radio galaxy associated with the galaxy NGC 7618 [arXiv:0705.3209]. Its tail is very weak, and a core-jet component dominates and flattens its integrated spectrum at high frequencies [arXiv:0705.3209].

sources.summary: WNB2317.4+4234 is a head-tail radio source associated with NGC 7618; only its weak head remains visible at high frequency, and its spectrum flattens due to an active core.

quotes.quote: WNB2317.4+4234: a head-tail source. The tail is very weak and only the head is detected at 5 and 8.4 GHz. At 327 MHz the tail cannot be seen because the rms noise of the map is rather high. The head has _ 0.33 ^ 1.4 = 0.7 and _ 5.0 ^ 8.4 =0.5 . The associated galaxy is NGC 7618.

Finally, WNB1408.8+4630, WNB1438.0+3720 and WNB2317.4+4234 show evident flattening of the integrated spectrum at high frequency. This flattening is due to the presence of a core-jet component that dominates the spectrum at the highest frequencies. This is confirmed by the images at higher resolution (not shown), in which only the core is visible.

gmw_00008563.name: 2MASX J23453268-0449256
gmw_00008563.overview: 2MASX J23453268-0449256 is an extremely massive, rapidly rotating spiral galaxy that launches relativistic jets [arXiv:2010.15131]. Deep observations have detected a hot gaseous halo extending out to 160 kpc, as well as X-ray emission associated with its northern and southern radio lobes [arXiv:2010.15131].

sources.summary: Deep XMM observations of J2345-0449 detect a hot gaseous halo to 160 kpc, estimate substantial gas and baryon masses, and find lobe-associated X-rays likely from inverse Compton scattering.

quotes.quote: The object 2MASX J23453268-0449256 (hereafter J2345-0449) is an extremely massive, rapidly rotating, relativistic-jet-launching spiral galaxy. Its radio and optical properties have been studied by Bagchi2014 , using the IUCAA Girawali Observatory 2m telescope. The kinematics of the optical Balmer H line reveals an extremely large rotation speed, V_ rot =371/ (i)=429 30 km s ^ 1 , in the asymptotic flat region at 10 kpc from the galactic center.

We have presented a deep XMM Newton observation of the extremely massive, rapidly rotating, relativistic-jet-launching spiral galaxy J2345-0449. We have been able to detect diffuse X-ray emission from the hot gaseous halo surrounding the galaxy out to about 160 kpc, out to about 35 per cent of r_ 200 . Beyond this radius, we have extrapolated our measurements to the virial radius.

Furthermore, we have also detected X-ray emission associated with the northern and southern lobes, possibly attributed to IC scattering of CMB photons by a population of the radio-synchrotron-emitting electrons. Fitting X-ray emission with the standard isothermal model, we have found that the enclosed gas mass within 160 kpc, the maximum radius out to which the X-ray emission is detected, is 1.15_ 0.24 ^ +0.22 10^ 11 M _ .

gmw_00008564.name: NGC 4562
gmw_00008564.overview: NGC 4562 is a companion galaxy located at an estimated distance of 12.5 Mpc [arXiv:1210.0609]. It has a total neutral hydrogen mass of 3.5 × 10^8 solar masses, with modeled gas flux extending to a radius of 10 kpc [arXiv:1210.0609].

sources.summary: The paper derives a basic model for companion NGC 4562, estimating its distance, H i mass, modeled radial extent, scale height, inclination, slight warp, and flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: While NGC 4562 is not our primary target, some information may be extracted from a basic model. We assume a distance of 12.5 Mpc based on values obtained from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). The total H i mass for this galaxy (including a factor of 1.36 to correct for He) is 3.5 10 ^ 8 M_ . Our models include flux extending out to a radius of 2.75' (10 kpc). This small angular size limits our modeling capabilities in this case.

gmw_00008565.name: IC 3571
gmw_00008565.overview: IC 3571 is a companion galaxy located north of NGC 4565 [arXiv:1210.0609, arXiv:1312.2008]. The two galaxies are interacting, as evidenced by a weak bridge of material connecting them and a warp asymmetry in NGC 4565 [arXiv:1210.0609, arXiv:1312.2008].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 3571 as a companion near NGC 4565, estimates its H i mass and size, and links it to a bridge and warp asymmetry.

quotes.quote: No model is created for IC 3571. However, assuming approximately the same distance as NGC 4565, its H i mass is found to be 5.6 10 ^ 7 M_ and the extent of its largest axis is approximately 1.8' (5.8 kpc).
