gmw_00008622.overview: J2312+3847 is a high-redshift core-jet quasar with an optical magnitude of 17.5 [arXiv:1111.3710]. The source exhibits a triple radio morphology at high frequencies, featuring a flat-spectrum radio core and a smoothly bending jet [arXiv:1111.3710].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J2312+3847 as a high-redshift core-jet quasar with a triple VLBI morphology, flat-spectrum core, bent jet components, and measured superluminal motions.

quotes.quote: Core-jet source J2312+3847 is a high-redshift ( z =2.17; 1 mas = 8.171 pc) quasar with an optical magnitude of 17.5 . The source exhibits a double-component structure in northeast-southwest direction at frequencies below 8 GHz, and the two components have almost equal flux densities (VCS archive). At 8.4 and 15.4 GHz, the southwest component is resolved into three sub-components, and the whole source displays a triple morphology Figure fig:j2312 : the current paper; Hen95 . The radio structure of the source can be fitted with four components, namely A, B, C and D from northeast to southwest.

D has a flat spectrum with a spectral index ^ 15.4GHz _ 8,4GHz =-0.3 and therefore is identified as the radio core (Paper II). The position angles of the jet components show an increasing trend from the innermost outwards, likely corresponding to a smooth jet bending. Linear regression fitting gives a proper motion of A, 29.5 4.6 as yr ^ 1 (or 2.5 0.4 ) along a position angle 54 .

gmw_00008623.name: GS9422
gmw_00008623.overview: GS9422 is a young, low-mass star-forming galaxy at a redshift of 5.943 [arXiv:2404.02194, arXiv:2404.06543]. While some studies suggest its spectrum is dominated by nebular emission from an extremely top-heavy stellar population [arXiv:2311.02051], other models indicate it is a normal galaxy hosting an obscured type-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.02194].

sources.summary: The paper maps GS9422 with JWST data, arguing its continuum and emission-line morphologies favor a normal high-redshift galaxy hosting an obscured type-2 AGN.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on an individual object, GS9422, a galaxy at z_ spec =5.943 with exquisite data from the JADES and JEMS surveys, including 14-band JWST/NIRCam photometry and deep NIRSpec prism and grating spectroscopy. We map the continuum emission and nebular emission lines across the galaxy on 0.2-kpc scales. GS9422 has been claimed to have nebular-dominated continuum and an extreme stellar population with top-heavy initial mass function. We find clear evidence for different morphologies in the emission lines, the rest-UV and rest-optical continuum emission, demonstrating that the full continuum cannot be dominated by nebular emission.

While multiple models reproduce the spectrum reasonably well, our preferred model with a type-2 active galactic nucleus (AGN) and local damped Ly- (DLA) clouds can explain both the spectrum and the wavelength-dependent morphology. The AGN powers the off-planar nebular emission, giving rise to the Balmer jump and the emission lines, including Ly- , which therefore does not suffer DLA absorption. A central, young stellar component dominates the rest-UV emission and -- together with the DLA clouds -- leads to a spectral turn-over. A disc-like, older stellar component explains the flattened morphology in the rest-optical continuum. We conclude that GS9422 is consistent with being a normal galaxy with an obscured, type-2 AGN -- a simple scenario, without the need for exotic stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS+53.12175-27.79763 as a z=5.943 nebular-dominated galaxy whose Balmer jump, UV turnover, and two-photon continuum imply an extraordinarily top-heavy IMF.

quotes.quote: Among this sample we identify one object, JADES-GS+53.12175-27.79763 ( \ hereafter), at z=5.943 , that exhibits a clear spectral discontinuity (Figure  fig:balmer_jump ) of 15.0 0.9  nJy (observed frame) at the location of the Balmer jump ( _ rest = 3645   ). This feature arises due to electron recombinations with ionized hydrogen to the first excited state and only appears in spectra of galaxies that contain young stellar populations with high ionizing photon production efficiencies ( _ ion ).

The fact that the predicted continuum from an independent temperature measurement nearly perfectly matches the observed continuum indicates that the UV turnover is undoubtedly a detection of the two-photon nebular continuum: the spectrum of \ is entirely dominated by nebular emission. In order for both the two-photon and free-bound continua to dominate over the stellar spectrum in the rest-frame UV and optical, the source population must be emitting significantly more ionizing photons than standard SSP models, necessitating blackbody temperatures of T 90,000  K (see Methods), much hotter than massive O stars (up to 50,000 K; ).

For the two-photon continuum to remain dominant, we require 112  M _ of a normal stellar population per one low-metallicity massive star, or 137  M _ per one Wolf-Rayet star. This necessitates 35 times, or 9.5 times, more massive stars compared to a typical IMF in the low-metallicity massive star scenario, or Wolf-Rayet star scenario, respectively (Methods). Either scenario implies that the IMF in \ must be extraordinarily top-heavy.

sources.summary: The paper finds GS9422 is a young, low-mass, highly ionized z=5.943 star-forming galaxy whose strong DLA likely arises in nearby circumcluster gas, not the galaxy itself.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive spectro-photometric analysis of the galaxy GS9422 from the JADES GTO survey located at z=5.943 , anomalously showing a simultaneous strong \ emission feature and damped \ absorption (DLA), based on NIRSpec and NIRCam observations. The best-fit modelling of the spectral energy distribution (SED) reveals a young, low-mass ( log (M_ /M_ ) = 7.8 0.01 ) galaxy, with a mass-weighted mean age of the stellar population of (10.9^ +0.07 _ 0.12 ) Myr. The identified strong nebular emission lines suggest a highly ionized ( O_ 32 = 59 ), low-metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H ) = 7.78 0.10 ) star-forming galaxy with a star-formation rate SFR = ( 8.2 2.8 ) M_ yr^ 1 over a compact surface area A_e = 1.85 kpc ^ 2 , typical for galaxies at this epoch. This corresponds to an intense SFR surface density of ( _ SFR / M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) = 1.14 0.30 .

We carefully model the rest-frame UV NIRSpec Prism spectrum around the \ edge, finding that the \ emission-line redshift is consistent with the longer-wavelength recombination lines and an escape fraction of f_ esc,Ly = 30 but that the broad DLA feature is not able to converge on the same redshift. Instead, our modelling suggests z_ abs = 5.40 0.10 , the exact redshift of a newly identified proto-cluster in nearby projection to the target galaxy. We argue that most of the \ gas producing the strong \ damping wing indeed has to be unassociated with the galaxy itself, and thus may indicate that we are probing the cold, dense circumcluster medium of this massive galaxy overdensity.

gmw_00008624.name: GMRT one-sided jet source
gmw_00008624.overview: The GMRT one-sided jet source is an active galactic nucleus characterized by a highly asymmetric, one-sided radio jet [arXiv:0705.3801]. This jet structure suggests strong Doppler boosting, making the object a potential blazar-like counterpart to nearby TeV gamma-ray emission [arXiv:0705.3801].

sources.summary: The paper describes a coordinate-identified GMRT one-sided jet source as a possible blazar-like AGN counterpart to the HEGRA/Whipple TeV emission, while cautioning that the association is uncertain.

quotes.quote: A source with a clearly asymmetric one-sided jet is located inside the Whipple ellipse at J2000.0 coordinates 20^h32^m 29 48 and +41^ 35^ 06 7, with a peak flux density of 2.8 mJy (see Table list for details). This object, included in the GMRT catalogue, is labelled with a green circle in Fig. gmrt_map and a more detailed contour plot is presented in Fig. gmrt_map_zoom . Jet one-sideness is usually interpreted in terms of strong Doppler boosting of a relativistic jet flowing close to the line of sight. This is the main fingerprint of blazar active galactic nuclei (AGN) which are well known sources of high energy gamma-rays (see e.g. ).

In this context, one could think of this GMRT one-sided jet source as another potential counterpart candidate for both the HEGRA/Whipple emission deserving further attention in the future. We note here that it could also be consistent with the HEGRA source when taking into account both the statistical and systematic error quoted in . Nevertheless, the extended nature of the HEGRA TeV emission, if confirmed, would be difficult to reconcile with a blazar origin for the radiation and therefore such suggestion has to be considered with caution.

gmw_00008625.name: Draco dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00008625.overview: The Draco dwarf spheroidal galaxy is characterized by an ancient stellar population [arXiv:1805.06934]. Its star formation shut down long ago, leaving a pristine central dark matter density profile that is consistent with a cold dark matter cusp [arXiv:1805.06934].

sources.summary: The paper uses Draco to measure a high central dark-matter density consistent with a CDM cusp, exploiting its ancient stellar population and radial-velocity data.

quotes.quote: We use a new mass modelling method, , to measure the central dark matter density profile of the Draco dwarf spheroidal galaxy. Draco's star formation shut down long ago, making it a prime candidate for hosting a `pristine' dark matter cusp, unaffected by stellar feedback during galaxy formation.

We then apply \ to the real Draco data. We find that Draco has an inner dark matter density of _ DM (150 pc ) = 2.4_ 0.6 ^ +0.5 10^8 M _ kpc ^ 3 , consistent with a CDM cusp. Using a velocity independent SIDM model, calibrated on SIDM cosmological simulations, we show that Draco's high central density gives an upper bound on the SIDM cross section of /m < 0.57 cm ^2 g ^ 1 at 99 confidence.

gmw_00008626.name: SDSS J0029+2544
gmw_00008626.overview: SDSS J0029+2544 is a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1811.03628]. Its reconstructed source structure consists of a main component located on the cusp of the caustic and a second, fainter component [arXiv:1811.03628].

sources.summary: The paper finds a western arc brightness peak in SDSS J0029+2544 and reconstructs a source structure that differs from earlier S16 models.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0029+2544 A particularly intense peak of surface brightness is visible in the images in the arc directly west from the lensing galaxy; this does not have a corresponding high-surface-brightness peak in the counter-image and is only partially recovered in our lens model. Multi-band observations would be required to identify its origin. While the image residuals and the mass parameters are in agreement with those reported by S16, our source has a different structure.

In particular, our source is composed of one main component located on the cusp of the caustic and a second and fainter component lying on its left while in the source reconstructed by S16 the second component is much brighter. We believe the origin of this discrepancy to lie on the specific choice of masking and related regularisation effects.

gmw_00008627.name: SDSS J0113+0250
gmw_00008627.overview: SDSS J0113+0250 is a strong gravitational lensing system composed of three lens galaxies [arXiv:1811.03628]. The background source galaxy in this system exhibits a particularly clumpy profile [arXiv:1811.03628].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0113+0250 as a three-lens system with a clumpy source, finding that adding the third lens improves evidence and residuals.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0113+0250 This system is composed of three lenses, with the two secondary ones bending the arc respectively in the north and southeast direction. The third lens is located at a projected distance of approximately 2 arcsec from the main lens. For all three deflectors, the brightness profile is fitted using only one S ersic component. The source has a particularly clumpy profile. Unlike S16, we find a significant improvement in terms of the Bayesian evidence and image residuals when the third lens is added to the mass distribution.

gmw_00008628.name: SDSS J0918+4518
gmw_00008628.overview: SDSS J0918+4518 is a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1811.03628]. It features two deflector galaxies of approximately equal lensing strength, both of which are nearly round with axis ratios of about 0.9 [arXiv:1811.03628].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J0918+4518 has lens light, mass, and source structures that diverge from S16, with two nearly round deflectors of similar strength.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0918+4518 Both the light and mass models of the lens are in disagreement with those reported by S16. The structure of the source is also significantly different, as expected from the divergent lens models. In particular, according to our model, both deflectors have approximately equal lensing strength and are both almost round with an axis ratio of q 0.9 .

Instead, one of the lenses in the model by S16 is strongly elliptical, with an axis ratio of q=0.132 , and has a different lensing strength. When using the lens parameters by S16 to model this system, we find that our model is preferred with a difference in Bayesian evidence of E 200. Moreover, our residuals are less prominent, and our results are consistent between our two independent lens modelling codes.

gmw_00008629.name: SDSS J0918+5104
gmw_00008629.overview: SDSS J0918+5104 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a systemic redshift of z = 2.4000 [arXiv:1912.04033]. The galaxy is strongly gravitationally lensed and magnified [arXiv:1912.04033], featuring a compact reconstructed source structure [arXiv:1811.03628] and strong nebular emission lines [arXiv:1912.04033].

sources.summary: The paper describes BG0918+5104 as a magnified lensed LAE at z_sys=2.4000 with strong nebular lines and likely the lowest stellar metallicity in the sample.

quotes.quote: These are SDSS J020121.39+322829.6, SDSS J074249.68+334148.9, SDSS J075523.52+344539.5, and SDSS J091859.21+510452.5 (hereafter BG0201+3228, BG0742+3341, BG0755+3445, and BG0918+5104, where ``BG'' stands for BELLS GALLERY). The LAEs BG0201+3228, BG0742+3341, and BG0918+5104 are magnified by factors of = 15 3 , 16 3 , and 18 3 , respectively shu2016b , and show similar lensed morphologies, with bright extended arcs and relatively compact counter-images on the other side of the lens in a cusp configuration.

For BG0918+5104, several nebular emission lines are detected with high significance ( 5 ), including the O iii ] 1660,1666 , and Si iii ] 1882,1892 doublets, for which we derive the systemic redshift of z_ sys = 2.4000 0.0003 (see Figure fig:fs ). The nebular C iii ] 1908 emission is also detected with high significance, but not the corresponding [ C iii ] 1906 line, whose profile is highly affected by the absorption of the Mg ii 2796,2803 doublet from an intervening absorption system at z = 1.312 0.001 (see Appendix low-z for more details). In addition, the fine-structure emission line Si ii* 1533 is also detected at z_ FS = 2.4004 0.0006 , consistent with the systemic redshift from H ii regions.

For BG0918+5104, both models with Z_ * / Z_ = 0.05 and 0.20 provide good fits to the GTC spectrum with ^ 2 /d.o.f. = 1.14 and 1.07 , respectively, and a between these two models of only 0.37. This suggests that the metallicity of BG0918+5104 should lie between these two values, being likely the lowest-metallicity LAE in our sample, which in turn might explain the strong nebular emission seen in its spectrum (see Figure fig:fs ).

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J0918+5104 requires stronger external shear and ellipticity than S16, with a compact reconstructed source closer to the caustic.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0918+5104 We find that a large external shear had to be allowed to account for the general lensing configuration of this system and the displacement of the counter-image towards the southwest direction. We find that the foreground object to the west of the primary lens is not contributing significantly to the lensing signal, and we did not include its contribution into the final mass model. However, we had to take into account for its brightness distribution which was modelled with two S ersic profiles, while only one component was necessary to account for the lens galaxy emission. Our mass model requires a stronger ellipticity and a stronger external shear in comparison with the one presented by S16. We remodelled this dataset with an SIE profile of fixed axis ratio q=0.985 and fixed shear strength = 0.18 , as determined by S16, and found that our model is preferred by the Bayesian evidence, with a E 300 .
Finally, we remark that our reconstructed source appears more compact and closer to the caustic. This result is probably due to the difference between the two mass models.

gmw_00008630.name: SDSS J1529+4015
gmw_00008630.overview: SDSS J1529+4015 is a strong gravitational lens system containing a central lens galaxy [arXiv:1811.03628]. The system features two distinct background sources at different redshifts, which are lensed into two separate sets of arcs [arXiv:1811.03628].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1529+4015 as a lens system containing two sources at different redshifts, masking the fainter second image set from lens reconstruction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1529+4015 This system is composed of two sources at two different redshifts, as can be seen from the different image separation of the two sets of arcs in Fig. fig:sample . The first source corresponds to the two bright images on the opposite sides of the lens galaxy, and the other source is lensed into the faint arc on the right with the counter-image located below the bright image on the left. Since the second set of images is too faint to be used to reconstruct the lens model it is masked out of the data.

gmw_00008631.name: VCC0608
gmw_00008631.overview: VCC0608 is a bright Virgo dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185] that exhibits a boxy shape [arXiv:2007.05021] and disky isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It contains an old stellar population with a high neutral hydrogen gas content [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185], and shows a strong kinematic misalignment that may stem from tidal interactions with Messier 100 [arXiv:2007.05021].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 608 (NGC 4323) as a bright, gas-rich Virgo dwarf elliptical with red color, old stellar population, exponential near-infrared profile, disky isophotes, and high HI content.

quotes.quote: VCC 608 (NGC 4323), classified as a dE4, N in the VCC, is the second brightest Virgo dwarf elliptical detected in neutral hydrogen, with M _ B = -16.6 . It was first detected in HI by Huchtmeier Richter (1986). VCC 608 also has a fairly red color with (B-V) = 0.87 0.06 (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), consistent with an old stellar population (see the argument in 3.5.2). Ryden et al. (1999) determined that this galaxy has a mean ellipticity of 0.39 with isophotal fits giving < a _ 4 /a > values consistent with a disky structure.

Gavazzi et al. (2001) fit surface photometry in the near-IR H(1.6 ) band, finding a good exponential fit, and a low concentration of light, further confirmation that this is morphologically similar to a dwarf galaxy. VCC 608 also has a ratio 2.5, based on its HI line width. It also contains a high HI gas content of 10 ^ 9 , and has one of the highest HI gas mass fractions in our sample (Table 3).

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC0608 as a boxy Virgo dE with strong kinematic misalignment, possibly caused by M100 tidal interactions or by a past nearly edge-on dwarf-disk merger.

quotes.quote: VCC0608 with = 42.9 ^ 12.5 ^ is the second dE in our sample showing significant misalignment between its kinematic and photometric minor axis. The observed kinematic misalignment can be the trace of past or on-going tidal interactions with the massive halo of Messier 100 (projected distance to M100 28.7 kpc at an assumed Virgo distance of 16.5 Mpc). VCC0608 is a boxy-shaped galaxy as its isophotes on the MUSE stacked image show A _ 4 /a 0.02 0.003 at 1 R_ e .

Following the same argument as in , the boxy shape of VCC0608 and its high kinematic misalignment angle may indicate that this galaxy is the remnant of a past nearly edge-on merger of two disk dwarf galaxies . In this case, the inner disk of the galaxy is believed to have formed from gas driven inward during the merger event 2012graham . In that case, a younger stellar population is expected to be observed in the central region of VCC0608.

gmw_00008632.name: VCC1836
gmw_00008632.overview: VCC1836 is an extended dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2308.16768]. It exhibits the highest stellar rotation velocity in its sample [arXiv:2007.05021], a truncated angular-momentum profile [arXiv:2007.05021], and a negative metallicity gradient [arXiv:2308.16768].

sources.summary: VCC1836 is the most extended dE in the sample, only sampled within 0.4 effective radii by MUSE, and has the largest negative metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: VCC1836: This dE, with R _ e = 6.05 kpc, is the most extended member of our sample and MUSE could only sample it up to 0.4 R _ e . Hence, the maps presented in Fig. stellar pops maps2 only show the stellar population properties of its inner regions. VCC1836 is dominated by stellar populations within the age range of 3 to 9 Gyr, the [ M/H ] range of -0.9 to -0.6 dex, and an average [ /Fe ] = 0.30 dex.

All nine dEs investigated in this study show significant negative metallicity gradients (within the range of -0.38 < _ M/H < -0.10), indicating the presence of more metal-rich stellar populations in their central regions. VCC1836 with _ M/H = -0.38 and VCC1896 with _ M/H = -0.10 have the largest and the smallest metallicity gradients, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports VCC1836 as the highest-rotation dE in the sample, with a possible velocity-dispersion gradient, truncated angular-momentum profile, and MUSE coverage limited by its extent.

quotes.quote: We find that the stellar component in our sample of dEs shows rotation with V_ max ranging from 5 km s ^ 1 in VCC 1833 to 37 km s ^ 1 in VCC1836.

A velocity dispersion gradient, although not statistically significant, is observed in VCC 1836, rising from 27.5 3 km s ^ 1 in the central regions to more than 40.0 18.0 km s ^ 1 in the outskirts of the galaxy.
The _ R profile of VCC1836 shows a truncation at about 0.40 R_ e . This galaxy is the most extended member of our sample and the MUSE field of view is too small for sampling it to 1 R_ e .

gmw_00008633.name: BB1
gmw_00008633.overview: BB1 is a background emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2007.05021]. It is projected near the galaxy VCC 1836 and has a measured redshift of z = 0.5528 based on its nebular emission lines [arXiv:2007.05021].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BB1 as a background emission-line galaxy projected near VCC1836, extracting its spectrum and measuring redshift z=0.5528 from nebular emission lines.

quotes.quote: We extracted the spectrum of this particular region (hereafter BB1) within an aperture with a radius of 5 pixels, corresponding to an area of 3 arcsec ^2 . We subtracted the possible light contamination of VCC 1836 by subtracting a spectrum extracted within an annulus of the same size around the detected source. The final spectrum is plotted in the right panel of Fig. BB1 . We measured the redshift of this background galaxy, using the Doppler shift of each nebular emission line. Based on the average redshift, this galaxy is located at z = 0.5528 0.0002 (also reported in Table newgalaxies ).

gmw_00008634.name: BB2
gmw_00008634.overview: BB2 is an extended background emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.349 [arXiv:2007.05021]. It has an estimated star formation rate of 0.66 solar masses per year [arXiv:2007.05021].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BB2 as an extended background emission-line galaxy in the VCC0170 cube, extracts its spectrum, measures z=0.349, and estimates its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Another source with strong emission lines was detected in the MUSE cube of VCC 0170 (hereafter BB2). In Fig. BB2 we marked the location of this extended source with a red box. As in the previous case, we extracted the spectrum of this source within a radius of 7 pixels, corresponding to an area of 6 arcsec ^2 .

The sky and the VCC 0170 emission were removed by subtracting a spectrum extracted within an aperture of the same size near the source. The resulting spectrum is plotted in the right panel of Fig. BB2 . We measured the redshift of this source using the nebular emission lines. This background galaxy is at a redshift z = 0.349 0.013.
Using this value, we measured the star formation rate of this background galaxy to be 0.66 M _ yr ^ 1 , following the method of .

gmw_00008635.name: PEARS-N-101687
gmw_00008635.overview: PEARS-N-101687 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z=6.57 [arXiv:1302.7005]. Discovered using slitless Hubble Space Telescope grism spectra, the galaxy is undetected in blue bands and exhibits an asymmetric emission line [arXiv:1302.7005].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PEARS-N-101687 as a z=6.57 Lyman-break galaxy found in deep HST PEARS grism spectra and confirmed by Keck/DEIMOS, with faint blue-band nondetections and a prominent emission line.

quotes.quote: Here we present observations of a galaxy at z=6.57 -- the end of the reioinization epoch --- identified using slitless HST grism spectra from the PEARS survey (Probing Evolution And Reionization Spectroscopically) and reconfirmed with Keck + DEIMOS. This high redshift identification is enabled by the depth of the PEARS survey.

The highest redshift object identified in the PEARS-Wide fields through this process was the galaxy , at equatorial coordinates . This object has magnitude z_ 850 = 26.16 , while it is undetected in the B_ 450 , V_ 606 , and i_ 775 bands. Its grism redshift estimate is z=6.6 0.1 based on the observed location of the Lyman break.

The galaxy shows a prominent \ line at redshift z=6.573 , consistent with the grism-based redshift of z=6.6 0.1 . This line is plainly detected in each of the three masks that have > 1 hour of exposure time. Averaging the spectra from the three useful masks together yields a better detection of the asymmetric line, along with hints of the continuum on the red side of the line.

gmw_00008636.name: ESO 342--G017
gmw_00008636.overview: ESO 342--G017 is an edge-on, late-type low surface brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0201141]. Deep photometry reveals that it hosts a thick stellar disk, which contributes an estimated 20% to 40% of the galaxy's total old stellar disk luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0201141].

sources.summary: The paper reports ultra-deep VLT photometry of ESO 342--G017, detecting a thick disk in this edge-on LSB spiral and estimating its scale height and luminosity contribution.

quotes.quote: Thick Disk in LSB Spiral ESO 342--G017 Neeser et al. We report the detection of a thick disk in the edge-on, low surface brightness (LSB), late-type spiral , based on ultra-deep images in the V and R bands obtained with the VLT Test Camera during Science Verification on UT1. All steps in the reduction procedure are fully described, which, together with an extensive analysis of systematic and statistic uncertainties, has resulted in surface brightness photometry that is reliable for the detection of faint extended structure to a level of V = 27.5 and R = 28.5 . The faint light apparent in these deep images is well-modeled by a thick exponential disk with an intrinsic scale height about 2.5 times that of the thin disk, and a comparable or somewhat larger scale length. Deprojection including the effects of inclination and convolution with the PSF allow us to estimate that the thick disk contributes 20-40 of the total (old) stellar disk luminosity of . To our knowledge, this is the first detection of a thick disk in an LSB galaxy, which are generally thought to be rather unevolved compared to higher surface brightness galaxies.

The total central surface brightness of the (thin + thick) disk is _ R,0 > 23.1 , which places \ securely in the category of low surface brightness (LSB) galaxies. This detection of a thick disk adds to only a few others known in external galaxies (see Sect. 1), and to our knowledge is the first known thick disk in an LSB galaxy. The thick and thin disks of \ have similar scale heights as their corresponding components in the Milky Way, but larger scale lengths.

gmw_00008637.name: HUDF-1
gmw_00008637.overview: HUDF-1 is classified as an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1810.00002]. Photometric analysis reveals that its surface brightness profile reaches the sky level smoothly, exhibiting a nearly exponential shape in its outer regions [arXiv:1810.00002].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HUDF-1 as an elliptical whose ABYSS profiles recover an extension removed in HUDF12 and XDF, producing smoother nearly exponential outskirts.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:HUDF1 we represent the surface brightness profiles of HUDF-1, another one of the elliptical galaxies studied in Buitrago2017 . We found a very similar result as in the case of HUDF-5. The surface brightness profiles of the ABYSS mosaics recover a significant extension that has been completely removed in the case of XDF and HUDF12.

Again, we find that the XDF is the most affected by over subtraction, with 1.5 \ with respect to the ABYSS profile at a surface brightness magnitude of 29 . We recover also a significant amount of light compared to the HUDF12, reaching 1.5 \ at the limiting radius in the F105W and 1 \ in the rest of the images. The XDF profiles show a down-bending break that is not that strong in the HUDF12, and it is completely removed in our version of the mosaics, where the surface brightness profile reaches the sky level smoothly, following a nearly exponential shape.

gmw_00008638.name: ABYSS-1
gmw_00008638.overview: ABYSS-1 is a spiral galaxy located in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field [arXiv:1810.00002]. Surface brightness profiles of the galaxy suggest the presence of an extended component at outer radii, which may represent an extended stellar halo [arXiv:1810.00002].

sources.summary: The paper treats ABYSS-1 as a spiral galaxy whose ABYSS profiles show brighter outskirts and a possible extended component, unlike some previous reductions.

quotes.quote: We continue the analysis with the spiral galaxy ABYSS-1 (see Table table:targets , and bottom left panel of Fig. fig:targets ). In Fig. fig:ABYSS1 we compare the surface brightness profiles based on our own reduction of the HUDF mosaics ( ABYSS ), the HUDF12, and the XDF, for the four different filters (F105W, F125W, F140W and F160W). We summarise the results in several points: As in the case of HUDF-5, the surface brightness profiles from our dedicated mosaics present higher intensity and signal-to-noise ratio on the outskirts, up to radius of R 7-7.5 arcsec.

Similarly to the previous case, the maximum differences in surface brightness range from =0.5-1.5 . The F140W band surface brightness profiles for the ABYSS and XDF version of the mosaics present a more similar shape than in the previous cases. The surface brightness profiles of the four filters of the ABYSS mosaics and the F140W from XDF suggest the presence of an extended component clearly detectable at R>5.5 arcsec, which could be due to PSF effects or an extended stellar halo.

gmw_00008639.name: ABYSS-2
gmw_00008639.overview: ABYSS-2 is a compact spiral galaxy [arXiv:1810.00002]. Deep imaging reveals that the galaxy possesses an extended-light tail that is not visible in previous versions of the Hubble Ultra Deep Field [arXiv:1810.00002].

sources.summary: The paper describes ABYSS-2 as a compact spiral whose profile differences between reductions are smaller, though ABYSS reveals an extended-light tail not seen previously.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:ABYSS2 we show the surface brightness profiles of the spiral galaxy ABYSS-2. Given that the aggressive sky background correction applied on the previous versions of the HUDF affected specially to the most extended objects, the surface brightness profiles of more compact objects should be more similar than the large objects, regardless of the version of the mosaics. Indeed, we find that the structure of the surface brightness profiles is more similar in the case of ABYSS-2 than in the previous ones, especially in comparison with HUDF-5.

The surface brightness differences are reduced to 0.5 \ at the limiting radius, and the relative differences between the HUDF12 and the XDF are notably smaller. Moreover, we still detect that the F140W profile of XDF has a more similar shape to our surface brightness profiles of ABYSS mosaics, suggesting that the over-subtraction could be less aggressive in this filter than in the rest. In addition to these results, we detect a tail of extended light in the ABYSS mosaics (possibly due to PSF scattered light) which dominates the surface brightness profile from R 2-2.5 arcsec and it is not visible on the previous versions on the HUDF.

gmw_00008640.name: ABYSS-3
gmw_00008640.overview: ABYSS-3, also known as CANDELS 15282, is a spiral galaxy with a stellar mass of 10 to the power of 9.23 solar masses [arXiv:1604.04632, arXiv:1810.00002]. Its structure features a clear exponential surface brightness profile with no signs of an inner bulge [arXiv:1810.00002].

sources.summary: The paper presents ABYSS-3 as the smallest selected spiral, with an exponential profile, no inner bulge signs, and profiles consistent across reductions.

quotes.quote: Finally, we analyse the surface brightness profile of ABYSS-3 (see Fig. fig:ABYSS3 ). This is the smallest object of all the six selected galaxies. The surface brightness profile shows a clear exponential decline without deviations (with small signs of a down-bending at R 1.5 arcsec) until the limiting radius.

We find no signs of a bulge at the inner regions. For this object we find a remarkably good agreement between the three versions of the mosaics, obtaining very similar limiting radii for all of them on each band ( R_ lim 2.8-3.2 arcsec) and no significant differences on the surface brightness profiles along all the visible radius. We do not find signs of extended scattered light, in contrast to the profiles of ABYSS-2.

sources.summary: The paper sets CANDELS 15282's H-alpha extinction to zero after a negative derived value and identifies it as the most massive object in its intermediate-mass bin.

quotes.quote: For CANDELS 15282 we got a value A (H ) <0 , thus, we assumed A (H ) =0 .
CANDELS 15282 is the most massive of the galaxies in this mass bin with (M/M_ ) =9.23 0.05 . Its properties are closer to the high-mass galaxies.

gmw_00008641.name: DLA towards Q0302 223
gmw_00008641.overview: DLA towards Q0302 223 is an absorbing galaxy at a redshift of 1.009 [arXiv:1310.6865]. Its absorption system is a triple complex spanning 170 km/s, and its spectrum exhibits numerous emission lines, including a rare high-redshift detection of oxygen III [arXiv:1310.6865].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Q0302 223 absorbing galaxy as a z=1.009 DLA with a 170 km/s absorption complex and many X-Shooter emission lines, including a rare high-redshift detection.

quotes.quote: The DLA towards Q0302 223: the absorber at =1.009 was first reported as a \ system by Petitjean Bergeron (1990), who also detected \ 2586, 2600 and \ 2852. In this system, the \ absorption is a triple complex spanning 170 km/s. An HST Faint Object Spectrograph (FOS) spectrum reveals several strong features from , , , , , \ and \ (Boiss e et al. 1998).

The DLA towards Q0302 223: many different emission lines are detected in the X-Shooter absorbing-galaxy spectrum of this DLA, including , both lines of \ (4960.29 and 5008.24 ), , , \ (3870.91), \ and the \ doublet. Figure f:Q0302_1D_XSH shows portions of the spectra with some of these lines. We note that the detection of \ emission at such redshift is rare.

gmw_00008642.name: DLA towards Q0452 1640
gmw_00008642.overview: DLA towards Q0452 1640 is a high-redshift damped Lyman-alpha absorbing galaxy [arXiv:1310.6865]. Observations of the system have detected numerous emission lines, including a rare detection of oxygen III emission at high redshift [arXiv:1310.6865].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q0452 1640 as a DLA absorbing galaxy with previous absorption-metallicity work, broad X-Shooter line detections, rare high-redshift emission, and consistent metallicity indicators.

quotes.quote: The DLA towards Q0452 1640: Nestor et al. (2008) reported \ and \ detections in this \ absorber using ISIS (Intermediate dispersion Spectrograph and Imaging System) on WHT (William Herschel Telescope). The quasar was first observed at high-resolution by P eroux et al. (2008) who then derived the metallicity of the absorber by fitting 13 components over 230 km/s to the absorption profile. The abundances for these systems are listed in Table t:HI_Metals .

The DLA towards Q0452 1640: in this system, all the strongest emission lines covered by the X-Shooter spectrum are detected with the exception of \ 6550. The detections therefore include , , \ 6585, both lines of , , , , \ and the \ doublet. Again, we note the rare detection of \ emission at such high redshift.

For Q0452 1640, our results show that the three different metallicity indicators lead to consistent results. We therefore compute the mean of the various metallicity indicators obtained from X-Shooter observations.

gmw_00008643.name: sub-DLA towards Q2352 0028
gmw_00008643.overview: The sub-DLA towards Q2352 0028 is an absorbing galaxy at a redshift of z=1.0318 [arXiv:1310.6865]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that the system has a disturbed morphology with interacting clumps and tidal streams stretching toward the quasar sight line [arXiv:1805.05960].

sources.summary: The paper discusses Q2352 0028 as a z=1.0318 sub-DLA absorbing galaxy, reporting component fitting, abundance constraints, limited X-Shooter emission-line detections, and a SINFONI nitrogen-line detection.

quotes.quote: The sub-DLA towards Q2352 0028: Meiring et al. (2009) obtained a Magellan/MIKE spectrum of this quasar to study its =1.0318 sub-DLA. They use 11 components to fit the absorber over v 220 km/s. The corresponding abundances are listed in Table t:HI_Metals .

The sub-DLA towards Q2352 0028: few lines are detected in the X-Shooter spectrum of this absorbing-galaxy. The only reliable detections are from \ and the \ doublet. Interestingly, the line of \ 6585 is significantly detected in the SINFONI data but is not seen in the X-Shooter spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Q2352 0028 DLA counterpart as a disturbed interacting system with tidal streams toward the quasar sight line and a low baryon fraction.

quotes.quote: The Q2352 0028 Field: The HST images show a disturbed morphology pointing towards the quasar. We discover clear tidal streams connecting the two clumps and also stretching towards the QSO sight line. This larger sky coverage of tidally stripped gas favours detecting interacting systems in absorption . Some tidal streams stretch out from the host galaxies and cause the strong absorption feature in the QSO spectrum. Without these tidal features, the CGM around galaxies could be less extended.
More often, the absorbing galaxies are within group structures or show indications of interacting clumps (Q0302 223, Q0452 1640, Q2352 0028).

gmw_00008644.name: multiple image 4a
gmw_00008644.overview: The galaxy multiple image 4a is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:1610.06576]. It is gravitationally lensed behind the galaxy cluster Abell 2261 and has a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of z=3.377 [arXiv:1610.06576].

sources.summary: The paper uses multiple image 4a as a lensed galaxy behind Abell 2261, giving its coordinates, prior photometric redshift, and a Keck/MOSFIRE spectroscopic redshift from emission lines.

quotes.quote: The H-band (14,500--17,770 ) mask included a slit placed on multiple image 4a at (17:22:28.56, +32:08:07.92), using the ID given in 2012ApJ...757...22C . The image was identified initially using the method of 2009MNRAS.396.1985Z , which is the one used here for lens modeling as well. 2012ApJ...757...22C obtained a photometric redshift of 3.48 0.03 for this galaxy, and the lens models presented therein similarly predict z 3.3 . Our spectroscopic observations, shown in Figure fig:spectroscopicredshift , indicate a redshift of z=3.377 based on the [ O ii ] (3727, 3729 ) doublet, and [ N iii ] (3869 ) line, in good agreement with the photometric and initial lens model prediction. This redshift measurement is important, and will allow us to recalibrate (for the first time) the lens model of Abell 2261.

gmw_00008645.name: SDSS J222217.13-071558.9
gmw_00008645.overview: SDSS J222217.13-071558.9 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0563 [arXiv:2202.08268]. It is classified as a green valley galaxy and hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.08268].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J222217.13-071558.9 as the elliptical z=0.0563 host of AT 2020zso, deriving green-valley stellar-population properties and Seyfert/AGN narrow-line diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy (SDSS J222217.13-071558.9) is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0563.

Using the median and 1- confidence intervals of the posteriors we derive a host stellar mass of (M_ /M_ )= 10.11^ +0.02 _ 0.02 , a metallicity of (Z/Z_ )=-0.34^ +0.04 _ 0.07 , A_V=0.23^ +0.03 _ 0.04 mag, t = 1.90^ +1.56 _ 0.94 Gyr, and ( _ sfh ) =2.42^ +0.35 _ 0.32 Gyr. The extinction is roughly consistent with the Galactic foreground extinction of E(B-V) = 0.06 Schlafly2011 . The estimated mass combined with the extinction-corrected rest-frame colour u- = 1.89 0.02 mag places the host galaxy near the “green valley” region Schawinski_2014 of the mass colour diagram, in which the TDE host galaxies seems to be over represented compared to the general galaxy population Law-Smith_17,van_Velzen_21,Hammerstein_21 .

We also use the narrow host galaxy line ratios to put the source on a Baldwin-Philips-Terlevic (BPT) diagram Baldwin . We find that the source falls well into the AGN/Seyfert part of the diagram, in line with the presence of high ionisation narrow emission lines (Figure fig:bpt ). We conclude that this galaxy hosts a Seyfert AGN.

gmw_00008646.name: NGC 5079
gmw_00008646.overview: NGC 5079 is a barred spiral galaxy with an estimated neutral hydrogen mass of 10^9 solar masses [arXiv:2103.03277]. Its extended radio emission and ionized gas tails make it a candidate donor or flyby trigger for the nearby galaxy NGC 5077 [arXiv:2103.03277].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5079 as a barred spiral companion whose gas and radio extension make it a candidate donor or flyby trigger for NGC 5077's accreted gas.

quotes.quote: Based on the orientation of the tails of ionised gas, both Holm 514D and NGC 5079 are candidates for having provided some of the gas for NGC 5077 or for flybys that triggered the accretion of gas from the outskirts of NGC 5077 (leftover gas from a major merger for example). NGC 5079 is a barred spiral galaxy (SB(rs)bc pec devaucouleurs91 ) at a projected distance of 40 kpc and V _ sys = 2242 km/s while for NGC 5077 V _ sys = 2806 km/s. The estimated neutral hydrogen (H I) mass of NGC 5079 is 10^ 9 M _ (although the source is possibly confused in their measurements). Radio observations at 1.4 GHz show that the emission in NGC 5079 is extended in the direction of NGC 5077 wrobel84 .

gmw_00008647.name: CIG 71
gmw_00008647.overview: CIG 71 is a disk galaxy featuring a discontinuous hydrogen disc that is shifted southwards relative to its near-infrared photometric centre [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its older stellar disc is symmetrical in the inner regions but becomes warped and more extended towards the south, where a luminous knot and far-ultraviolet emission are also observed [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper maps CIG 71 with a south-shifted discontinuous H disc, a prominent southern knot, warped old stellar light, and FUV emission spread southward plus a northern cloud.

quotes.quote: CIG 71 (UGC 1391): The H monochromatic map shows a discontinuous disc mainly shifted Southwards from the photometric centre of the NIR band exceeding the radius of the old stellar disc. A prominent and luminous knot is observed at 30 arcsec Southwards from the NIR photometric centre. The K _ s band image shows a symmetrical disc within the ellipse fitted to the 3 brightness level, but the disc becomes more extended and warped towards the South. The FUV emission is concentrated near the galactic disc centre and then spreads Southwards at lower brightness. This map also shows a FUV emission cloud in the North.

gmw_00008648.name: CIG 95
gmw_00008648.overview: CIG 95 is a disk galaxy featuring a central bulge surrounded by an asymmetric, warped old stellar population disk [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its outer structure includes an extended, warped far-ultraviolet disk and a detached hydrogen cloud that extends radially beyond the old stellar disk [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 95 as having a detached H cloud, extended H emission above the disc, an asymmetric warped old stellar disc, and extended warped UV emission.

quotes.quote: CIG 95 (UGC 1733). Figure Fig:maps-c95 : The H map shows a detached cloud from the disc that exceeds radially the old stellar population disc observed in the NIR band emission. The brightest H emission region is much wider than the old stellar disc and exceeds it in the z-direction. The 2.22 m image reveals a central bulge surrounded by an asymmetric warped old stellar population disc that extends Southward. The FUV emission displays an extended and warped disc. The NUV emission traces a well-bound large disc with a brighter Southeastern tip and a sharpened, fainter Northwestern tip.

gmw_00008649.name: CIG 159
gmw_00008649.overview: CIG 159 is a disk galaxy featuring a large, thin warped stellar disk with a central bulge [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its hydrogen emission reveals a discontinuous disk of bright clouds, while its ultraviolet emission displays an asymmetric warped disk that is brighter on its eastern side [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 159 as a warped disc galaxy with discontinuous H clouds, a large thin old stellar disc, faint central FUV clouds, and asymmetric NUV brightness.

quotes.quote: CIG 159 (UGC 3326). Figure Fig:maps-c159 : The H emission displays a discontinuous disc composed of bright clouds uniformly distributed along the major axis with a size similar to that of the K _ s band 1 isophote. The K _ s band image of the galaxy shows a large thin warped disc with a central bulge. The GALEX FUV image shows a few faint clouds mostly at the photometric centre. The NUV emission draws a warped disc brighter on the East side than on the West side. The GALEX NUV and FUV images have low SNR which may yield spurious surface brightness profiles.

gmw_00008650.name: CIG 171
gmw_00008650.overview: CIG 171 is a disk galaxy featuring a mostly symmetric old stellar disk with feathered edges [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its ultraviolet disk is slightly warped, while its ionized gas is densely distributed in the south and forms detached clouds in the north [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper reports asymmetric H brightness in CIG 171, dense southern ionized gas, northern detached clouds, a mostly symmetric old stellar disc, and a slightly warped UV disc.

quotes.quote: CIG 171 (UGC 3474). Figure Fig:maps-c171 : The H emission is brighter in the South than the North likely shadowed by dust and stars emitting in UV. In the South the ionized gas has a dense distribution while in the North it traces detached clouds. The old stellar population disc is mostly symmetric with feathering along the edges. The NUV and FUV emissions trace a slightly warped disc brighter in the North than in the South of the NIR photometric centre. The UV surface brightness profiles are flat in the inner 60 arcsec and fall exponentially outward.

gmw_00008651.name: CIG 183
gmw_00008651.overview: CIG 183 is a galaxy featuring an asymmetric H-alpha emission disc that is displaced to the northeast relative to its old stellar population disc [arXiv:2401.02876]. This stellar disc is mostly symmetrical with a central bulge and two plumes, while the galaxy's ultraviolet emission matches the asymmetric H-alpha structure [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 183 with a northeast-displaced asymmetric H disc, northern bright regions, two H tails, symmetric old stellar light with plumes, and matching UV structure.

quotes.quote: CIG 183 (UGC 3791). Figure Fig:maps-c183 : The H emission displays an asymmetric disc displaced to the Northeast outwards of the old stellar population disc. It also shows two big bright regions located at the North of the photometric centre and several remnant clouds that draw two tails in both sides of the H disc. The 2.22 m emission shows a mostly symmetrical disc with a bulge and two plumes at the ends of the galactic major axis. The NUV and FUV emissions agree with the structure of the H monochromatic map. The maxima of the NUV , FUV and 2.22 m emissions match within a circle of 5 arcsec radius in the galactic centre.

gmw_00008652.name: CIG 201
gmw_00008652.overview: CIG 201 is classified as an SAc disk galaxy that contains an inner pseudoring [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its multiwavelength morphology displays a central, east-pointing cone shape and filamentary structures that suggest the presence of a galactic wind, though H I emission data indicates the galaxy is not interacting [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 201 as a SAc galaxy with matching filamentary multiwavelength structure, an east-pointing central cone, no H I interaction signature, and morphology suggesting a galactic wind.

quotes.quote: All the maps of CIG 201, presented in Figure Fig:maps-c201 , outline the same filamentary structure and display a cone shape at the galactic centre pointing Eastwards reaching different heights above the stellar disc. From SDSS data, classified CIG 201 as a SAc galaxy and identified an inner pseudoring (see Table table:general ). In addition, from H i emission data, determined that this galaxy is not interacting. The multi-wavelength morphology of CIG 201 suggests that the galactic disc may host a galactic wind.

gmw_00008653.name: CIG 329
gmw_00008653.overview: CIG 329 is classified as an SAb galaxy that features an inner pseudoring and a central bulge with a dust lane [arXiv:2401.02876]. Photometric analysis reveals a boxy bulge, while its hydrogen-alpha emission is distributed in a nearly symmetric ring structure [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 329 as an SAb system with nearly symmetric ring-like H emission, an inner pseudoring, a central bulge and dust lane, and a boxy bulge.

quotes.quote: CIG 329 (UGC 5010). Figure Fig:maps-c329 : The H emission is distributed in a nearly symmetric ring structure. Indeed, classified it as a SAb galaxy identifying an inner pseudoring (see Table table:general ). The 2.22 m emission displays a long and symmetric disc with a central bulge and dust lane, with filaments along the edge. Following the photometric analysis of the K _ s band image, this galaxy has a `boxy' bulge ( B_4<0 ). The brightest FUV emission regions agree with the H emission, while the faintest ones draw a filamentary warped disc and few detached clouds in the North.

gmw_00008654.name: CIG 416
gmw_00008654.overview: CIG 416 is a disk galaxy featuring a small stellar disk dominated by a bright central bulge [arXiv:2401.02876]. It exhibits asymmetric, filamentary ultraviolet and hydrogen-alpha emission disks, along with a western tail and detached ionized gas clouds [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper reports CIG 416 has asymmetric filamentary H emission, a western tail, detached ionized gas clouds, a bulge-dominated small disc, and more extended asymmetric UV discs.

quotes.quote: CIG 416 (UGC 5642). Figure Fig:maps-c416 : The H emission map displays an asymmetric filamentary wide and radially extended disc with a tail-shape at the Western tip and a few detached ionized gas clouds. The K _ s band image of this galaxy shows a small disc dominated by a bright central bulge. Beyond the K _ s band isophotal perimeter of the 3 level, the old stellar population disc is asymmetric with a filamentary structure and a tail shape pointing Northwards at the West extreme. The phenomenon triggering the Western tail could be the reason for the non-linear relationship the B_4 parameter and the radius, however, neither the graphics nor the K _ s band image show the presence of a bar. The NUV and FUV images show even more extended asymmetric discs and larger detached clouds than the H emission image.

gmw_00008655.name: CIG 593
gmw_00008655.overview: CIG 593 is a strongly barred galaxy featuring a central bright bulge and an asymmetric, truncated stellar disc [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its ionized gas distribution is also asymmetric and truncated, with faint hydrogen clouds forming an integral-sign arm on the northern side [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 593 as a strongly barred galaxy with faint northern H clouds, an asymmetric truncated ionized gas distribution, an integral-sign arm, and a central bulge.

quotes.quote: CIG 593 (UGC 8598). Figure Fig:maps-c593 : Despite of a long exposure of 223 minutes ( 4 hours), we only detected a few faint H clouds tracing an asymmetric and truncated ionized gas distribution only visible on the North side, slightly displaced with respect to the major axis of the old stellar population. It looks an `integral sign arm' starting from a small bar. Indeed, classified this galaxy as a strongly barred (SB, see Table table:general ). The stellar disc shows a central bright bulge. The most diffuse NIR emission displays an asymmetric and truncated disc.

gmw_00008656.name: CIG 847
gmw_00008656.overview: CIG 847 is a disk galaxy featuring a nearly symmetric old stellar disk [arXiv:2401.02876]. It also hosts a radially extended, asymmetric warped hydrogen disk, which is characterized by a southern ionized-gas tail and a detached northern cloud [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 847 with a radially extended asymmetric warped H disc, southern ionized-gas tail, northern detached cloud, nearly symmetric old stellar disc, and warped NUV disc.

quotes.quote: CIG 847 (UGC 11132). Figure Fig:maps-c847 : The H emission depicts a radially extended asymmetric and warped disc more prominent and brighter towards the South than to the North along the major axis with the maximum located towards the Southeast from the NIR photometric centre. The galaxy has a ionized gas tail-shape at the Southern tip and a detached cloud at the Northern edge. The 2.22 m emission reveals an almost symmetric elongated disc with small warp. The FUV emission only shows large disengaged clouds along the major axis instead of a dense disc giving as a result an inaccurate surface brightness profile. The NUV emission traces a long and warped disc with feathering along the borders of the disc, populating mainly the Northwest and with the maximum immediately to the North of the NIR photometric centre.

gmw_00008657.name: CIG 906
gmw_00008657.overview: CIG 906 is a galaxy featuring a symmetric, elongated old stellar population disk with filaments, a southwest plume, and a well-defined dust lane [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its ultraviolet emission reveals wider, brighter disks with detached clouds, while only faint hydrogen-alpha knots and a northern ionized gas cloud are detected [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper reports CIG 906 has faint H knots, a northern ionized gas cloud, a symmetric old stellar disc with filaments and dust lane, and wider brighter UV discs.

quotes.quote: CIG 906 (UGC 11723). Figure Fig:maps-c906 : After an exposure of 186 minutes ( 3 hours) with FP GHASP, only faint H clouds as knots were detected along the galactic major axis. A faint ionized gas cloud is located over the Northern edge of the stellar disc. The K _ s band image shows an old stellar population disc elongated and symmetrical with some filaments along the disc and a plume at the Southwest edge and a well-defined dust lane. The NUV and FUV emission display wider and brighter discs than the NIR one, both show filaments all along the edges of the disc and even some detached clouds. The surface brightness profiles of NUV and FUV emission show a discontinuity between the ellipses fitted to the 1 and 3 brightness levels of the K _ s band image, this is from 30 and 45 arcsec.

gmw_00008658.name: CIG 922
gmw_00008658.overview: CIG 922 is a galaxy featuring an asymmetric, warped H-alpha emission disc dominated by large detached clouds in its northern region [arXiv:2401.02876]. This ionized gas disc is wider and longer than the old stellar population disc, which displays a central bulge, a northeastern warp, and a southwestern plume [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 922 with a warped asymmetric H disc dominated by northern clouds, a larger ionized gas disc, outer H knots, and warped/plumed old stellar light.

quotes.quote: CIG 922 (UGC 11785). Figure Fig:maps-c922 : The H emission disc is asymmetric and warped, it dominates the Northern galactic region and it is composed by large detached clouds. The ionized gas disc is wider and longer than the old stellar disc. The H cloud furthest North is faint and exceeds radially the old stellar disc tracing an arrow-shape, while to the South there are two H detached bright knots just outside of the NIR disc edge. The old stellar population disc is mostly symmetric and elongated, it has a central bulge. The faintest NIR emission shows a warp at the Northeastern and a plume outside the Southwestern tip of the stellar disc, both peculiarities are pointing to the Southeast.

gmw_00008659.name: CIG 936
gmw_00008659.overview: CIG 936 is an asymmetric disk galaxy featuring a central bulge [arXiv:2401.02876] and a classic Type II edge-on truncation profile [arXiv:2307.09521]. Its structure displays a stellar flare beyond 24 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2307.09521], centrally concentrated warm gas [arXiv:2401.02876], and warped ultraviolet emission tips [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes deep GTC imaging of UGC 11859, finding a Type II edge-on truncation, stellar flare, one-sided warp, blue outer colors, and possible interaction with a faint companion.

quotes.quote: Radial surface brightness profile Fig. fig:UGC11895_radialprofiles presents the g and bands radial surface brightness profiles of UGC 11859 that follow the classic shape of a galactic bright bulge in the inner regions (radius <5  kpc) with a central surface brightnesses of =21.52^ +0.01 _ 0.02 \ and = 20.32^ +0.02 _ 0.03 \ in the g and bands, respectively. Beyond that radius, the disk presents a relatively smooth exponential profile that reaches 24 kpc in radius, where the most notable feature of the surface brightness profile is observed: a clear down-bending break that follows the shape of a edge-on truncation. This break appears in both bands ( g and , Fig. fig:UGC11895_radialprofiles ), indicating that \ has a "classic" Type II profile.

Our analysis shows that at 24 kpc, both g and bands trace a sudden increase in the scale-height of the galactic disk of , from an average value of <h_ z,g >=4.53^ +0.58 _ 0.41 kpc in the band g and <h_ z, >=4.95^ +0.69 _ 0.59 kpc in the band to a peak of <h_ z,g >=11.7^ +6.3 _ 6.2 kpc and <h_ z, >=19.0^ +14.7 _ 6.5 kpc in the g and bands respectively (thus, a statistically significant change of the \ in both bands). Interestingly, the increase in \ values occurs: in both g and bands, in both sides (East and West) of the galaxy (see Fig. fig:flare_sides_galaxy , in Appendix A1:flare_sides ), in all cases, beyond the 24 kpc galactocentric limit, associated with the edge-on truncation of the galaxy radial light profile. These attributes of the vertical profile feature suggest that the break could be related to the presence of a flare in the disk of .

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 936 as a large disc whose K-band and H emission are centrally concentrated, with central cone-shaped warm gas, an asymmetric bulged disc, and warped UV structure.

quotes.quote: CIG 936 (UGC 11859). Figure Fig:maps-c936 : The K _ s band and most of H emissions were detected in the central 30 arcsec of a galaxy with an optical diameter of D_ 25 (B)=185.4 arcsec (see Table table:general and Figure Fig:maps-c936 blue a ). The H monochromatic image shows most of the warm gas compressed at the centre within the old stellar population disc, drawing a cone-shape. The K _ s band image shows an asymmetric disc with a central bulge. The FUV emission draws an extended thin disc (isophotal radius r_ FUV =47.6 arcsec, see Table table:ellipseParams ) whose tips are warped pointing to the Northwest. The NUV disc emission is wider than the FUV one, with filaments mainly along the Southeastern side of the disc with the largest and broadest filament at the galactic centre.

gmw_00008660.name: CIG 1003
gmw_00008660.overview: CIG 1003 is a disk galaxy featuring an elongated, symmetric stellar disc with a central bulge [arXiv:2401.02876]. Its central region contains compressed ionized gas and displays a cone-shaped structure pointing Southwest, which may indicate a galactic wind triggered by a recent interaction [arXiv:2401.02876].

sources.summary: The paper describes CIG 1003 with central compressed ionized gas, cone-shaped H and NIR/UV structure, symmetric stellar disc, matched southern UV/H knots, and possible wind from interaction.

quotes.quote: CIG 1003 (UGC 12304). Figure Fig:maps-c1003 : The ionized gas is compressed in the central region of the disc which presents three bright knots, one at the centre and the other two symmetrically placed 15 arcsec away. The H emission map displays filaments of ionized gas in all the edges, but the most prominent of them are around the central knot tracing a cone-shape pointing to the Southwest. The K _ s band image shows an elongated and symmetric stellar disc with a central bulge. The diffuse NIR emission displays a cone-shape at the galactic centre pointing Southwest and a hook-shaped extension at the Northern tip pointing Northeast. The NUV emission image shows a prominent filament of 15 arcsec length pointing to the Southwest; in addition, the cone-shape in the central region is more noticeable in this emission.

CIG 1003's multiwavelength morphology, position on the region of bona fide isolated galaxies (see Figure Fig:isolation ) and H i classification as not isolated Jones2018 , suggest that it has a galactic wind, possibly due to a recent interaction.

gmw_00008661.name: AS1063-arc
gmw_00008661.overview: AS1063-arc is a gravitationally lensed grand-design spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.6 [arXiv:1802.08451, arXiv:1907.11586]. It features a symmetrical rotation with maximum velocities up to 200 km/s and a bright star-forming region at the edge of one spiral arm [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: The paper describes AS1063-arc as a grand-design spiral with a bright arm-edge star-forming region, smooth rotation near 200 km/s, central dispersion, and clumps not tied to dispersion changes.

quotes.quote: AS1063-arc This galaxy is a beautiful example of a grand design spiral galaxy whose most striking feature is a gigantic and bright \ region at the edge of one spiral arm. The [ ] kinematics of the galaxy was briefly studied by Karman2015 , based on the same MUSE observations used in this paper. They found a smooth velocity field with a symmetrical rotation with maximum velocities of up to 200 km s ^ 1 .

We confirm this result and also derive the velocity dispersion map that, after being corrected for beam-smearing effects, still displays a higher central velocity dispersion. Despite the presence of several star forming clumps visible in the HST imaging, none of these coincide with any increases or decreases in the velocity dispersion (Fig. fig:2d_kin ). This is true with or without the beam smearing correction.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes AS1063-arc as a gravitationally lensed z=0.6 disc, using MUSE metallicity maps to test resolved star-forming and mass-metallicity relations.

quotes.quote: For the two lowest redshift galaxies analysed here, AS1063-arc and A370-sys1 (lensed galaxies in the clusters AS1063 and A370), we use MUSE data to derive metallicity maps from optical emission lines ( , , , and ).

We find that both the rSFMS and rMZR are in place for galaxies AS1063-arc ( z=0.6 ) and A370-sys1 ( z=0.7 ), although with different slopes as the ones observed in the local Universe (Fig.  fig:scaling_relations ). For these two galaxies, we also find a correlation between the residuals of the rSFMS and the rMZR ( =0.19 and 0.67, Fig.  fig:scaling_relations ), which might indicate the presence of a rFMZ. We notice however, that we find the opposite correlation (with higher rSFMS residuals corresponding to higher rMZR residuals) to what is found in other works. Moreover, the correlations are different for the two galaxies tested, which suggests that the relation evolves with redshift. A larger sample is needed in order to confirm these results.

gmw_00008662.name: A370-sys1
gmw_00008662.overview: A370-sys1, also known as "the dragon," is a gravitationally lensed disk galaxy at a redshift of z=0.7 [arXiv:1907.11586, arXiv:1802.08451]. It features blue spiral arms populated by numerous star-forming regions, a possible central bar, and regular gas rotation [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes A370-sys1 as a gravitationally lensed z=0.7 disc, using MUSE metallicity maps to test resolved star-forming and mass-metallicity relations.

quotes.quote: For the two lowest redshift galaxies analysed here, AS1063-arc and A370-sys1 (lensed galaxies in the clusters AS1063 and A370), we use MUSE data to derive metallicity maps from optical emission lines ( , , , and ).

We find that both the rSFMS and rMZR are in place for galaxies AS1063-arc ( z=0.6 ) and A370-sys1 ( z=0.7 ), although with different slopes as the ones observed in the local Universe (Fig.  fig:scaling_relations ). For these two galaxies, we also find a correlation between the residuals of the rSFMS and the rMZR ( =0.19 and 0.67, Fig.  fig:scaling_relations ), which might indicate the presence of a rFMZ. We notice however, that we find the opposite correlation (with higher rSFMS residuals corresponding to higher rMZR residuals) to what is found in other works. Moreover, the correlations are different for the two galaxies tested, which suggests that the relation evolves with redshift. A larger sample is needed in order to confirm these results.

sources.summary: The paper describes A370-sys1 as the dragon, a gravitational arc with multiple magnified images, blue spiral arms, many star-forming regions, regular gas rotation, and a possible bar signature.

quotes.quote: A370-sys1 A370-sys1, sometimes called "the dragon", was the first gravitational arc to be spectroscopically confirmed Lynds1986, Soucail1988 . It extends over 23" in the sky and is composed of several multiple images: the "head" of the dragon is the complete image of the galaxy, and has a somewhat low ( 10) magnification factor, while the "tail" (most elongated structure) is composed by two highly magnified ( 35) multiple images, mirroring each other, and that are only a fraction of the complete galaxy (details in Richard2010a ). Blue spiral arms are clearly seen in the complete image, with a dozen of strongly star forming regions populating them. As for AS1063-arc, the map shows no particular features at the location of these clumps. The analysis of kinematics also reveals a regular gas rotation, with a twist of the zero-velocity usually associated to the presence of a bar, unknown before this analysis.

gmw_00008663.name: A2390-arc
gmw_00008663.overview: A2390-arc is a gravitationally lensed galaxy that overlaps with a foreground object [arXiv:1802.08451]. It exhibits a smooth velocity field with an inclination of 57 degrees and a maximum velocity of 220 km/s [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: The paper finds A2390-arc has a measurable smooth velocity field despite foreground overlap, fits it as one arc, and derives a lower inclination and higher maximum velocity than earlier VIMOS work.

quotes.quote: A2390-arc A2390-arc has been extensively studied in the past both spectroscopically Pello1991 and photometrically Cowie2002,Rigby2008,Olmstead2014 . Its kinematics was studied by Swinbank2006 , using VIMOS IFU data. The authors derived a maximum velocity (corrected for inclination) of 187 km s ^ 1 , with an inclination of 69 degrees. However, the authors divide this arc in two parts (arcA and arcB, in their figure 3), with arcB corresponding to the z 1 object seen overlapping with the arc (see Fig. fig:sample ). The MUSE data also allows to confirm that there is a foreground object at z 1 , but we can still reliably measure the velocity field of A2390-arc at this point.

The full arc has a smooth velocity field that is well described by a single velocity model, so we do not divide this arc in two objects as in Swinbank2006 . The derived kinematic parameters are at odds with the previous analysis by Swinbank2006 : we derive a lower inclination, 57 degrees, and consequently higher maximum velocity of 220 km s ^ 1 . The better quality of the MUSE data, that allows larger radii to be explored as well as the fact that we do not divide this object in arcA and arcB, may explain this difference.

gmw_00008664.name: M0416-sys28
gmw_00008664.overview: M0416-sys28 is a compact, low-mass galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.5 solar masses [arXiv:1802.08451]. Its gas kinematics are best fit with an arctangent model, which yields a maximum velocity of 157 km/s and a velocity dispersion of 15 km/s [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: M0416-sys28 is the sample's lowest-mass, most compact galaxy; the paper fits its gas kinematics with an arctangent model and finds velocities consistent with prior lensed-galaxy measurements.

quotes.quote: M0416-sys28 This galaxy has the lowest mass on this sample 9.5 0.25 10^9 M _ and it is also the most compact, making it difficult to fit of the gas kinematics. For example, the transition radius ( r_t ) is not correctly fit for any model, reaching limit values of 12-14 kpc (we impose a limit of 15). We estimate a maximum velocity of 157 7 and a V / ratio of 6, best fit with an arctangent model. This galaxy is included in the Mason2017 sample of lensed galaxies (ID 394) and from H kinematics the authors derive a maximum velocity of 133 22 , compatible within errors with our estimate. The authors also derive an intrinsic velocity dispersion of 12 15 from the integrated spectra, which is in good agreement with our intrinsic dispersion velocity value derived from the 2D map (15 7 ).

gmw_00008665.name: A2667-sys1
gmw_00008665.overview: A2667-sys1 is a gravitationally lensed spiral disc galaxy [arXiv:1802.08451]. The galaxy exhibits galactic outflows in its outer regions, where shock ionization contributes significantly to gas excitation [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: The paper describes A2667-sys1 as a spiral-disc lensed galaxy with resolved metallicity work, shock-driven outer outflows, and a MUSE metallicity higher than prior OSIRIS estimates.

quotes.quote: A2667-sys1 The redshift of this galaxy was firstly derived by Covone2006 using VIMOS IFU observations. Using HST and IRAC data, Cao2015 derived its SED and source plane morphology revealing a disc with visible spiral arms, and a negative radial colour gradient, most likely due to a stellar metallicity gradient. Yuan2012 used OSIRIS data to investigate the resolved metallicity of this galaxy through the [ ] /H ratio. The central part of the galaxy, that seems to be unaffected by shocks, yields a metallicity of 12+ (O/H) = 8.57 0.03, with a global value of 12+ (O/H) = 8.606 0.051 for the entire galaxy, using the PettiniPagel2004 calibrations.

The authors also measure a radially increasing [ ] /H ratio, but observe that there is evidence of galactic outflows in the outskirts of the galaxy and estimate that shock ionisation (originated by the outflows) contributes significantly to the excitation of [ ] . Yuan2012 conclude that the observed ratio gradient is probably due to the presence of shocks in the outer parts of the galaxy and not by a positive gas-phase metallicity gradient. From the MUSE data, we derive a high-metallicity of 12+ (O/H) = 9.08 , in strong disagreement with the 12+ (O/H) = 8.6 value previously derived by Yuan2012 .

gmw_00008666.name: M1149-sys1
gmw_00008666.overview: M1149-sys1 is a high-redshift spiral galaxy that is visible in four different multiple images due to strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1802.08451]. Kinematic observations of these multiple images are well fit by an arctangent model, and the galaxy has an adopted metallicity of 12+ (O/H) = 8.70 +/− 0.11 [arXiv:1802.08451].

sources.summary: M1149-sys1 is described as the sample's highest-redshift spiral, visible in multiple images, with MUSE kinematics fit by an arctangent model and adopted metallicity from HST grism data.

quotes.quote: M1149-sys1 Finally, MACS1149, the highest redshift galaxy in the sample, is a spectacular spiral seen in 4 different multiple images. From the MUSE data, only the [ ] doublet can be seen (besides the \ and \ emission and absorptions, studied by Karman2016 ). Also here, the arctangent model provides a better fit to the observed kinematics of the 3 multiple images. Yuan2011 , using OSIRIS data, derived the velocity field and metallicity gradient of this galaxy, concluding that it drops from 12+ (O/H) = 8.54 0.04 at the centre of the galaxy to 8.05 0.05 in the edges, with a global value of 8.36 0.04 . More recently, Wang2017 also derived the metallicity of this galaxy using HST grism data (ID 04054), arriving to a value of 12+ (O/H) = 8.70 0.11 , that we adopt in this paper.

gmw_00008667.name: DLA towards TXS0311+430
gmw_00008667.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system towards TXS0311+430 is a high-redshift absorber [arXiv:0805.2940] that exhibits a low 21 cm spin temperature [arXiv:0805.2940]. It is also characterized by a relatively high metallicity, estimated to be at least one-sixteenth of the solar value [arXiv:0805.2940].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the DLA towards TXS0311+430 as unusually metal rich for the sample and notes its published low 21 cm spin temperature.

quotes.quote: The three exceptions are the DLAs towards TXS0311+430, TXS1313+200 and the z_ abs = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187. Taking into account the two estimates of [ Si/H ] in the DLA towards TXS0311+430, and acknowledging that the curve of growth value may actually be a lower limit, this DLA appears to have a metallicity of at least 1/16, and possibly as high as 1/4, of the solar value Our 2 \ curve of growth abundance, [ Si/H ] = 0.6 is slightly lower than the value derived by York et al. (2007), who found [ Si/H ] = 0.48 . This is due to a re-assessment of the continuum level. . This DLA has also been found to be the only known case to date of a high redshift DLA with low spin temperature, T _ s = (138 36)  K (York et al. 2007), supporting the anti-correlation between metallicity and spin temperature tentatively detected in earlier DLA samples (Kanekar Chengalur 2001; Kanekar Briggs 2004).
We have already published an \ 21 cm detection towards one of these DLAs (TXS0311+430, z_ abs =2.289 , T _ s = (138 36)  K; York et al. 2007).

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 82 as a distant compact steep-spectrum quasar with extremely luminous radio lobes, unusually large jet power, and a powerful high-ionization broad-line wind.

quotes.quote: 3C 82 at a redshift of 2.87 is the most distant 3C (Third Cambridge Catalogue) quasar. Thus, it is a strong candidate to have the most luminous radio lobes in the Universe. 3C 82 belongs to the class of compact steep spectrum radio sources. We use single dish and interferometric radio observations in order to model the plasma state of these powerful radio lobes. It is estimated that the long-term time-averaged jet power required to fill these lobes with leptonic plasma is Q 2.66 1.33 10^ 47 ergs/sec , among the largest time averaged jet powers from a quasar.

gmw_00008668.name: DLA towards TXS1313+200
gmw_00008668.overview: The radio source TXS1313+200 is a quasar originating from the University of Texas survey that exhibits little flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. Its radio structure consists of an unresolved 5 GHz core and faint extended emission to the north-northwest [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper flags the DLA towards TXS1313+200 as an unusually metal-rich absorber, possibly with low spin temperature and massive-galaxy origin needing higher-resolution spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: The three exceptions are the DLAs towards TXS0311+430, TXS1313+200 and the z_ abs = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187.

If this is indeed the case, then the DLA towards TXS1313+200 and the z_ abs = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187 whose metallicities appear to be 1/3 -- 1/10 and 1/3 of the solar value respectively, may also have low spin temperatures. It will therefore be interesting to confirm the unusually high metallicities of these DLAs with higher resolution spectroscopy. If they indeed arise in massive galaxies, as may be expected from the mass-metallicity relation observed in high redshift galaxies (Savaglio et al. 2005; Erb et al. 2006; Liu et al. 2008), Hubble Space Telescope images may be able to confirm their morphologies and luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1313+200 as a University of Texas survey quasar with little variability, an unresolved 5 GHz core, and faint northern extended emission.

quotes.quote: 1313+200 This object originated in the University of Texas 365 MHz survey (Wills Wills ). Our integrated 5 GHz flux density of 0.29 Jy -- of which 0.27 Jy is in an unresolved component -- is not indicative of strong variability by comparison with B91 (0.33 Jy). Our image shows an unresolved core and faint extended emission, 0.7 arcsec NNW (Fig. 10).

gmw_00008669.name: z_ abs = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187
gmw_00008669.overview: The z_abs = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187 is a high-redshift damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:0805.2940]. It is characterized by an unusually high metallicity estimated at approximately one-third of the solar value, which suggests it may have a low spin temperature and originate in a massive galaxy [arXiv:0805.2940].

sources.summary: The paper highlights the z = 2.752 absorber towards TXS2039+187 as unusually metal rich, possibly low in spin temperature, and worth follow-up for massive-galaxy origin.
