Harrison:2015 .

gmw_00008678.name: SDSS J133039.82 001035.7
gmw_00008678.overview: SDSS J133039.82 001035.7 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.238 [arXiv:1910.02969]. It features two Gaia matches separated by 0.5 arcseconds, which may indicate a dual or offset active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1910.02969].

sources.summary: The paper flags SDSS J133039.82 001035.7 as the only AGN with two close Gaia matches, requiring follow-up to test a dual or offset AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: There is only one AGN (SDSS J133039.82 001035.7 at z=0.238 ), with two Gaia matches within 0 3 from the SDSS position. The two Gaia sources are separated by 0 5 and have no parallax/proper motion parameters. Its PanSTARRS images do not show resolved structure at this scale. A follow-up observation with spatially-resolved spectroscopy is necessary to determine whether this object is a dual or offset AGN.

gmw_00008679.name: XID 2608
gmw_00008679.overview: XID 2608 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.125 [arXiv:1409.1867]. It is classified as a Compton-thick candidate [arXiv:1409.1867, arXiv:0901.2347] that exhibits the optical spectrum of an emission-line galaxy [arXiv:0901.2347].

sources.summary: The paper presents XID 2608 as the lowest-redshift, brightest Compton-thick candidate, noting prior identification, low-redshift selection issues, and joint XMM/NuSTAR constraints.

quotes.quote: Source XID 2608, the lowest redshift, brightest CT candidate, was identified as a CT candidate already in Hasinger et al. (2007, the so called 'pink source', for its peculiar \ 'colors'), and in Mainieri et al (2007).

Indeed our lowest redshift source (XID 2608, z=0.125) is also the one with the largest number of counts, and would be probably classified as a soft source (see the spectrum in Fig. 4), in case of shallower \ data.

A similar case is for XID 2608, the best CT candidate in the sample, and the only one being CT both from our analysis and from B14 (marked with a star in Fig. nhnh ): the fit of the \ data alone gives as best fit an \ of only 1.06 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 (see Tab. 1), while the fit of \ data alone gives an >10^ 25 cm ^ 2 from both our analysis and B14. The joint fit of all the data (including also MOS) give an intermediate and well constrained value (see Tab. 2).

sources.summary: The paper describes XID= 2608 as a Compton-thick AGN with an emission-line galaxy spectrum and X-ray colours consistent with reflection-dominated emission plus leaked soft flux.

quotes.quote: An interesting source is XID= 2608 which has been classified as a Compton-Thick AGN by mainieri07 and has07 but its optical spectrum is that of an emission line galaxy. In has07 a small number of sources (including XID= 2608) was found to have hardness ratios that could be interpreted as being due to heavily absorbed (possibly Compton thick) high energy spectra with some fraction of leaking unabsorbed soft flux.
In particular the source ID= 2608 shows X-ray colours typical of a spectrum dominated by a pure reflection component with 3 of the original flux leaking out.

gmw_00008680.name: XID 54490
gmw_00008680.overview: XID 54490 is a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1409.1867]. The galaxy has a neighboring source and exhibits hints of a disturbed morphology, which may indicate a merger [arXiv:1409.1867].

sources.summary: The paper upgrades XID 54490 from a borderline source to the final Compton-thick sample and discusses possible disturbed morphology through neighboring-source evidence.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, two of the border-line sources, namely XID 54490 and XID 70145 show, in the joint fit, a column density which is actually higher than the one measured by the \ pn fit alone, and in the CT regime, and a strong Fe K line, again consistent with these sources being CT. We therefore include them in the CTK _f sample, see Tab. 2.

For the neighbor of sources XID 54490 there is a spectroscopic redshift (from zCOSMOS) of z=0.527 but with very low quality flag, while for the neighbors of source XID 60342 there is no redshift information, even if a hint of a disturbed morphology can be seen in both sources. All these morphological indications would translate into a merger/disturbed morphology fraction of 35 for our CTK _f sources up to z 2 , that could be even higher, depending on the interpretation for sources XID 54490 and XID 60342.

gmw_00008681.name: XID 70145
gmw_00008681.overview: XID 70145 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.548 [arXiv:1409.1867]. It hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus and is undetected in optical wavelengths, though it is visible at longer wavelengths starting from the K band [arXiv:1409.1867].

sources.summary: The paper includes XID 70145 in the final Compton-thick sample, notes its weak mid-infrared torus constraint, and describes optical nondetection but longer-wavelength visibility.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, two of the border-line sources, namely XID 54490 and XID 70145 show, in the joint fit, a column density which is actually higher than the one measured by the \ pn fit alone, and in the CT regime, and a strong Fe K line, again consistent with these sources being CT. We therefore include them in the CTK _f sample, see Tab. 2.
We note that all the sources need an AGN torus component, in order to reproduce the MIR photometric points, with the only exception of XID 70145, probably due to the non-detection at 24 m .

Finally source XID 70145 at z=2.548 is not detected in the optical, while it becomes visible at longer wavelengths, from K band, to Spitzer-IRAC MIR channels, and up to 24 m . However, the much poorer resolution of ground-based optical, or IR observations does not allow us to draw any conclusion about the morphology of these two high redshift sources, and they are therefore excluded from the following discussion.

gmw_00008682.name: XID 54514
gmw_00008682.overview: XID 54514 is a heavily reddened type-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1409.1867]. The galaxy has a close companion at the same spectroscopic redshift, with a possible structure connecting the two systems [arXiv:1409.1867].

sources.summary: The paper describes XID 54514 as a Compton-thick source with strong type-2 AGN emission lines, heavy reddening, and a close same-redshift companion.

quotes.quote: The only exception is the source XID 54514, which has strong emission lines with FWHM 900 km/s, resolved in the VIMOS spectrum but well in the range expected for type-2 AGN.
However, on top of that, we need to invoke also a correction factor of 0.7 for Log(L_ [ OIII ] ) for dust reddening for sources 60152 and 54514, or even more (a factor 1.3 ) for 2608.

Interestingly, between 3 and 5 of the 8 sources that we can resolve up to z 2 , show signs of merger/interaction: sources XID 60152 and XID 60361 clearly show post-merger morphologies, source XID 54514 has a close companion at the same spectroscopic redshift, with some structure possibly connecting the two.

gmw_00008683.name: XID 60361
gmw_00008683.overview: XID 60361 is an active galactic nucleus with a photometric redshift of z=1.73 [arXiv:1409.1867]. It is a highly obscured Compton-thick source that exhibits a post-merger morphology [arXiv:1409.1867].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID 60361 as the most obscured final Compton-thick source, with uncertain luminosity limits, no optical redshift, obscuration evidence, and post-merger morphology.

quotes.quote: For the most obscured source of the CTK _f sample (XID 60361) the upper boundary of the \ distribution is not constrained, due to the very low quality of the spectrum (see Fig. 4). As a result, also the 2-10 keV absorption corrected luminosity has no upper boundary. However, assuming as upper limit of = 10^ 25 cm^ 2 , we can estimate the resulting Log( ) upper limit to be 45.4 .

The optical spectrum of the source 60361 does not provide a redshift, but it is shown in the figure to stress the absence of strong emission lines in the wide spectral range covered. The photometric redshift adopted through the text for this source is z=1.73 . If the photometric redshift is correct, the absence of strong emission lines is a further indication of obscuration, even if the observing band would allow only the Mg II 2800 to be observed.

Interestingly, between 3 and 5 of the 8 sources that we can resolve up to z 2 , show signs of merger/interaction: sources XID 60152 and XID 60361 clearly show post-merger morphologies, source XID 54514 has a close companion at the same spectroscopic redshift, with some structure possibly connecting the two.

gmw_00008684.name: XID 70082
gmw_00008684.overview: XID 70082 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1409.1867] with a photometric redshift of 2.429 and a tentative spectroscopic redshift of 2.71 [arXiv:1409.1867]. The source is detected as a low-surface-brightness object in CFHT and Subaru optical bands, but it is not detected in ACS imaging [arXiv:1409.1867].

sources.summary: The paper gives XID 70082 a tentative high redshift, uses a photometric redshift in analysis, and reports optical low-surface-brightness detection but no ACS detection.

quotes.quote: Finally, source XID 70082 has a tentative spectroscopic redshift of z=2.71 , based on a faint Ly in emission with a continuum break across the line. Because of the uncertain identification, we used through the analysis the photometric redshift of z=2.429 . If the identification of the Ly is indeed correct, the measured \ and \ would be underestimated by a factor 15 and 30 , respectively, i.e. the source would be even more extreme in terms of obscuration and luminosity.
Source XID 70082 at z =2.429 is not detected in the ACS imaging while it is detected as a low surface brightness object in CFHT and Subaru optical bands.

gmw_00008685.name: PGC 3097920
gmw_00008685.overview: PGC 3097920 is an elliptical companion galaxy located at a projected distance of approximately 47 kiloparsecs from NGC 3923 [arXiv:1505.07146]. It is aligned with a stellar stream and is the likely progenitor of NGC 3923's S3 shell [arXiv:1505.07146].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 3097920 as an elliptical galaxy aligned with a stream in NGC 3923 and likely progenitor of shell S3 and perhaps other shells.

quotes.quote: A compact galaxy lies on the symmetry axis of this stream. The stream gets thinner close to the galaxy. It is likely the progenitor of the shell S3 and possibly of some others. The projected distance of this galaxy from the center of NGC 3923 is 474 \ (around 47 kpc supposing that it lies close to NGC 3923). It is the elliptical galaxy PGC 3097920.

According to the HyperLeda http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ database hyperleda , it has a heliocentric radial velocity of 1663 39 km s ^ 1 , while that of NGC 3923 is 1550 15 km s ^ 1 , and so they can be in physical proximity.
A probable progenitor of at least one of the shells was found. It is the galaxy PGC 3097920.

gmw_00008686.name: UGC 10064
gmw_00008686.overview: UGC 10064 is a highly inclined lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1106.3071] that hosts a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.5665]. The gas-poor galaxy lacks strong star formation activity [arXiv:1011.5665] and exhibits asymmetric low-surface-brightness emission possibly caused by an interaction with UGC 10063 [arXiv:1106.3071].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 10064 as the S0 host of SN 2009dc, deriving disk and bulge parameters, noting its high inclination, asymmetric low-surface-brightness emission, and possible interaction with UGC 10063.

quotes.quote: SN 2009dc is located 15 8 W and 20 8 N of the S0 host galaxy UGC 10064 (Figure fig:sn09dc ) at a projected distance of 12 kpc ref:Silverman_2011 . The parameters derived from GALFIT show that the host has an exponential disk scale length of 13 8 ( 6 kpc) and its bulge (Sersic index 3.8 ) has an effective radius of 8'' ( 3.5 kpc). This indicates that the SN exploded 2 exponential disk scale lengths away from center of UGC 10064. The host is very highly inclined with GALFIT estimates for the axis ratio of 0.1 for both the disk and the bulge components.

Although the SN is located outside of 99 of the galaxy's light, the SDSS g' and ' band images show low surface brightness emission at the position of the SN that is fairly asymmetric around the galaxy, with more light in the side where the SN is (NW) compared to the opposite side (SE). This appears consistent with a fairly inclined disk, perhaps an extended disk. It has also been proposed by that SN 2009dc might be associated with a faint and narrow tidal stream connecting the host with the 10 times less massive neighboring galaxy UGC 10063 and the progenitor system may have formed a few hundred Myr ago during an interaction of these two galaxies. It is impossible to measure the gas phase oxygen abundance or the metallicity gradient of this gas-poor galaxy directly using gas-phase abundances as we did for the host of SN 2003fg, but the central region of UGC 10064 appears to have a relatively undisturbed S0 profile despite being in an interacting system, and it is reasonable to expect a metallicity gradient fairly similar to that of a relatively undisturbed spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 10064 as SN 2009dc's possible S0 host, finding an old stellar population, little local star formation, weak AGN-like emission, and a long-delay progenitor if the SN belongs to it.

quotes.quote: As mentioned in Section Distance and extinction , SN 2009dc is located in the outskirts of the S0 galaxy UGC 10064. Although lenticular galaxies are by no means dead stellar systems e.g. Emsellem07,Kawabata09 , such a host galaxy might still be considered as an indication for a predominantly old stellar population. This is consistent with the lack of region emission lines in the spectra of SN 2009dc, suggesting no strong star formation activity at the SN location.

Using the methods described by Childress10 , we measure the emission-line fluxes of UGC 10064 by subtracting a best-fit stellar template. Though weak emission lines are detected, their flux ratios indicate ionisation by an active galactic nucleus according to the BPT bpt81 diagram boundaries defined by kewley06 . This indicates that the UV excess and weak-emission-line flux in UGC 10064 are likely to be caused by an active nucleus, and that no significant star-formation has occurred in the galaxy for at least several Gyr.

gmw_00008687.name: UGC 10063
gmw_00008687.overview: UGC 10063 is an irregularly shaped, strongly distorted galaxy with a distinctly blue color [arXiv:1011.5665]. It features a young post-starburst stellar population and a tidal tail bridging the gap to its larger neighbor, UGC 10064 [arXiv:1011.5665].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10063 as a blue, distorted companion to UGC 10064, with a tidal tail near SN 2009dc and a young post-starburst population possibly triggered by their encounter.

quotes.quote: At the same redshift, about 98.1 arcsec to its north-west (corresponding to a projected distance of 44.6 kpc), there is the irregularly shaped, strongly distorted galaxy UGC 10063. Almost an order of magnitude less luminous than UGC 10064, UGC 10063 is characterised by a distinctly blue colour, indicative of luminous, young stars. It shows a tidal tail bridging the gap to its larger neighbour, and, as already mentioned by Silverman10 , SN 2009dc is located at the approximate endpoint of this tail (see Fig. fig:environment ).

In contrast to the old stellar population of UGC 10064 is the much younger population of UGC 10063. The best fitting zpeg template is that of an Sd galaxy whose luminosity-weighted stellar age is 1.5 Gyr. The spectrum of UGC 10063 shows no emission from recent star formation, and is dominated by strong Balmer absorption features indicating the galaxy to be in a post-starburst phase as was also noted by Silverman10 . This implies that UGC 10063 underwent a strong burst of star formation a few hundred Myr before the explosion of SN 2009dc, perhaps induced by a close encounter with UGC 10064.

gmw_00008688.name: CTS 1020
gmw_00008688.overview: CTS 1020 is an H II dwarf galaxy that exhibits a disturbed but comparatively ordered disc-like rotation [arXiv:1808.04508]. Its velocity dispersion is driven by virial motion, though it is likely also affected by stellar feedback in its outermost regions [arXiv:1808.04508].

sources.summary: The paper maps CTS 1020 with Gemini IFU data, finding disturbed but comparatively ordered disc-like rotation, virial-motion-driven velocity dispersion, and some stellar-feedback influence in outer regions.

quotes.quote: We have used integral field spectroscopy to study the internal kinematics of the H ii galaxies CTS 1020 and UM 461. We based our analysis on the velocity and velocity dispersion maps, and intensity-velocity dispersion ( I- ) and velocity-velocity dispersion ( V_r- ) diagrams.

At first glance, CTS 1020 seems to show a more ordered motion with a gradient of 50 km s ^ 1 (from velocity field top to bottom). The velocity field can be seen as a rotating disc, even though the velocity field is disturbed in several regions.

On the other hand, in CTS 1020 decreases along with monochromatic intensity outwards the galaxy, implying that is driven by virial motion Moiseev2015 . However, is also probably affected by the injection of mechanical energy from the stellar population that increases the velocity dispersion in the outermost parts of the galaxy, where it should be lower in the proposed scenario.

gmw_00008689.overview: 0317 023 is a radio source with an overall angular size of 7 arcsec [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. It features an unresolved nucleus, southern components, and a curved small-scale jet extending toward the northeast, with no indications of flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper detects 0317 023 as a 7 arcsec radio source with an unresolved nucleus, southern components, a curved northeastern small-scale jet, and no clear variability.

quotes.quote: 0317 023 We detect a 5 GHz integrated flux density of 0.26 Jy, of which 0.16 Jy is in an unresolved nucleus and 0.10 Jy in additional components, towards the south and in a curved small scale jet towards the NE (Fig. 6). The overall angular size of 0317 023 is 7 arcsec. Neither the Green Bank single dish flux density (0.28 Jy -- G95) nor the earlier Parkes figure (0.29 Jy -- Wall ) are indicative of variability.

gmw_00008690.overview: The object 0458 020 is a strongly variable radio quasar [arXiv:9911474] that exhibits a red color [arXiv:astro-ph/0507418] and acts as a marginal gamma-ray source [arXiv:9911474]. It features a bright radio core surrounded by extended emission [arXiv:9911474], and its line of sight contains a famous damped HI-absorbing system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra data for B0458 020, finding high Galactic absorption and no significant added QSO- or DLA-redshift absorber, while leaving associated absorption as an unconfirmed explanation for its red color.

quotes.quote: We have retrieved archival Chandra ACIS data for B0458 020 (ObsID 2985) with a total exposure of 77.76 ksec. The data were analysed in the 0.3-10.0 KeV energy range using CIAO 3.2.1 software and the latest calibration files. B0458 020 suffers from relatively high Galactic absorption, corresponding to \ = 7.5 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , which hinders the detection of intrinsic absorption which is redshifted to the soft (low energy) part of the spectrum. Indeed, a spectral fit assuming a simple power-law spectrum with only Galactic absorption yields a good fit to the data, i.e. ^ 2 _ red = 0.8 for 155 degrees of freedom.

The inclusion of absorption at either the QSO or the DLA redshift gave no significant improvement to the fit: N(H) < 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 with 99 confidence (F-test statistics F 0.002 for ^ 2 7.3 and 1 additional parameter). Using the Pei (1992) extinction laws this corresponds to an upper limit E(B-K) < 2.8 and < 0.7 , for a Galactic and SMC reddening-to-dust ratio if the absorber is located in the host galaxy. Associated absorption could therefore be the cause of the red color of this QSO, although better X-ray data would be required to confirm this.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q0458 02 as a famous HI-absorbing damped system, adopts a high HI column, notes an 8 h^-1 kpc size lower limit, and infers high neutral fraction.

quotes.quote: Q0458 02, z = 2.040 This damped system is 'famous' for exhibiting HI absorption. In particular, Briggs et al. \ (1989) have used VLBI radio observations to place a lower limit on its size of 8 h_ 100 ^ 1 kpc. In our analysis we adopt HI = 21.65 0.09 taken from Pettini et al. \ (1994). A plot of the metal-line profiles is given in Figure vpQ0458 and Table Q0458-clm lists the column densities.

Assuming the ^2P_ 3/2 excited fine-structure state is populated by e ^- collisions and C^+ < 10^ 17 2 (which follows by assuming [ C/H ] < [ Zn/H ] ), the limit on CII^* indicates n_e > 0.1 3 . The system, with one of the highest measured HI values, must have a high neutral fraction implying n_ H > 0.1 3 . Because FeII 1608 is saturated, we base the Fe abundance on the FeII 1611 transition.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0458 020 as a strongly variable radio quasar and marginal EGRET gamma-ray source with a bright core, extended emission, and 3.5 arcsec structure.

quotes.quote: 0458 020 Well-known variable object at cm-mm wavelengths (e.g., K uhr et al. , G95), and `marginal' EGRET ray source (Thompson et al. ). Our integrated 5 GHz flux density of 3.7 Jy implies a factor two of increase in comparison to earlier data (K uhr et al. ). A strong core (3.55 Jy at 5 GHz) is straddled by 0.15 Jy extended emission, yielding an overall angular size of 3.5 arcsec (Fig. 7). A tapered map demonstrates the reality of the faint emission NE of the nucleus. Our image is consistent with a MERLIN 408 MHz image (F. Briggs, priv. comm.; image also shown in Punsly ).

gmw_00008691.overview: The radio-loud quasar 1116+128 features an unresolved core that exhibits strong radio variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. Its extended radio emission includes a secondary component or knot located to the northwest of the core [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474, arXiv:1802.04714], alongside possible diffuse halo emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper reports strong core variability in 1116+128, detecting a 1.7 Jy core, a northwestern secondary component, and diffuse or halo emission in comparison images.

quotes.quote: 1116+128 Our measurements in comparison with literature data indicate fairly strong core variability. We detect 1.7 Jy in an unresolved core, and 0.1 Jy in a secondary component 2.7 arcsec towards the NW (Fig. 9). Our integrated 5 GHz flux density of 1.8 Jy is inconsistent with earlier values, which are in the range 1.3 -- 1.6 Jy (K uhr et al. ). Our image is consistent with the lower resolution image of Murphy et al. ( ), although the latter shows some evidence for additional low surface brightness halo emission. O'Dea et al. ( ) also find evidence for diffuse emission, besides the unequal double morphology (having 1.5 Jy in the unresolved core).

sources.summary: The note describes 1116 + 128 as having one northwest radio knot that is not detected by Chandra and has no apparent HST counterpart.

quotes.quote: 1116 + 128 (4C + 12.39) The extended radio emission consists of a single knot at 2.5 \ northwest of the core. The knot is not detected in the Chandra data. No features are apparent in the HST image at the radio knot.

gmw_00008692.overview: The object 1402 012 is a radio source with a 5 GHz flux density of 0.34 Jy [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. It is strongly variable and shows evidence of small resolution effects in the north-south direction [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1402 012 as an unresolved, strongly variable source with a 0.34 Jy 5 GHz flux and slight north-south resolution effects.

quotes.quote: 1402 012 An unresolved source, having 5 GHz flux density 0.34 Jy. Wall ( ) reports a Parkes value of 0.81 Jy: the object must be strongly variable. Deconvolution gives evidence for small resolution effects in direction N-S. The radio map is not shown.

gmw_00008693.overview: The active galaxy 1442+101, also known as OQ172, is a high-redshift gigahertz-peaked spectrum quasar [arXiv:1703.04066, arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. Very Long Baseline Array observations reveal a highly bent core-jet structure [arXiv:1703.04066, arXiv:astro-ph/9911474] that exhibits polarized radio emission [arXiv:1703.04066] and suggests a blazar classification [arXiv:2209.10760].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBA polarimetry of the high-redshift GPS quasar OQ172, measuring bent core-jet structure, polarized radio emission, rotation measures, and circumnuclear absorption conditions.

quotes.quote: Multi-frequency Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) polarimetry observation of the GHz-Peaked Spectrum (GPS) quasar OQ172 (J1445+0958) has been performed at 1.6, 2.2, 4.8, 8.3 and 15.3 GHz in 2005. Core-jet structures are detected in all bands with the jet strongly bent at about 3 mas from the core. The radio emission of the source is polarised at all five bands. We study the Faraday Rotation in the core and jet components at all five bands, and find good linear fits of Faraday Rotation in the core and jet components at 4.8 and 8.3 GHz.

We presented a VLBA polarimetry observation of the high-redshift GPS quasar OQ172 at five frequency bands. Our conclusions are as follows: A core-jet morphology has been found at all five frequencies with sharp bending at 3 mas from the core clearly visible at 4.8, 8.3, and 15.3 GHz. Linearly polarised emission has been detected in OQ172 at all five frequencies, with a typical low fractional polarisation in the core, and high in the jet components.

sources.summary: The paper studies J1445 + 0958, also OQ 172, as a bent high-redshift jet source whose first measured proper motion and high brightness temperature support a blazar interpretation.

quotes.quote: J1445 + 0958 This object, also known as OQ 172, has a rich jet structure starting toward the west and turning the south. It was once identified as a GHz-peaked spectrum (GPS) galaxy due to the less variable radio and optical emission, the non-flat radio spectrum and the lack of pc-scale structural evolution see and the references therein 2015ApJ...812...79P . Previous multi-band VLBI observations revealed a clockwise jet trajectory from high-frequency to low-frequency images, which was attributed to the interaction between the relativistic jet and the narrow-line region medium 2017MNRAS.468.2699L .

From seven epochs spanning 21 yr, we were able to obtain jet proper motion in J1445 + 0958 for the first time. Among the three jet components, J1 stands out as the fastest one, with a proper motion of 0.13 0.01 mas yr ^ 1 . The mean T_ B of this source is 15.5 6.4 10^ 10 K, suggesting a highly beamed jet. From the spectral index map, the high brightness temperature, and the Gaia optical position, the source could be a blazar.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1442+101 with OQ 172, describing it as a GPS quasar unresolved by VLA but showing a bent milliarcsecond core-jet structure in VLBI data.

quotes.quote: 1442+101 This is a well known GPS object, identified with the pre-1982 QSO redshift record holder OQ 172. The radio source is unresolved on the 0.1 arcsec scale, hence the radio map is not shown. VLBI observations on the other hand have revealed a highly bent core-jet structure on the milliarcsec scale (Udomprasert et al. ). Our flux density measurement of 1.22 Jy is consistent with earlier ones, e.g., K uhr et al. ( ), thereby indicating little or no variability.

gmw_00008694.overview: The radio-loud quasar 1542+042 features a 5 GHz core with a flux density of 0.45 Jy [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474]. It exhibits an extended eastern component and faint halo emission on both sides of its nucleus, alongside modest variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper measures 1542+042 with a 0.45 Jy core and extended eastern component, plus faint halo emission on both sides and modest variability relative to prior data.

quotes.quote: 1542+042 We measure a 5 GHz core flux density of 0.45 Jy and an additional flux density of 0.04 Jy in extended emission. Most of this extended emission originates in a secondary component 1.3 arcsec E of the nuclear component (Fig. 11). There is evidence for additional low surface brightness halo emission both E and W of the nucleus, which is confirmed by a tapered image. Other measurements (B91: 0.41 Jy; G95: 0.54 Jy; Shimmins et al. : 0.47 Jy) are indicative of a small level of variability.

gmw_00008695.overview: The galaxy 020294045 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. It features two projected components, including a northern companion with a steep velocity gradient separated from the principal galaxy by 2.9 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 020294045 as a major merger, with two projected components, a northern companion with a steep velocity gradient, matching GALFIT components, and small luminosity and velocity separations.

quotes.quote: 020294045 (Fig. src_020294045_i ). Major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components. The companion is toward the north and presents a steep velocity gradient compared with the principal galaxy. The GALFIT model with two components in the i band reproduces both the position of the two kinematical components in the velocity map and the high velocity dispersion in the overlapping region between both components. The luminosity difference is m_ i = 0.4 . The separation between the sources is 2.9 h^ 1 kpc and their relative velocity is v 180 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00008696.overview: The galaxy 020386743 features two projected components in its velocity map [arXiv:1208.5020]. It has a northern companion galaxy separated by 3.7 kiloparsecs, but due to their luminosity difference, the system is not classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper finds 020386743 has two projected components and a northern companion, but its luminosity difference leads the authors to reject a major-merger classification.

quotes.quote: 020386743 (Fig. src_020386743 ). No major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components. The companion is toward the north and presents a different velocity than the northern part of the principal galaxy. The GALFIT model with two components suggests that we are only detecting the western part of the companion galaxy, with the eastern part being too faint. This is also consistent with the velocity dispersion map, which shows a regular pattern in the overlapping region. The separation between the sources is 3.7h^ 1 kpc and the luminosity difference is m_ i = 1.8 .

gmw_00008697.overview: The galaxy 020461235 is a close projected system with a companion located toward the south-west [arXiv:1208.5020]. This highly distorted companion exhibits a nearly constant velocity, but the large luminosity difference between the two components suggests the system is not a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper describes 020461235 as a close projected system with a distorted south-western companion, but the large luminosity difference argues against a major merger.

quotes.quote: 020461235 (Fig. src_020461235 ). No major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components. The companion is toward the south-west and presents a nearly constant velocity, in contrast with the velocity gradient of the principal galaxy. The GALFIT model with two components suggests that the companion is highly distorted, perhaps because the system is in an advanced merger stage (i.e., after first pericenter passage or pre-coalescence). The luminosity difference from the GALFIT model is m_ i = 1.7 , while from the residual maps is m_ i = 1.9 . This difference strongly suggests that this system is not a major merger.

gmw_00008698.overview: The galaxy VVDS140096645 exhibits broad emission lines and high global and nuclear nitrogen-to-hydrogen ratios, making it a candidate for nuclear activity [arXiv:1111.3697]. It displays characteristics of a LINER galaxy [arXiv:1111.3697] and possesses a small kinematic companion located approximately 1.2 arcseconds away [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: VVDS140096645 is singled out for [O I] detection, high global and nuclear N2 ratios, broad lines, LINER-like features, and possible nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: For many galaxies in our sample, the low SNR of the data cubes prevents us from measuring in each spaxel, with high confidence, the emission lines surrounding : namely , , and [ O i ] 6300 which is detected only in one galaxy: VVDS140096645.

We investigated in more detail the galaxy in this bin: VVDS140096645. It shows the following high N2 ratios: N2_ global =-0.252 and N2_ nuclear =-0.294 . Looking further into its integrated spectrum (global and nuclear, see 645 ), we noticed that: (i) the emission lines are broad, which is a possible sign of nuclear activity, (ii) the two nitrogen lines are clearly visible, as is the sulfur doublet, and the [ O i ] 6300 line, which altogether are characteristics of LINER galaxies, often associated to AGNs heck80 - or violent episodes of star-formation in high metallicity galaxies terl85 . The galaxy with the next highest global N2 value is VVDS140258511 ( N2_ global =-0.509 and N2_ nuclear =-0.345 ). Its global and nuclear spectra show no obvious features of nuclear activity, and the N2 ratio are both < -0.3 . We conclude that our sample does not suffer from significant AGN contamination. The only candidate for nuclear activity is VVDS140096645.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a small kinematic companion to 140096645, then uses fake-source injection and completeness limits to conclude a major companion is unlikely.

quotes.quote: 140096645 (Fig. src_140096645 ). No major merger . A small companion is identified in the velocity map about 1.2 arcsec away from the principal galaxy. There is no continuum detection in the i- band image in the position of the H companion, although there is a 1 _ sky excess emission. To estimate if the non-detected companion could be bright/massive enough to lead to a major merger, we injected fake companion sources in the i- band image and studied their detection fraction as a function of the fake companion luminosity m_ i,2 .

We find that even for extended sources, we are 90 complete at m_ i,2 24 , while the limiting magnitude for a major companion is m^ MM _ i,2 = 23.8 (vertical line in the right panel of Fig. src_140096645 ). Because of the small probability of non detection of a major companion, we classify this system as no major merger.

gmw_00008699.overview: The galaxy 220397579 is a major merger system consisting of a nearly face-on principal galaxy and a nearly edge-on companion [arXiv:1208.5020]. The principal galaxy exhibits intense star formation [arXiv:1208.5020], while the system displays a high gas infall rate despite a low inferred dark matter halo mass [arXiv:1111.3697].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 220397579 as a major merger and interprets it as a face-on principal galaxy with intense star formation plus a more luminous edge-on companion.

quotes.quote: 220397579 (Fig. src_220397579 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 14.4h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 340 km s ^ 1 . The companion is toward the north-west. The luminosity difference is m_ i = 0.4 , suggesting a major merger. The difference in the K_ s band is m_ K_ s = -1.4 .

The suggested picture is that the system comprises a nearly face-on principal galaxy with intense star formation and low dust reddening, and a nearly edge-on companion galaxy with either a low level of star formation or strong dust reddening. We classify the system as a major merger.

sources.summary: VVDS220397579 deviates from the infall trend with low inferred halo mass and high infall rate; the authors argue its face-on disk geometry likely underestimates halo mass.

quotes.quote: Two MASSIV galaxies deviate from the global trend in fig:infall . VVDS220397579, located at the left end of the graph, has a very low DM halo mass but an important infall rate ( 650 M_ yr^ 1 ), while VVDS220376206 has by far the highest infall ( >10^3 M_ yr^ 1 ) and star formation ( >250 M_ yr^ 1 ) rates . For VVDS220397579 it is very likely that its DM halo mass has been underestimated. Indeed, this galaxy could be a face-on disk as the kinematical modeling returned a very low rotation velocity of 9 km s . As the DM halo mass is directly proportional to the maximum rotation velocity (see equation eq:mhalo ) the DM halo mass for VVDS220397579 is most probably only a lower limit.

gmw_00008700.overview: The galaxy 220544394 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. The system features two distinct velocity components with a small projected separation of 7.1 kiloparsecs and a companion located to the north [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 220544394 as a major merger with two velocity components, a northern companion, and a small projected separation and velocity offset.

quotes.quote: 220544394 (Fig. src_220544394 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 7.1h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 50 km s ^ 1 , that are also well recovered by the GALFIT model with two components. The companion is toward the north, while the luminosity difference is m_ i = 1.3 . We classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008701.overview: The galaxy 020167131 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. Its velocity map reveals two distinct components separated by 15.2 kiloparsecs, with a companion galaxy located toward the south-east [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 020167131 as a major merger, based on two velocity components, a south-eastern companion galaxy, and small optical and near-infrared luminosity differences.

quotes.quote: 020167131 (Fig. src_020167131 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 15.2h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 130 km s ^ 1 . The line targeted in this case is [ O iii ] 5007 , which explains the low signal. The companion galaxy is toward the south-east, while the luminosity differences are m_i = 0.2 and m_ K_ s = 0.1 . We classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008702.overview: The galaxy 020218856 is associated with a faint kinematic companion detected in its velocity map [arXiv:1208.5020]. This companion is located approximately 1.6 arcseconds from the principal galaxy, but the system is not classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper detects a faint kinematic companion to 020218856 but, using completeness tests and continuum non-detection, concludes the system is not a major merger.

quotes.quote: 020218856 (Fig. src_020218856 ). No major merger . A faint companion is detected in the velocity map about 1.6 arcsec from the principal galaxy. There is no continuum detection of this companion at the depth of the i- band image. We followed the same steps than for the source 140096645 to estimate the detection probability of companion galaxies, finding that we are 100 complete up to m _ i,2 = 26 .

We note that the curves for extended, normal and compact galaxies are similar, reflecting that the size of the PSF is larger than the assumed size of the fake sources. Because a major companion should be brighter than m^ MM _ i,2 = 25.32 (vertical line in the left panel of Fig. src_020218856 ), and the non detection in the continuum means that the companion should have m_i 2 , we do not classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008703.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 020240675 is associated with a faint companion detected in its velocity map [arXiv:1208.5020]. This companion is located approximately 2.4 arcseconds from the principal galaxy, but the system is not classified as a major merger due to a lack of continuum detection [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper detects a faint velocity-map companion to 020240675, but similar completeness curves and continuum non-detection make a major-merger classification unlikely.

quotes.quote: 020240675 (Fig. src_020240675 ). No major merger . A faint companion is detected in the velocity map about 2.4 arcsec from the principal galaxy. There is no continuum detection of this companion at the depth of the i- band image. We followed the same steps than for the source 020218856 to estimate the detection curves of companion galaxies. We find similar completeness curves. Because a major companion should be brighter than m^ MM _ i,2 = 25.15 (vertical line in the left panel of Fig. src_020240675 ), and the non detection in the continuum means that the companion should have m_i 2.5 , we do not classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008704.overview: The galaxy 020283083 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. Its velocity map and a two-component model indicate a nearby companion galaxy with a small luminosity difference of 0.7 magnitudes [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 020283083 as a major merger because its velocity map and two-component GALFIT model indicate a nearby companion with a small luminosity difference.

quotes.quote: 020283083 (Fig. src_020283083 ). Major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components separated by 3.8 h^ 1 kpc and 5 km s ^ 1 , with an extended region in the north-west. The GALFIT model with two components reproduces the shape of the velocity map and suggests that the extended region is due to the companion galaxy. The luminosity difference from the models is m_ i = 0.7 . Thus, we classify this system as a major merger.

gmw_00008705.overview: The galaxy 020283830 is a two-component system that features a faint companion detected in H-alpha emission [arXiv:1208.5020]. Because the companion is fainter than the major-merger limit, the system is not classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper describes 020283830 as a two-component system with a faint H-alpha companion, but photometric modeling and subtraction indicate the companion is below the major-merger limit.

quotes.quote: 020283830 (Fig. src_020283830 ). No major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 8.5h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 500 km s ^ 1 . The companion only presents six detected pixels in the H map, already suggesting a minor companion. We used GALFIT to model the system, finding m_ i = 1.9 .

The measurement with SExtractor in the image with the principal galaxy subtracted suggests m_ i = 2.1 . Since the companion is fainter than the major merger limit, we do not classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008706.overview: 020465775 is a major merger system with a companion galaxy located toward the north-west [arXiv:1208.5020]. The system exhibits compact central H-alpha emission, and its abnormal velocity pattern is explained by the position of this companion [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 020465775 as a major merger, with a north-west companion, compact central H-alpha emission, and a velocity-dispersion peak explained by component overlap.

quotes.quote: 020465775 (Fig. src_020465775 ). Major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components. The GALFIT model with two components finds that the companion galaxy is toward the north-west of the principal galaxy, at r_ p = 3.6h^ 1 kpc and v 40 km s ^ 1 . The H emission of this system is located in the central part of the galaxies, since the i- band models match the velocity map at 9 _ sky level, i.e., we do not detect emission from the outer parts of the galaxies. The position of the companion explains the abnormal velocity pattern and the high velocity dispersion peak in the maps. The luminosity difference of the system is m_i = 0.7 , so we classify it as a major merger.

gmw_00008707.overview: The galaxy 220376206 is a non-major merger system with a companion located to its north [arXiv:1208.5020]. The system displays two distinct velocity components separated by 13.4 kiloparsecs with a relative velocity of 400 kilometers per second [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper finds 220376206 has two velocity components and a northern companion, but its large luminosity difference makes it a non-major merger.

quotes.quote: 220376206 (Fig. src_220376206 ). No major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 13.4h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 400 km s ^ 1 . The companion, located toward the north, presents ten detected pixels and its velocity is inconsistent with that expected from the velocity field of the principal galaxy. We find m_i = 2.4 , so the system is not a major merger.

gmw_00008708.overview: The galaxy 220544103 is classified as a major merger system [arXiv:1208.5020]. It consists of an edge-on southern component and a nearly face-on northern component separated by approximately 5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 220544103 as a major merger, describing edge-on and face-on components with different velocity gradients, small separation, and a favorable luminosity ratio.

quotes.quote: 220544103 (Fig. src_220544103 ). Major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components. The southern component presents a large velocity gradient and defines the kinematical centre of the system, while the northern component is more extended and has a nearly flat velocity field. The GALFIT model with two components recovers the configuration in the velocity map and suggests that the southern component is edge-on, while the northern component is nearly face-on, thus explaining the observed high and null velocity gradients. The separation between the components is r_ p = 5h^ 1 kpc, while their relative velocity is v 75 km s ^ 1 . From the GALFIT models we estimate m_i = -1.1 , and we classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008709.overview: The galaxy 910154631 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. It is a close pair system featuring two projected components with distinct velocity gradients and an enhanced velocity dispersion in their overlapping region [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 910154631 as a major merger after modeling contamination, finding two components, two velocity gradients, and an overlapping-region velocity-dispersion enhancement.

quotes.quote: 910154631 (Fig. src_910154631 ). Major merger . The velocity map suggests two projected components, with the companion toward the north-west. The i- band image shows another two well separated sources close to the MASSIV target. These sources are not detected in H . To avoid contamination from these sources in the i- band photometry, we performed a four component fitting, with two components for the MASSIV target and one component for each nearby source.

We explored the reduced data cube of the source, and we find (i) there are two clear velocity gradients in the cube, reinforcing the presence of two different components and (ii) there is an OH sky-line in the channels in which we expect the northern part of the companion, explaining the non detection in the maps. In addition, the higher velocity dispersion of the MASSIV target occurs in the expected overlapping region between both components. We conclude that this is a close pair system with r_ p = 4.2h^ 1 kpc, v 130 km s ^ 1 and m_i = 0.8 . Thus, we classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008710.overview: The galaxy 910296626 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. The system features a companion located toward the north-east, and its velocity map reveals two distinct components separated by 12.1 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 910296626 as a major merger, with two velocity components, a north-eastern companion, and optical and near-infrared luminosity ratios favoring merger status.

quotes.quote: 910296626 (Fig. src_910296626 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 12.1h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 165 km s ^ 1 . The companion is located toward the north-east. We find m_i = -0.1 and m_ K_ s = -0.2 , so the system is a major merger.

gmw_00008711.overview: The galaxy 910337228 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. Its velocity map reveals two distinct components separated by 9.5 kiloparsecs, with a companion galaxy located to the west [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 910337228 as a major merger, based on two velocity components, a western companion, and a luminosity difference within the major-merger threshold.

quotes.quote: 910337228 (Fig. src_910337228 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 9.5h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 220 km s ^ 1 . The companion is toward the west and has m_i = 1.4 . Thus, we classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008712.overview: 020116027 is classified as a major merger with a companion galaxy located toward the north-west [arXiv:1208.5020]. The system exhibits two distinct velocity components separated by 26.8 kiloparsecs with a relative velocity of approximately 100 kilometers per second [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 020116027 as a major merger, with two velocity components, a north-west companion, and matching optical and K-band luminosity differences.

quotes.quote: 020116027 (Fig. src_020116027 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 26.8h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 100 km s ^ 1 . The companion is toward the north-west. The luminosity difference is m_i = 0.7 , suggesting a major merger. The difference in the K_ s band is m_ K_ s = 0.5 , confirming the previous major merger classification.

gmw_00008713.overview: The system 910186191 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. It consists of two components with a relative velocity of 450 km/s and a stellar mass ratio of one-third [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 910186191 as a major merger despite conflicting band diagnostics, using VVDS stellar masses to confirm a one-third mass ratio between components.

quotes.quote: 910186191 (Fig. src_910186191 ). Major merger . The velocity map shows two different components with a separation of r_ p = 12.7h^ 1 kpc and a relative velocity of v 450 km s ^ 1 . The MASSIV target is only detected in 6 pixels because there is an OH sky-line in the position of H at its redshift. The companion, located toward the west, is well detected. We find m_i = -0.2 , this is, the companion is slightly brighter than the principal galaxy.

However, the MASSIV target is barely detected in the K_ s band, with m_ K_ s = -2.4 , suggesting a low mass system. This is the only system in which the classification in the two bands is different. Fortunately, both sources are VVDS targets, and we have an estimation of their stellar masses from SED fitting. The difference in stellar mass is 1/3 , so we classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008714.overview: The galaxy 910274060 is classified as a major merger [arXiv:1208.5020]. It is a close pair system consisting of two sources separated by 3.4 h^-1 kpc with a velocity difference of approximately 10 km s^-1 [arXiv:1208.5020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 910274060 as a major merger after two-source GALFIT modeling resolves a complex system with aligned photometric and kinematic position angles.

quotes.quote: 910274060 (Fig. src_910274060 ). Major merger . The velocity map is consistent with one single component. However, the position angle (PA) from the i- band photometry, PA = 105^ (North has PA = 0^ and East has PA = 90^ ), is nearly perpendicular to that from the kinematical modelling, PA = 33^ . This suggests a complex system, so we performed the GALFIT modelling with two sources.

We recover well two sources, one in the north and the other in the south. The photometric PAs of these two sources, provided by the GALFIT fitting, are now in better agreement with the kinematical one (PA _1 = 75^ , PA _2 = 41^ ), supporting that this is a close pair system. The separation between the components is 3.4 h^ 1 kpc, with v 10 km s ^ 1 , and the luminosity difference is m_ i = 0.2 . Thus, we classify the system as a major merger.

gmw_00008715.name: ESO 216-39
gmw_00008715.overview: ESO 216-39 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.017 [arXiv:2003.09709]. It hosted the supernova SN 2013L, which was discovered in the galaxy's outskirts [arXiv:2003.09709].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 216-39 as SN 2013L's host, locates the supernova in its outskirts, estimates host metallicity, and gives the galaxy redshift and distance.

quotes.quote: SN 2013L was discovered by monard13 in ESO 216-39 with an unfiltered apparent magnitude of 15.6.

The SN is located 20 east and 24 south of the galaxy core. A finding chart of SN 2013L in ESO 216-39 is provided in Fig. FC . In taddia15met , we computed the deprojected distance of SN 2013L from its nucleus, which turned out to be 1.09 times the galaxy radius ( _ 25 ), that is, its location lies in the outskirts of the host.

The host-galaxy center is characterized by a N2 pettini04 metallicity of 12+log(O/H) = 8.68 0.18 dex, which when assuming a typical 0.47 dex _ 25 ^ 1 metallicity gradient pilyugin04 implies that SN 2013L was located in a subsolar metallicity environment, that is, 12+log(O/H) = 8.17 0.23 dex. This is typical of long-lasting Type IIn SNe, such as SN 1988Z and SN impostors taddia15met . ESO 216-39 is located at z = 0.01738 (A17; NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu lists a slightly lower redshift (0.016992 0.000150), which corresponds to a distance of 72 ^ +8.5 _ 5 Mpc (A17).

gmw_00008716.name: Donatiello I
gmw_00008716.overview: Donatiello I is a low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal galaxy located beyond the Local Group [arXiv:2001.05746]. It is a quenched system that lacks young stars and neutral hydrogen emission, and it may be a satellite of the lenticular galaxy NGC 404 [arXiv:2001.05746].

sources.summary: The review describes Donatiello I as a low-surface-brightness dwarf found near Andromeda, beyond the Local Group, quenched, and possibly associated with NGC 404.

quotes.quote: A very interesting example in this context is Donatiello I (Do I; Mart nez-Delgado et al. 2018), a low-surface brightness ( _ V =26.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) stellar system located at a projected distance of one degree from Mirach ( And) (Fig. 13, left panel) and discovered by the italian amateur astronomer Giuseppe Donatiello. This dwarf galaxy was first found during a deep image visual inspection of the Andromeda galaxy region taken with a 12.7-cm refractor ED . This discovery was confirmed by a visual inspection of the SDSS DR9 images and follow-up observations using the Telescopio National Galileo 3.6-m and the Gran Telescopio de Canarias 10.4-m (La Palma, Spain; Fig. 13). The color--magnitude diagram revealed this system is beyond the Local Group with a stellar content similar to the "classical" Milky Way companions Draco and Ursa Minor.

The absence of young stars and HI emission in the ALFALFA survey are also typical of quenched dwarf galaxies. Crowding effects in the images did not enable the secure establishment of Do I's distance but it provided a tentative distance modulus for this galaxy of (m-M)=27.6 0.2 (3.3 Mpc) and an absolute magnitude of M_ V =-8.3 (Fig. 13, right top panels). Its projected position and distance of Do I are still consistent with being a dwarf satellite of the closest S0-type galaxy NGC 404 ("Mirach's Ghost").

gmw_00008717.name: NGC 6849
gmw_00008717.overview: NGC 6849 is classified as an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1410.7515]. It features an inner bar that appears as a peak in its ellipticity profile, alongside a strong isophotal twist at its break radius that coincides with a local ellipticity minimum [arXiv:1410.7515].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6849 as an S0 galaxy with a strong break-radius isophotal twist, an ellipticity minimum, and an inner bar seen in its ellipticity profile.

quotes.quote: Also evident in Fig. FigV5 , is that despite the twists, the galaxies' orientations at their break radii are fairly aligned with the spheroid's outer orientation except for five galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC NGC 3842, NGC 4589, NGC 5061 and NGC 6849) with inner position angles which are twisted by 27^ from the outer position angle. NGC 0741, NGC 3842 and NGC 6849 show strong isophotal twists at their break radii coincident with local minima in their ellipticities, but for NGC 5061 the strong 30^ twist and the ellipticity minimum which occurs between 3 and 10 may be a result of tidal effects (Tal et al. \ 2009). While the correlation between high twist amplitudes and low isophote ellipticities has already been known for some time (e.g., Galleta 1980; Nieto et al. \ 1992; Rahman Shandarin 2004), these four galaxies have interesting features discussed in the literature. NGC 0741 is a nucleated galaxy (e.g., Dullo Graham 2012), NGC 3842 is a bright cluster galaxy that has an ultramassive black hole ( M_ BH = 9.7 10^ 9 M_ ) with a 1 .2 radius of influence (McConnell et al. \ 2011) whereas the S0 galaxy NGC 6849 has an inner bar which manifests as a peak in the ellipiticity profile (see Dullo Graham 2013).

gmw_00008718.name: NGC 5061
gmw_00008718.overview: NGC 5061 is an elliptical galaxy that exhibits a strong inner position-angle twist of 30 degrees, which may be the result of tidal effects [arXiv:1410.7515]. It features a tidal tail and possesses the largest Sersic index in the Dullo & Graham (2014) sample [arXiv:1410.7515].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5061 as an elliptical with a strong inner position-angle twist, possible tidal effects, the sample's largest Sersic index, and a prominent tidal tail.

quotes.quote: Also evident in Fig. FigV5 , is that despite the twists, the galaxies' orientations at their break radii are fairly aligned with the spheroid's outer orientation except for five galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC NGC 3842, NGC 4589, NGC 5061 and NGC 6849) with inner position angles which are twisted by 27^ from the outer position angle. NGC 0741, NGC 3842 and NGC 6849 show strong isophotal twists at their break radii coincident with local minima in their ellipticities, but for NGC 5061 the strong 30^ twist and the ellipticity minimum which occurs between 3 and 10 may be a result of tidal effects (Tal et al. \ 2009). While the correlation between high twist amplitudes and low isophote ellipticities has already been known for some time (e.g., Galleta 1980; Nieto et al. \ 1992; Rahman Shandarin 2004), these four galaxies have interesting features discussed in the literature. NGC 0741 is a nucleated galaxy (e.g., Dullo Graham 2012), NGC 3842 is a bright cluster galaxy that has an ultramassive black hole ( M_ BH = 9.7 10^ 9 M_ ) with a 1 .2 radius of influence (McConnell et al. \ 2011) whereas the S0 galaxy NGC 6849 has an inner bar which manifests as a peak in the ellipiticity profile (see Dullo Graham 2013). The elliptical galaxy NGC 5061 has the largest S ersic index and the biggest M_ def /M_ BH ratio from the Dullo Graham (2014) sample, also, Tal et al. \ (2009) identified a prominent tidal tail associated with this galaxy.

gmw_00008719.name: NGC 4700
gmw_00008719.overview: NGC 4700 is an edge-on spiral galaxy featuring an irregular stellar disk and patchy dust morphology [arXiv:0804.3819]. It exhibits complex H-alpha emission, with faint filaments protruding into the halo and radio continuum emission extending beyond the disk plane [arXiv:0804.3819, arXiv:astro-ph/0607102].

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 4700 as an irregular, patchy-dust edge-on spiral with central stellar concentrations, complex H emission, halo filaments, and possible lobe-like ejected material.

quotes.quote: The stellar disk of NGC 4700 looks more irregular than that of NGC 4634. The dust morphology is patchy, rather than forming a continuous band along the disk plane. The most prominent star concentrations are found in the central area.

The H image reveals a very complex morphology. There are numerous faint filaments protruding into the halo region. The circumnuclear region does not host strong SF activity, and it seems as if the bulk of the emission has been ejected into two lobe regions. These are, despite the higher densities, located within the galactic plane (roughly aligned with the disk plane) and not perpendicular to it. Nonetheless, considerable structure (e.g., filaments) is also revealed perpendicular to the disk north and south of the nuclear region.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4700 in its radio-halo sample, using 1.4 GHz and H imaging and showing associated disk and extraplanar radio/optical-line emission.

quotes.quote: The 1.4 GHz maps of NGC 4700 and NGC 7090 are from Dahlem et al. (2001).
The H images of NGC 4700 and NGC 7090 originate from Rossa and Dettmar (2003a,b).

An example of a (previously unpublished) dataset used here is provided in Fig. fig:n4700over , where we display a contour overlay of a new VLA 1.49 GHz continuum map of NGC 4700 on our H line image. One can see directly related radio continuum and optical line emission, not only in the disk plane, but also beyond.

gmw_00008720.name: KK 208
gmw_00008720.overview: KK 208 is a northern stellar stream located in the outer gas disk of the galaxy M 83 [arXiv:1607.03365]. Its morphology and kinematics are consistent with a past tidal interaction, suggesting it may be the remnant of an interloper responsible for the disturbance [arXiv:1607.03365].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KK 208 as a northern stellar stream in M 83's outer gas disk and suggests it may be the remnant interloper behind the disturbance.

quotes.quote: There is also an object present in the outer \ disk, cataloged by karachentseva_karachentsev_1998 as KK 208. We return to this interesting object in subsection:environment .

The stellar stream that has previously been cataloged as KK 208 is visible in Figure figure:coldens . The stream's shape and extent is outlined for clarity in the optical image, as well as in the \ column density map, velocity field, and velocity dispersion map.

The morphology and kinematics of the extended \ are consistent with a tidal interaction in the past, and we suggest that the northern stellar stream (previously cataloged as KK 208) may be the remnant of the interloper responsible for the disturbance.

gmw_00008721.name: C1
gmw_00008721.overview: C1 is a massive post-starburst galaxy that exhibits a long tail spanning over 80 kiloparsecs without a stellar counterpart [arXiv:1309.2587]. This tail was likely created by ram-pressure stripping, while the galaxy itself shows compact central star formation [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C1 is described as a massive post-starburst EIG parent with a long starless tail likely produced by ram-pressure stripping and compact central star formation.

quotes.quote: The EIG from C1 was a long ( > 80 kpc) tail without a stellar counterpart. Moreover no obvious distortion of the disk is recognized. The tail would have been made by RPS. As mentioned in Section sec:nature , C1 was classified as a post-starburst (k+a) galaxy Oemler2009 . Nakata2014 measured the equivalent width of \ of C1 as EW( )=11.3 ; they classified it as e(a), emission with strong Balmer absorption.

gmw_00008722.name: C2
gmw_00008722.overview: C2 is a galaxy that exhibits an asymmetry in its stellar distribution [arXiv:1309.2587]. It features a clumpy, extended emission gas structure with a bright star-forming region, as well as strong mid-infrared emission associated with an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C2 has a clumpy extended emission feature with an H-region-like bright spot, stellar asymmetry, and strong mid-infrared emission attributed to an AGN rather than the EIG.

quotes.quote: The EIG from C2 includes a bright spot near the galaxy, and extended and clumpy \ emission to the northeast. The line ratios from our FOCAS spectrum showed that the bright spot would be an \ region (Figure fig:BPT ). In the ACS image in F814W shows a sign of stars along the \ extension. This resembles the fireball features found in the Coma cluster Yoshida2008,Yoshida2012 .

Strong 24 m emission of C2 (3.5 mJy) would be because of the AGN. Since the EIG has a spatial offset from the core, and the spectrum of the spot in the EIG shows signs of \ region, this EIG is unlikely to be of AGN origin. C2 shows an asymmetry of stellar distribution. On the other hand, it has extended \ emission that seems to have no association with stellar emission.

gmw_00008723.name: C3
gmw_00008723.overview: C3 is a disturbed spiral galaxy with loose spiral arms that exhibit emission regions [arXiv:1309.2587]. The galaxy is likely interacting with a neighboring spiral galaxy located 50 kiloparsecs to its northeast and is classified as a member of the North subcluster [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C3 is described as a disturbed spiral with emission along loose arms, likely interacting with a nearby spiral and belonging to the North subcluster.

quotes.quote: C3 shows loose and disturbed spiral arms, and the \ emitting regions along the arms. Its appearance implies that it is interacting with a neighbor spiral at northeast. The projected distance between them is 50 kpc. EIG may have been created by the interaction, though the redshift of the neighbor is currently not available. C3 has a redshift of z=0.3956, and classified as a member of North subcluster by Oemler2009 .

gmw_00008724.name: C4
gmw_00008724.overview: C4 is a galaxy located in the North subcluster [arXiv:1309.2587]. It features a flocculent disk and a smooth emission extension toward the southeast, which may be the result of an interaction with the galaxy C5 [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C4 is presented as a North-subcluster galaxy with a flocculent disk and smooth emission extension that may come from interaction with C5.

quotes.quote: C4 shows a flocculent disk, and smooth extension of \ toward the southeast. EIG may be a result of interaction with C5. The difference of the recession velocity of C4 and C5 is somehow as large as 720 km s ^ 1 . Though C4 is not cataloged in Oemler2009 , its position and redshift suggest that it is a member of North subcluster.

gmw_00008725.name: C5
gmw_00008725.overview: The galaxy C5 exhibits a distorted disk and several remote, compact star clusters to its south-southwest [arXiv:1309.2587]. These features, along with its H-alpha emission, may be the result of an interaction with the neighboring galaxy C4 [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C5 shows remote compact clusters, disk distortion, and emission aligned with C4 interaction signatures, while its e(a) spectrum suggests a dust-obscured starburst.

quotes.quote: In the ACS image, C5 shows several remote and compact objects in the south-southwest, which are thought to be star clusters. The direction is the same as the \ extension. The distortion of the disk, the remote star clusters and the \ emission may be a result of the interaction with C4. However, it shows a slight sign of \ emission without stars in the southeast direction.
The spectrum of C5 is classified as e(a) by Oemler2009 . The spectral class is thought to be a dust-obscured starburst Poggianti1999,Dressler1999,Dressler2009,Oemler2009 .

gmw_00008726.name: C6
gmw_00008726.overview: C6 is an emission-line galaxy that may belong to either the North or Core subcluster of its environment [arXiv:1309.2587]. The galaxy exhibits a westward, starless feature with strong Balmer emissions, which may represent a pure gas-stripping event driven by ram pressure [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C6 is described as an emission-line EIG parent whose westward starless feature may be a pure ram-pressure gas-stripping event.

quotes.quote: C6 EIG is barely recognized toward the west. There is no counterpart in F814W image, and therefore it could be a pure gas-stripping event by RPS. FOCAS spectrum showed strong Balmer emissions, ( , , \ and \ emissions), and , \ and a sign of \ 5876 emissions. As shown in Figure fig:zd , C6 could be a member of either the North subcluster or the Core subcluster.

gmw_00008727.name: C7
gmw_00008727.overview: The galaxy C7 features three remote emission regions to its southeast, which resemble features of stripped dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1309.2587]. Its spectrum shows strong Balmer absorption, indicating that the galaxy is currently transitioning from a star-forming phase to a quiescent phase [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C7 has remote emission regions resembling stripped dwarf-galaxy features, transition-phase spectral signatures, and corrected line ratios comparable to H regions.

quotes.quote: C7 Three remote \ regions are recognized at southeast. Its appearance resembles that of some dwarf galaxies in the Coma cluster (e.g., GMP4232) and those found in z=0.2 clusters Cortese2007 . The FOCAS spectrum of the parent galaxy showed \ and \ emissions in absorption, indicating that it is in a transition from a star-forming phase to a quiescent phase. As discussed in Section sec:nature , the strong Balmer absorption would also affect / \ and / , but the diagnostic line ratios after maximum correction are still comparable to those of \ regions. Since the ACS/HST image did not cover C7, it is unclear whether the stellar distribution in the disk shows a distortion or not.

gmw_00008728.name: C8
gmw_00008728.overview: The galaxy C8 is associated with a chain of two galaxies located to its south [arXiv:1309.2587]. Emission follows this southern chain and turns westward into an area with no stellar counterpart, while the northern galaxy of C8 exhibits a post-starburst spectrum [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C8 is associated with a chain of southern galaxies, westward emission without stellar counterpart, and a post-starburst north-galaxy spectrum.

quotes.quote: C8 In the ACS image, a chain of two galaxies is recognized to the south of C8. \ emission follows the chain, and then turns to the west, where no stellar counterpart is seen. In the FOCAS spectrum of C8, the north galaxy shows the typical signatures of a post-starburst galaxy; blue continuum, strong \ absorption, and no emissions.

gmw_00008729.name: C9
gmw_00008729.overview: C9 is a compact spiral galaxy that exhibits a warped disk [arXiv:1309.2587]. It features a spatial extension toward the northeast that lacks a stellar counterpart, which is consistent with ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1309.2587].

sources.summary: C9 is described as a compact warped spiral with northeast emission lacking stellar counterpart and spectral extension consistent with ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: C9 C9 is a compact spiral with a warped disk in the ACS image. The \ extension is toward the northeast without a stellar counterpart, and resembles ram-pressure stripped objects. The parent galaxy showed , , and \ emissions. In FOCAS spectrum, this spatial extension was also recognized in \ and .

gmw_00008730.name: G
aliases_external: foreground lensing galaxy G in PS J0147+4630
PS J0147+4630 deflector / lens galaxy G
gmw_00008730.overview: Galaxy G is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of 0.678 [arXiv:1911.12123, arXiv:2309.04285]. It acts as a gravitational lens for the quadruply imaged background quasar system PS J0147+4630 [arXiv:1911.12123, arXiv:2309.04285].

sources.summary: G is the foreground lensing galaxy in PS J0147+4630; the paper extracts its spectrum, identifies it as early-type, and measures redshift and stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: This recently discovered system mainly consists of four images ABCD of a background quasar around a foreground galaxy G that acts as a gravitational lens. First, we use long--slit spectroscopic data in the Gemini Observatory Archive and a multi--component fitting to accurately resolve the spectra of A, D, and G. The spectral profile of G resembles that of an early--type galaxy at a redshift of 0.678 0.001, which is about 20 higher than the previous estimate.

Here, we concentrate on the optical spectrum of the main lensing galaxy. The spectral slopes of G, as well as its Ca ii H K doublet at about 6600 6660 \ and its G--band around 7225 , are in very good agreement with an early--type galaxy template SDSS spectral template No. 23 at http://classic.sdss.org/dr7/algorithms/spectemplates/index.html at z = 0.678 (see Figure fig:f1 ). After doing an initial estimate for z_ G from the observed absorption features, we accurately measured the lens redshift using the Penalized PiXel--Fitting (pPXF) package https://www-astro.physics.ox.ac.uk/ mxc/software/ 2004PASP..116..138C,2017MNRAS.466..798C . This software compares the spectrum of G and stellar spectra, providing a correction to the initial value of z_ G and measuring the stellar velocity dispersion. From the MILES Library of Stellar Spectra http://miles.iac.es/ 2006MNRAS.371..703S, 2011A&A...532A..95F , we obtained z_ G = 0.678 0.001 and _ G = 313 14 km s ^ 1 (see Figure fig:f2 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies lens galaxy G as an early-type galaxy at z=0.678, constrains its photometric and mass-profile structure, and links its mass slope to Hubble-constant modeling.

quotes.quote: The three brightest images (A, B and C) form an arc that is about 3 \ from the faintest image D, and the main lens galaxy G is located between the bright arc and D. This configuration is clearly seen in the left panel of Figure fig:f1 , which is based on Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) data. Early optical spectra of the system confirmed the gravitational lensing phenomenon and revealed the broad absorption--line nature of the quasar, which has a redshift z_ s 2.36 2017A&A...605L...8L,2018ApJ...859..146R . 2018MNRAS.475.3086L also performed the first attempt to determine the redshift of G from spectroscopic observations with the 8.1 m Gemini North Telescope (GNT). An accurate reanalysis of these GNT data showed that the first estimate of the lens redshift was biased, by enabling better identification of G as an early--type galaxy at z_ l = 0.678 0.001 with stellar velocity dispersion _ l = 313 14 km s ^ 1 2019ApJ...887..126G , in good agreement with the recent measurements of z_ l and _ l by 2023A&A...672A..20M .

Their solution for the lensing mass relies on a lens scenario consisting of a singular power--law ellipsoid (SPLE; describing the gravitational field of the main lens galaxy G) and an external shear (ES; accounting for the gravitational action of other galaxies). While our solution for the mass of the early--type galaxy G is characterised by a convergence a little shallower than isothermal ( < 2; see Table tab:t4 ), Shajib et al.'s and Schmidt et al.'s solutions for the surface mass density are more centrally concentrated ( 2), suggesting this is a key reason to infer such high values of H_0^ model from previous models e.g., 1994RPPh...57..117R, u..symp..117K,2015LRR....18....2J . Time delays are very sensitive to the slope of the mass profile of the main lens galaxy G e.g., u..symp..117K , and the new model incorporates a surface mass density less centrally concentrated than previous ones.

sources.summary: The paper studies PS J0147+4630 as a quadruply imaged broad-absorption-line quasar lens, measuring time delays, microlensing variations, and implications for Hubble-constant modeling.

quotes.quote: The gravitational lens system PS J0147+4630 (Andromeda's Parachute) consists of four quasar images ABCD and a lensing galaxy. We obtained -band light curves of ABCD in the 2017 2022 period from monitoring with two 2--m class telescopes. Applying state--of--the--art curve shifting algorithms to these light curves led to measurements of time delays between images, and the three independent delays relative to image D are accurate enough to be used in cosmological studies (uncertainty of about 4 ): t_ AD = 170.5 7.0, t_ BD = 170.4 6.0, and t_ CD = 177.0 6.5 d, where image D is trailing all the other images. Our finely sampled light curves and some additional fluxes in the years 2010 2013 also demonstrated the presence of significant microlensing variations.

Optical frames from the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System Pan--STARRS; 2016arXiv161205560C led to the serendipitous discovery of the strong gravitational lens system with a quadruply--imaged quasar (quad) PS J0147+4630 2017ApJ...844...90B . Due to its position in the sky and the spatial arrangement of the four quasar images, this quad is also called Andromeda's Parachute e.g., 2018ApJ...859..146R .
